gmw_00006430.name: J1036 + 0221
gmw_00006430.overview: J1036 + 0221 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy in a post-merger state [arXiv:1807.04298]. The system contains two interacting nuclei separated by 2 kpc, a tidal tail, and extended emission likely ionized by shocks [arXiv:1807.04298].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1036 + 0221 as a ULIRG post-merger with two nuclei, a tidal tail, broad absorption, AGN-like line ratios, and shock-ionized extended emission.

quotes.quote: J1036 + 0221 This system (VIII Zw 090) is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy ULIRG; kilerci2014 and a post-merger ellison2013 , i.e. it consists of galaxies which are in the final stages of their interaction. This system consists of two interacting nuclei (separated by 2 kpc) and a tidal tail (see left panel of Fig. fig:j1036_saltspec ). One of the interacting nuclei shows radio continuum emission in our GMRT 1.4 GHz map. We have detected broad ( 450 ) absorption towards this radio source.
But the high log / \ value ( 0.2 ) in the extended emission seen in the two-dimensional spectrum suggests that shocks are the likely source of ionization.

gmw_00006431.name: J1100 + 1002
gmw_00006431.overview: J1100 + 1002, also known as UGC 6081, is a major merger system consisting of two interacting galaxies [arXiv:1807.04298]. The system contains two heavily reddened Seyfert 2 nuclei [arXiv:1711.09936] that host active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1807.04298] and exhibit extended, photoionized emission within their tidal debris [arXiv:1711.09936].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6081 as a dual-Seyfert system whose tidal debris contains extended AGN-ionized emission, with no strong evidence that either nucleus has faded.

quotes.quote: UGC 6081 : This pair has two heavily reddened Sy 2 nuclei and shows AGN ionization along an extent of 58" (41 kpc). The extended emission might fall in the same category of radial extent > 10 kpc as the ``Voorwerpjes" mnras2012 , depending on which of the AGN lights it up. We do not have the three-dimensional information to tell which is responsible (if indeed one dominates in the tidal debris seen at large radii).

The tunable-filter data are compared in Fig. fig-ugc6081 . They reveal rich structure around both galaxies, with emission extending 25 (18 kpc) from both AGN. The emission regions occur within the tidal debris and loop seen in the starlight continuum, fitting with a common pattern of kinematically quiescent EELRs as photoionized tidal tails.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1100 + 1002 as a major merger with two radio-emitting galaxies, confirmed absorption toward the northwest radio source, central AGN ionization, and extended emission.

quotes.quote: J1100 + 1002 This system, UGC 06081, consists of two galaxies (nuclei separated by 11 kpc) undergoing a major merger. Since this system has two radio sources (separation 16'' ), which were not resolved in the previous single dish spectral line observations, the source of the absorption had remained ambiguous. Using our interferometric spectral line observations, we confirm that the absorption arises towards the stronger radio source in the north-west (see panel () of Fig. fig:overlay ). No absorption is detected towards the weaker radio source in the south-east (see Table tab:results ).
Emission line ratio diagnostic BPT diagram; baldwin1981 indicates that the gas in the central regions of both the galaxies is likely to be ionized by an AGN following kewley2001,kauffmann2003 .

gmw_00006432.name: J1108 1015
gmw_00006432.overview: J1108 1015 is a system consisting of two interacting elliptical galaxies with a projected nuclear separation of 6 kpc [arXiv:1807.04298]. Both galaxies are non-star-forming, and the system exhibits resolved radio emission [arXiv:1807.04298].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1108 1015 as two interacting, non-star-forming elliptical galaxies with resolved radio emission, detected H I emission, and no absorption toward continuum components.

quotes.quote: J1108 1015 This system, also known as NGC 3537, consists of two interacting elliptical galaxies, with their nuclei at a projected separation of 6 kpc. Absorption from , , \ and \ are detected in our SALT long-slit spectra of both the galaxies (see Fig. fig:j1108_saltspec ), indicating that these galaxies are non-star-forming. We estimate the redshift of the two galaxies marked `A' and `B' in Fig. fig:j1108_saltspec , as 0.0273 0.0003 and 0.0245 0.0003, respectively.

The peak of the \ absorption lines from the two galaxies are separated by 800 . We do not detect any extended emission between the two galaxies in the two-dimensional spectra. The radio emission is resolved in our GMRT 1.4 GHz continuum map, with the peak emission accounting for only 7 of the total emission.

gmw_00006433.name: J1214 + 2931
gmw_00006433.overview: J1214 + 2931, also known as Was 49, is an isolated dual active galactic nucleus system undergoing a minor merger [arXiv:1807.04298, arXiv:1612.03163]. The system consists of the disk galaxy Was 49a and a highly luminous, radio-loud companion Seyfert 2 nucleus designated Was 49b [arXiv:1807.04298, arXiv:astro-ph/0002460].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1214 + 2931 as the Was 49 dual-AGN system, with a disk galaxy, radio-loud companion AGN, velocity offset, and no detected absorption.

quotes.quote: J1214 + 2931 This system was observed as part of our `Miscellaneous Sample' of quasar-galaxy-pairs dutta2017a . Also known as Was 49, this is an isolated, dual AGN system consisting of the disk galaxy Was 49a, with a low luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus, and the Type 2 AGN Was 49b, corotating at a projected radial separation of 8 kpc from the nucleus of Was 49a moran1992 . The velocities of the optical emission lines from the two nuclei differ by 70 .

The extreme luminosity of Was 49b is unusual for an AGN in the smaller galaxy of a minor-merger system secrest2017 . Was 49b is also radio-loud, and its radio emission is resolved in our GMRT 1.4 GHz continuum map. We do not detect absorption towards any of the continuum points, which leads to \ 2 10^ 20 , for \ = 100 K and \ = 1, towards the continuum peak.

sources.summary: The paper describes WAS 49b as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with an unusual flat X-ray spectrum, possible Compton-thin behavior, and a flat contribution attributed to hot intracluster gas.

quotes.quote: WAS 49b The Seyfert 2 galaxy WAS 49b presents a very unusual X-ray spectrum. A good analytical fit is provided by a single powerlaw with photon index =0.5 , a very low value typical of reflection-dominated spectra. In contrast with this interpretation, the iron line is only marginally detected with an equivalent width EW 250 200 eV and the 2-10 keV flux is relatively high with respect to the infrared emission (F _ 2-10 = 1.2 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ), thus indicating that the source is Compton - thin. A similar good fit is obtained by a model composed by a powerlaw with =1.7 absorbed by a column density N_H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and a flat ( =0.6 ) powerlaw with a 1 keV normalization equal to 1/6 that of the first component. Since WAS 49b is part of a triple sistem, we suggest that this flat contribution is due to the diffuse emission of the hot intra-cluster gas.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Was 49 as an isolated dual-AGN minor merger whose off-nuclear dominant AGN has a large black hole in a low-mass, dwarf-elliptical-like stellar counterpart.

quotes.quote: We present a combined morphological and X-ray analysis of Was 49, an isolated, dual AGN system notable for the presence of a dominant AGN in the disk of the primary galaxy , at a projected radial distance of 8 kpc from the nucleus. Using X-ray data from , , and , we find that this AGN has a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 2 10^ 45 , with a black hole mass of M_ BH =1.3^ +2.9 _ 0.9 10^8 . Despite its large mass, our analysis of optical data from the Discovery Channel Telescope shows that the supermassive black hole is hosted by a stellar counterpart with a mass of only 5.6^ +4.9 _ 2.6 10^9 , making the SMBH potentially larger than expected from SMBH-galaxy scaling relations, and the stellar counterpart exhibits a morphology that is consistent with dwarf elliptical galaxies. Our analysis of the system in the and K bands indicates that Was 49 is a minor merger, with a mass ratio of to between 1:7 and 1:15.

sources.summary: The paper calls Was 49 the best low-redshift cross-ionization candidate, with AGN-ionized slit positions and extended [O III] around the AGN and companion disc.

quotes.quote: Was 49 : Identified in this context by , this may remain the best low-redshift cross-ionization candidate. Detailed study of the AGN by stresses its high luminosity and implied black-hole mass relative to the very modest stellar mass of the host galaxy. The [ O III ] structure around the AGN is elongated perpendicular to the line between the galaxies (Fig. fig-was49o3 ); the images in show that part of this emission is in a single bright discrete source just northwest of the AGN.

All the locations along our spectroscopic slits in this pair are AGN-ionized except the nucleus of the large companion galaxy; the AGN emission is largely continuous with the portion of the large galaxy's rotation curve sampled in our data.

gmw_00006434.name: J2054 + 0041
gmw_00006434.overview: J2054 + 0041 is a merging system at z ~ 0.2 that consists of two interacting galaxies with a projected separation of 11 kpc [arXiv:1807.04298]. One of these galaxies is the flat-spectrum radio source PKS 2052 + 005, which exhibits broad multi-component absorption tracing diffuse circumnuclear gas [arXiv:1807.04298].

sources.summary: The paper finds J2054 + 0041 is a merging system, not merely a quasar-galaxy pair, with two interacting galaxies and broad multicomponent absorption toward the radio source.

quotes.quote: J2054 + 0041 This system was initially selected as a quasar-galaxy-pair to study the cold gas around low- z galaxies `Miscellaneous Sample' of dutta2017a . However, our SALT long-slit observations revealed it to be a merging system. It consists of two interacting galaxies at z 0.2 , at a projected separation of 11 kpc. One of them is the radio source PKS 2052 + 005, which is classified as a flat-spectrum radio source healey2007 . We detect strong and broad multi-component absorption towards the radio source using VLA.
Thus, the \ absorption is most likely tracing the diffuse gas in the circumnuclear region of this merger.

gmw_00006435.name: C4-09
gmw_00006435.overview: C4-09 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 3.2 [arXiv:0907.4380, arXiv:astro-ph/0001050]. The source consists of four bright emission knots [arXiv:0907.4380] and exhibits strong [O III] emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0001050].

sources.summary: The paper describes C4-09 as a four-knot Lyman-break source with double-peaked emission, absorption redshift constraints, spatially extended blue emission, and ambiguous outflow versus multi-knot dynamics.

quotes.quote: C4-09 Object C4-09 in the catalog of ste96b is a remarkable source consisting of four bright knots of emission with nearly identical colors, all within an area barely more than 1 \ across. zep97 investigated and finally rejected the source as a possible gravitational lens system. As reported by ste96b , the spectrum shows strong emission with two peaks at 5118.7 and 5132.6 . Continuum is clearly detected redward of the emission but not blueward, supporting the interpretation of the emission lines as \ at redshifts 3.211 and 3.222, respectively, slightly lower than the z=3.226 reported by ste96b . Weak absorption lines are seen at 5314, 5489, 5624, and 5633 , corresponding to , , , and , respectively at an average absorption redshift z=3.216 , between the redshifts of the emission line peaks.

sources.summary: The paper describes E as a z~3.2 HDF-N target with strong [O iii] emission, lying above local starburst galaxies and in a region similar to Lyman-break galaxies.

quotes.quote: The SEDs of other two objects (D,E) for [ O iii ] are also shown in figure 5b.

In the case of the [ O iii ] emission line (figure 6b), objects D and E are located in a higher region compared with the distribution of the local starburst galaxies, and reside in a region similar to other Lyman-break galaxies.

We also detected strong [ O iii ] emission lines from both targets with the known spectroscopic redshift of z 3.2. We infer that the high- z star-forming galaxy tends to show strong [ O iii ] emission, just like the Lyman-break galaxies reported by Pettini et. al (1998).

gmw_00006436.name: C4-06
gmw_00006436.overview: C4-06 is a bright, large high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:0907.4380]. It exhibits a linear structure roughly 1.3 arcseconds in extent, containing two major knots and several sub-knots [arXiv:0907.4380].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C4-06 as the bright, extended Hot Dog Galaxy, reporting resolved continuum, absorption-line redshift evidence, and no extractable dynamical information.

quotes.quote: C4-06 C4-06 from the catalog of Steidel et al. (1996) is one of the brightest, largest sources in our sample. The HDF image shows a linear structure -- dubbed the ``Hot Dog Galaxy'' bun01 - roughly 1 3 \ in extent, with two major knots and several sub-knots. The LRIS mask slit was aligned along that structure. Strong, spatially resolved continuum is clearly detected in the two-dimensional spectrum, although it appears as a single spatially continuous source, rather than the separate knots seen with HST resolution.

gmw_00006437.name: hd4 1006 0680
gmw_00006437.overview: The high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy hd4 1006 0680 is located at a redshift of z=2.969 [arXiv:0907.4380]. It appears as a very close pair of emission knots with a separation of only 0.3 arcseconds [arXiv:0907.4380].

sources.summary: The paper treats hd4 1006 0680 as a close-pair Lyman-break galaxy, measuring double emission peaks, spatial offsets, redshift, velocity structure, and a system dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: hd4 1006 0680 This galaxy appears as a very close pair in the HDF image, with a separation of only 0 3 . coh00 report a redshift for the source z=2.969 . Our mask slitlet was aligned along the same PA, 73 , as the two emission knots; however, 1 \ ground-based seeing certainly blurs the two into a single unresolved source in our LRIS observations. The spectrum shows very strong emission at 4823.2 and weaker emission at 4812.3 . The stronger line appears extended both spatially and spectrally: the spatial FWHM of the line is 1 2, comparable to the seeing, but the emission profile's faint wings extend to cover 3 3.

gmw_00006438.name: hd2 0698 1297
gmw_00006438.overview: The galaxy hd2 0698 1297 is a Lyman-break system consisting of two distinct knots embedded in an asymmetrical blob of diffuse emission [arXiv:0907.4380]. This system is paired with the nearby unresolved source hd2 0705 1366 [arXiv:0907.4380].

sources.summary: The paper describes hd2 0698 1297 as a two-knot diffuse Lyman-break system paired with hd2 0705 1366, deriving redshift, line-width limits, and a large system dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: hd2 0698 1297 hd2 0698 1297 consists of two distinct knots embedded in an asymmetrical blob of diffuse emission. The knots are 0 5 apart, and the diffuse emission visible in the HDF F814W image extends for 1 2. The system is 2 7 from hd2 0705 1366, an unresolved point source (measured FWHM= 0 14) in the HDF image. Because of slit mask orientation constraints, we observed this pair at two different position angles: for 25.5 ks at 42 , and for 10.2 ks at 58 . Due to the close proximity of the two sources, the 1 1-wide slitlets at both PAs include emission from both targets.

gmw_00006439.name: hd3 1633 1909
gmw_00006439.overview: The high-redshift galaxy hd3 1633 1909 is an elongated, wispy Lyman-break source [arXiv:0907.4380]. It consists of two clumpy wings separated by a 0.5-arcsecond gap and aligned roughly east-west [arXiv:0907.4380].

sources.summary: The paper describes hd3 1633 1909 as an elongated clumpy high-redshift Lyman-break source, reporting faint continuum, a single emission line, redshift, and linewidth.

quotes.quote: hd3 1633 1909 An elongated, wispy source consisting of two clumpy wings extending over 2 1, with a 0 5 gap separating them, and aligned roughly east-west. The source's total isophotal colors in the HDF Vesion 1 catalog satisfy the B dropout criteria of low97 . However, in the HDF Version 2 catalog, the two sides' colors are significantly different, with the western side bluer by 1.30 mag in B_ 450,AB V_ 606,AB and 0.52 mag in V_ 606,AB I_ 814,AB . Furthermore, in the Version 2 catalog photometry, neither component individually satisfies either the U dropout or the B dropout criterion. Our LRIS slit covered both wings, as well as the source hd3 1824 1945 7 4 away and two other sources, one on either end of the slit.

gmw_00006440.name: NGC 4786
gmw_00006440.overview: NGC 4786 is a giant elliptical galaxy classified as a cD pec galaxy [arXiv:2403.00181]. Located at an adopted luminosity distance of 64.1 Mpc, its central region contains a rotating circumnuclear disk of molecular gas [arXiv:2403.00181].

sources.summary: The paper measures a gas-dynamical black hole mass for NGC 4786, describing its circumnuclear disk, rotating molecular gas, dust modeling, and distance assumptions.

quotes.quote: We present molecular gas-dynamical mass measurements of the central black holes in the giant elliptical galaxies NGC 4786 and NGC 5193, based on CO(2 1) observations from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) and Hubble Space Telescope near-infrared imaging. The central region in each galaxy contains a circumnuclear disk that exhibits orderly rotation with projected line-of-sight velocities of 270 km s^ 1 . We build gas-dynamical models for the rotating disk in each galaxy and fit them directly to the ALMA data cubes. At 0 31 resolution, the ALMA observations do not fully resolve the black hole sphere of influence (SOI), and neither galaxy exhibits a central rise in rotation speed, indicating that emission from deep within the SOI is not detected.

NGC 4786 is classified as a cD pec galaxy in the Third Reference Catalog of Bright Galaxies (RC3; 1991rc3..book.....D ). Redshift-independent distances for this galaxy in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu (NED) are between 65 - 75 Mpc when using a CDM cosmology with H_0 = 67.8 Mpc ^ 1 . We adopt a Hubble-flow distance, using a value of H_0 = 73 Mpc ^ 1 based on more recent estimates of H_0 from nearby ( <100 Mpc) galaxies 2021ApJ...911...65B,2022ApJ...934L...7R,2022ApJ...935...83K , a recessional velocity of cz = 4623 from preliminary gas-dynamical models, _M = 0.31 , and _ = 0.69 . These assumptions set a luminosity distance of 64.1 Mpc, an angular diameter distance of 62.1 Mpc, and an angular scale where 1 corresponds to 301 pc.

gmw_00006441.name: NGC 5193
gmw_00006441.overview: NGC 5193 is a giant elliptical galaxy classified as an E pec galaxy [arXiv:2403.00181]. It contains a rotating circumnuclear disk of molecular gas in its central region and is associated with the Abell 3565 cluster [arXiv:2403.00181].

sources.summary: The paper measures a gas-dynamical black hole mass for NGC 5193, describing its early-type classification, cluster association, distance estimates, and dust-driven systematics.

quotes.quote: We present molecular gas-dynamical mass measurements of the central black holes in the giant elliptical galaxies NGC 4786 and NGC 5193, based on CO(2 1) observations from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) and Hubble Space Telescope near-infrared imaging. The central region in each galaxy contains a circumnuclear disk that exhibits orderly rotation with projected line-of-sight velocities of 270 km s^ 1 . We build gas-dynamical models for the rotating disk in each galaxy and fit them directly to the ALMA data cubes. At 0 31 resolution, the ALMA observations do not fully resolve the black hole sphere of influence (SOI), and neither galaxy exhibits a central rise in rotation speed, indicating that emission from deep within the SOI is not detected.

NGC 5193 is classified as an E pec galaxy by RC3. In past literature, this galaxy has been associated with and sometimes identified as the brightest cluster galaxy in the Abell 3560 1989ApJS...70....1A cluster with a recessional velocity of cz 3800 - 4000 1998ApJ...499..577L,2002ApJ...567..294O . However, and instead associate NGC 5193 with the Abell 3565 cluster based on findings from that indicate Abell 3560 has a recessional velocity of cz 14,850 . This implies that Abell 3560 is a background galaxy cluster and that NGC 5193 is not a member.

gmw_00006442.name: NGC1229
gmw_00006442.overview: NGC1229 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1809.03747]. Observations indicate that the source is unambiguously heavily obscured, with a column density that is consistent with being Compton-thick within the errors [arXiv:1809.03747].

sources.summary: The NuSTAR analysis finds NGC1229 heavily obscured, consistent with being Compton-thick within errors, but with a best-fit column density below the formal threshold.

quotes.quote: According to these authors four objects appear to have column densities somewhat lower than 10^ 24 cm^ 2 . These are NGC1194, NGC6232, NGC1229 and 2MASSXJ09235371-3141305. According to our NuSTAR analysis, NGC1194 is above the Compton-thick threshold while NGC1229 and 2MASSXJ09235371-3141305 are consistent with being Compton-thick within the errors. In the case of NGC1229, although the source is unambiguously heavily obscured ( N_H 6^ +2.6 _ 1.4 10^ 23 cm^ 2 ) the column density derived lies well below the Compton-thick limit.

gmw_00006443.name: HE 0413-4031
gmw_00006443.overview: HE 0413-4031 is a highly luminous active galactic nucleus, or quasar, located at a redshift of z = 1.389 [arXiv:2005.09071]. Spectroscopic monitoring reveals that its magnesium II broad line emission varies in response to changes in the continuum [arXiv:2005.09071].

sources.summary: The paper monitors quasar HE 0413-4031, measures its MgII reverberation lag, interprets a shortened time delay from high accretion, and reports line-continuum variability.

quotes.quote: We present the monitoring of the AGN continuum and MgII broad line emission for the quasar HE 0413-4031 ( z=1.38 ) based on the six-year monitoring by the South African Large Telescope (SALT). We managed to estimate a time-delay of 302.6^ +28.7 _ 33.1 days in the rest frame of the source using seven different methods: interpolated cross-correlation function (ICCF), discrete correlation function (DCF), z transformed DCF, JAVELIN, two estimators of data regularity (Von Neumann, Bartels), and ^2 method. This time-delay is below the value expected from the standard radius-luminosity relation. However, based on the monochromatic luminosity of the source and the SED modelling, we interpret this departure as the shortening of the time-delay due to the higher accretion rate of the source, with the inferred Eddington ratio of 0.4 .

The MgII line luminosity of HE 0413-4031 responds to the continuum variability as L_ line L_ cont ^ 0.43 0.10 , which is consistent with the light-travel distance of the location of MgII emission at R_ out 10^ 18 cm . The quasar HE 0413-4031, located at redshift z = 1.389 according to the NED database, is a very bright source: veron_cetty2001 report the V mag of M_ V =16.5 mag. Its position on the sky (04h 15m 14s; -40 ^ 23' 41") made it a very good target for the spectroscopic monitoring with the SALT. The source has been monitored since 21 Jan 2013 till August 8, 2019.

gmw_00006444.name: J2312+0919
gmw_00006444.overview: J2312+0919 is a Fanaroff-Riley II high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:2107.06896, arXiv:1903.10768] located at a redshift of 0.233 [arXiv:2107.06896]. It features a central point-like nuclear source [arXiv:astro-ph/0412388] and a compact emission line region that exhibits rotation and high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1903.10768].

sources.summary: The paper flags J2312+0919 as a strong milli-lens candidate, notes its optical redshift, and identifies it with the FRII high-excitation radio galaxy 3C 456.

quotes.quote: Among the 40 candidates, the two sources which best satisfy these criteria are J0527+1743, and J2312+0919. A third promising source is J1143+1834, for which no spectral index information is available. We also investigated the optical counterparts of the 40 lens candidates, using the publicly available optical catalogs in Aladin Sky Atlas . Only 15 sources have associated optical counterparts and among them, only 4 sources (J0024-4202, J0210-2213, J2214-2521, J2312+0919) have known redshifts.
J2312+0919 : Associated with a Fanaroff-Riley II (FRII) - high excitation radio galaxy, 3C 456, e.g., Macconi2020 ; the optical counterpart is located at redshift z=0.233.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 456 as an FR II/HEG source with compact emission lines confined within 10 kpc, clear rotation, and high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: 3C 456: FR II/HEG, 1 = 3.74 kpc. The emission line region in this source is compact, confined within 10 kpc. Nonetheless, it clearly shows rotation around PA 45 ^ and large velocity dispersion up to 350 .

sources.summary: The paper compares 3CR 456 with NICMOS imaging, finding a clear nuclear point source and a K-band luminosity estimate from color-gradient modeling that agrees with the HST value.

quotes.quote: For 3CR 456 and 3CR 460 NICMOS-NIC2 images are available in the public archive at STScI. A central point-like source is clearly present in 3CR 456 with a luminosity of 10 ^ 43.63 0.09 at 2.05 m. After modeling the outer color gradient, we estimated L_ K^ = 10 ^ 43.46 0.04 , within 2 from the HST value.

gmw_00006445.name: SDSS J002101.03+005248.1
gmw_00006445.overview: SDSS J002101.03+005248.1 is a compact H II galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244]. It exhibits unusually broad emission lines, which cause the H-alpha and [N II] lines to blend in its spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J002101.03+005248.1 as a compact HII galaxy with unusually broad lines, blended H and [N ii] emission, and larger WHT-SDSS flux differences than the other targets.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of SDSS J002101.03+005248.1 presents very wide lines (FWHM 7.5 \ for 6600 ) for the expected velocity dispersion in a low mass galaxy of this type. This could be due to an intrinsic velocity dispersion in this object, the interaction with another unobservable object or a projection effect on the line of sight. As a consequence, lines that should be resolved are blended in the spectrum of SDSS J002101.03+005248.1. Such is the case of H and [ N ii ] 6548 \ emission lines.

Strong emission line fluxes relative to H measured on WHT and SDSS spectra differ by less than 10 for SDSS J003218.60+150014.2 and SDSS J162410.11-002202.5 and about 30 for SDSS J002101.03+005248.1. In fact, given the difference in aperture between both sets of observations, 0.5 and 3 arcsec for WHT and SDSS respectively, some differences are to be expected.

gmw_00006446.name: SDSS J003218.60+150014.2
gmw_00006446.overview: SDSS J003218.60+150014.2 is a compact emission-line starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244]. Its optical spectrum exhibits strong underlying stellar absorption, which is visible in the wings of its Balmer lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J003218.60+150014.2 to illustrate strong underlying stellar absorption, derives its abundances with an assumed [N ii] temperature, and finds the closest WHT-SDSS agreement for this compact object.

quotes.quote: The upper panel of Fig. under shows an example of this effect for the Balmer lines (H13 to H ) on an enlargement of the spectrum of SDSS J003218.60+150014.2, the object that presents the most relevant and appreciable absorption lines . The pseudo-continuum used to measure the line fluxes is also shown. We can clearly see the wings of the absorption lines implying that, even though we have used a pseudo-continuum, there is an absorbed fraction of the emitted flux that we are not able to measure with an acceptable accuracy (see discussion in D iaz 1988). In order to quantify the effect of the underlying absorption on the measured emission line intensities, we have performed a multi-gaussian fit to the absorption and emission components seen in this galaxy. The difference between the measurements of the absorption subtracted lines and the ones obtained with the use of the pseudo-continuum is, for all Balmer lines, within the observational errors and, in fact, the additional fractional error introduced by the subtraction of the absorption component is almost inappreciable for the stronger lines.

In the case of SDSS J003218.60+150014.2, the [ N ii ] 5755 \ line could not be measured and therefore we have assumed that T( [ N ii ] ) is equal to T( [ O ii ] ) and we have derived directly the N/O ratio according to the expression given in . The best agreement between the two sets of measurements is found for SDSS J003218.60+150014.2, which is probably the more compact object, as evidenced by the difference in the measured H fluxes which is only a factor of 2 and the close agreement between the measured equivalent widths on the two spectra.

gmw_00006447.name: SDSS J162410.11-002202.5
gmw_00006447.overview: SDSS J162410.11-002202.5 is classified as an H II emission-line galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244]. Its strong emission line fluxes measured on WHT and SDSS spectra differ by less than 10 percent [arXiv:astro-ph/0608244].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J162410.11-002202.5 as one of three HII galaxies, noting an atmospheric-band-affected [S iii] line, direct nitrogen-temperature work, and close WHT-SDSS flux agreement.

quotes.quote: The only exception is the emission line [ S iii ] 9069 for SDSS J162410.11-002202.5 which is affected by the strong narrow water-vapor lines present in the 9300 9500 wavelength region 1985MNRAS.212..737D . We have listed the value of the measurement of this line in table inten3 , but all the physical parameters depending on its intensity were calculated using the theoretical ratio between this line and [ S iii ] 9532, I(9069) 2.44 I(9532) 1989agna.book.....O .

The ionic abundance of nitrogen, N ^ + /H ^ + has been derived from the intensities of the 6548, 6584 \ lines and the measured electron temperature of [ N ii ] for objects SDSS J002101.03+005248.1 and SDSS J162410.11-002202.5. Then, the N/O abundance has been derived under the assumption that N O = N^+ O^+
Strong emission line fluxes relative to H measured on WHT and SDSS spectra differ by less than 10 for SDSS J003218.60+150014.2 and SDSS J162410.11-002202.5 and about 30 for SDSS J002101.03+005248.1.

gmw_00006448.name: UGC05414
gmw_00006448.overview: UGC05414 is a rotationally supported irregular galaxy classified with an IABm morphology [arXiv:2206.11431]. Despite its irregular structure, the radial mass profile of its dark matter scales with its luminous mass to produce a linear relation [arXiv:2206.11431].

sources.summary: The paper compares UGC05414 with NGC4636, emphasizing that despite its rotationally supported irregular morphology and smaller scale, its enclosed dark-to-luminous mass also scales linearly.

quotes.quote: For NGC1705 and UGC05414, panels (a) and (d) respectively, the mass profiles are dissimilar. Never the less, the ratio of enclosed dark to luminous mass scales as in both galaxies. By contrast, the mass profiles are similar for UGC05414, Fig. fig:dmfig2 (d), and for NGC4636, Fig. fig:dmfig1 . NGC4636, however, is a pressure supported elliptical of E0 morphology, whereas UGC05414 is a rotationally supported irregular of IABm morphology. The radius of NGC4636 exceeds that of UGC05414 by a factor of 12, and its mass exceeds that of UGC05414 by a factor > 2 dex.

None the less, for both galaxies, the radial scaling of enclosed dark to luminous mass follows a linear relation. Consistent with NGC4636 (Fig. fig:dmfig1 ), in each galaxy of Fig. fig:dmfig2 , the radial mass profile of dark matter scales with luminous mass to produce a linear scaling of enclosed dark to luminous mass.

gmw_00006449.name: J0031.1-6642
gmw_00006449.overview: J0031.1-6642 is a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio source featuring a bright, star-like core aligned with two hotspot-like lobes [arXiv:1202.4516]. No radio bridge connects the core to the northern hotspot, and a relic-like extended feature lies offset from the radio axis to the south [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0031.1-6642 as an FR-II radio source with aligned hotspot-like lobes, no bridge to the northern hotspot, and a southern relic-like feature offset from the radio axis.

quotes.quote: J0031.1-6642 (Fig. 23): A 66 arcsec FR-II radio source with four components. The bright core is identified with a star-like object. Towards the south is a hotspot-like lobe and to the north (at much larger separation than the southern hotspot) is another hotspot-like lobe. All three are aligned. There is no bridge connecting the northern hotspot to the core.

Interestingly, to the south of the closer, southern hotspot is a relic-like extended feature (with no galaxy identification). This extended source is oriented at an angle with respect to the radio axis formed by the core and the two hotspots.

gmw_00006450.name: J0031.8-6727
gmw_00006450.overview: J0031.8-6727 is a giant radio quasar with a star-like optical core that exhibits broad emission lines [arXiv:1202.4516]. The source is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy, featuring asymmetric edge-brightened lobes and a faint fourth component aligned along its radio axis [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0031.8-6727 as a restarted 845 kpc giant radio quasar with a bright core, asymmetric lobes, a faint aligned fourth component, and broad emission lines.

quotes.quote: J0031.8-6727 (Fig. 26): This 102 arcsec FR-II source has four components. This is a classic triple with a bright core and two edge-brightened lobes straddling it. This triple is asymmetric in lobe separation.

There is the fourth component to the extreme NE separated from the NE lobe by a gap in emission. This component is faint and lies exactly on the radio axis and on the side of the closer lobe. The core is identified with a star-like object. We have obtained its optical spectrum. It shows at least two broad emission lines.

gmw_00006451.name: J0023.6-6710
gmw_00006451.overview: J0023.6-6710 is a radio source with an aligned triple structure consisting of three separate components [arXiv:1202.4516]. This structure includes a strong central core, a weak southeastern extension, and a strong northwestern component associated with a galaxy group [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper interprets J0023.6-6710 as a likely restarted FR-II radio source whose aligned triple structure includes a strong core, outer components, and a galaxy-group-associated northwest lobe.

quotes.quote: J0023.6-6710 (Fig. 3) is a source with a triple structure. It is a 1 arcmin source with three separate components all aligned along an axis. The strong core is accompanied by a weak extended feature to the south-east and to the north-west by a strong extended source. The NW source is the strongest and it has a jet-like short extension to the NW, again aligned with the main radio axis.

This stronger and more extended NW component is identified with a galaxy that appears to be a member of a close group of four galaxies. We have a redshift measurement available for the central galaxy associated with the NW component: z=0.78 . The redshift we infer for the galaxy associated with the central core is z 0.72 . Given their common axis and the extensions along that axis for two outer sources it appears likely they are all related.

gmw_00006452.name: J0028.9-6631
gmw_00006452.overview: J0028.9-6631 is a restarted Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy featuring a bright core [arXiv:1202.4516]. The core is straddled by two highly dissimilar lobes, consisting of an edge-brightened northern lobe with a hotspot and a weak, elongated southern relic lobe [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0028.9-6631 as a restarted FR-II radio galaxy with a bright core, dissimilar lobes, a hotspot-bearing northern source, and a relic-like southern lobe.

quotes.quote: J0028.9-6631 (Fig. 15) is a source similar to J0023.6-6710. It is triple with a bright core identified with a faint object. The core is straddled by two very dissimilar radio sources. The northern source is edge-brightened and extended towards the core and at high resolution shows a hotspot. This NW lobe is associated with a group of three bright galaxies.

The southern source is a weak elongated relic-like lobe extended towards the core. We have classified the source as a restarted FR-II radio galaxy where one lobe shows a hotspot whereas the opposite lobe is a relic.

gmw_00006453.name: J0034.0-6639
gmw_00006453.overview: J0034.0-6639 is a giant radio galaxy with an angular size of 17 arcminutes [arXiv:1202.4516]. Its giant radio lobes are amorphous and have an extremely low surface brightness, containing a compact feature in the northern lobe that may represent the leading head of a new jet [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J0034.0-6639 as the largest ATLBS angular-size source, a giant radio galaxy with extremely low-surface-brightness amorphous lobes and a possible new jet head.

quotes.quote: The lobes associated with the 17-arcmin giant radio galaxy, J0034.0-6639 (Fig. 29) are the faintest (1 mJy arcmin ^ 2 ; also see next section) followed by the lobes of J0022.7-6652 (Fig. 2) and the 4-arcmin cluster source, J0027.2-6624 (Fig. 11) both with surface brightness about 3.7 mJy arcmin ^ 2 . The lowest surface brightness source component observed in the ATLBS survey are the lobes of the giant radio galaxy J0034.0-6639 (Fig. 29), which have a surface brightness of 1 mJy arcmin ^ 2 that is three times lower than that of the faintest radio structures known: the 3 mJy arcmin ^ 2 cluster-wide relic in Coma.

J0034.0-6639 is the largest angular size source in the ATLBS. It has giant radio lobes that are amorphous over their whole extent except for a relatively small embedded edge-brightened component in the northern lobe. The structure of this compact feature is best represented in the 6 arcsec resolution image. The location of this component is close to midway along the northern lobe and its structure indicates that it might be the leading head of a new jet propagating through the older radio lobe.

gmw_00006454.name: J0057.1-6633
gmw_00006454.overview: J0057.1-6633 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy with edge-darkened lobes and a bright center [arXiv:1202.4516]. Its core is associated with a faint optical counterpart, and the galaxy is accompanied by a possible detached relic emission feature to the southwest [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0057.1-6633 as an FR-I radio galaxy with a possible detached relic feature, very low surface brightness emission, edge-darkened lobes, and a faint optical core counterpart.

quotes.quote: J0057.1-6633 (Fig. 75): This is an interesting lobe-type, 35 arcsec FR-I radio galaxy that is accompanied by a possible relic emission visible as a weak, extended feature to the SW, aligned with the radio axis but separated by a large gap in emission. At 4" resolution it is completely resolved out except for a weak compact source. Several faint galaxies are seen within the extent of the relic feature.

The nature of this SE extended emission region is unclear nor its relation to the FR-I radio source. Its surface brightness is among the lowest known, 2.8 mJy arcmin ^ 2 . The FR-I source is bright at the centre and has edge-darkened lobes. A core is seen at 4" resolution; it is associated with a faint object in R.

gmw_00006455.name: J0102.6-6750
gmw_00006455.overview: J0102.6-6750 is a large Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1202.4516]. It exhibits a highly asymmetric morphology with mismatched emission regions, displaying features that partly resemble a head-tail radio source [arXiv:1202.4516].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0102.6-6750 as a large FR-I radio galaxy with asymmetric morphology, mismatched emission regions, and features that partly resemble a head-tail radio source.

quotes.quote: J0102.6-6750 (Fig. 92) is a large angular size FR-I radio galaxy with a very asymmetric morphology. But for the short jet-like feature to the east and the detached weak source it points to the source could be classified as a head-tail radio soource. Not only are the emission regions on the two sides very different in morphology they are also different in angular extent and total flux.

gmw_00006456.name: TXS 0106 + 612
gmw_00006456.overview: TXS 0106 + 612 is a TeV blazar classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked source [arXiv:1607.02053, arXiv:1709.09342]. First discovered in a radio survey of the Galactic plane [arXiv:1607.02053, arXiv:1709.09342], its X-ray spectrum is well described by a power law with fixed NH and shows no curvature in the studied energy range [arXiv:1709.09342].

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0106+612 as a possible blazar/LSP observed by XMM-Newton during a Fermi flaring state, but with no genuine X-ray IDV claim.

quotes.quote: TXS 0106+612 In SIMBAD this source is tagged as ``possible blazar". But MOJAVE collaboration classified it as LSP. This source is also known as Fermi J0109+6133 (J0109+6134), or GT 0106+613. The source was first discovered by Gregory Taylor (1981) in a radio survey of the Galactic plane.

During the XMM--Newton's pointing in 2010 February, the source was in flaring state detected by Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) (Vandenbroucke et al. 2010). AGILE and Swift satellites also partly covered this event in gamma-ray and X-rays bands, respectively. No significant variation was detected with Swift satellite in X-ray band (Vandenbroucke et al. 2010). The single observation studied here show F_ var 11 with SNR 1.19 which is below detection limit. Hence, we can not claim that the source has shown genuine IDV.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0106 + 612 as an LSP TeV blazar whose X-ray spectrum favors a fixed-NH power law and shows no significant curvature.

quotes.quote: TXS 0106 + 612: This LSP was first discovered by Gregory Taylor (1981) in a radio survey of the Galactic plane. This source is a TeV blazar (e.g. Tsujimoto et al. 2015). The X-ray spectrum of this blazar is better described with a power law with fixed N_ H rather than log parabolic model. Also, we fit the spectra with free N_ H but did not get any improvement in the ^ 2 values. We did not find any significant curvature in the studied energy range.

gmw_00006457.name: PKS 1334 127
gmw_00006457.overview: PKS 1334 127 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603268, arXiv:1709.09342] and a gamma-ray loud blazar [arXiv:1709.09342]. Its X-ray emission is well described by a simple power-law model with Galactic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0603268, arXiv:1709.09342].

sources.summary: The paper presents a first hard-X-ray look at flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 1334-127, finding a simple absorbed power-law fit and a photon index consistent with earlier soft-X-ray data.

quotes.quote: This flat-spectrum radio quasar has been poorly observed in hard X-rays: the only available observations are with ROSAT and Einstein (see, e.g., Maraschi et al. 1995a). The present analysis is also the first look at the hard X-ray emission ( E>4 keV) of this source. A simple power law model, with absorption in excess of the Galactic column, provides the best fit. The photon index is in the middle of the values from Einstein and ROSAT (Maraschi et al. 1995a), and consistent with both within the 90 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1334 127 as a gamma-ray loud FSRQ whose XMM-Newton spectrum is well fit by a power law with index 1.8.

quotes.quote: PKS 1334 127: This FSRQ is a ray loud blazar studied by Foschini et al. (2006) using XMM Newton observations and found it to be well fitted with power law model with =1.8 and Galactic absorption equal to 6.7 0.9 in 0.4--10 keV energy band. We also analyzed this spectrum in the (0.6--10) keV and found it to be well described by the power law model with =1.8. We did not find any significant improvement by keeping N_ H as free parameter in our fitting parameters.

gmw_00006458.name: NGC 3389
gmw_00006458.overview: NGC 3389 is a distant background galaxy located at an estimated distance of 25.2 Mpc [arXiv:2302.10948]. It exhibits a high peculiar velocity of 652 km/s, though it is practically at rest relative to the cosmic microwave background [arXiv:2302.10948].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3389 as a distant background galaxy with an accurate 25.2 Mpc distance, high peculiar velocity, and near rest relative to the CMB.

quotes.quote: At the same time, the galaxy NGC 3389 with an accurate distance estimate of 25.2 1.6 Mpc (marked by a star), measured by supernova luminosity pejcha2015 , is present among them. It also has a high peculiar velocity, 652 115 km s ^ 1 . However, we should note that in an absolute frame of reference associated with the cosmic microwave background (CMB), the radial velocity of NGC 3389 amounts to 1656 km s ^ 1 , i.e. the galaxy practically rests relative to the CMB reference frame.

gmw_00006459.name: 102(N)
gmw_00006459.overview: The spiral galaxy 102(N) is tidally interacting with its close companion galaxy, 102(S), which is projected 10 kpc to the southwest [arXiv:1211.0523]. This interaction likely causes 102(N) to exhibit an asymmetric molecular gas distribution, with its CO intensity maximum offset from the optical center [arXiv:1211.0523].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 102(N) as having an asymmetric, offset CO distribution, likely tied to tidal interaction with its close companion 102(S).

quotes.quote: In two cases, 079 and 102(N), the offset between the CO and optical intensity maxima was significantly larger than the pointing uncertainty and the FWHMs of the fits were significantly greater than those of the other spirals, irrespective of optical size. Both signatures are indicators of an abnormal molecular gas distribution.

102(N) The \ spectra (Figure offs102 ) show an asymmetry in the distribution of CO gas along the major axis. The \ Gauss fit (Table gauss ) also indicates the intensity maximum is offset 11.1 11.2 arcsec (4.5 kpc) to the NW of the optical centre, but the uncertainty is of the same order as the offset. There is strong evidence this galaxy is tidally interacting with its close companion, 102(S), projected 0.42 arcmin (10 kpc) to the SW. The EW( ) for 102(N) is low at 2 \ but the weak \ emission distributed towards the north and west may be indicative of a more dispersed molecular distribution and is consistent with the large FWHM of the \ Gauss fit of 56.9 5.2 arcsec (Figure gffwhm ).

gmw_00006460.name: host galaxy of XRF 050824
gmw_00006460.overview: The host galaxy of XRF 050824 is an extended, moderately luminous blue star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.828 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701736]. It has a physical size of approximately 6 kpc and exhibits a star formation rate of 1.8 solar masses per year [arXiv:astro-ph/0701736].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the XRF 050824 host as an extended, moderately luminous blue star-forming galaxy at z=0.828, with low extinction, roughly 6 kpc size, and near-solar-subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: Our late R band image from December 7, 2005, 104 days past explosion, shows an extended source with magnitude R =23.70 0.15 at the position of the afterglow. This is the host galaxy of XRF 050824. An image obtained under very good seeing conditions at VLT in October 2005 is shown in Fig. f:host . Note that all late observations (past 35 days) are consistent with this being the host, with little contribution from the afterglow (or a supernova).

The host is extended with a size of roughly 0.8 arcsec, which at a distance of 5.24 Gpc corresponds to a linear scale of 6 kpc. Finally, from the [ O II ] lines we can estimate the star formation rate (SFR). The flux of this line, corrected for Galactic extinction, corresponds to a SFR of 1.8 yr ^ 1 , following .
The star formation rate and size thus indicates a fairly normal galaxy, similar to other GRB host galaxies e.g., lefloch03,christensen04,sollerman05 .

gmw_00006461.name: PG 1411+442
gmw_00006461.overview: PG 1411+442 is a highly absorbed quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0703350]. Its spectrum exhibits broad excess emission at energies above 2 to 3 keV, which is well described by an absorbed power-law component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703350].

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1411+442 as one of four highly absorbed PG quasars, finding that its spectrum needs an absorbed power-law component and agrees with prior spectral results.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the remaining four objects (PG 1004+130, 1411+442, 1535+547, and 2214+139) show broad excess emission at energies above 2--3 keV. We added an absorbed power-law component to the model used above to represent this component. This model provides a good description of the spectra. The hydrogen column densities of the absorbed power law are 10^ 23 cm^ 2 for all the cases.
Spectra of PG 1411+442 and PG 2214+139 are presented in Brinkmann et al. (2004). The results of PG 1411+442 are consistent with ours.

gmw_00006462.name: PG 1448+273
gmw_00006462.overview: PG 1448+273 is a highly accreting narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2005.09982] that hosts a robust, highly ionized ultra-fast outflow [arXiv:2011.08212]. The host galaxy lies along the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2307.06087] and exhibits patchy central dust extinction [arXiv:1311.1978].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1448+273 as an NLSy1 with a robust Fe-K ultra-fast outflow, constraining its ionization, column density, velocity, launching radius, covering factor, and kinetic power.

quotes.quote: We study two XMM-Newton archival observations of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLSy1) PG 1448+273. We concentrate on the latest observation, whose spectrum is characterized by a strong absorption feature in the Fe K band. This feature represents the spectral imprint of a UFO, as confirmed by other independent analyses.

Our detection of the UFO in PG 1448+273 is very robust. The outflowing material is highly ionized, = 5.53_ 0.05 ^ +0.04 erg s ^ 1 cm, has a high column density, N_ H = 4.5_ 1.1 ^ +0.8 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , is ejected with a maximum velocity v_0 = 0.24^ +0.08 _ 0.06 (90 confidence level errors), and attains an average velocity v_ avg = 0.152 . WINE succeeds remarkably well to constrain a launching radius of r_0=77_ 19 ^ +31 r_ S from the black hole. We also derive a lower limit on both the opening angle of the wind ( > 72 ) and the covering factor ( C_ f > 0.69 ). We find a mass outflow rate M _ out =0.65^ +0.44 _ 0.33 M_ yr ^ 1 = 2.0^ +1.3 _ 1.0 M _ acc and a high instantaneous outflow kinetic power E _ out =4.4^ +4.4 _ 3.6 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 = 24 L_ bol = 18 L_ Edd ( 1 errors).

sources.summary: The paper finds 1448+273 has the highest central extinction, mixed H II/AGN diagnostic ratios, patchy central extinction, and a compact H-emitting star-forming knot embedded in a larger O III plume.

quotes.quote: The values of A_V that we find for the centres of our quasars range from 1.1 in 0026+129 to 2.7 in 1448+273 , with a median value of 1.9.

In the remaining three objects, 0026+129 , 0838+770 , and 1448+273 , about half of the points fall in the overlap between the H 2 region area and the AGN narrow-line region area, suggesting that we are either observing H 2 regions distributed within the AGN narrow-line region or we are observing star-forming regions only but with a range of ages (i.e., ages that increase towards the centre of the host galaxy). We consider the former interpretation less likely because (a) the candidate AGN narrow-line region pixels correspond to uncharacteristically low values of log ( O 3 / O 2 ) (the distribution of observed values of log ( O 3 / O 2 ) for AGN narrow-line regions peaks between 0.4 and 1.0), and (b) the candidate AGN narrow-line region pixels are typically further away from the quasar than the star-forming regions, where the intensity of the ionizing radiation from the quasar is low (the line ratio map of 1448+273 in Figure fig:bpt is a good example).

We see patchy extinction and line emission in some objects; for instance, the A_V maps for 1448+273 and 2214+139 in Figure fig:reddening show localized patchy clumps of higher extinction less than a kpc in size scattered around the core. As discussed in Section sec:sfr , 1448+273 has an extremely compact region of H emission approximately 0 2 from its centre. This H knot coincides with a region of relatively high extinction ( A_V 2 in Figure fig:reddening ) with line ratios that indicate star formation (see Figure fig:bpt ). Interestingly, it is also embedded in a larger plume in the O 3 image.

sources.summary: The paper studies PG 1448+273 as a highly accreting narrow-line Seyfert 1 with an unambiguous ultra-fast outflow, weak warm absorption, and variable X-ray wind signatures.

quotes.quote: Here we study a highly accreting Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy PG 1448+273. We apply an automated, systematic routine for detecting outflows in accreting systems and achieve an unambiguous detection of a UFO in this AGN. The UFO absorption is observed in both soft and hard X-ray bands with the \ observatory. The velocity of the outflow is (26900 600) km/s ( 0.09c), with an ionisation parameter of ( / erg cm s ^ 1 )=4.03_ 0.08 ^ +0.10 and a column density above 10^ 23 .

PG 1448+273 is a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 AGN Boller+96, Grupe+04 located at a redshift of z=0.0645 Alam+15 . Its mass is (9 2) 10^ 6 M_ from single-epoch optical spectroscopy Vestergaard+06 . PG 1448+273 has an estimated bolometric luminosity of 10^ 45.5 erg/s Grupe+04 , resulting in an Eddington ratio above unity ( L/L_ Edd 3 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1448+273 as a normal broad-line blue quasar whose narrow-line ratios misclassify it as an HII galaxy in BPT diagrams.

quotes.quote: (= SDSS J145108+270926.92, also known as PG 1448+273) is always a common broad line blue quasar, since its first reported in the PG (Palomar-Green) quasar sample of bg92 . As the shown spectra in Fig.  spec from SDSS (Sloan Digital Sky Survey) (the SDSS spectrum) and from the KPNO (Kitt Peak National Observatory) 2.1m telescope bg92 (the KPNO spectrum),   is an un-doubtfully normal broad line blue quasar (a type-1 AGN). However, based on flux ratios of narrow emission lines well discussed in Section 2 and in Section 3,   can be well classified as a HII galaxy instead of an AGN in the BPT diagram bpt81, kb01, ka03, kb06, ke13a, kb19, zh20 . Therefore, in the manuscript, some special properties of   is studied.
The extremely unique case of the mis-classified quasar J1451+2709 deserves further study in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1448+273 as a prior CO-observed NLSy1 with CIGALE stellar mass consistent with literature and a main-sequence position in the SFR plane.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 and PG 1448+273 (hereafter PG 1211 and PG 1448) which were observed in CO(2-1) with ACA Shangguan_2020a .
For Mrk 110 and PG 1448, the stellar mass computed by CIGALE is consistent with the literature.

Six out of ten NLSy1 fall in the regime of starbursts, while Mrk 110, Mrk 1044 and PG 1448 are located along the MS. The last galaxy (Mrk 205) lie in between the two regimes. We therefore used a CO-to-H _2 conversion factor of 0.8 M_ (K km s^ 1 pc^2)^ 1 for the six galaxies which lie high above the MS, and 4.3 M_ (K km s^ 1 pc^2)^ 1 for Mrk 110, Mrk 205, Mrk 1044 and PG 1448.

sources.summary: The paper models PG 1448+273 as an extreme NLS1 whose optical-to-soft-X-ray spectrum requires a slim disk with very high accretion rate, implying a young black hole system.

quotes.quote: We pick up one NLS1, namely PG 1448+273 with an inferred of 10^ 6.4 , among the highest candidates. The broadband spectral distribution is successfully reproduced by the model with an extremely high ( = 1000) and the viscosity parameter of 0.01. This implies that this object, as well as some other highest systems, is really young: the inferred age, / , is about 10^6 years.

The standard disk model ( 10 ) does not fit the broadband spectrum of PG 1448+273 (dot and a tie-bow error), and thus a slim disk model is required. The Soft X-ray photon index of PG 1448+273 ( 3.2 ) is larger than normal BLS1s (see e.g., Boller 1996), but is not the largest one among the NLS1s ( 2.5 - 4.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper newly analyzes PG 1448+273 and finds that its soft excess cannot be fit with one blackbody, requiring a second blackbody component for a good fit.

quotes.quote: We summarize the results of our spectral analysis of quasars newly presented in this paper. Their spectra are shown in Figure fig: other quasars . We fitted the spectra with the canonical model described in Section 3.1; results are listed in Table tab: other qso . They are well reproduced by the model except for one case (PG 1448+273). The soft excess component in PG 1448+273 is not explained by a single blackbody component. We added an additional blackbody component and obtained a good fit.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1448+273 as a variable X-ray NLS1 with ultra-fast outflows, super-Eddington accretion, unresolved VLA emission, and no reported VLBI detection.

quotes.quote: PG 1448+273 PG 1448+273 is a NLS1 galaxy 2015ApJS..219...12A showing high variability in X-rays 2012A&A...542A..83P and ultra-fast outflows 2020MNRAS.495.4769K . The black hole mass is 9 2 10^ 6 M _ 2006ApJ...641..689V , and the bolometric luminosity is 10 ^ 45.5 erg s ^ 1 , suggesting a super-Eddington accretion AGN with an Eddington ratio of L/L_ Edd 3 . In VLA images, it is unresolved 1994AJ....108.1163K . There is no reported VLBI detection so far including the non-detection in our observation, probably due to the intrinsic suppression of jet activity in the high accretion state.

sources.summary: The paper flags PG 1448+273 as a narrow-line outlier with one of the smallest [OIII] widths and a comparatively large black-hole mass; excluding it strengthens the fitted MBH relation.

quotes.quote: As seen in Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb , three objects, Mrk 382, Mrk 734 and PG 1448+273, which have the smallest [ OIII ] widths, deviate from the other NL objects. The reason that they have somewhat larger MBHs than others is not known. However, if excluding them, the correlation becomes even stronger with R_ s =0.762 ( P_s=2.0 10^ 15 ), which can be fitted (using OLS bisector) by M_ BH = 10^ (7.81 0.006) M_ FWHM _ OIII /2.35 200 km s ^ 1 ^ 3.70 0.51 , as shown by the dotted-long dashed line in the right panel of Figures  fig:msigma_mf and fig:msigma_geb .

gmw_00006463.name: Leo I
gmw_00006463.overview: Leo I is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2111.04770]. It is one of the brightest and most distant dwarf spheroidal satellites of the Milky Way [arXiv:2111.04770].

sources.summary: The paper measures Leo I's central kinematics and surface brightness, models its dark matter and central black hole, and finds evidence favoring a black hole over no-black-hole models.

quotes.quote: We measure the central kinematics for the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Leo I using integrated-light measurements and previously published data. We find a steady rise in the velocity dispersion from 300 \ into the center. The integrated-light kinematics provide a velocity dispersion of 11.76 0.66 \ inside 75 .

In this work we analyze such a system, Leo I, using the same rigorous dynamical models applied to larger galaxies. Being among the brightest and furthest of the Milky Way dSphs has made Leo I the subject of many in-depth dynamical studies. Metallicity studies ( Bosler2006 ), proper motion ( gaia2018,sohn2012 ) and radial velocity measurements ( Koch2007, sohn2007 ) have accrued a significant amount of data on the galaxy.

gmw_00006464.name: PKS 1543+005
gmw_00006464.overview: PKS 1543+005 is a highly variable blazar with a total radio extent of less than one kiloparsec [arXiv:2303.11357]. Although it morphologically resembles a compact symmetric object, its apparent symmetry is attributed to projection effects and relativistic beaming [arXiv:2303.11357].

sources.summary: The paper rejects PKS 1543+005 as a bona fide CSO because its compact two-sided morphology is attributed to projection, variability, high luminosity, and relativistic beaming.

quotes.quote: PKS 1543+005 PKS 1543+005 was classified as a cso by 2000ApJ...534...90P . In fig:noncsos we show its morphology and its spectrum including variability. Like PKS 1413+135, morphologically PKS 1543+005 resembles a cso since there is no doubt that it shows emission on both sides of the nucleus (component ``B'') on these small scales, and it is less than a kpc in total extent. Like PKS 1413+135, it is a highly variable blazar, as shown by . PKS 1543+005 also has a spectral peak luminosity over three times greater than any of the 79 agn we identify as bona fide cso in this paper.

On account of its high variability and high luminosity there can be no doubt that the radio emission from PKS 1543+005 is highly boosted by relativistic beaming and, thus, cannot be classified as cso . Thus PKS 1543+005 falls right into one of the two cases of misidentified cso that we are trying to avoid in the cso class, as specified in sec:criteria : those closely aligned with the line of sight whose apparent symmetry is due to approaching structure being projected onto both sides of the nucleus.

gmw_00006465.name: 2MASS 0918+2117
gmw_00006465.overview: 2MASS 0918+2117 is a type 1.5 quasar that exhibits a red nucleus and strong optical polarization [arXiv:0707.0979]. Between observations, the galaxy has shown changes in its X-ray flux, continuum shape, absorption, and soft excess [arXiv:0707.0979].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASS 0918+2117 as a type 1.5 QSO whose X-ray flux, continuum shape, absorption, soft excess, red nucleus, and optical polarization changed between observations.

quotes.quote: When observed by \ in 2003 the type 1.5 QSO M \ was found to be in a low state, with an X-ray flux 4-5 times fainter than during an earlier \ observation. The 2-6 keV spectrum was unusually hard (photon index 1.25), with evidence for a reflection-dominated continuum, while a soft excess visible below 1 keV prevented confirmation of the anticipated low energy absorber (Wilkes \ 2005). In a second \ observation in 2005 the X-ray flux is found to have recovered, with a 2-10 keV continuum spectrum now typical of a broad-line active galaxy ( 2) and a deficit of flux below 1 keV indicative of continuum absorption in a column N _ H 4 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . We find the preferred ionisation state of the absorbing gas to be low, which then leaves a residual soft excess of similar spectral form and flux to that found in the 2003 \ observation. Although observed at different epochs we note that dust in the absorbing column could also explain the red nucleus and strong optical polarisation of M. galaxies: active -- galaxies:general -- galaxies: individual:2MASS 0918+2117 -- galaxies:QSO -- X-ray:galaxies

gmw_00006466.name: PSB J1150 0107
gmw_00006466.overview: PSB J1150 0107 is a post-starburst galaxy characterized by extremely blue optical-to-near-infrared colors and low-metallicity spectral features [arXiv:1205.4717]. The galaxy exhibits residual star formation, which is indicated by hydrogen-alpha emission and a detection at 22 micrometers in the WISE survey [arXiv:1205.4717].

sources.summary: The paper treats PSB J1150 0107 as an anomalous post-starburst galaxy with unusually blue optical-NIR colours, low-metallicity spectral features, and residual star formation indicated by H emission and WISE detection.

quotes.quote: PSB J1150 0107 and PSB J1206 + 0918 are characterised by extremely blue optical-NIR colours ( m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 0.2 ) and intermediate/red optical colours ( m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 0.3 ). When plotted on the colour-colour diagrams of Fig. fig:diag_optNIR_SSP , fig:diag_optNIR_cSP , fig:diag_optNIR_cSP_20 and fig:diag_optNIR_cSP_sameZ , they significantly depart from the sequences both of observed data-points and model tracks. None of them is able to reproduce such a combination of intermediate/red optical colours and very blue optical-NIR ones: for the given m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 , an offset of 0.1 to 0.2 mag in m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 would be required to bring them back in agreement with the models, or, for the given m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 , an offset of 0.3 mag in m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 .

Significant residual star formation is witnessed in PSB J1150 0107 by EW(H ) =-2.43 0.24 and a marginal detection at 22 m in the WISE all-sky survey. PSB J1206 + 0918, however, does not display any sign of star formation, with EW(H ) =-0.65 0.26 and no detection at 22 m. This suggests that residual star formation or processes related to nuclear activity are not the cause of the anomaly common to these two galaxies. We note that the shape of the spectra of these two galaxies is peculiar in two main aspects: i) the continuum appears to bend down, i.e. the slope becomes steeper, going from 0.55 to 0.8 m, whereas all other galaxies display an approximate constant slope or even an up-bending shape in this region; ii) a significant and featureless drop long-ward of 0.5 m is observed instead of the Fe and Mg absorption complexes, which is probably due to the low metallicity of these two galaxies, among the lowest of the entire sample.

gmw_00006467.name: PSB J1206 + 0918
gmw_00006467.overview: PSB J1206 + 0918 is a post-starburst galaxy characterized by extremely blue optical-to-near-infrared colors [arXiv:1205.4717]. The galaxy shows no signs of current star formation and exhibits a low-metallicity spectral behavior [arXiv:1205.4717].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSB J1206 + 0918 as an anomalous post-starburst galaxy with unusually blue optical-NIR colours, low-metallicity spectral behavior, and no evidence for current star formation.

quotes.quote: PSB J1150 0107 and PSB J1206 + 0918 are characterised by extremely blue optical-NIR colours ( m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 0.2 ) and intermediate/red optical colours ( m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 0.3 ). When plotted on the colour-colour diagrams of Fig. fig:diag_optNIR_SSP , fig:diag_optNIR_cSP , fig:diag_optNIR_cSP_20 and fig:diag_optNIR_cSP_sameZ , they significantly depart from the sequences both of observed data-points and model tracks. None of them is able to reproduce such a combination of intermediate/red optical colours and very blue optical-NIR ones: for the given m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 , an offset of 0.1 to 0.2 mag in m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 would be required to bring them back in agreement with the models, or, for the given m_ 0.55 m_ 0.70 , an offset of 0.3 mag in m_ 0.55 m_ 1.40 .

Significant residual star formation is witnessed in PSB J1150 0107 by EW(H ) =-2.43 0.24 and a marginal detection at 22 m in the WISE all-sky survey. PSB J1206 + 0918, however, does not display any sign of star formation, with EW(H ) =-0.65 0.26 and no detection at 22 m. This suggests that residual star formation or processes related to nuclear activity are not the cause of the anomaly common to these two galaxies. We note that the shape of the spectra of these two galaxies is peculiar in two main aspects: i) the continuum appears to bend down, i.e. the slope becomes steeper, going from 0.55 to 0.8 m, whereas all other galaxies display an approximate constant slope or even an up-bending shape in this region; ii) a significant and featureless drop long-ward of 0.5 m is observed instead of the Fe and Mg absorption complexes, which is probably due to the low metallicity of these two galaxies, among the lowest of the entire sample.

gmw_00006468.name: NGC 1415
gmw_00006468.overview: NGC 1415 is a moderately inclined spiral galaxy located near the center of the Eridanus group [arXiv:2303.00626]. It features an inner boxy stellar bar [arXiv:1906.04564], multiple ring-lens structures [arXiv:1702.02013], a truncated star-forming disk [arXiv:2303.00626], and a bipolar nuclear outflow [arXiv:1906.04564, arXiv:2303.00626].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 1415, describing long-slit spectroscopy of its central gas, a bipolar outflow from H knots, and an inner boxy stellar bar.

quotes.quote: A long slit spectrum from the barred galaxy NGC 1415 has been obtained with the 2.1m Guillermo Haro telescope in Cananea, M exico at position angle 155 and shows the kinematics of Na I D lines (in absorption) and H 6562.8 , [ NII ] 6548 , [ NII ] 6584 , [ SII ] 6716 , and [ SII ] 6731 lines in emission from the central regions and the disk. Our previous H continuum-free imaging of the central region showed mainly two central bright H knots straddling the nucleus, and H emission regions along the south-east and north-west inner spiral arms. Velocities of the Na I D absorption lines are taken as representative of the rotation curve of NGC 1415. Our kinematical data indicates that the central bright H straddling the nucleus have velocities in excess of the Na I D velocities. We interpret these velocity excesses of the central bright H knots as due to a geyser (bipolar outflow) with V _ outflow 140 km s ^ 1 at a P.A.+165 .

Additionally we have determined _ gas , radial resonances (R) and estimated the value of the pattern angular speed of an inner boxy stellar bar in NGC 1415, _ bar , from the Na I D rotation curve assuming R = 1 , _ bar 134 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: NGC 1415 is described as a barred, outer-ring spiral near the group center, HI deficient and weakly perturbed, with a possible AGN-driven nuclear geyser and truncated star-forming disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1415 NGC 1415 is classified as a moderately inclined spiral galaxy with S-shaped mixed bars and an outer ring. It is located in the central region of the Eridanus group and is the closest galaxy to the group centre in our sample. NGC 1415 has been measured to be HI deficient as compared to its expected HI mass and integrated disk stability. Wangs2022 find NGC 1415 to be weakly perturbed by tidal interactions; however, it has the second highest summed tidal interaction strength for this sample.

Evidence of a nuclear geyser (bipolar outflow) is apparent in the H and measured kinematics of NGC 1415, which is suggested to be caused by AGN ; however, we do not identify any jet structure within NGC 1415. This galaxy shows a truncated star forming disk compared to the entire stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1415 is best described as an S0 with multiple ring-lens structures that dominate NUV images, perturb luminosity profiles, and indicate an underlying disk.

quotes.quote: In short, recent classifications tend to describe the galaxy as an S0 having an outer ring-lens (RL) system, without bar, with an inner ring-lens ('l) and a nuclear ring (nr). The system of rings dominates our NUV images, especially in the M2 and W2 filters, and reflects on the shape of the luminosity profiles: there is not a peak of luminosity in the center since the luminosity of the nuclear ring prevails. The outer ring, well visible in the NUV bands, shows a position angle different from the inner one as pointed out by Comeron14 . We measure the position angle, PA, and, at the outer edge of the NUV features, the semi-major, a and semi-minor, b , projected axes. The nuclear ring (nr) has PA=166 ^ 2^ , a=10.8 , b=5.2 ; the inner ring-lens ('l) PA=139 ^ 2^ , a=50 , b=14 ; the outer ring (RL) PA=150 ^ 2^ , a=152.8 , b=60.3 .

gmw_00006469.name: NGC 1426
gmw_00006469.overview: NGC 1426 is an early-type group galaxy located in the Fornax Eridanus cloud [arXiv:1803.03967]. While historically classified as an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1803.03967, arXiv:1702.02013], structural analysis reveals a disk, suggesting a lenticular classification [arXiv:1803.03967].

sources.summary: NGC 1426 is described as an early-type group galaxy reclassified from E4 to S0, lacking UV peculiarities or H I, with simulations favoring a major-merger origin.

quotes.quote: Early-type galaxies in groups NGC 1426 is classified as an E4 but our multi-wavelength structural analysis shows that there is a disk, suggesting a new classification as S0. The galaxy is located in the Fornax Eridanus cloud with an environmental galaxy density of =0.66 galaxy Mpc ^ 3 Tully88 . No peculiar features, neither in the near nor in the far ultraviolet, are revealed by both GALEX and Swift observations. NGC 1426 is considered an old and passively evolving galaxy.

NGC 1426 is not detected in HI, and Trinchieri15 measured an upper limit to the X-ray luminosity of its hot gas of log L _X< 38.95 erg sec ^ 1 . Figure fig:3 shows the evolutionary path, in the rest-frame (NUV- ) - M _ color magnitude diagram, predicted for NGC 1426 by the SPH-CPI simulation which best fits its global properties. The derived SED, the morphology and total magnitude, including the ultraviolet and optical luminosity profiles, are consistent with all the observational constraints in preparation Mazzei18 .
The evolution of NGC 1426 is driven by a major merger between two halos of equal mass (M _ tot =2 10^ 12 M _ ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1426 as a nominal elliptical whose NUV luminosity profiles lack peculiar features and whose CGS fit values remain lower than a classic de Vaucouleurs law.

quotes.quote: Our integrated NUV magnitudes (Table tab5 ) agree well with those in Hodges2014 . The bottom panel of Figure NGC1426-sb shows that our NUV luminosity profiles do not present peculiar features. The range of indexes, obtained with a single S ersic fit out to a 90 , is 2.65 n 2.86 . The CGS surface photometry extends out to 230 , although with large photometric errors in the outskirts, well beyond our NUV data. The value of our S ersic fit of the CGS data, over the same range as our NUV analysis, outlines values lower than the classic ^ 1/4 law, just as in the NUV bands (Figure analysis-1 ).

gmw_00006470.name: NGC 3818
gmw_00006470.overview: NGC 3818 is a bulge-dominated, E5 early-type elliptical galaxy located in the Crater Cloud group [arXiv:1702.02013, arXiv:2404.13459]. It features a disk embedded in a boxy bulge [arXiv:1702.02013, arXiv:2404.13459], slightly disky isophotes [arXiv:1702.02013], and a passive mid-infrared spectrum indicating little-to-no current activity [arXiv:2404.13459].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3818 as an E5, bulge-dominated early-type galaxy with an embedded disk, unremarkable optical images, slightly disky isophotes, and a nearly constant red colour profile.

quotes.quote: NGC 3818 This galaxy is classified E5 in the RSA and the RC3. A more detailed description is given by Scorza98 that consider NGC 3818 a bulge-dominated ETG, having a disk fully embedded in a boxy bulge. Our optical images do not show remarkable features (top left panel of Figures NGC3818-sb ). The S ersic index from NUV luminosity profiles is in the range 2.73 0.18<n<3.59 0.25 . Since the galaxy does not belong to the CGS sample we use our UVOT data-set to study the optical wavelengths.

Our S ersic index estimate in the UVOT V band is 3.09 0.17 (Figure analysis-1 ) in agreement with the value of Hopkins09 , n=2.81^ +0.06 _ 0.06 , in the same band. We found that the galaxy is slightly disky, as shown in Figure a4M2 and Table tab5 . Figure color-M2-V shows that this galaxy has a red color profile, almost constant around an average value, ( M2-V ) 5.1.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3818 as a bulge-dominated E5 early-type galaxy in the Crater Cloud, with passive mid-infrared spectra, low fine structure, and dry-merger abundances unchanged from normal ETGs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3818 is a bulge-dominated, E5 galaxy, with a disk fully embedded in a boxy bulge and the least amount of fine structure out of the three ETGs 1998MNRAS.300..469S . NGC 3818 is located in a low-density environment (density of galaxies brighter than M_B = -16 is 0.2) in the Crater Cloud group of galaxies 1988ngc..book.....T . 2011A&A...528A..10P studied mid-infrared (MIR) spectra of NGC 3818 and determined it as a passive ETG, with no emission lines in its spectra, indicating little-to-no current activity within the galaxy.

NGC 3818 NGC 3818 does not show any abundance differences to ETGs without fine structure. NGC 3818 experienced its major merger earlier than NGC 2865, and which could explain the agreement of [ /Fe ] ratios to ETGs without fine structure. The fine structure constant of NGC 3818 is the lowest of the 3 ETGs studied in this work at =1.3 In the case of NGC 3818, its main fine structure feature is a boxy isophote with an embedded disk, which are both structures associated with high mass, gas poor (dry) mergers 2023MNRAS.520.5870G . If the fine structure was formed in a dry merger, then we would expect a lack of chemical differences to ETGs without fine structure, as there would have been little-to-no star formation during the merger. The abundances we observe in NGC 3818 demonstrate that its dry merger event did not chemically alter the system.

gmw_00006471.name: NGC 3962
gmw_00006471.overview: NGC 3962 is an isolated elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1502.06148, arXiv:1702.02013] that contains a rich system of globular clusters [arXiv:1502.06148]. It hosts an extended, arc-like ionized gas structure [arXiv:1502.06148] and contains a cold dust component estimated at 12 K [arXiv:astro-ph/0406011].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3962 as an isolated field elliptical with a rich globular-cluster system, doubtful color bimodality, arc-like ionized-gas color structures, and possible older merger evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 3962 is part of the smith04 sample of field ellipticals, with no companions as confirmed by madore04 , and no signs of any tidal disturbance tal09 . annibali07 measured an age of 10 Gyr, while serra10 give an age of 2.5 Gyr.

The most interesting case is given by NGC 3962. Despite being the galaxy in our sample with the richest GCS and with deep observations, its peak separation is barely larger than 2, the kurtosis is very close to zero and has the highest p-value, making the case for bimodality very doubtful. Intermediate-age globular clusters are known to smear out bimodality e.g. richtler12 , but the luminosity function shows no sign of intermediate-age clusters (see Sect. sec:lumfun ).

Fig. fig:colorn3962 shows the g-i color map for NGC 3962. The same structures detected by buson93 using H imaging can be seen. First, an extended, slightly off-centered arc-like structure about 15 from the galaxy center, with redder color than the underlying galaxy light. The SAM images (Fig. fig:colorn3962 , right panel), also show a central elongated component, misaligned with the major and minor axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3962 as an E-type galaxy with NUV profile breaks, differing optical and NUV Sersic indices, and a constant red colour profile.
