quotes.quote: NGC 3962 The galaxy is classified E1 in the RSA and the RC3 and E+3-4 by Buta15 . In the NUV bands we find that its average ellipticity is in the range _ 80 0.18 - 0.24 (Table tab5 ). The galaxy does not show an obvious disk structure, although the value of the a_4/a isophotal shape profile tends to increase towards the outskirts. Furthermore, the NUV luminosity profiles show two distinct trends with a break at a^ 1/4 =2.45 arcsec ^ 1/4 . This break, visible at the same radius also in the CGS luminosity profiles, is emphasized by our S ersic fit.

S ersic index values range from 2.52 to 3.28 in the NUV, and from 3.10 to 4.41 in the optical bands (see Figure analysis-1 ). Huang13 find that the V-band profile is well fitted with three S ersic laws with indexes 3.25, 0.51 and 1.46. The best fit of the sum of a cusp and a S ersic law in Hopkins09 in the same band gives n=3.74 .

sources.summary: The SED analysis adopts a 24 K dust fit for NGC 3962, notes upper-limit constraints, and estimates a possible 12 K dust component comparable to warmer dust.

quotes.quote: For temperature values determined here, a choice of primarily affects the SEDs longward of 100 , so that for NGC 3962 and NGC 5354, for which there are only flux upper limits or no FIR to submm data points longward of 100 , the providing the best fit (i.e., lowest ^2 ) for these objects cannot be discriminated. For NGC 4374, the differences in 60 and 100 \ fluxes are the dominant contributor to the ^2 ; and therefore, the ^2 with different 's for this object are about the same. Mindful of the fitting uncertainties, the general deduction in this paper is that the reduced ^2 's that are listed in Table tab:one analysis demonstrate that single temperature greybodies with =2 produced reasonable SEDs fits for the objects presented here (see also Section sec:2tsed below). For NGC 3962 and NGC 4697 that appear to have better =1.5 , ^2 than =2 , ^2 , submm upper limits not included in the ^2 determination fall below the =1.5 model plots but are consistent with the =2 plots.

The 12 K dust mass estimate for NGC 3962 is also of the same order as the warmer 24 K dust mass determined for this galaxy. In this case, this is all that can be said because only upper limit fluxes are available in the submm and no radio observations have been made to constrain any low frequency flux origin for this galaxy.

gmw_00006472.name: Galaxy 17251
gmw_00006472.overview: Galaxy 17251 is an emission line galaxy that shows evidence of being an ongoing merger [arXiv:1812.04129]. Its spectra reveal a complex velocity structure in the [OIII] emission line, which may be influenced by merger-associated shocks [arXiv:1812.04129].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Galaxy 17251 as an unusual LyC candidate with extremely high R23, complex [OIII] velocity structure, low O32, merger evidence, and possible shock effects.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 17251 is plotted with an open, purple circle was found to have an extremely large R23 value of 43.8, which is roughly 5 times higher than the average value for galaxies in our sample with reliable H detections. This large value is also at odds with theoretical expectations from photoionization modeling, which give an upper bound of 10 kewley02,nagao06 . Examining the 2D spectra from MOSFIRE, we find significant velocity structure in the [ OIII ] ( 5007 ) emission line as shown in the boxcar smoothed (kernel width = 1 pixel) cutout in Figure fig:17251 . The primary galaxy is located at emission peak A while peaks B and C are found to have velocity offsets for the [ OIII ] ( 5007 ) emission line of +38 and +52 km s ^ 1 respectively.

Galaxy 17251 in particular, with the lowest O32 in our sample, has additional evidence of being an on-going merger from the complex [ OIII ] ( 5007 ) in our 2D spectrum (see Figure fig:17251 ). The low O32 (lower limit) LCE from fletcher18 has a low S/N in their HST imaging, thus assessing the possibility of a merger from morphology is not possible. As we have discussed in Sections section:17251 and section:msfr , shocks associated with mergers can enhance [ OII ] ( 3727 ) relative to [ OIII ] ( 5007 ), thus explaining to low observed O32 in these galaxies rich15,epinat18 .

gmw_00006473.name: IRAS F04332+0209
gmw_00006473.overview: IRAS F04332+0209 is an infrared galaxy [arXiv:1211.2023] that hosts a starburst nucleus [arXiv:1211.2023] and an OH kilomaser [arXiv:1211.2023]. The galaxy exhibits low infrared, CO, and OH luminosities [arXiv:1310.1957], and its kilomaser displays possible luminosity variability [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper clearly detects the IRAS F04332+0209 kilomaser, notes possible luminosity variability and a starburst nucleus, and finds Stokes V behavior indicating large Zeeman splitting.

quotes.quote: IRAS F04332+0209 : This OH kilomaser was first reported by Martin1989b with = 0.44 , but no spectrum or other properties of the maser were published. We clearly detect this source, shown in Figure fig:04332 . It has a 1667 MHz peak flux density of 16 mJy, and an isotropic luminosity = 0.29 , assuming a distance of 51 Mpc. This is slightly lower than the value reported by Martin1989b .

As a kilomaser host, this galaxy is unlike the majority of the galaxies observed in this survey. It has an infrared luminosity of 2.9 10^ 10 L_ , well short of meeting the definition of a LIRG, which requires > 11 . Nevertheless, Baan1998 characterized this galaxy as having a starburst nucleus in their optical classification of maser hosts. The maser is also unusual in that the Stokes V spectrum for this maser is unlike any other in this survey.

sources.summary: The paper reports CARMA constraints on IRAS F04332+0209 gas surface density, but flags it as a low-luminosity sample exception where standard assumptions and inclination correction may not apply.

quotes.quote: We also used the Combined Array for Millimeter-wave Astronomy (CARMA) to place constraints on the gas surface densities in two more galaxies, IRAS F04332+0209 and IRAS F13126+2453.

The possible exception is IRAS F04332+0209, which is at the very low end of the sample in terms of L_ OH , L_ FIR , and L_ CO , such that some of the assumptions that we make about conditions in (U)LIRGs may not apply. In particular, we do not apply the inclination correction to Equation eq:dyn_mass for IRAS F04332+0209, as its CO luminosity is inconsistent with such a large dynamical mass.

gmw_00006474.name: SDSS J153542.4+090341
gmw_00006474.overview: SDSS J153542.4+090341 is a peculiar quasar [arXiv:1802.04272, arXiv:1609.00064] characterized by a weak continuum [arXiv:1609.00064] and an optical spectrum dominated by high equivalent width Fe II emission [arXiv:1802.04272]. This emission is interpreted as resonant scattering from extended, blueshifted outflows [arXiv:1802.04272], while the source also exhibits weak apparent X-ray emission [arXiv:1802.04272].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1535+0903 as a redshift outlier ERQ with an unusual Fe II-dominated optical spectrum, a blueshifted [OIII] outflow, and weak X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, SDSS J1535+0903 is a redshift outlier with a unique optical spectrum.
The object most weakly detected with Chandra (SDSS J1535+0903) has 3 counts at E 1-7 keV.

SDSS J1535+0903 is at a substantially lower redshift ( z 1.5 ) compared with z 2.3 -3.2 characteristic of the remainder of the ERQs considered here. This object was recognized as having an extremely unusual optical spectrum ross15 which was analyzed by wang16b . The optical spectrum is dominated by high equivalent width Fe II emission which wang16b interpret as being resonantly scattered by extended outflows that are viewed nearly edge-on. The [ OIII ] profile perr17 shows a strong blue-shifted outflow component. This source has weak apparent X-ray emission and is only marginally detected (S/N 2 ) at E 1 -7 keV, which, as in the case of SDSS J0834+0159, is suggestive of intrinsic X-ray weakness and/or heavy obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J153542.4+090341 as the prototype peculiar quasar spectrum, with strong UV spikes, weak continuum, resonant Fe II scattering, and modeled outflow covering factor.

quotes.quote: One of these quasars, SDSS J153542.4+090341 has already been noted in Ross et al. (2015); and another one (SDSS J163246.6+340526) appeared on the list of unusual quasars in Meusinger et al. (2012). The most striking features of these objects are weak continua, strong ultraviolet spikes and emission lines, and a relatively narrow line. SDSS J153542.4+090341: The BOSS spectrum shows three prominent spikes due to UV1,2,3 and 62,63, and , and lines.

To summarize, SDSS J1535+0903 represents a prototype peculiar spectrum, while the spectra in other objects can be modeled as a combination of this and a I Zw 1-like component. The presence of strong , but very weak or missing similar component of ] and Si iii ] , which are usually much stronger, in SDSS J1535+0903, indicates that resonant scattering is the dominant excitation mechanism in this object. In Appendix A, we demonstrate that a pure fluorescent model can indeed reproduce the observed band ratios in SDSS J1535+0903 very well.

gmw_00006475.name: SDSS J100552.6+493448
gmw_00006475.overview: SDSS J100552.6+493448 is a peculiar quasar characterized by strong ultraviolet spikes [arXiv:1609.00064]. The object displays very strong high-ionization forbidden and permitted lines, as well as strong low-ionization lines [arXiv:1609.00064].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J100552.6+493448 as a peculiar quasar with strong UV spikes, broad high-ionization lines, and outflow-related forbidden emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J100552.6+493448: In addition to strong ultraviolet spikes, this object displays very strong high-ionization forbidden and permitted lines: [ ] 4363, [ Ne iii ] 3869,3987, [ Ne v ] 3346,3426, [ Ne iv ] 2422, [ C iii ] 1906,1909, as well as low ionization lines H , H , [ O ii ] 3726,3729, C ii ] 2326, Mg ii , and Al iii 1855, 1863. In fact, the spectrum can be reproduced by a supposition of broadened SDSS J1535+0903 emission lines and the narrow lines mentioned above (See Figure 1). The strength can be well accounted for by the SDSS J1535+0903 component.

However, note that SDSS J1005+4934 also displays strong forbidden lines of similar width ( 3000 ), different from traditional intermediate broad lines. In SDSS J1005+4934, we also detect strong high ionization forbidden or semi-forbidden lines that are excited by collisional process, while the spectrum as well as / ratio, are nearly the same as SDSS J1535+0903. By doing so, we obtain covering factors of 0.05, 0.20, 0.01, and 0.06 for SDSS J2125+0044, SDSS J1632+3405, SDSS J1005+4934 and J1535+0903.

gmw_00006476.name: SDSS J163246.6+340526
gmw_00006476.overview: SDSS J163246.6+340526 is a quasar characterized by a spectrum dominated by narrow ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:1609.00064]. The galaxy exhibits blueshifted emission lines, which are attributed to outflows and suggest a small inclination angle [arXiv:1609.00064].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J163246.6+340526 as an unusual quasar with narrow UV-line dominance, a lower spike-to-gap ratio, and blueshifted lines tied to outflows.

quotes.quote: SDSS J163246.6+340526: The spectrum is dominated by narrow ultraviolet lines. The spikes/gap ratio (3.2) is less extreme than in J1535+0903. We adopt the redshift of narrow [ O II ] as the systemic redshift.

Blue shifted and lines are seen in the SDSS J1632+3405, while emission lines in other quasars do not display systematic blueshift. The blue shifted lines can be easily explained as from an outflow since one expects that the far side of the outflow is subject to large dust extinction and would not be seen. In the continuum fit, we found that SDSS J1632+3405 has the smallest inclination angle among these objects.

gmw_00006477.name: SDSS J212546.9+004455
gmw_00006477.overview: SDSS J212546.9+004455 is a peculiar quasar characterized by relatively strong continuum emission [arXiv:1609.00064]. The object exhibits two blueshifted ultraviolet components and contains outflow features with a modeled covering factor of 0.05 [arXiv:1609.00064].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J212546.9+004455 as a peculiar quasar with stronger continuum, two blueshifted ultraviolet components, absorption features, and modeled outflow covering factor.

quotes.quote: SDSS J212546.9+004455: Unlike other objects, the continuum emission in this object is relatively strong. The narrow line has the same redshift as [ O ii ] , which is considered as the systematic redshift. A comparison with the template of I Zw 1 and the spectrum of SDSS J1535+0903 suggests the presence of two blue-shifted ultraviolet components.

A broadened SDSS J1535+0903-like component is shifted by 5700 and a I Zw 1-like ultraviolet component is blueshifted by 2850 . The I Zw 1-like component has weaker , , Si iii and lines but broader line widths than I Zw 1. By doing so, we obtain covering factors of 0.05, 0.20, 0.01, and 0.06 for SDSS J2125+0044, SDSS J1632+3405, SDSS J1005+4934 and J1535+0903.

gmw_00006478.name: Tucana-II
gmw_00006478.overview: Tucana-II is a recently discovered ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1804.07542]. It is a satellite galaxy characterized by a high mass-to-light ratio and a large line-of-sight stellar velocity dispersion [arXiv:1804.07542].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on Tucana-II as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy and dark-matter target, reporting a weak gamma-ray excess and annihilation cross-section limits.

quotes.quote: Tucana-II (Tuc-II), a recently discovered and confirmed Ultra Faint Dwarf Spheroidal galaxy, has a high mass to light ratio as well as a large line-of-sight stellar velocity dispersion, thus making it an ideal candidate for an indirect dark matter (DM) search. In this paper, we have analyzed nine years of ray data obtained from the Fermi LAT instrument from the direction of Tuc-II. The fact that a very weak significant ray excess ( 2.2 ) over the background of Tuc-II have been detected from the location of this galaxy.

We have observed that this excess of ray emission from the of location Tuc-II rises with longer periods of data. It is then called for an estimation of the 95 confidence level upper limit of the possible velocity weighted self-annihilation cross-section of the DM particles (WIMPs) within Tuc-II by fitting the observed ray flux with spectra expected for DM annihilation. Motivated by such increasing interest in the indirect search for DM in the UFDs/dSphs, in the present paper, we focus our attention to a recently discovered dwarf satellite, namely Tucana-II (Tuc-II; DES J2251.2-5836) .

gmw_00006479.name: UGC 7605
gmw_00006479.overview: UGC 7605 is a blue irregular galaxy with a distinctive horseshoe shape [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414]. Its brightest blue stars are concentrated near the core, while the outer regions are redder and smoother [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 7605 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This is a blue irregular galaxy shaped like a horseshoe. The brightest blue stars in UGC 7605 are concentrated towards the core, and the outlying galaxy parts are redder and smooth. From the luminosity of the brightest stars Makarova et al. (1998) derived a galaxy distance of 4.4 0.9 Mpc. The CM diagram of UGC 7605 (Fig. 2) shows the RGB population giving a distance of 4.43 0.53 Mpc, which is in close agreement with the previous estimate.

gmw_00006480.name: IC 3687
gmw_00006480.overview: IC 3687 is an irregular dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 4.57 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414]. It exhibits a cometary morphology with intense, off-center star-forming regions that are decoupled from its older stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030].

sources.summary: The survey groups this Magellanic irregular with cometary systems whose off-center, intense star-forming regions are decoupled from the smoother old stellar light.

quotes.quote: UGC 7326 (classified Im), UGC 7866 (an IABm) and UGC 8098 (an SBm, also named NGC 4861; see also Conselice et al. who present ground-based and Hubble Space Telescope imaging of this galaxy) all show highly asymmetric or `cometary' morphologies (Fig. Figd ) in their continuum-subtracted H + [ N ii ] images, with extremely intense star formation centres which are displaced very significantly from the centres of the old stellar distributions. This is reflected in long central troughs in the EW curves, with sharp rises to very high values when the star formation centres are reached (all growth curves are centred on the peaks of the old stellar light in the R band images).

The decoupling between the smooth old stellar distribution and the highly clumped and asymmetric sites of ongoing star formation is particularly marked in these galaxies. The overall EW values for these three galaxies lie in the range 5--20 nm, much higher than is typical for even late-type spiral galaxies. Local EW values in apertures centred on the H ii complexes lie in the range 50-100 nm for these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper discusses IC 3687 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: IC 3687 is an irregular dwarf galaxy with several regions of current star formation activity. Its CM diagram shows a mixed stellar population with a pronounced RGB with I (TRGB) = 24.29 0.22 mag, which corresponds to a distance of 4.57 0.48 Mpc. Our distance for IC 3687 differs from the previous distance, 3.0 0.6 Mpc, obtained by Makarova et al. (1998) via the brightest stars.

gmw_00006481.name: KK 166
gmw_00006481.overview: KK 166 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy of very low surface brightness located in the CVn I cloud [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414]. It has a dominant red giant branch stellar population and has not been detected in the neutral hydrogen line [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KK 166 as a very low surface brightness dwarf spheroidal candidate in CVn I and measures its RGB-based distance and photometry.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is unique in terms of being the only dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy of very low surface brightness identified so far in the CVn I region. (Another possible dSph in CVn I is DDO 113 = KDG 90). The galaxy has been observed but not detected in the H i line by Huchtmeier et al. (2000). The CM diagram shows a dominant RGB population with I (TRGB) = 24.36 0.34 mag, yielding a distance of 4.74 0.69 Mpc, which confirms KK 166 as a likely member of the CVn I cloud. Apart from stellar photometry, we also carried out surface photometry in circular apertures.

gmw_00006482.name: UGC 8833
gmw_00006482.overview: UGC 8833 is a blue irregular dwarf galaxy located at the eastern edge of the CVn I cloud [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414, arXiv:2303.05104]. It is a gas-rich system with an oblate dark matter halo and several regions of intense, ongoing star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414, arXiv:2303.05104].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 8833 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This blue irregular galaxy looks like a binary system because of several regions with ongoing intense star formation. UGC 8833 is situated at the eastern edge of the CVn I cloud. According to Makarova et al. (1998) its distance derived from its brightest stars is 3.2 0.6 Mpc. Our photometry of the WFPC2 images yields a TRGB distance of 3.19 0.21 Mpc, which confirms the previous distance estimate.

sources.summary: The paper includes UGC8833 from VLA ANGST and classifies it among gas-rich dwarf galaxies with q=0.2-0.3 and generally oblate halos.

quotes.quote: We also included two galaxies NGC 4190 and UGC 8833 from the VLA ACS Nearby Galaxy Survey Treasury (VLA ANGST) survey ott.etal.2012 . Table 1 shows the sample of 20 galaxies, of which first 8 are relatively large, massive spirals (NGC 0628, NGC 3184, NGC 4736, NGC 5194, NGC 5236 and NGC 6946), and the remaining 12 are smaller spiral, dwarf or irregular galaxies. The procedure for determining the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks as a function of radius is described in ianjamasimanana12 and ianjamasimanana.etal.2017 as well as in das.etal.2020 . We basically co-added the individual velocity profiles over radial bins to obtain azimuthally averaged, high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) velocity dispersion values for each radial bin. The stacked profiles were fitted with single Gaussian functions, where the half width of the fitted Gaussian represents the velocity dispersion of the gas. This method gives a better estimate of the velocity dispersion compared to the moment2 maps. Figure 4 in the appendix shows the radial variation of the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks. Its value is less than 10 kms ^ 1 at the R _ 25 radius, except for a few cases (NGC 5194, NGC 4190, DDO 187 and IC 10), where _ H i lies between 11 and 14 kms ^ 1 .

Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

It is interesting to note that the gas rich galaxies (9 of them) all have oblate halos, with q<0.6 but the stellar dominated galaxies have a range of q values with q 0.2 (very oblate) to q 1.25 (prolate). Perhaps the most important result from this study is that the gas rich galaxies all have oblate halos at R_ 25 (Fig. 3). There are 9 gas rich galaxies that have M(gas)/M(baryon>0.5 and they are all dwarf galaxies : Holmberg1, Holmberg2, M81dwfA, DDO46, DDO53, DDO75, DDO187, F564-V3 and UGC8833. The q varies between 0.11 in DDO187 to 0.55 in Holmberg2. The stellar dominated galaxies, however, have a variety of q values ranging from q=0.21 in IC10 to q=1.25 for NGC5194 at R_ 25 .

gmw_00006483.name: IOK--1
gmw_00006483.overview: IOK--1 is a compact Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy with a redshift of z = 6.965 [arXiv:1107.3159, arXiv:1204.3426]. Its ultraviolet emission indicates a star formation rate of 16 solar masses per year, while its far-infrared star formation rate is constrained to less than 50 solar masses per year [arXiv:1204.3426].

sources.summary: The paper reobserves SDF-63544, alias IOK-1, detecting its Ly line at higher signal-to-noise and resolution and confirming a z=6.965 Ly-emitting dropout.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:2d1dspectra shows a prominent emission line in the spectra of SDF-63544 and SDF-46975. We fit Gaussian profiles to these lines using the IDL |MPFIT| routine markwardt2009 http://www.physics.wisc.edu/ craigm/idl/idl.html , and find the emission lines correspond to the observed central wavelengths 9683 (SDF-63544) and 9536 (SDF-46975) The best-fit central wavelengths are slightly shorter ( 3 ), if we fit an asymmetric Gaussian profile in which the standard deviation on the blue side is smaller than that on the red side, as a better approximation of the observed profiles but with an additional free parameter. . One z dropout galaxy, SDF-63544, was previously identified as a Ly emitter, IOK-1, whose redshift was confirmed spectroscopically by . However, the signal-to-noise ratio ( S/N ) of the Ly line in their spectrum is only 5 . We deemed it worthwhile to detect the line at higher S/N and with higher resolution in order to verify the line detection and identify the line based on line profile analysis.

Thus, we conclude that all of the detected lines are redshifted Ly . The redshifts derived from the Ly emission centroids are z_ spec = 6.965 for SDF-63544, z_ spec = 6.844 for SDF-46975, and z_ spec = 7.213 for GN-108036.

sources.summary: The paper treats IOK--1 as a z=6.96 LAE, giving its UV SFR, compact HST size, and FIR-based SFR upper limit from PdBI observations.

quotes.quote: The second source is IOK--1 , a LAE at z=6.96 (Iye et al. \ 2006). This LAE is similar in both Ly-- and continuum brightness to those in other samples at z 6.6 (see Fig. 1). The (rest--frame) UV emission implies a SFR of 16 M _ yr ^ 1 and HST imaging of IOK--1 gives a source size of <1" (Cai et al. \ 2011).

Conservatively assuming a SED shape typical of local spiral galaxies we derive upper limits for the FIR--based star formation rates (SFRs) of 70 M _ yr ^ 1 , 50 M _ yr ^ 1 and 40 M _ yr ^ 1 for Himiko , IOK--1 and GRB 090423 , respectively.

gmw_00006484.name: GRB 090423
gmw_00006484.overview: GRB 090423 is a very high-redshift galaxy with a redshift estimated at approximately 8.1 to 8.26 [arXiv:1204.3426]. Its afterglow has been detected in the millimeter continuum at 3 mm wavelengths, while its far-infrared flux density remains constrained by upper limits [arXiv:1204.3426].

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 090423 as a very high-redshift GRB host target, summarizing its redshift, continuum constraints, and FIR-based SFR upper limit.

quotes.quote: The third source is GRB 090423 (Krimm et al. \ 2009). VLT spectroscopy of GRB 090423 gave a redshift of 8.26 ^ +0.07 _ 0.08 , based on a continuum break due to Gunn--Peterson absorption (Tanvir et al. \ 2009). A similar estimate was reported by Salvaterra et al. \ (2009) using spectroscopy at the TNG (z=8.1 ^ +0.1 _ 0.3 ). This makes GRB 090423 amongst the highest redshift GRBs discovered. The afterglow has been detected in the millimeter continuum by the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer (PdBI) at 3 mm wavelengths with a flux density of 0.3 0.1 mJy (Castro--Tirado et al. \ 2009, de Ugarte Postigo et al. \ 2012, obtained on 2009, April 23 24).
An upper limit of the afterglow and the host galaxy FIR flux density at 1.2 mm wavelengths of 0.23 0.32mJy using MAMBO has also been reported (Riechers et al. \ 2009, obtained on 2009, April 25).

gmw_00006485.name: NGC 178
gmw_00006485.overview: NGC 178 is a star-forming galaxy located approximately 20.6 megaparsecs away [arXiv:1311.2227]. Its ionized gas is divided between two distinct areas, consisting of one region around the bulk of the observed continuum and a separate extra-planar region [arXiv:1311.2227].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 178 is star forming and has ionized gas in two regions, but its ionization parameter map indicates optically thick nebulae.

quotes.quote: NGC 178 is forming stars at a rate of 0.55 /yr b:Oey_apj07 and lies 20.6 Mpc away b:Meurer_apjs06 . In the emission-line images we see that ionized gas is split between two areas, one around the bulk of the observed continuum, and a separate extra-planar region (Figure f:img_n178 ). Since ionizing radiation emitted at the edge of galaxies is more likely to escape b:Gnedin_apj08 , it might be reasonable to expect to find some evidence of density bounding in this region of the ionization parameter map. However, the ionization parameter map shows that all nebular regions are optically thick.

gmw_00006486.name: NGC 7126
gmw_00006486.overview: NGC 7126 is an SA(rs) spiral galaxy located approximately 45.5 Mpc away [arXiv:1311.2227]. It features an extended spiral structure filled with H II regions and is actively interacting with its nearby companion galaxy, NGC 7125 [arXiv:1311.2227].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7126 as the sample's most distant SA(rs) galaxy, with extended spiral emission, interaction with NGC 7125, and no catalogued wind.

quotes.quote: NGC 7126 is an SA(rs) galaxy b:deVaucouleurs_rc391 . At 45.5 Mpc away b:Meurer_apjs06 , it is the most distant galaxy in our sample. The emission-line images reveal an extended spiral structure filled with \ regions (Figure f:img_n7126 ). \ observations show that it is interacting with its nearby companion NGC 7125 b:Nordgren_aj97 . NGC 7126 does not have a catalogued wind, and, with its high star formation rate, acts as a control against this parameter in the sample b:Hanish_apj10 .

gmw_00006487.name: SDSS J115244.11+254027.1
gmw_00006487.overview: SDSS J115244.11+254027.1 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.05403 [arXiv:1912.10354]. It is known to be the host galaxy of the transient supernova SN 2018bvw [arXiv:1912.10354].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J115244.11+254027.1 as the star-forming host of SN 2018bvw and gives the transient offset from the host center.

quotes.quote: The position is 4.71 \ from SDSS J115244.11+254027.1, a star-forming galaxy at z=0.05403 0.00001 (248.85 ; Alam2015 ). The transient position with respect to the host galaxy is shown in Figure fig:cutout , with the host galaxy image constructed from SDSS g , , and i band cutouts using the method in Lupton2004 . At this distance, the projected offset between \ and the center of the host corresponds to d=5.68 kpc.

gmw_00006488.name: TXS 0025+197
gmw_00006488.overview: TXS 0025+197 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=1.552 [arXiv:2403.13713]. The source exhibited enhanced gamma-ray activity in late 2019, prompting observations by the MAGIC telescope [arXiv:2403.13713].

sources.summary: The paper identifies TXS 0025+197 as the highest-redshift FSRQ in the analyzed sample and reports MAGIC observations during enhanced Fermi-LAT gamma-ray activity.

quotes.quote: TXS 0025+197 ( z=1.552 , 2018A&A...613A..51P ) is the FSRQ with the highest redshift among the analyzed sources. The Fermi LAT observed an increased ray flux on 14th August 2019. Preliminary analysis indicates that the source reached a peak daily flux ( E>100 MeV) of (1.0 0.2) 10 ^ 6 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 2019ATel13032....1B . MAGIC observed TXS 0025+197 in September and November-December 2019, during an increased activity observed by Fermi LAT in the ray band (with a flux 50 -- 60 times higher than ) and collected 5 hours of good quality data.
The Fermi LAT LC, focusing on the time of the MAGIC observation is shown in Fig  fig:plot_txs0025 . Unfortunately, simultaneous optical KVA data were not available.

gmw_00006489.name: B2 2234+28A
gmw_00006489.overview: B2 2234+28A is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.790 [arXiv:1601.03859, arXiv:2202.00950] with an optical spectrum transitional between a flat-spectrum radio quasar and a BL Lac object [arXiv:1601.03859, arXiv:2202.00950]. It exhibits highly variable gamma-ray, optical, and near-infrared emission driven by a Doppler-boosted relativistic jet [arXiv:2104.04436, arXiv:2202.00950].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2234+282 as a z=0.79 gamma-ray CSO candidate with transitional optical spectrum, Fermi detection, low-frequency turnover, and CSO-like radio structure.

quotes.quote: 2234+282 (also named as CTD 135) has a redshift z = 0.79 . Its optical spectrum was described by Shaw12 as transitional between flat-spectrum quasars and BL Lac type objects. It was detected by Fermi with a statistical significance of 52.1 , corresponding to 3FGL J2236.3+2829 and 2FGL J2236.4+2828 Acero15 , but not detected by the Energetic Gamma Ray Experiment Telescope EGRET; Hartman99 of the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory, probably due to insufficient sensitivity. The radio spectrum of the total flux density is complex above 1 GHz due to the variability of the non-simultaneous data points (Figure fig2 ) http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . Except for that, there is a significant decrease in the flux density at 408 MHz, suggesting a low frequency turnover around 1 GHz in the spectrum.

To summarize, all pieces of evidences, including the compact triple structure, steep spectrum, and low jet speed are consistent with each other to imply that 2234+282 is a CSO. The brightness temperature and moderate variability of the core indicate that the innermost jet is Doppler boosted.
The observational properties of 2234+282 make it a ray CSO candidate, increasing the number of ray CSO candidates from 2 to 3.

sources.summary: The paper describes CTD 135 as a compact symmetric object with highly variable GeV emission, a blazar-like core jet, Doppler boosting, and symmetric radio lobes attributed to bubble-like remnants.

quotes.quote: The ray emission properties of CTD 135, a typical compact symmetric object (CSO), are investigated with 11-year Fermi/LAT observations. We show that it has bright and significantly variable GeV emission, with the ray luminosity of L_ 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and a variation index of TS _ var =1002 . A quasi-periodic oscillation (QPO) with a periodicity of 460 days is detected in the global 95 false-alarm level. These ray emission features are similar to that of blazars.

The high luminosity and obvious variability as well as the QPO signature in the ray band demonstrate the strong Doppler boosting effect in CTD 135. The strong Doppler boosting effect implies a small viewing angle of the core jet for CTD 135, which is not coincident with its symmetric radio structure. We proposed that an episodic jet exists in the core region of CTD 135. Its pc-scale lobes are remnants from the previous period of jet activity similar to the Fermi bubbles in the Galaxy while its rays originate from a relaunched relativistic jet.

sources.summary: The paper revisits CTD 135, noting its quasar aliases, Fermi association, and z=0.790, but finds VLBI, variability, and SED evidence show it is a blazar-like AGN, not a CSO.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension RA_ VLBI = 22^ h 36^ min 22.4708467^ s and declination DEC_ VLBI = 28^ 28^ 57.412755 ^

quotes.quote: The quasar CTD 135 (2234 + 282, J2236 + 2828) emerged as a promising candidate, as it is associated with a Fermi source (4FGL J2236.3 + 2828, ), its radio morphology observed with cm-wavelength VLBI imaging at multiple frequencies is reminiscent of a CSO, and its radio spectrum based on total flux density measurements appears to peak at around 1 GHz . However, determining the shape of the radio spectrum could be uncertain if the source is highly variable in time and the flux density measurements are not simultaneous. The spectroscopic redshift of CTD 135 is z=0.790 . Its optical spectrum is characteristic of blazars in a bright continuum-dominated state, but a broad Mg II emission line with observed-frame equivalent width of 4.9 was detected in the spectrum in a low state . Therefore the optical spectrum is transitional between flat-spectrum radio quasars (FSRQs) and BL Lac objects, affected by flux variability .

Indeed, as it is suggested by independent observational evidence collected in Section observations , CTD 135 shares its properties with blazars, AGN with relativistic jets pointed towards the observer. In particular, the existence of the compact flat/inverted-spectrum SW component (Figure spx ), clearly resolved with VLBI at 43 GHz and characterised by high brightness temperature , the highly variable ray and mid-infrared emission, and the broad-band SED indicating Doppler-boosted emission are all against the CSO classification tentatively proposed by . We find it important to point out that CTD 135 did not pass the scrutiny of a detailed multi-band investigation as a CSO candidate, and it is in fact not a CSO.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 2234+28A as an active FSRQ observed by MAGIC during optical and GeV high states, then modeled as a possible BLR-absorbed VHE source.

quotes.quote: B2 2234+28A ( z = 0.790 , 2012ApJ...748...49S ) displayed notable activity that the Guillermo Haro Observatory recorded. In particular, a significant increase in the source's luminosity in the near-infrared (NIR) band was detected. On 26th November 2010, the luminosity of the source in the NIR band increased approximately by a factor of 11 on a daily timescale 2010ATel.3056....1C . Later in 2016, the same observation revealed a sixfold increase 2016ATel.8572....1C .

MAGIC observed B2 2234+28A during its increased activity in the optical and GeV energy bands observed by KVA and Fermi LAT (flux 1.3 -- 2 times higher than ), respectively, in September 2018 and June/July 2019 and collected 6.7 hours of good-quality data. Fig.  fig:plot_mwl_b2_2234 illustrates the UVOT and XRT light curves, demonstrating that the source was undergoing a phase of amplified activity during the period of MAGIC observations.

Regarding the other two sources, B2 2234+28A and CTA 102, the MAGIC ULs around 100 GeV are below the Fermi LAT EBL extrapolation model. Therefore, those two objects are plausible candidates for sources in which absorption in BLR could introduce an extra cut-off from absorption in the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 2234+282 has been claimed as a compact symmetric BL Lac with a two-sided jet, but adopts the northernmost map feature as the core.

quotes.quote: 2234+282 (CTD 135): have claimed this BL Lac to be a compact symmetric object with a two-sided jet, based on a comparison of VLBA maps at 8.4 and 15 GHz. However, the angular resolution is insufficient to determine a spectral index for the putative core feature. For the purposes of our kinematic analysis we have identified the core with the northernmost jet feature in our maps.

gmw_00006490.name: TXS 2241+406
gmw_00006490.overview: TXS 2241+406 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=1.171 [arXiv:2403.13713]. The source has exhibited strong optical variability and multiple gamma-ray outbursts, though it was later observed in a low-activity state [arXiv:2403.13713].

sources.summary: The paper presents TXS 2241+406 as a promising VHE candidate with strong gamma-ray and optical variability, followed by MAGIC during a later low-activity period.

quotes.quote: TXS 2241+406 ( z=1.171 , 2012ApJ...748...49S ): While being a promising candidate to emit VHE gamma rays, it also showed exceptional variability at past times. For the first time in February 2015, Fermi LAT observed a gamma-ray outburst from this source on a daily timescale 2015ATel.8319....1O . During that period, TXS 2241+406 was also monitored with KVA, showing optical variability spanning over 2.5 mag. As can be seen in the MWL LC (Fig.  fig:plot_txs_2241 and Fig.  fig:plot_LC_Fermi_12year ), the variability of the source has significantly increased since 2015 compared to the previous six years of Fermi LAT observations, encouraging monitoring with the MAGIC telescopes.

In August 2017, MAGIC followed this source for the first time and subsequently conducted a 27-hour observational campaign from July to December 2019. Unfortunately, during this period, the activity of the source was low, and it was either not detected by Fermi LAT or the flux was below the average Fermi LAT flux .

gmw_00006491.name: Mrk 177
gmw_00006491.overview: Mrk 177 is a dwarf host galaxy with a total stellar mass of approximately 10^8.55 solar masses [arXiv:2101.07797]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology indicating that it is a post-merger galaxy [arXiv:2101.07797].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 177 as the dwarf host of SDSS1133, giving its luminosity, stellar mass, disturbed post-merger morphology, metallicity, star formation, WISE colors, and weak nuclear X-rays.

quotes.quote: The SDSS1133's host galaxy Mrk 177 is a dwarf galaxy with the g band absolute magnitude of M_ g = -16.6 mag, which is comparable to the SMC. The total stellar mass of Mrk 177 is estimated to be M_ * 10^ 8.55 M_ koss14 . The disturbed morphology of Mrk 177 indicates that Mrk 177 is a post-merger galaxy see for details koss14 . The gas-phase oxygen abundance is estimated to be 12+ ( O/H )=8.58 from the SDSS optical spectroscopy of the Mrk 177's nucleus. The low metallicity galaxies are preferred sites for massive star formation, thus the low metallicity in Mrk 177 may be consistent with the idea that the progenitor of SDSS1133 is a massive star like other LBVs.

gmw_00006492.name: IRAS 07598+6508
gmw_00006492.overview: IRAS 07598+6508 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy hosting a type 1 low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:2105.13460, arXiv:2405.18130]. The system exhibits polar dust [arXiv:2405.18130], starburst-like systemic excitation [arXiv:0907.4370], and extreme blue-shifted neon outflows [arXiv:0907.4370], though it remains undetected in NuSTAR X-ray imaging [arXiv:1408.3633].

sources.summary: F07599+6508 is a ULIRG loBAL quasar with multiphase outflows, but the NOEMA CO data show no resolved high-velocity outflow or clear complex motions beyond a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: F07599+6508 is a ULIRG low89 with a Type 1 low-ionization broad absorption line (loBAL) quasar lanzetta93,lipari94,hines95 nucleus and unusually strong Fe  ii emission lawrence88 . In these and other respects, it is reminiscent of better-known Mrk 231 rupke05 . The host galaxy is dominated by the bright AGN point source sanders88 , but shows some relatively symmetric extended structure in Hubble Space Telescope (HST) H band imaging veilleux06 . Outflows have already been detected in multiple gas phases.

We do not detect a \ outflow in F07599+6508 on the arcsecond scales probed with our observations. The \ emission is not well resolved spatially, detected at very high velocities ( >500   ), nor do we observe clear signs of complex kinematic motions beyond a rotating disk. There is some spatial extension in several velocity channels (e.g., 120   , 180--240  ), but higher spatial resolution observations would be required to identify departures from a Keplerian motion.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 07598+6508 as a type-1-AGN ULIRG whose NIR spectrum requires polar dust, with SMART fits improving when polar dust is included.

quotes.quote: The galaxy IRAS 07598+6508 is the only ULIRG in the HERUS sample (apart from 3C 273 which we do not fit, as it clearly has a strong contribution in the submillimetre from radio emission), which shows clear signs of a type 1 AGN with a strong optical continuum with a power-law shape. This ULIRG was therefore fitted with a type 1 AGN by . In Fig. fig:6508 we fit IRAS 07598+6508 with all four combinations of models without polar dust. We observe that in all cases there is evidence that the torus model by itself can not reproduce the (near-infrared) NIR spectrum. We attribute this to the presence of polar dust in this object, which is more visible because of the assumed face-on inclination.
The ^ 2 _ min, of the CYGNUS fit without polar dust is much higher compared to the fit with polar dust shown in Table A1. The SED fit plot with polar dust is shown in Fig. A2.

sources.summary: The paper reports two neon velocity components in IRAS 07598+6508, starburst-like systemic excitation, no available 1.4 GHz flux, and extreme blue-shifted [Ne v] outflow behavior.

quotes.quote: For other sources two gaussian components can be discerned, one at systemic velocity, the other at blue shifted velocities. Examples are the [ Ne ii ] line of IRAS 07598+6508 and the [ Ne iii ] line of IRAS 15462--0450.

In pure starburst galaxies the excitation of the nuclear starburst as measured from the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] line ratio is usually low and in the range of 0.05--0.2 thornley00,bernard-salas09 A notable exception may be the pure starburst galaxy NGC 7714, which has a measured [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio of 0.75 bernard-salas09 . In our sample of sixteen excitation conditions like these are met for the systemic components of IRAS 05024--1941, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 12127--1412NE, IRAS 15462--0450 and IRAS 15225+2350. Among these IRAS 15225+2350 is the only source optically classified as a starburst.

Note that IRAS 07598+6508 is not included, as it lacks a 1.4 GHz flux measurement. A striking result of our study is the identification of a sizeable subsample of sources which show a strong blue shift of the [ Ne v ] line center in combination with a smaller blue shift of the [ Ne iii ] line center (Figs. fig2 and fig3 ) in their spectra. A per-source comparison of the [ Ne ii ] , [ Ne iii ] and [ Ne v ] profiles strongly suggests these shifts to be the result of an increase in ionization with increasing blue shift in the line. This is supported by the behavior of the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] and [ Ne v ] / [ Ne iii ] line ratios as a function of line velocity. The most extreme sources in this subsample do not even exhibit a [ Ne v ] component at systemic velocity. Examples are IZw1, IRAS 05189--2524, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 11119+3257 and IRAS 15462--0450. This strongly suggests that at least in these sources the observed [ Ne v ] emission cannot arise in NLR gas in keplerian rotation.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 07598 in its NuSTAR BAL-quasar sample, finding the targets strongly X-ray weak and this object undetected in the imaging.

quotes.quote: The _ OX constraints for the targets are shown in Figure 2, which indicate that their rest-frame 2  keV luminosities are 14 to >330 times weaker than expected from the Steffen2006 _ OX - L_ 2500  relation. These _ OX values have not been corrected for any intrinsic optical/UV reddening, which would render the values even more negative. Note that the spread of the Steffen2006 data points constrains the expected amount of quasar variability e.g., Gibson2012 , and after accounting for the measured flux variability of our targets (see Section 3 below), they are still significantly X-ray weak.

The other three targets, PG 0043, IRAS 07598, and IRAS 14026, are not detected; we also verified the non-detections via visual inspection of the smoothed images. None of the targets is detected in the 24--79 keV band, and the constraints from the non-detections in this band are not as tight as those in the other bands.

gmw_00006493.name: VV 340a
gmw_00006493.overview: VV 340a is a luminous infrared spiral galaxy viewed nearly edge-on [arXiv:2405.18130] with a large-scale dust lane and a nuclear disk [arXiv:1811.09281]. It is in an early-stage merger [arXiv:1811.09281] and hosts an obscured, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2405.18130, arXiv:1811.09281] with an X-shaped radio structure [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: SMART fits VV 340a better with a disk host viewed nearly edge-on and predicts an obscured AGN consistent with prior XMM-Newton evidence, but a higher SFR.

quotes.quote: To explore the option of fitting with a disc as a host galaxy component, we fitted the LIRG VV 340a, which belongs to a pair of spiral galaxies known as Arp 302, with the northern galaxy, VV 340a, being viewed edge-on and the southern galaxy, VV 340b, viewed face-on. We observe that the disc model appears to fit better not just the photometry but also the Spitzer spectroscopy. The difference in the ^ 2 _ min, of the two different fits is also significant, with the disc model providing the minimum value of 2.84, while the spheroidal model provides a ^ 2 _ min, of 5.65, a factor of two higher. The predicted disc morphology is consistent with the nature of this galaxy.

It is very interesting that the fit of VV 340a predicts an almost edge-on view and this agrees with the image of the galaxy. presented XMM-Newton data of VV 340a and VV 340b from which they find evidence of a heavily obscured Seyfert 2-like AGN in VV 340a. This is in agreement with our fit, which predicts the presence of an obscured AGN with an anisotropy-corrected AGN luminosity of 0.25^ 0.06 _ 0.04 10^ 12 L_ . Our model, however, predicts a much higher SFR compared with the estimate of of 1 2 M_ /yr for this galaxy, which is based entirely on X-ray data.

sources.summary: The paper classifies VV 340a (N) as a radio AGN, describing an X-shaped 1.49 GHz radio structure, steep outward spectral-index values, a NeV AGN detection, and conflicting starburst optical/PAH classifications.

quotes.quote: VV 340a (N) Only the north companion of the interacting system has been observed at both 1.49 8.44 GHz. The x-shaped radio structure at 1.49 GHz suggests a double-double radio source or an X-shaped radio source , i.e. a restarted radio source associated with an AGN. We do not have further observations at 8.44 GHz to cover the whole area seen at 1.49 GHz.

Based on our classification and the steep values of ( > 1 going outwards), we classify it as radio-AGN. This object has a [ NeV ] detection which indicates the presence of an AGN, while the 6.2 m EQW classifies it as a SB. Probably there is a circumnuclear PDR region that does not get affected by the AGN.

sources.summary: VV 340a is an early-stage merger with a Compton-thick AGN, a large-scale dust lane, and a nuclear disk, but the OSIRIS line-ratio map shows no coherent outflow structure.

quotes.quote: VV 340a VV 340a is at an early stage of merging as categorized in . It hosts a Compton-Thick AGN that was detected by Chandra but not by Swift BAT . Its large scale disk features a solid dust lane while its nuclear disk is seen in the continuum and emission (see nuclear disk properties in Paper I). We see no coherent structure in the / map that could be associated with outflows, which, if warranted by the depth of the data, could place an upper limit on how early outflows are triggered in the nuclei of merging progenitors.

gmw_00006494.name: MS 0205.7+3509
gmw_00006494.overview: MS 0205.7+3509 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0402105] hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0402105]. It exhibits strong X-ray absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0402105], which is likely caused by a nearby foreground companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0402105].

sources.summary: The paper treats MS 0205.7+3509 as a BL Lac/lensing candidate, using XMM-Newton data to argue that its strong X-ray absorption likely arises in a nearby foreground companion galaxy.

quotes.quote: MS 0205.7+3509 is another such rare candidate, and while deep imaging has revealed that the BL Lac is centred on a host galaxy that is likely an elliptical and not offset in a spiral host as had been inferred from previous observations 1995ApJ...454...55S , a companion galaxy was also detected very close to the BL Lac line of sight 1997A&A...321..374F which had caused the previous inference of decentering in a spiral host to be made. X-ray observations with ROSAT 1995ApJ...454...55S and ASCA 1999A&A...345..414W showed the existence of absorption well above the Galactic level, indeed at a level second only among BL Lacs to PKS 1413+135 2002AJ....124.2401P . These results, on MS 0205.7+3509, imply that the X-ray absorber is in the companion galaxy which is foreground to the AGN.

Though somewhat lower in terms of column density, MS 0205.7+3509 is six times brighter in X-rays than PKS 1413+135, making it one of the best available cases in which to study absorption in the hot phase of the ISM in a galaxy that is not at low redshift. XMM-Newton observations were performed in an attempt to determine the nature of the X-ray absorber in MS 0205.7+3509, in particular in the context of a foreground lensing galaxy.

It is possible that the absorber is associated with the BL Lac host galaxy. But the lack of gas and dust in elliptical galaxies 1999sfet.conf..119K , the typical hosts of BL Lacs, and the proximity of the companion galaxy 2.3 , \ corresponding to an apparent linear distance of 11 kpc, 1997A&A...321..374F strongly suggest a link between the companion and the absorbing gas, as proposed by 1997A&A...321..374F and 1999A&A...345..414W .

gmw_00006495.name: J123555.14
gmw_00006495.overview: J123555.14 is a submillimeter galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.09356]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations of the source reveal a compact radio core with a brightness temperature of approximately 5.2 × 10^5 K, which is significantly higher than the limit for star-forming regions [arXiv:2004.09356].

sources.summary: The paper detects compact VLBI emission from J123555.14, infers a brightness temperature above the star-forming limit, and identifies its radio emission as AGN-related.

quotes.quote: Out of the four SMGs observed, we detect one source, J123555.14, that has an integrated EVN flux density of 201 15.2 Jy, corresponding to a brightness temperature of 5.2 0.7 10 ^5 K. We therefore identify that the radio emission from J123555.14 is associated with an AGN.

J123555.14 has a brightness temperature of 5.2 0.7 10 ^5 K, which is approximately five times higher than the star-forming envelope. This supports the interpretation that J123555.14 contains an AGN core. The source detected by VLBI, J123555.14, lies within the FoV of eMERGE.

gmw_00006496.name: J123600.10
gmw_00006496.overview: J123600.10 is a galaxy at a redshift of 2.710 that exhibits a disk-like morphology with close companions [arXiv:2004.09356]. Its optical morphology suggests it is possibly an edge-on merger, while its high radio luminosity indicates that extended jet emission from quasar activity may exist [arXiv:2004.09356].

sources.summary: The paper reports no VLBI detection for J123600.10, but eMERGE detects extended emission, a disk-like morphology with companions, and high radio luminosity suggesting possible jet or quasar activity.

quotes.quote: J123600.10 was not detected in our VLBI observations with a 3- brightness temperature limit of < 5.6 10^5 K and it is located at a redshift of 2.710 chapman2005 . This source is detectable in the eMERGE re-weighted image with an angular size of 859 253 mas ^2 , and the measured peak and integrated flux densities are 52.4 1.7 Jy beam ^ 1 and 83.5 4.1 Jy, respectively. This yields a brightness temperature of 523 K.

The source shows a disc-like morphology with close companions. The source has a low VLBI-to-VLA flux ratio upper limit of 0.16, suggesting that a significant fraction of the radio emission is extended and probably associated with star-formation processes. This is supported by other measurements chapman2003 suggest that this galaxy is possibly an edge-on merger from its optical morphology. However, this source has a total 1.4 GHz VLA flux density of 262.0 17.1 Jy, which corresponds to a radio luminosity of 1.66 0.11 10 ^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 .
The high luminosity indicates that extended jet emission from a jet associated with quasar activity may exist in this source jarvis2019, muxlow2005 , which was undetected in these VLBI observations.

gmw_00006497.name: J131225.73
gmw_00006497.overview: J131225.73 is a galaxy located at a redshift of 1.554 that exhibits a steep radio spectral index of 0.7 [arXiv:2004.09356]. Although it has a radio excess, its lack of detection in VLBI observations may be due to extended active galactic nucleus jet structures that dominate the total flux density [arXiv:2004.09356].

sources.summary: The paper reports no VLBI detection of J131225.73 despite radio excess and low q values, suggesting its AGN-related radio emission may be extended jet structure.

quotes.quote: J131225.73 was not detected in our VLBI observations with a 3- brightness temperature limit of < 2.4 10 ^ 5 K and it is located at a redshift of 1.554 chapman2005 . A point source was detected at 8.4 GHz by the VLA at a resolution of 6 fomalont2002 with a total flux density of 200.3 6.5 Jy. The implied spectral index of the source is relatively steep: 0.7.

However, although this is a steep spectrum radio source, it does have a significant radio-excess with relatively low q values. This source has a S_ 850 m of 4.1 1.3 mJy (see Table t1 ), and it is clearly identified as an outlier in Figure f1 . Indeed, it has the lowest value of q^ 850 m _ 1.4 GHz out of all of the sources in the chapman2005 sample.

Given the main observational properties of J131225.73, it is rather surprising that this source goes undetected by the EVN at 1.6 GHz. One possible explanation is that while the radio emission is indeed associated with an AGN, it is extended in nature, which is possibly due to the presence of extended jet features that dominate the total flux density of the source and extend spatially over kiloparsec scales.

gmw_00006498.name: SDSS1605-01
gmw_00006498.overview: SDSS1605-01 is a radio-quiet, low-ionization broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=4.92 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312402, arXiv:1802.03400]. It exhibits extremely deep carbon IV absorption tracing high-velocity outflows [arXiv:astro-ph/0312402, arXiv:1802.03400], a redder slope than average quasars [arXiv:astro-ph/0312402], and a circumgalactic Lyman-alpha nebula [arXiv:1802.03400].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS1605-01 as an extreme z=4.92 LoBAL with exceptionally deep CIV absorption, very high balnicity, MgII absorption evidence, reddening, and possible redshifted absorption.

quotes.quote: SDSS1605-01: an extreme LoBAL at z=4.92 The Amici+NICS spectrum of this extreme BAL is shown in Fig.  fig2 . This has the deepest CIV absorption of all BALs found at high redshift. Its balnicity index of about 9300 2000 km/s is among the highest value ever found among BALs known so far (again, the large error on the balnicity index is dominated by the large uncertainty on the CIV emission profile).

The CIV absorption is so deep that it has ``eaten'' nearly all of the CIV emission. Balnicity indices higher than 8000 km/s are only found among LoBAL quasars. Indeed, also in this object there is indication of MgII absorption (though with a low S/N), confirming the LoBAL nature of this object. This is the only BAL clearly characterized by a redder slope than the average of quasars.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1605-0112 as an extreme z=4.92 LoBAL quasar whose MUSE observations show BAL outflow signatures and a Ly nebula on circumgalactic scales.

quotes.quote: J1605-0112, a radio-quiet quasar at z=4.92 , is one of the most extreme Low Ionization Lines-BAL (LoBAL) quasar in the Universe, both in terms of luminosity and in terms of depth and velocity of the absorption features (see Table tab:properties_quasar ). Indeed, the CIV 1549 absorption troughs are blueshifted by more than 30.000 km s ^ 1 , tracing very high velocity outflowing gas, and have a balnicity index The balnicity index is similar to the Equivalent Width expressed in km s ^ 1 , as defined in . of 9300 km s ^ 1 (see Maiolino2004 ).

A full 1D spectrum obtained with MUSE and a discussion on the BAL features are reported in the Appendix sec:MUSE_spectrum . MUSE observations of J1605-0112 have been acquired as part of the ESO programme ID 095.A-0875 (PI: R. Maiolino) between May and August 2015 at the UT4 ESO/VLT.
Following the data processing steps described in Section sec:data_reduction we detect a Ly nebula, extended on circumgalactic scales around the quasar J1605-0112, at z 4.92 .

gmw_00006499.name: SDSS0756+41
gmw_00006499.overview: SDSS0756+41 is a high-redshift quasar at z=5.09 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312402]. It exhibits weak CIV broad-absorption line features and a blue continuum that indicates an absence of dust reddening [arXiv:astro-ph/0312402].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS0756+41 as a z=5.09 quasar with weak but significant CIV BAL absorption, possible MgII or Fe absorption, and a blue continuum indicating no dust reddening.

quotes.quote: SDSS0756+41 (z=5.09) The Amici+NICS spectrum of this quasar is shown in Fig.  fig4 , which is significantly noisier with respect to the previous spectra. Our spectrum suggests a slightly lower redshift of 5.08 than previously reported and both CIV and CIII ] appear blueshifted with respect to the template. There is a blueshifted absorption (9000 km/s) of CIV with balnicity index of 270 60 km/s.

This absorption is much weaker than in the previous cases, but still quite significant (5 ), also given that the absorption is consistently observed in three different spectra (taken on different nights), which were coadded to produce the final spectrum shown in Fig.  fig4 . Some absorption may be observed blueward of MgII, but with a slightly different velocity (6000 km/s). We also find a dip in the blue region of the FeII bump, which might trace Fe absorption and would identify this as a FeLoBAL.
The continuum slope is slightly bluer than the non-BAL template ( =-1.67 ), again indicating no dust reddening.

gmw_00006500.name: CGCG 229-009
gmw_00006500.overview: CGCG 229-009 is a host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.02825 [arXiv:1805.03205]. Integral field spectroscopy of the galaxy has revealed twenty-five distinct H II regions mapped across its structure [arXiv:1805.03205].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 229-009 as SN 2016esw's host, measures its redshift from IFU spectroscopy, maps H II regions, and derives global host-galaxy properties and extinction.

quotes.quote: The transient was located at = 18^ h 58^ m 04 09 0 1 , = +43^ 56'22 60 0 5 (J2000.0), and it is associated with the galaxy CGCG 229-009 Also called 2MASX J18580350+4356090. at redshift z=0.02831 (see Section sec:host ). With respect to the galaxy nucleus, the object is situated 6 85 east and 13 45 north.

From the IFU spectroscopy of the SN 2016esw host galaxy and using the forbidden ionised nitrogen doublet [ N II ] ( 6583, 6548) together with the strong H emission lines ( 6563), we measure a redshift of z = 0.02825 0.0002 , consistent with the published redshift (0.02831; shivvers16 ). In the rest of the paper, we adopt the redshift derived from the IFU spectroscopy observation.

Host Galaxy We obtained integral field spectroscopy (IFS) of the SN 2016esw host galaxy CGCG 229-009 ( 3 900  s, where the second and third exposures are taken with an offset) with the Potsdam Multi Aperture Spectrograph PMAS; roth05 in PPAK (331 science fibres and 36 for sky measurements) mode verheijen04 mounted on the 3.5 m telescope of the Centro Astron omico Hispano Alem an (CAHA) located at Calar Alto Observatory in Almer ia, Spain. The IFS is an asset, providing additional information about both the SN position and those of the entire host galaxy. From the H emission two-dimensional (2D) map, we select 25 H II regions by segregating clumps of emission using HIIexplorer http://www.caha.es/sanchez/HII_explorer/ (see sanchez12 and galbany18 for criteria selection). Finally, to estimate the galaxy global properties, we sum up all spectra in the 3D datacube with signal-to-noise ratio larger than 1.

gmw_00006501.name: GMP0756
gmw_00006501.overview: GMP0756 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1103.3414] with a highly elliptical, nearly edge-on structure [arXiv:0710.2190]. It features a mildly disky morphology [arXiv:0710.2190] and hosts an extended, young stellar disk [arXiv:1103.3414].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/WFPC2 central photometry for GMP 0756 and describes it as highly elliptical, mildly disky, and nearly edge-on.

quotes.quote: As part of the HST Proposal 10884 (P.I. G. Wegner), the galaxies GMP 0756, GMP 1176, GMP 1990, and GMP2440 were observed with Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) on board the HST on 18--24 April 2007.
It gives rms of 0.01 mag for GMP 0756, 0.066 mag for GMP 1990, 0.08 mag for GMP 1176 and 0.038 mag for GMP 2440.

GMP 0756 and GMP 1990 have also high ellipticity ( 0.65 ) but only mild ( 2 ) diskiness. GMP 2440 is slightly disky. Overall, the high ellipticities and the morphology of the dust distribution indicate that all the four objects are very nearly edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper flags GMP0756 as a Coma early-type galaxy with low stellar mass-to-light ratio, young stellar populations, an extended young stellar disk, and unusual dark-halo scaling behavior.

quotes.quote: There are two galaxies (GMP0756 and GMP1176) with distinctly lower stellar mass-to-light ratios than in the rest of the sample. As Fig. fig:ml_tau shows, these two galaxies have overall young stellar populations.
These are GMP0756 and GMP1176, which have extended and relatively young ( 3 ) stellar disks.

Most of the difference with respect to Ber03 goes back to four galaxies (GMP0144, GMP0756, GMP1176, and GMP5975) that are distinct from the rest of the sample in several respects: (1) they have young central stellar cores ( _0 < 7 ; cf. Meh03 ); (2) at least two of them have an extended thin stellar disk; (3) they follow different dark halo scaling relations Tho09 .

gmw_00006502.name: GMP1176
gmw_00006502.overview: GMP1176 is an early-type galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:1103.3414]. It features an extended, young stellar disk [arXiv:1103.3414], a low stellar mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:1103.3414], and central isophotes with extremely high ellipticity and strong diskiness [arXiv:0710.2190].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/WFPC2 photometry for GMP 1176, reporting calibration precision plus extremely high ellipticity and strong diskiness in its central isophotes.

quotes.quote: As part of the HST Proposal 10884 (P.I. G. Wegner), the galaxies GMP 0756, GMP 1176, GMP 1990, and GMP2440 were observed with Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) on board the HST on 18--24 April 2007.
It gives rms of 0.01 mag for GMP 0756, 0.066 mag for GMP 1990, 0.08 mag for GMP 1176 and 0.038 mag for GMP 2440.

GMP 1176 has extremely high ellipticity ( 0.75 ) and diskiness a_4 exceeding 10 . In this case the isophote fit program of bender1987 delivers a non-zero a_2 coefficient and a satisfactory description of the isophotes requires orders as high as a_ 12 . The corresponding coefficient profiles are shown in Figure fig:phot1176 and listed in Table tab:phot1176 .

sources.summary: The paper flags GMP1176 as a Coma early-type galaxy with low stellar mass-to-light ratio, young stellar populations, an extended young stellar disk, and unusual dark-halo scaling behavior.

quotes.quote: There are two galaxies (GMP0756 and GMP1176) with distinctly lower stellar mass-to-light ratios than in the rest of the sample. As Fig. fig:ml_tau shows, these two galaxies have overall young stellar populations.
These are GMP0756 and GMP1176, which have extended and relatively young ( 3 ) stellar disks.

Most of the difference with respect to Ber03 goes back to four galaxies (GMP0144, GMP0756, GMP1176, and GMP5975) that are distinct from the rest of the sample in several respects: (1) they have young central stellar cores ( _0 < 7 ; cf. Meh03 ); (2) at least two of them have an extended thin stellar disk; (3) they follow different dark halo scaling relations Tho09 .

gmw_00006503.name: GMP5568
gmw_00006503.overview: GMP5568, also known as NGC 4816, is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2301.05269] located in a nearby group infalling toward the Coma Cluster [arXiv:2301.05269]. It features an exceptionally large effective radius [arXiv:1103.3414] and hosts an extended globular cluster system [arXiv:2301.05269].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4816, the brightest S0 in a nearby Coma group, as a comparison object with an extended globular-cluster system and kinematics consistent with first infall.

quotes.quote: We compare the GC system of NGC 4839 with that of NGC 4816, which is the brightest member (S0) of the nearby group and lies at a similar distance in the west from the Coma center. Interestingly the spatial distribution of the GCs in NGC 4839 is significantly more compact than that of the GCs in NGC 4816. In addition, the radial number density profile of the GCs in NGC 4839 shows an abrupt drop at R_ N4839 80 kpc, while that of the GCs in NGC 4816 shows a continuous slow decline even in the outer region at 80<R_ N4816 <500 kpc. The effective radius of the NGC 4839 GC system is about three times smaller than that of the NGC 4816 GC system.

On the other hand, the relative velocity of the NGC 4816 group with respect to the main cluster is only +35 and the NGC 4816 group is located along the large scale filament connecting with Abell 1367. Considering these we infer that the NGC 4816 group is infalling to the cluster center in the sky plane. In addition, the GC system of the NGC 4816 shows an extended structure with a continuously declining radial number density profile. These results indicate that the NGC 4816 is in its first infall.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP5568 as an outlier whose stars appear older than its halo, with an exceptionally large effective radius that may affect inferred dark-matter density and halo assembly redshift.

quotes.quote: The galaxy on the bottom-right, where the stars seem significantly older than the halo is GMP5568. As discussed in Tho09 , dry mergers can move galaxies below the one-to-one relation in Fig. fig:zz . Besides this possibility the effective radius of GMP5568 is exceptionally large ( 27 ).

Therefore, the dark matter density refers to a spatial region significantly larger than in any of our other Coma galaxies. If the effective radius of this galaxy was overestimated, then the average dark matter density would be underestimated and so would be the halo assembly redshift. This could also explain the high dark matter fraction of GMP5568 (cf. Tab. tab:dmfrac ), since it generally increases with the physical distance from the galaxy centre.

gmw_00006504.name: object J164137.53+285820.3
gmw_00006504.overview: Object J164137.53+285820.3 is a small, faint host galaxy with a magnitude of 21.1 [arXiv:1405.5237]. It has a redshift of 0.0907 and hosted the supernova PTF10qts at a projected physical offset of 2.4 kpc [arXiv:1405.5237].

sources.summary: The paper identifies object J164137.53+285820.3 as the small, faint host galaxy of PTF10qts, giving its redshift, projected supernova offset, and non-detection in P48 images.

quotes.quote: A small, faint ( = 21.1 mag) host galaxy, object J164137.53+285820.3, is visible in the SDSS catalogue 1.2" away from the supernova. Its redshift, z=0.0907 , was measured from H and [ O ii ] narrow emission lines in spectra of PTF10qts. At the host's distance (414.9 Mpc), the supernova offset from the centre of this corresponds to a projected physical distance of 2.4 kpc. No host is discernible in the P48 images.

gmw_00006505.name: LARS01
gmw_00006505.overview: LARS01 is a strong Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406] with a highly irregular morphology [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1803.07207]. Its structure features a UV-bright star-forming center [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406], a southwestern tail [arXiv:1308.6578], and faint contours that suggest a recent merger [arXiv:1803.07207].

sources.summary: LARS 01 (Markarian 259) is described as a low-metallicity, modest-SFR, strong Lyman-alpha-emitting galaxy with a UV star-forming center, southwest tail, and fan-like emission structure toward the northeast.

quotes.quote: LARS 01 LARS 01 (Markarian 259) is studied in detail in Paper I and also enters the sample of Wofford2013 , so the reader is directed to these publications for more extensive information. It has a metallicity of 0.25Z_ (Paper I; Asplund2009 ), 0.1 magnitudes of differential extinction ( ) in the gas phases, has a modest SFR of a few , and strong \ emission with an EW of 400 . By these measures it is roughly comparable to ESO 338--IG04 Hayes2005,Atek2008,Ostlin2009 . Indeed it is a strong \ emitting galaxy: with an aperture EW of 35 \ and >50 \ globally, it would be likely selected as a high- z LAE; a luminosity of almost 10^ 42 \ suggests that it could be detected by moderately deep surveys.

Morphologically, it consists of a bright UV star-forming center, with an extended tail to the SW: there is a bright hot-spot of \ emission that emanates from the major star-forming condensation, and flows out in a diffuse fan-like structure preferentially in the NE direction.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 1 as a strong Ly-emitting, high-redshift-survey-selected analog with irregular morphology, a UV-bright northeastern complex, rotation-like kinematics, and localized Ly escape.

quotes.quote: LARS 1 ( Mrk 259 ) With L_ Ly = 8 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 and EW _ Ly =33 LARS 1 is a strong Ly emitting galaxy. A detailed description of the photometric properties of this galaxy was presented in Ostlin2014 . If LARS 1 would be at high- z , it would easily be selected in conventional narrow-band imaging surveys Guaita2015 . The galaxy shows a highly irregular morphology. Its main feature is UV-bright complex in the north-east that harbours the youngest stellar population.

sources.summary: LARS01 has an extended, irregular young central population, highly irregular faint contours suggestive of a recent merger, and colors best matched by mixed stellar populations.

quotes.quote: LARS01 The central region seems dominated by a young population, as indicated by the behavior of the color radial profiles. This region is extended and irregular, as suggested by the U-B spatial map. The three contours in panel D of Fig. fig:lars01 are at 24.5, 25.6 , and 26.5 mag/arcsec ^2 , respectively. The faintest contour is highly irregular, hinting at a recent merger event. Comparison to the SEMs in panels E and F suggests that at any radial distance from the center, a SSP cannot explain the observed photometry. The latter seems much more consistent with the mixed populations model, with a young mass fraction of 10^ 2 in the central region, which decreases towards 10^ 3 in the outskirts. No dust correction is needed to match the mixed populations tracks.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 1 as a strong global Lyman-alpha emitter whose Si II absorption lines are mostly clean, with covering fractions near the absorbed fraction and nearly complete optical thickness.

quotes.quote: LARS 1 is one of the five strong global emitters in the sample, ``strong'' being defined by an integrated f_ esc ^ Ly > 10 see LARSII . All four Si ii line profiles fall inside the detector ranges and do not seem to be polluted by other features. The strength of the absorption differs somewhat, especially for 1304 , which has the lowest resonator strength of them all, indicating that this system might not be completely optically thick. Nonetheless, as can be seen from Figure fig:liproplot , the fitted covering fractions are still close to the absorbed fraction, indicating that complete optical thickness is still a good approximation.

gmw_00006506.name: LARS02
gmw_00006506.overview: LARS02 is a dwarf-like [arXiv:1308.6578], star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1803.07207] with a disturbed velocity field [arXiv:1511.05406] and irregular faint contours that suggest a recent merger [arXiv:1803.07207]. It is largely dust-free [arXiv:1308.6578] and exhibits the highest global escape fraction of Lyman-alpha photons in the LARS sample [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1308.6578].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 2 as the sample’s strongest Ly escape case, with the highest escape fraction and Ly/H ratio, low shear, and disturbed kinematics.

quotes.quote: LARS 2 ( Shoc 240 ) Everywhere in LARS 2 where H photons are produced, Ly photons emerge along the line of sight. LARS 2 is the galaxy with the highest global escape fraction of Ly photons ( f^ Ly _ esc. =52.1 ) and the highest Ly /H ratio (Ly /H =4.53 ). With v_ shear = 23 2 km s ^ 1 , LARS 2 shows the smallest v_ shear in our sample. However, we consider this value as a lower limit since we missed in our pointing the southernmost star forming knot. Our observed velocity field appears to be disturbed, but we could envision a kinematical axis orthogonal to the photometric major axis - i.e. roughly from west to east.

sources.summary: LARS02 shows an offset star-forming region, irregular faint contours indicating a recent merger, and radial colors consistent with mixed central populations and older outskirts.

quotes.quote: LARS02 A star-forming region, offset from the geometric center, is evident in the radial profiles, RGB image, and U-B spatial map. The three contours in panel D are at 24.5 , 26.2 , and 26.9 mag/arcsec ^2 , respectively. The faintest contour is irregular, hinting at a recent merger. Comparison to the SEMs in panels E and F in Fig. fig:lars02 shows that our mixture of two stellar populations with a young mass fraction of 10^ 2 is more consistent with the central regions of the galaxy, while the outskirts of the radial profiles are well-matched by a 1 2 Gyr-old, metal poor stellar population. The latter is also a good match for the integrated color. The decrease in the M/L ratio around 10 arcsec corresponds to the offset star-forming region to the south-east from the center of integration (pink cross in panel D).

sources.summary: LARS 02 is described as dwarf-like, largely dust-free, and the strongest LARS emitter, with rapidly rising emission growth curves and much of its emission emerging within 3.5 kpc.

quotes.quote: LARS 02 This galaxy shows a metallicity and \ EW roughly similar to LARS 01, however with a SFR of around 1 \ it is among the more dwarf-like objects in our sample. It is largely dust-free, and according to all the related quantities is the strongest emitting LARS galaxy: \ EW almost 100 , / =4.5 , =52 inside the 2 r_ P20 radius. Here again we see that the \ EW and / \ ratio locally exceed their recombination values by large factors on kpc scales, and the curves-of-growth for all the \ quantities rise very rapidly. Furthermore, this photometric curve-of-growth is rising so steeply that this galaxy emits 75 of its \ photons by a radius of 3.5 kpc.

gmw_00006507.name: LARS03
gmw_00006507.overview: LARS03 is a luminous infrared galaxy undergoing an early-stage merger with a companion [arXiv:2106.15041, arXiv:2403.19212]. The system features a compact central molecular gas concentration that fuels a strong nuclear starburst [arXiv:2106.15041, arXiv:2403.19212], resulting in a complex overall structure [arXiv:1803.07207].

sources.summary: Arp 238-E is described as a luminous infrared galaxy with high SFR, a compact central CO source, small deconvolved size, and SFE enhanced by more than 1 dex.

quotes.quote: Arp 238-E (UGC8335-E), a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG: L_ IR 10^ 11 L_ ), has the second highest SFR among galaxies in S+S pairs (see Table tab:results ). Interestingly, the SFE in Arp 142 is 30 times lower than that in Arp 238-E Lisenfeld2019 .
Compared to the control sample, Arp 238-E shows an SFE enhancement of more than 1 dex whereas Arp 238-W has an enhancement of 0.7 dex.

For Arp 238, CO is detected only in two compact sources at the centers of the two galaxies. Both sources can be fitted well with elliptical 2-D Gaussian functions of FWHM = 4.4 3.8 with P.A. = 80 (Arp 238-E) and and FWHM = 4.4 3.3 with P.A. = 90 (Arp 238-W), respectively. Neglecting the small difference between the P.A. of each source and that of the beam ( P.A. = 88 , Table tab:overview_obs_data ), the intrinsic sizes of these sources after the deconvolution are 2.8 2.3 (Arp 238-E) and 2.8 1.3 (Arp 238-W), respectively. These are smaller than the NOEMA beam size (see Table tab:overview_obs_data ), and slightly larger than the sizes of radio continuum sources ( 1 - 2 ) detected in the high resolution VLA maps of Condon1991 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J13153506+6207286 as a strongly enhanced early-stage merger in Arp 238, likely on a low-inclination, low-velocity coplanar orbit with a tidal-torque-driven nuclear starburst.
