quotes.quote: The remaining seven galaxies with strong enhancement are in earlier-stage mergers with the two component galaxies clearly separated. Among them, J01183414-0013417, J13153506+6207286, and J16024257+4111501 (panel a, e of Figure fig:pseudo and panel of Figure fig:classical ) are likely in interaction systems with low-inclination orbits and low orbital velocity (all having v < 70 km s^ 1 where v is the measured relative radial velocity of the companion). Particularly J13153506+6207286 (panel e of Figure fig:pseudo ), which is in Arp 238 ( v = 6 km s^ 1 ), has been confirmed to have a coplanar orbit by a simulation of 2016MNRAS.459..720H . 2021ApJ...918...55X found a very compact molecular gas concentration in its nucleus which is fueling a strong active nuclear starburst. They argued that the nuclear starburst is triggered by a strong tidal torque which has been predicted by simulations for galaxies in low-speed coplanar interactions 1996ApJ...471..115B,2009ApJ...691.1168H .

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS03 as a complex of two merging galaxies whose ground-based data only support surface-brightness and B-K color profiles, both showing complex structure.

quotes.quote: LARS03 At the resolution of the ground-based data it is hard to separate the two merging galaxies in the LARS03 complex, which is why we selected not to complement the missing U and I bands for this galaxy with available HST imaging, which only covers the eastern part. Thus, only radial surface brightness and B-K color profiles are presented for this galaxy in Fig. fig:lars03 . Both indicate a complex structure.

gmw_00006508.name: LARS04
gmw_00006508.overview: LARS04 is a merger galaxy that features an offset star-forming region located away from its geometric center [arXiv:1803.07207]. Its central region contains a mixed stellar population, while its outskirts are consistent with a metal-poor population that is 1 to 2 Gyr old [arXiv:1803.07207].

sources.summary: LARS04 has an offset star-forming region, merger-like morphology, central colors matched by mixed populations, and outskirts consistent with a 1-2 Gyr metal-poor population.

quotes.quote: LARS04 A star-forming region, offset from the geometric center, is evident in the radial profiles, RGB image, and U-B spatial map. The three contours in panel D in Fig. fig:lars04 are at 21.9 , 24.9 , and 26.9 mag/arcsec ^2 , respectively, with the faintest contour tracing the shape of the brighter regions. This is clearly a merger. Similar to LARS02, the central region seems more consistent with a mixed populations SEM with a young mass fraction of 10^ 2 , while the outskirts are well-matched by a 1 2 Gyr-old, metal-poor, SSP model. The integrated color is consistent with a 1 Gyr-old population.

gmw_00006509.name: LARS06
gmw_00006509.overview: LARS06 is a low-luminosity galaxy [arXiv:1803.07207] with a disturbed morphology [arXiv:1803.07207] and a stellar tail extending to the south [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406]. It exhibits a low star-formation rate [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406] driven by offset star-forming regions [arXiv:1803.07207] and shows strong Lyman-alpha absorption [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406].

sources.summary: LARS 06/KISSR 2019 is the sample's weakest star-former and lowest-metallicity object, effectively unreddened, with one major star-forming condensation, a southern stellar tail, and strong surface absorption.

quotes.quote: LARS 06 KISSR 2019 is the most feebly star-forming galaxy in the sample by both FUV and \ measures, and also exhibits the lowest nebular metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H ) =8.08 ; Paper I). It is effectively unreddened according to ; the observation of which is of insufficient quality to trace the extinction over some of the outer of regions where we detect . In the UV and \ it reveals a morphology comprising one major star-forming condensation around which no hint of \ emission is seen. Most wavelengths also reveal a tail of stars extending to the south, although there is no evidence for this in \ either. Interestingly the tail is also rather weak in , which suggests that the stars of which it is made up have evolved to the stage where they remain UV-bright, but are not hot enough to produce strong nebular lines; this may be evidenced also by its age of 10 Myr.
The galaxy shows strong \ absorption and an almost complete absence of emission over the surface.

sources.summary: The paper says LARS 6 has the lowest star-formation rate in the sample, Ly absorption on small scales, moderate shearing, and low velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: LARS 6 ( KISSR 2019 ) With 1 M _ yr ^ 1 conversion of gas into stars LARS 6 shows the lowest star formation rate in the sample Hayes2014 . The main star forming knot is in the north with a tail of much smaller and less luminous knots extending to the south. In our seeing limited data cubes we can not disentangle these individual knots photometrically. Ly is seen in absorption, even on the smallest scales. Shearing is observed between the main component and the tail, although the amplitude is moderate ( v_ shear = 52 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: LARS06 is the faintest LARS galaxy, with offset star-forming regions, disturbed morphology, and a mass-to-light decrease linked to off-center star formation.

quotes.quote: LARS06 This is the faintest LARS galaxy, with several star-forming regions offset to the north and north-east from the geometric center. The three contours in panel D of Fig. fig:lars06 are at 23.9 , 24.3 , and 24.4 mag/arcsec ^2 , respectively. The faintest contour, albeit quite bright due to the low-luminosity nature of this galaxy, shows a very disturbed morphology, consistent with the presence of offset SF regions. We note that the center of integration (pink cross in panel D), is not on the star-forming region. This is reflected in the M/L ratio profile which decreases at 4 arcsec distance from the center.

gmw_00006510.name: UGC10028
gmw_00006510.overview: UGC10028 is a nearby field spiral galaxy [arXiv:1803.07207]. It is likely interacting with its neighbor LARS06, as evidenced by their shared velocity, confused neutral hydrogen gas, and an optical bridge connecting the two galaxies [arXiv:1803.07207].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC10028 as a nearby field spiral likely interacting with LARS06, with shared velocity, confused H I gas, and an optical bridge as evidence.

quotes.quote: As discussed in Pardy2014 , LARS06 may be interacting with the nearby field spiral UGC10028, located 40 arcsec to the south. These authors find that both galaxies are at the same velocity and the HI gas in LARS06 is significantly confused with the HI gas from its neighbor. Further supporting evidence for interaction between the two is the presence of an optical bridge at the 24.3 mag/arcsec ^2 level, visible in panel D.

gmw_00006511.name: LARS07
gmw_00006511.overview: LARS07 is an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1308.6578] that resembles a highly inclined disk with an extended central star-forming region [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1803.07207]. The system shows evidence of a recent merger [arXiv:1511.05406], featuring a visible tail, faint southwestern substructures [arXiv:1803.07207], and a large-scale, low-surface-brightness halo [arXiv:1308.6578].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 7 as an inclined disk-like system with H and Ly structures, red lobes suggesting a recent merger, and disturbed low-shear kinematics.

quotes.quote: LARS 7 ( IRAS F13136+2938 ) In the continuum LARS 7 resembles a highly inclined disk comparable to LARS 5. But, in H an almost azimuthally symmetric structure emerges that is highly distended with respect to the elongated continuum morphology. Moreover, two extended low surface brightness red lobes are found at the opposite ends of the inclined disk, reminiscent of a shell like structure. This is suggestive of a recent merger event e.g. Bettoni2011 . In Ly images LARS 7 appears even more extended, with the bright isophotes following the H shape and low-surface brightness isophotes resembling a more scaled up version of the apparent disk.

sources.summary: LARS07 has an extended central star-forming region, older surrounding population, faint southwestern substructures, a visible tail, and color models indicating mixed central populations.

quotes.quote: LARS07 The central region of this galaxy shows an extended central SF region, surrounded by an older population, with the radial color profiles, RGB image, and U-B map clearly reflecting such morphology. The two contours in panel D of Fig. fig:lars07 are at 24.8 and 26.4 mag/arcsec ^2 . The faintest contour reveals two fainter substructures to the south-west, engulfed by the contour, and curiously aligned with the visible tail to the north-east of the central galaxy complex. It is possible that one or both of these substructures passed through the galaxy and are responsible for the observed morphology. The SEMs diagrams suggest a possible mixed population with a young mass fraction of 10^ 2 towards the central regions, with the very center being more consistent with pure gas emission after correcting for dust attenuation, while the outskirts are consistent with an old ( 1.5 Gyr), metal-poor, single stellar population.

sources.summary: LARS 07 is identified with IRAS 1313+2938 and KISSR 242 and described as an edge-on disk with compact continuum emission, faint extraplanar gas, a low-surface-brightness halo, and polar filaments.

quotes.quote: LARS 07 Also known as IRAS 1313+2938 and KISSR 242, LARS 07 has already been studied in \ spectroscopically with HST/COS France2010,Wofford2013 . Morphologically LARS 07 resembles LARS 05 as an edge-on disk and very compact in \ and the stellar continuum, but there is evidence of some faint nebular gas extending away from the disk that is also somewhat extinguished as evidenced by / . It also shows a large-scale and low surface brightness \ halo although this halo is somewhat extended in the direction of the disk. Like LARS05 it also shows filamentary structure in \ that extends away from the plane of the galaxy in the polar directions, again reminiscent of a RT unstable outflow.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NSA 427201 as a TDE candidate, citing broad emission, non-stellar radio emission, unusually strong Fe X, and inconsistent prior broad-line/radio detections.

quotes.quote: NSA 427201: This galaxy was labelled as a star-forming galaxy with broad H by their object N reines2013 , and not detected in the radio by . We recover the broad component first seen by , and also find that the observed radio emission in both VLASS and FIRST cannot be explained by stellar processes. Additionally, the \ emission seen in this object is 19 times higher than that seen in SN2005ip. Given the lack of broad-line emission seen by and radio emission seen by reines2020 , we conclude this object is a TDE candidate.

gmw_00006512.name: LARS10
gmw_00006512.overview: LARS10 is a galaxy in an advanced merger state, featuring an irregular morphology with a UV-bright core and a southeastern tail [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406]. While the central regions exhibit Lyman-alpha absorption, a faint diffuse halo emerges at larger radii [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406].

sources.summary: LARS 10/Mrk 61 is described as a late-stage merger with a UV-bright core and southeast tail, central mixed emission and absorption, and a faint diffuse halo emerging by 5 kpc.

quotes.quote: LARS 10 Mrk 61 has the optical morphology of the later stage of a merger, showing a UV-bright core that extends over 5 kpc and an extended tail to the south-east. In the central few kpc, it shows a patchy, fine-scale mixture of \ emission and absorption, but absorbs in total within =2 kpc. In this region, \ EWs do not exceed around 50 , and / \ ratios are found in general not to suggest re-scattering of photons in this galaxy -- inside 2 r_ P20 , this object shows 0.3 , and it seems likely that \ photons can not escape the central regions. Some re-scattering of \ does occur, however, and at 5 kpc a faint diffuse halo structure begins to emerge; the net \ within this radius is sufficient to LARS 10 a LAE analog. The rather smooth, flat, curves-of-growth suggest that much of the \ has been captured by roughly 12 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper says LARS 10 resembles LARS 9 more moderately: a weak global emitter and strong COS absorber with near-static black-centered metal absorption that blocks Ly escape.

quotes.quote: Much of what was written of LARS 9 above also applies to LARS 10, although on a more moderate scale. This galaxy is also a weak emitter globally and a strong absorber in the COS aperture, although not as strong as LARS 9. The metal absorption lines, while black at line center and almost static, are not as broad and not quite as strong as those of LARS 9. Instead of a slow inflow, the line is centered at a weakly outflowing velocity, but the line is still black at systemic velocity, all of which efficiently blocks Ly escape. Like for LARS 9, the Si ii lines follow each other except for 1260, which is deeper in the blue wing than the rest.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 10 as an advanced-merger-like system with shell-like outskirts, central Ly absorption but a faint halo that could meet high-z LAE selection.

quotes.quote: LARS 10 ( Mrk 0061 ) Similar to LARS 1 this galaxy appears morphologically to be in an advanced merger state. It possesses a large UV bright core in the north-west and a spur of smaller, less luminous star forming regions towards the south-east. Several redder low-surface brightness structures are seen outside the main body of the galaxy, reminiscent of the appearance of shell galaxies e.g. Marino2009,Bettoni2011 . From the central star-forming parts Ly is only seen in absorption, while at larger radii a faint halo emerges. When considering solely the central parts the galaxy would remain undetected in high- z LAE surveys since EW_ Ly =8 .

sources.summary: LARS10 has blue extended central regions, radial colors requiring a two-population mixture, irregular faint isophotes suggesting a recent merger, and mixed-population colors.

quotes.quote: LARS10 An extended central region with relatively blue colors is observed in the radial profiles, the RGB image, and the U-B map. In both color-color panels, sections of the radial color profiles are only consistent with the two population mixture. The three contours in panel D of Fig. fig:lars10 are at 24.8 , 26.1 , and 26.3 mag/arcsec ^2 . The contour shows the irregular morphology of the faintest isophote we can reach, clearly suggesting a recent merger. At intermediate radii, a SSP model of solar metallicity and an age of 2.5 Gyr seems consistent with observations. The center, outskirts, and the integrated colors are more consistent with a mixed stellar population with a young mass fraction 10^ 3 .

gmw_00006513.name: LARS11
gmw_00006513.overview: LARS11 is a highly inclined edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406] that exhibits a clearly disturbed morphology [arXiv:1803.07207] and is interacting with a northern spiral companion [arXiv:1803.07207]. The galaxy features widespread star formation that is more active in its southeastern region [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406].

sources.summary: LARS11 has unreliable outer isophotal profiles, disturbed morphology, an interacting northern spiral companion, dust-sensitive color fits, and the second-highest nebular extinction in LARS.

quotes.quote: LARS11 Due to the highly elliptical projection of this galaxy, the isophotal surface brightness profile starts to suffer from missing pixels in the outer isophotes very quickly. We are only confident in the first five radial steps of this profile, and omit the rest. All optical data are HST images resampled to K resolution. The morphology is clearly disturbed, as seen in the radial profiles, RGB image, U-B map, and contour plots. The three contours in panel D of Fig. fig:lars11 are at 20.6 , 22.1 , and 24.2 mag/arcsec ^2 .

The spiral galaxy to the north of LARS11 is also clearly disturbed and it is difficult to confidently distinguish which debris belongs to LARS11. The two galaxies are interacting, as seen by CO tracing (Puschnig et al., in prep.).
LARS11 has a nebular extinction of 0.477 within the SDSS aperture and is thus the second most extinguished galaxy in LARS.

sources.summary: LARS 11 is described as a 40 kpc edge-on disk with widespread star formation, a more active southeast half, and faint rim emission along the southerly edge.

quotes.quote: LARS 11 This is a highly inclined edge-on disk that extends over 40 kpc in the ultraviolet. The whole disk appears to be lit with ongoing star formation, as shown by the \ images; the total SFR is on the order of 20--30 , and its stellar mass is the largest in the sample. Projected on the sky with a major axis that runs SE to NW, the SE half is somewhat more active in star formation. None of the brighter star-forming regions appear to emit their \ directly, and instead a faint rim of \ emission is seen running along the lower (more southerly) edge of the disk. This may hint at some projection effect where we see only \ from the near side.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 11 as an inclined edge-on disk with stronger southeastern star formation, a mild Ly halo, disk absorption, and rotation-like shear.

quotes.quote: LARS 11 ( SDSS J140347.22+062812.1 ) This galaxy appears as a highly inclined edge-on disk in the continuum and UV that is slightly thicker when seen in H . According to the H and UV emission, stronger star formation occurs in the south-eastern zone of the projected disk. In Ly a mildly extended halo above and below the plane is visible, with the isophotal contours approximately preserving the axis ratio of H . Within the disk Ly occurs exclusively in absorption. Running along the disk from the north-west to the south-east a strong gradient in the v_ LOS map is apparent.

gmw_00006514.name: LARS12
gmw_00006514.overview: LARS12 is a ultraviolet-bright merging system [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1308.6578] that features a compact morphology with debris to the northwest [arXiv:1803.07207]. The galaxy exhibits central absorption that transitions to emission at larger radii [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1308.6578], surrounded by a diffuse, low surface brightness halo [arXiv:1511.05406, arXiv:1308.6578].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts J0938+5428 with J0926+4427, finding broader systemic absorption, little residual flux, weaker fluorescent emission, stronger dust attenuation, and residual flux arising farther from the brightest stars.

quotes.quote: These two galaxies show distinct line profiles: in J0926+4427, the \ line exhibits a relatively narrow, blue-shifted absorption with significant residual flux ( v_ cen 300 km s ^ 1 and R_f 0.5). On the other hand, in J0938+5428, the line profile is broader, centered around the systemic velocity, and shows little residual flux ( R_f 0.1). Additionally, the EW of the fluorescent emission in J0926+4427 is typically two times larger than in J0938+5428.

This region is more significantly attenuated by dust in the J0938 direction than in the J0926 direction. In both cases, the spectra extracted from the brightest continuum region exhibit fully-covered absorption lines (no residual flux) and no fluorescent emission, indicating that the brightest stars are facing optically thick gas in these two directions.

We observe different spatial origins for the residual flux in the J0926 and J0938 directions. For J0926, it emerges from a relatively small and compact region surrounding the brightest stellar cluster. For the J0938 direction, it arises from an extended region further away from the brightest stars. In both directions, the 50 contours enclose pixels where we do not detect any absorption features, suggesting that some fraction of the residual flux comes from gas-depleted regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 12 as a UV-bright merger with modest Ly equivalent width, central Ly absorption, outer Ly emission, a faint halo, and complex kinematics.

quotes.quote: LARS 12 ( LEDA 27453 ) Because of its modest EW _ Ly =13 this UV bright merger would not be selected as a LAE at high- z . At its brightest knot in UV and H the galaxy shows Ly only in absorption. Only at larger radii, where a number of fainter star forming regions can be appreciated, Ly appears in emission. Moreover, LARS 12 is embedded in a faint, low surface brightness Ly halo. The non-regular H velocity field indicates complex kinematics.

sources.summary: LARS12 has flat radial colors, star-formation-dominated U-B structure, compact K-band morphology with northwest debris, and outskirts consistent with a 0.5-1 Gyr population.

quotes.quote: LARS12 All optical data are HST images resampled to K resolution. The radial color profiles are relatively flat, although B-K shows a clear trend of increasing domination of an older population. The U-B map shows that the SF region seemingly dominates most of the galaxy visible in these data. The underlying gray scale image in panel D of Fig. fig:lars12 is the K band because it is deeper than the HST optical data. The two contours are at 21.6 and 24.4 mag/arcsec ^2 . The faintest contour in K shows a fairly compact morphology, with a hint of debris to the north-west. The outskirts of the galaxy (last radial point) are consistent with a metal-poor SSP in the range 0.5 1 Gyr, while the integrated color mimics a 300 Myr-old population. The central region tends toward a mixed stellar population with a young mass fraction >10^ 2 .

sources.summary: LARS 12/SBS 0934+547 is described as a UV-bright merging system where central absorption changes to emission beyond 2 kpc and a diffuse halo extends over the same area.

quotes.quote: LARS 12 SBS 0934+547 is a UV-bright merging system. In front of the brightest star-forming regions it shows strong \ absorption. However this changes to emission at >2 kpc, and the aperture-integrated \ measurements all increase rapidly out to 10 kpc, after which it roughly converges; a diffuse \ halo can be seen in the images extending over roughly this area. While very luminous in net , the EW never exceeds about 15 \ (5 \ at 2 r_ P20 ) and LARS 12 would not be selected as a LAE if projected to the high- z universe.

gmw_00006515.name: LARS13
gmw_00006515.overview: LARS13 is a compact Lyman-break analog and starburst system with a highly irregular morphology [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406]. It is an interacting merger that displays complex velocity gradients and patchy, weak Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406].

sources.summary: LARS 13/IRAS 0147+1254 is described as a Lyman-break analog and merger with variable dust attenuation, patchy weak emission, no substantial halo, and small EW and escape fraction.

quotes.quote: LARS 13 IRAS 0147+1254 is also a Lyman-break analog system Hoopes2007 , and is also clearly a merging system. It shows variable dust attenuations in the nebular phase which is strongly variable and scattered across the face of the object. \ emission is also rather patchy and varies between absorption and weak emission. Unlike LARS 12, however, this emission never becomes particularly strong and LARS 13 has no substantial halo emission component. Overall, LARS 13 emits more \ than it absorbs, but has both small EW (6 ), and \ (1 per cent) in the 2 r_ P20 aperture.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 13 as an interacting starburst with low Ly escape, complex velocity gradients, and overlapping ionized-gas components from not-yet-coalesced progenitors.

quotes.quote: LARS 13 ( IRAS 01477+1254 ) Based on its highly irregular morphology this starburst system is clearly interacting. As LARS 13 shows a narrow Ly equivalent width ( EW _ Ly = 6 ), it would not be selected as a LAE at high redshift in conventional narrow-band surveys. Overall only 1 of Ly photons are escaping and the resulting Ly luminosity is L_ Ly =7 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . The complex v_ LOS map of this galaxy is characterised by a gradient from blue- to redshifts along the east-western axis for the northern component, as well as an gradient from blue-to-redshifts from south to north for the eastern component. In the north-western zone where the longitudinally aligned structure overlaps with the latitudinally aligned one, our single component Gaussian is not an optimal representation of the observed H line profiles.

sources.summary: LARS13 is compact with nearly flat radial colors and mass-to-light ratio, but its faint contour shows disturbed, complex morphology consistent with a merger.

quotes.quote: LARS13 All optical data are HST images resampled to K resolution. Similar to LARS12, this galaxy shows a compact morphology with a seemingly uniform single or well-mixed stellar population. Excluding the very center of the galaxy, the radial color profiles remain flat, indicating no change in population. This is also reflected in the flat M/L ratio profile. Panel D of Fig. fig:lars13 shows a gray scale K band image, and the two contours are at 21.4 and 24.1 mag/arcsec ^2 . The faintest contour indicates a clearly disturbed, complex morphology, indicating a merger event. The radial color profile data points are clustered in both SEM diagrams, supporting the lack of significant population change with radius.

sources.summary: J0150 is an LBA with extended or clumpy low-surface-brightness emission, well-modeled recombination lines, and X-ray plus radio detections while retaining ionization properties typical of low-redshift star-forming galaxies.

quotes.quote: We find evidence for extended or clumpy low-surface brightness emission that is missing in the shallower observations (e.g., in J0150 and J2104).
For J0150, J2104, and J2116b, all available recombination lines are well-described by the model.

Overall, there is considerable diversity in the correlation between compact radio emission, X-ray emission, excitation properties, and presence of a DCO in LBAs: J0150 and J1434 are detected in X-rays (and J0150 in radio) but have ionization properties otherwise typical of low- z star-forming galaxies, J0213 has a DCO but does not show signs of enhanced excitation, and J2104 has no radio emission but hosts a DCO and is characterized by anomalously high He 1 / H 1 ratio.

gmw_00006516.name: SDSS J095909.51+460014.3
gmw_00006516.overview: SDSS J095909.51+460014.3 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.399 [arXiv:2212.04036]. It is identified as a gamma-ray emitting active galaxy that exhibits violent infrared variability and hosts a relativistic jet [arXiv:2212.04036].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J095909.51+460014.3 as a gamma-ray NLS1, linking infrared flares and Fermi localization to J0959+4600 and modeling its jet SED.

quotes.quote: We report on the identification of a new ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy ( NLS1), SDSS J095909.51+460014.3 (hereinafter J0959+4600, z = 0.399), by establishing its association with a ray source 4FGL 0959.6+4606, although its low-energy counterpart was suggested to be a radio galaxy 2MASX J09591976+4603515 (hereinafter J0959+4603). WISE long-term light curves of these two sources reveal diverse infrared variability patterns. Violent infrared variations of J0959+4600 with an amplitude up to one order of magnitude has been detected, while variability is mild for the other one.

A specific analysis of 15-month Fermi-LAT data, aiming at the high ray flux state, yields a significant source (TS =41). The corresponding localization analysis suggests that only J0959+4600 falls into the uncertainty area, further supporting the updated association relationship. A broadband spectral energy distribution of J0959+4600 has been drawn and well described by the classic single-zone homogeneous leptonic jet model. The jet properties of J0959+4600 are investigated and found to be comparable with other NLS1s.

gmw_00006517.name: 2MASX J09591976+4603515
gmw_00006517.overview: 2MASX J09591976+4603515 is an edge-on galaxy [arXiv:2212.04036] that exhibits moderate radio loudness [arXiv:2212.04036]. It displays mild infrared variability [arXiv:2212.04036] and maintains a quiescent state in its gamma-ray flux [arXiv:2212.04036].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates 2MASX J09591976+4603515 as the previously suggested radio-galaxy counterpart, finding mild infrared variability, quiescent gamma-bright behavior, edge-on morphology, and moderate radio loudness.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J09591976+4603515 (hereinafter J0959+4603), an edge-on galaxy ( z = 0.148, 2021AJ....162..177P ), is listed as its low-energy counterpart. J0959+4603 is known as an infrared source 2006AJ....131.1163S . Its WISE 2010AJ....140.1868W colors are similar with those of blazars and hence it is believed as a potential ray emitter 2012ApJ...748...68D . Adopting FIRST 1.4 GHz 2015ApJ...801...26H and SDSS DR16 2020ApJS..249....3A g band measurements, its radio loudness R is yielded as 20.

WISE long-term light curves of these two sources reveal diverse infrared variability patterns. Violent infrared variations of J0959+4600 with an amplitude up to one order of magnitude has been detected, while variability is mild for the other one. More importantly, the peaking time of the infrared flare of J0959+4603 is coincident with the time of brightening of 4FGL 0959.6+4606. At the same time, the flux level of J0959+4603 maintains in the quiescent state.

gmw_00006518.name: Ton 1388
gmw_00006518.overview: Ton 1388 is a quasar [arXiv:2006.02665] with a high accretion rate and a strong accretion disc [arXiv:2006.02665]. Radio observations of the source reveal a triple, core-jet-like structure on VLA scales [arXiv:2210.16764].

sources.summary: The disc-corona modeling identifies Ton 1388 as one of two highest-accretion-rate, quasar-luminosity objects whose SED favors a strong disc and comparatively weaker corona.

quotes.quote: In particular, m is smaller than 0.3 in 18 out of the 20 objects. In only two sources relatively larger m are yielded, namely Ton 1388 ( 0.57 ) and RX J1355.2+5612 ( 0.4 ). Therefore, the distribution of m from the spectral fitting supports the basic assumption of our model, that the gravitational energy is efficiently released via the standard Shakura--Sunyaev disc ( 0.1 ).

For the other two objects, which have the highest accretion rates among the sample, Ton 1388 ( m 0.57 and L_ bol 2.5 10^ 46   erg   s^ 1 ) and RX J1355.2+5612 ( m 0.4 and L_ bol 1.6 10^ 45   erg   s^ 1 ), about half of the accreted energy ( f 0.5 ) is released in the disc. The inner regions of their discs (up to radii R < \ 100 R_ S ) are completely dominated by radiation pressure, although the reduced heating and hence the disc radiation can mitigate somewhat the photon-trapping effect therein. Compared to other AGNs in the sample, a strong disc and relatively weak corona scenario is favoured for them.

sources.summary: PG 1116+215 has an associated absorber in the doublet and Ly absorption, assessed as unlikely to arise from quasar-related gas and confirmed by partial-coverage analysis.

quotes.quote: PG 1116+215 ( =0.1655) -- This associated absorption-line system is detected in the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet and Ly . The system was first reported by tls98 in Ly \ absorption, and then by see their 5.16,7.1 sembach04b in absorption. Both papers claim, based on velocity alignments with galaxies in the field of the quasar tls98 , and the lack of evidence for diluted troughs in the doublet, that the system is unlikely to arise from gas related to the quasar. We confirm that assessment with our partial coverage analysis. As also pointed out by both papers, this system appears to be a satellite of a stronger, lower-ionization Ly \ system at =0.1661 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1116+215 as having VLA-scale triple, core-jet-like radio structure, but only upper-limit or non-detections in VLBA observations.

quotes.quote: PG 1116+215 The VLA-D image of PG 1116+215 showed a triple structure with a faint central component and two bright components distributed on each side extending along north-south; the VLA-A image only detected the brightest southern component 1994AJ....108.1163K . The overall structure looks like a core-jet structure, with the core lying at the southernmost end of the jet. PG 1116+215 (Ton 1388) was detected with an upper limit flux density less than 0.3 mJy at 8.4-GHz by VLBA 1998MNRAS.299..165B . It is not detected in our 5-GHz VLBA image either.

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1116+215 as a quasar sightline from prior STIS BLA work, summarizing seven candidates, tentative detections, S/N, and inferred line density.

quotes.quote: So far, two sight lines observed with STIS towards the quasars PG 1259+593 ( z_ em =0.478 ) and PG 1116+215 ( z_ em =0.176 ) have been carefully inspected for the presence of BLAs, and a number of candidates have been identified (Richter et al. 2004; Sembach et al. 2004).

Toward PG 1116+215, Sembach et al. (2004) find seven BLA candidates along a total, unblocked redshift path of z=0.133 . We regard four of these absorbers as tentative detections (see Table 2, last column). With an average S/N of 17 , the study implies dN_ BL /dz 23 for log (N/b) 11.2 for the reliably detected systems ( 53 including the uncertain cases).

gmw_00006519.name: RX J1355.2+5612
gmw_00006519.overview: RX J1355.2+5612 is an active galactic nucleus with a typical quasar luminosity [arXiv:2006.02665]. It exhibits a high accretion rate and a red optical/UV spectrum, with only about half of its accretion energy transported to the corona [arXiv:2006.02665].

sources.summary: The model places RX J1355.2+5612 among high-accretion-rate, quasar-luminosity AGN with red optical/UV spectra and only about half of accretion energy transported to the corona.

quotes.quote: In only two sources relatively larger m are yielded, namely Ton 1388 ( 0.57 ) and RX J1355.2+5612 ( 0.4 ). Therefore, the distribution of m from the spectral fitting supports the basic assumption of our model, that the gravitational energy is efficiently released via the standard Shakura--Sunyaev disc ( 0.1 ).

We now consider the spectral deviations in the optical/UV band in some of the objects, e.g. RX J1209.8+3217, RX J1355.2+5612, and RX J1702.5+3247. In fact, their observed spectra are redder than those of the other sample objects and than the prediction of the standard Shakura--Sunyaev disc 2019MNRAS.487.3884C . This is most likely not caused by dust reddening since the sample objects were selected to be essentially free from dust extinction, as indicated by their Balmer decrement in the optical spectra 2019MNRAS.487.3884C .

Specifically, the two objects with the highest accretion rates, Ton 1388 m 0.57 , L_ bol 2.5 10^ 46   erg   s^ 1 ; 2017A&A...597A..79P and RX J1355.2+5612 ( m 0.4 , L_ bol 1.6 10^ 45   erg   s^ 1 ), are of typical quasar luminosities. Their observed SEDs require only about half of the accretion energy transported into the corona ( f 0.5 ), mainly due to a much higher accretion rate than the others ( m 0.57 and 0.4 , respectively), and their observed X-ray luminosities are much lower than the optical/UV luminosities.

gmw_00006520.name: SDSS J1634+2049
gmw_00006520.overview: SDSS J1634+2049 is an infrared-luminous quasar and active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1807.06598, arXiv:1603.02397] that exhibits violent, obscured star formation [arXiv:1603.02397]. The galaxy features a 30 kpc off-centered collisional ring [arXiv:1603.02397] likely formed through a recent head-on collision with its companion dwarf galaxy, C1 [arXiv:1807.06598, arXiv:1603.02397].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1634+2049 as an AGN-host collisional ring galaxy whose arc likely formed through interaction with companion C1, motivating simulations of ring formation.

quotes.quote: Recently, a new ring galaxy has been studied by liu2016sdss . This is a host galaxy of an active galactic nucleus (AGN), SDSS J163459.82+204936.0. A ring-like structure (i.e., an arc structure surrounding the central nucleus) has been found at a projected distance of around 16 to the galactic center. In the extended direction of the galactic center to the arc, there are two dwarf galaxies, namely C1 and C2, at a projected galactocentric distance of around 35.7 and 44.0 , respectively.

The redshifts of the two dwarf galaxies are close to that of the host galaxy of SDSS J1634+2049, thus they are considered as companion galaxies of the AGN-host galaxy. The arc structure is proposed to be the dense region of a knotty ring around the AGN. The host galaxy of SDSS J1634+2049 seems to be a CRG that formed in the collision with C1 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J163459.82 + 204936.0 as a local infrared-luminous quasar with an obscured star-forming host, AGN outflows, and a 30 kpc collision-formed ring.

quotes.quote: SDSS J163459.82 + 204936.0 is a local ( z = 0.1293 ) infrared-luminous quasar with L_ IR = 10^ 11.91 . We present a detailed multiwavelength study of both the host galaxy and the nucleus. The host galaxy, appearing as an early-type galaxy in the optical images and spectra, demonstrates violent, obscured star formation activities with SFR 140 yr^ 1 , estimated from either the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission or IR luminosity.

Taking advantages of both the absorption lines and outflowing emission lines, we find that the outflow gas is located at a distance of 48 - 65 pc from the nucleus, and that the kinetic luminosity of the outflow is 10^ 44 - 10^ 46 . \ has a off-centered galactic ring on the scale of 30 kpc that is proved to be formed by a recent head-on collision by a nearby galaxy for which we spectroscopically measure the redshift. Thus, this quasar is a valuable object in the transitional phase emerging out of dust enshrouding as depicted by the co-evolution scenario invoking galaxy merger (or violent interaction) and quasar feedback.

gmw_00006521.name: J0904 + 1435
gmw_00006521.overview: J0904 + 1435 is a complex merger consisting of three interacting galaxies [arXiv:1908.02291]. Its resolved radio emission does not coincide with any of the optical nuclei, which may explain the lack of detected neutral hydrogen absorption along its sightline [arXiv:1908.02291].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0904 + 1435 as the only new-sample non-detection, a complex three-galaxy merger whose radio sightline may miss nuclear neutral gas.

quotes.quote: The only non-detection in the new sample presented here is towards the complex merger J0904 + 1435 consisting of three galaxies (see Fig. fig:overlay ). The radio emission is resolved in the GMRT map, with the strongest continuum peak having 14 of the total flux and not coinciding with any optical nuclei. Lack of absorption in this case could be related to the radio sightline not probing the nuclear region and absence of \ gas in the region probed between the merging galaxies.

gmw_00006522.name: SDSS J105621.45+313822.1
gmw_00006522.overview: SDSS J105621.45+313822.1 is a low-mass, low-metallicity galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.161 [arXiv:2004.11295]. It hosts a broad-line active galactic nucleus that is heavily obscured and detected as a weak X-ray source [arXiv:2004.11295].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J105621.45+313822.1 as a low-metallicity broad-line AGN with low mass, a weak X-ray source, and evidence for heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: Here we present a multi-wavelength study of a low metallicity, broad line AGN, SDSS J105621.45+313822.1 (hereafter J1056+3138), a galaxy which is identified as a QSO broad line object by SDSS DR12, and in past catalogs has been classified as a broad line QSO dabrusco2009,richards2009,souchay2012,souchay2015,richards2015 or broad line AGN toba2014,rakshit2017 . Based on the MPA/JHU catalog, the galaxy has a redshift of z=0.161 and a stellar mass of 10^ 9.89 M_ , roughly 2.5 the mass of the Large Magellanic Cloud.

We detected an X-ray point source coincident with the SDSS optical source with an apparent luminosity, uncorrected for intrinsic absorption, of L_ X, 2-10 keV = (2.3 0.9) 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , with six counts combined in both hard and soft bands. Due to the low number of counts, we explored the binomial no-source probability of the detection, P_ B , which is proportional to the probability that the measured counts are due to spurious background activity (see weisskopf2007,lansbury2014 for the mathematical expression for this statistic). Adopting the requirement that real sources (not due to background activity) satisfy a threshold of P_ B <0.002 satyapal2017 , and noting that the X-ray source yields a no-source probability of (P_ B ) 6.4 , we conclude that the X-ray source is unlikely to be due to spurious background activity.

Further, prestwich2013 also show that ULXs are more common in low metallicity systems, adding further to the ambiguity of the origin of the X-ray emission in J1056+3138 and making an AGN identification by this observed X-ray luminosity alone tentative. The observed luminosity is two orders of magnitude lower than what is expected from the L_X - L_ 12 m relation ( 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) secrest2015 . Taking the relation between [ Si 6 ] and L_X from lamperti2017 , an estimate of L_X 10^ 43 is calculated, also two orders of magnitude above the detected luminosity.

gmw_00006523.name: VLBI J142923.6466
gmw_00006523.overview: VLBI J142923.6466 is a radio galaxy with an estimated redshift of approximately 0.2 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. Its active nucleus is hosted within an elliptical optical galaxy that exhibits compact radio emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VLBI J142923.6466 with an elliptical optical host, measures compact VLBI emission, and argues its high brightness temperature makes it probably a radio galaxy at estimated redshift about 0.2.

quotes.quote: VLBI J142923.6466 locates the active nucleus within the optical host, NDWFS J142923.6 + 352851, an elliptical galaxy with total I 16.3^ m and K 13.9^ m jan04 . Figure 2 was analysed with AIPS task JMFIT to yield the position, peak flux density S_ P (VLBI) , and integrated flux density S_ I (VLBI) entered in Table 2. Since S_ I (VLBI) 0.46 mJy, less than ten percent of the integrated WSRT flux density has been recovered. A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 4.5 10^ 6 K, suggesting this WSRT source is probably a radio galaxy. Assuming the strong correlation between K-band magnitude and the redshift of luminous radio galaxies also applies to fainter, less luminous systems (the K-z relation e.g. jar01 ), we estimate z 0.2 for this source.

gmw_00006524.name: VLBI J142934.7033
gmw_00006524.overview: VLBI J142934.7033 is a radio galaxy resolved as a compact double source [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. It is misaligned with its optical host galaxy, NDWFS J142934.7+352859, suggesting that its emission is powered by a black hole rather than star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper resolves VLBI J142934.7033 as a compact double, links it to an optical host galaxy, and argues radio-optical misalignment indicates black-hole-powered emission at estimated redshift about 0.6.

quotes.quote: Figure 5 shows that VLBI J142934.7033 is resolved into a double separated by about 5 mas. The integrated flux density is S_ I (VLBI) 0.46 mJy (Fig. 3, Table 2), so most of the integrated WSRT flux density has been recovered. A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 2.8 10^ 6 K. Moreover, this north-south double is misaligned with its east-west host galaxy, NDWFS J142934.7 + 352859, which has a total I 19.6^ m and K 16.3^ m jan04 . This radio-optical misalignment strongly suggest that this sub-mJy WSRT source is energised by a black hole rather than by star formation.

gmw_00006525.name: NGC 5646
gmw_00006525.overview: NGC 5646 is a disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.03 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. Its radio emission follows the optical isophotes and is driven by star formation rather than an active nucleus, as confirmed by a lack of VLBI detection in its central regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a WSRT target with disk galaxy NGC 5646, reports no VLBI detection across central regions, and concludes the radio emission is produced by star formation.

quotes.quote: One of the brightest potential targets in the 0-2 survey region, this WSRT source is identified with the disk galaxy NGC 5646 at z 0.03 dev02 . This target is not detected with VLBI at resolutions of 10-100 mas ( 6-60 pc). Figure 6 presents the full resolution image of the central NGC 5646 target region. Indeed, for this source, several regions each spanning 6 ( 3.6 kpc) on a side, were placed across the extended WSRT source, in case luminous SNe or SNR might be detectable. This VLBI non-detection, together with the fact that the WSRT emission follows the optical isophotes mor02 and obeys the FIR-radio correlation , strongly supports a star formation origin for this mJy WSRT source.

gmw_00006526.name: VLBI J142910.2224
gmw_00006526.overview: VLBI J142910.2224 is a quasar identified with the optical counterpart NDWFS J142910.2+352946 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. Its milli-Jansky emission is dominantly energized by a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper treats VLBI J142910.2224 as an in-beam calibrator quasar, recovers its WSRT flux density, identifies its optical counterpart, and attributes its mJy emission to a supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: VLBI J142910.2224 has previously been detected by wro04 , who validated its suitability as an in-beam calibrator for this study. The naturally weighted image of the source, obtained via the initial data analysis of one of the three VLBI observations, recovers essentially all of the integrated WSRT flux density (Fig. 7, Table 3). Identified as a quasar of unknown redshift, VLBI J142910.2224 is identified with NDWFS J142910.2 + 352946 ( I 18.4^ m ) jan04 . A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 1.5 10^ 9 K. This mJy WSRT source must be dominantly energised by a super-massive black hole.

gmw_00006527.name: VLBI J143002.5631
gmw_00006527.overview: VLBI J143002.5631 is an unresolved radio source associated with the host galaxy NDWFS J143002.5 + 353035 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. The host galaxy has a brightness of I = 16.5 mag and may possess a blue companion galaxy to its southwest [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper finds VLBI J143002.5631 unresolved, associates it with host galaxy NDWFS J143002.5 + 353035, and uses brightness temperature and K-z reasoning to suggest a radio-galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: VLBI J143002.5631 is not resolved and has an integrated flux density S_ I (VLBI) 0.51 mJy (Fig. 8, Table 2), recovering all of the integrated WSRT flux density. The host galaxy NDWFS J143002.5 + 353035 has a total I 16.5^ m and K 17.0^ m , and may have a blue companion to the south-west jan04 . A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 1.7 10^ 5 K. If this sub-mJy WSRT source is a radio galaxy, then the K-z relation suggests z 0.8 , for which 10 mas corresponds to 75 pc.

gmw_00006528.name: VLBI J142835.5389
gmw_00006528.overview: VLBI J142835.5389 is resolved as a double radio source with a combined flux density of 2.6 mJy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. The source is likely a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z > 1, associated with a faint host galaxy that lacks a K-band detection [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper resolves VLBI J142835.5389 as a double source with a faint host, no K-band detection, and a brightness-temperature lower limit suggesting it is probably a radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: VLBI J142835.5389 is resolved into a double with a combined flux density of 2.6 mJy (Fig. 9, Table 6). More than 30 of the the integrated WSRT flux density has been recovered. The host galaxy NDWFS J142835.5 + 353154 has a total I 22.8^ m jan04 . The source is not detected in K-band ( K > 18.5^ m for a 50 completeness limit) suggesting it may be located at z > 1 . A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 1.5 10^ 6 K suggesting that this mJy WSRT source is probably a radio galaxy.

gmw_00006529.name: VLBI J142835.9570
gmw_00006529.overview: VLBI J142835.9570 is an unresolved radio source with a brightness temperature of at least 300,000 K [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. If the source is a radio galaxy, its optical and K-band magnitudes suggest a redshift greater than one [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBI J142835.9570 as an unresolved source, gives its host-galaxy optical and K-band magnitudes, and suggests that a radio-galaxy interpretation implies redshift above one.

quotes.quote: VLBI J142835.9570 is not resolved and has an integrated flux density S_ I (VLBI) 0.46 mJy (Fig. 10, Table 6) that recovers at least 40 of the integrated WSRT flux density. A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 3 10^ 5 K. The host galaxy NDWFS J142835.9 + 352537 has a total I 21.2^ m and K 18.3^ m jan04 . If this sub-mJy WSRT source is a radio galaxy, then the K-z relation suggests z > 1 .

gmw_00006530.name: VLBI J142842.5476
gmw_00006530.overview: VLBI J142842.5476 is a radio source resolved into a double component structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. This double structure is misaligned with its elongated host galaxy, NDWFS J142842.5+354327, suggesting the source is powered by a black hole rather than star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper resolves VLBI J142842.5476 into a double, connects it to an elongated host galaxy, and argues radio-optical misalignment indicates black-hole rather than star-formation power.

quotes.quote: Figure 11 shows that VLBI J142842.5476 is resolved into a double. With S_ I (VLBI) 5.4 mJy (Fig. 11, Table 7), at least half of the integrated WSRT flux density has been recovered. A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 5 10^ 5 K. Moreover, this double is misaligned with its elongated host galaxy, NDWFS J142842.5 + 354327, which has a total I 19.9^ m and K 16.4^ m jan04 . This radio-optical misalignment strongly suggests that this mJy WSRT source is energised by a black hole rather than by star formation.

gmw_00006531.name: VLBI J142906.6095
gmw_00006531.overview: VLBI J142906.6095 is a strong, compact radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. It features a high brightness temperature component and a very faint optical counterpart, suggesting the galaxy may be located at a redshift greater than one [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI J142906.6095 as a strong compact radio source with recovered VLBI flux, high brightness temperature, a very faint optical counterpart, and possible redshift above one.

quotes.quote: Figure 12 presents VLBI J142906.6095. With S_ I (VLBI) 13 mJy (Fig. 12, Table 7), much of the integrated WSRT flux density is recovered. A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 2.7 10^ 6 K. The optical counterpart must be fainter than I 25.6^ m bro03,jan04 , quite unusual for such a strong radio source. There is no detection at K-band ( K > 18.5^ m ) suggesting the source may be located at z > 1 .

gmw_00006532.name: VLBI J142941.6843
gmw_00006532.overview: VLBI J142941.6843 is a radio source resolved into a double structure with a compact component brightness temperature of at least 1.3 × 10^5 K [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176]. The source has a candidate host galaxy, NDWFS J142941.8+351257, and its lack of K-band detection suggests it may be located at a redshift greater than one [arXiv:astro-ph/0410176].

sources.summary: The paper resolves VLBI J142941.6843 into a double, measures its compact brightness temperature, identifies a candidate host galaxy, and notes missing K-band emission suggesting redshift above one.

quotes.quote: Figure 13 shows that VLBI J142941.6843 is resolved into a double. With S_ I (VLBI) 0.87 mJy (Fig. 13, Table 7). A lower limit for the brightness temperature of the VLBI component of the source is 1.3 10^ 5 K. A candidate host galaxy, NDWFS J142941.8 + 351257, has a total I 23.3^ m jan04 . There is no detection at K-band ( K > 18.5^ m ) suggesting the source may be located at z > 1 .

gmw_00006533.name: SDSS J0159+0105
gmw_00006533.overview: SDSS J0159+0105 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1512.08730] at a redshift of 0.217 [arXiv:1512.08730] that has also been classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1303.3585]. It exhibits periodic optical variability [arXiv:1512.08730, arXiv:1801.06189] and broad H-alpha profile changes [arXiv:1512.08730], which may indicate a supermassive black hole binary system [arXiv:1512.08730].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J0159+0105 as a radio-quiet quasar with periodic optical variability, broad-line changes, and an unusual SED consistent with a centi-parsec supermassive black-hole binary.

quotes.quote: We report a candidate centi-parsec supermassive black hole binary (SMBHB) in the radio-quiet quasar SDSS J0159+0105 at z=0.217 . With a modified lomb-scargle code ( GLSdeDRW ) and the auto-correlation analysis (ACF), we detect two significant (at P>99 ) periodic signals at 741 day and 1500 day from the 8.1-year Catalina V-band light curve of this quasar. The period ratio, which is close to 1:2, is typical of a black-hole binary system with a mass ratio of 0.05 <q< 0.8 according to recent numerical simulations. SDSS J0159+0105 has two SDSS spectroscopic observations separated by 10 years. There is a significant change in the broad H profile between the two epochs, which can be explained by a single broad-line region (BLR) around the binary system illuminated by the aforementioned mini-disks, or a stream of gas flowing from the circumbinary disk to one of the SMBHs.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J015910.05+010514.5 as a Seyfert-galaxy contaminant in its gamma-ray association procedure, notes its ROSAT X-ray counterpart, and contrasts it with blazar-like candidates.

quotes.quote: One is a Seyfert galaxy: SDSS J015910.05+010514.5 is a contaminant of our association procedure (although our method suggests a better candidate, for 2FGLJ0158.4+0107). The remaining one, NVSS J161543+471126 shows the optical spectrum similar to that of an X-ray Bright, Optically Normal Galaxy XBONG comastri02 . The source SDSS J015836.23+010632.0, another candidate counterpart of 2FGLJ0158.4+0107 is described as a quasar at redshift 0.723 in Schneider et al. (2007) and Hu et al. (2008). In addition to these 4 sources, spectroscopic information is also available for WISE J230010.16-360159.9 a possible low-energy counterpart of 2FGLJ2300.0-3553, classified as quasar according to Jones et al. (2009). A quasar-like spectrum is then available for SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 candidate counterpart of 2FGLJ1614.8+4703.

In the UGS sample without ray analysis flags, only 3 objects have X-ray counterparts in the ROSAT all-sky catalog: the Seyfert 1 galaxy SDSS J015910.05+010514.5, the quasar SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 (both described in Section sec:optical ) and WISE J164619.95+435631.0 associated with 2FGLJ1647.0+4351. In addition, SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 is also detected in the \ source catalog: CXO J161434.7+470419 as occurs NVSS J161543+471126, alias CXO J161541.2+471111 evans10 .

Notably, all the sources for which an optical spectrum was available in literature clearly show blazar-like features, being either featureless or having broad emission lines typical of quasars, the only exception being SDSS J015910.05+010514.5, one of the counterparts associated with 2FGLJ0158.4+0107.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J0159+0105 as a quasar candidate with two periodic variability components; G15 saw a 740-day component but did not classify it as periodic.

quotes.quote: Another recent candidate, quasar SDSS J0159+0105, was identified to have two periodic components in its variability with a frequency ratio 1:2 from a smaller sample of 350 low-redshift quasars in CRTS Zheng2016 . This quasar was also included in the sample analyzed in G15, but was not identified as periodic. The shortest periodic component of 740 d was identified with both the wavelet and the autocorrelation function analysis in G15, but did not have enough power to be classified as periodic, potentially due to the second periodic component (M. Graham; private communication). Identifying periodic variability in quasars is extremely challenging, because quasars show stochastic variability.

gmw_00006534.name: R.A.=10:36:53.7 and Dec= 58:35:47
aliases_external: candidate background galaxy at R.A.=10:36:53.7 Dec=58:35:47
gmw_00006534.overview: R.A.=10:36:53.7 and Dec= 58:35:47 is a candidate background galaxy with a morphology reminiscent of an edge-on spiral [arXiv:2210.01101]. Its elongated emission is asymmetrical, suggesting an off-center bulge, and its disk-like emission may be bisected by a dust lane [arXiv:2210.01101].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a coordinate-selected candidate background galaxy in the NGC 3324 JWST field, describing elongated edge-on spiral-like morphology, asymmetric emission, and possible F200W dust-lane structure.

quotes.quote: An elongated object with coordinates R.A.=10:36:53.7 and Dec= 58:35:47 is visible in multiple filters (F187N, F200W, F335M, F444W, F470N, F1280W, and F1800W). The morphology is reminiscent of an edge-on spiral galaxy (see Figure fig:edge-on a) but the elongated emission is asymmetrical suggesting an off-center bulge. Structure in the F200W image hints at a dust lane bisecting the disk-like emission but this feature is not seen at any other wavelength. The nearest object in the Simbad database wenger2000 is >30 away from the coordinates of this object.

gmw_00006535.name: R.A.=10:37:07.2 and Dec= 58:35:36
gmw_00006535.overview: The object at R.A.=10:37:07.2 and Dec= 58:35:36 is a candidate galaxy [arXiv:2210.01101]. Although it resembles a bright point source near a diffraction spike, a disk-like structure is visible in F200W-F090W imaging [arXiv:2210.01101].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this coordinate-selected object as a candidate galaxy, noting point-source-like appearance near a diffraction spike, disk-like F200W-F090W structure, and offset from cataloged sources.

quotes.quote: A third candidate galaxy is seen at R.A.=10:37:07.2 and Dec= 58:35:36 (see Figure fig:edge-on ). At first glance, the object looks like a bright point source in the middle of a diffraction spike from a nearby bright star. However a disk-like structure can be seen in a F200W--F090W image (the source is not visible in the F090W filter so we use this to suppress the diffraction spike). Like the other candidate galaxies, the nearest Simbad source is offset nearly 20 \ from the position of this object.

gmw_00006536.name: R.A.=10:36:55.2 and Dec= 58:38:09
aliases_external: candidate elliptical galaxy at R.A.=10:36:55.2 Dec=58:38:09
gmw_00006536.overview: R.A.=10:36:55.2 and Dec= 58:38:09 is a candidate elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2210.01101]. The source has a round shape, appears resolved in JWST NIRCam F187N imaging, and is located near an RR Lyrae candidate [arXiv:2210.01101].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this coordinate-selected source as a candidate elliptical galaxy, describing it as round, resolved compared with a neighboring star, absent in HST F658N, and near an RR Lyrae candidate.

quotes.quote: A candidate elliptical galaxy at R.A.=10:36:55.2 and Dec= 58:38:09 is noted in Figure fig:pms_field1 and shown in Figure fig:edge-on d. The source is round and appears resolved compared to the somewhat fainter star projected next to it in the JWST NIRCam F187N image, but is not visible in the HST F658N image. The object is 0.29 \ from an RR Lyrae candidate Gaia DR3 5350681737823554176 which is likely the neighboring point source.

gmw_00006537.name: central radio galaxy
aliases_external: BCG / central radio galaxy in ZwCl 235
gmw_00006537.overview: The central radio galaxy is the brightest cluster galaxy of the ZwCl 235 cluster [arXiv:2211.09141]. It features extended radio lobes spanning 15 to 20 kiloparsecs and active galactic nucleus activity that has excavated a pair of cavities in the surrounding medium [arXiv:2211.09141].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the central radio galaxy/BCG in ZwCl 235, finding radio lobes, AGN-driven cavities, uplifted gas, a compact X-ray corona, and ongoing feedback fueling.

quotes.quote: We aim to investigate the AGN/ICM interplay in ZwCl 235, a galaxy cluster with high X-ray flux, an extended central radio galaxy, and evidence of multi-phase gas at its center. Using archival data from the Chandra telescope, the VLASS survey, the LOTSS survey and the VLBA telescope, we perform a complete study of ZwCl 235, dissecting the dynamics of the ICM, the thermodynamic state of the central gas, and the properties of the BCG. By means of radial profiles and 2D spectral maps, we measure the temperature, entropy and cooling time of the ICM, and we compare the morphology of the central radio galaxy with the surrounding medium. We find evidence that ZwCl 235 is a sloshing cool core cluster in which the activity of the central galaxy has excavated a pair of cavities and possibly uplifted enriched gas to an altitude of 30 kpc.

The central radio galaxy In this section we discuss the morphology and spectral properties of the radio galaxy associated with the BCG of ZwCl 235, combining our new results with those presented in the literature. In the upper panel of Fig. figcav_paper we show the 144 MHz LOFAR observation of the cluster, that reveals extended radio lobes (each 20 kpc long) oriented north-east (NE) to south-west (SW), with a position angle of 130 ^ . Using this observation, 2020MNRAS.496.2613B measured the source total flux density S_ =151 24 mJy. The lower panel of Fig. figcav_paper shows the VLASS 3 GHz image of the radio galaxy at the center of ZwCl 235. The morphology is slightly resolved, showing a bright core, two opposite radio lobes extending NE to SW for roughly 15 kpc, and two small west protrusions.

gmw_00006538.name: Segue 1
gmw_00006538.overview: Segue 1 is a nearby dwarf satellite galaxy located at high Galactic latitude [arXiv:1205.3620]. Although its nature was previously disputed as a disrupted star cluster, kinematic studies of its member stars have confirmed its dwarf galaxy status [arXiv:1205.3620].

sources.summary: The paper treats Segue 1 as a nearby dwarf satellite galaxy with disputed nature, strong expected dark-matter annihilation signal, uncertain density profile, and no significant detected gamma-ray emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: centered on _ J2000 =151.767^ and _ J2000 =16.0819^ , the nominal position of Segue 1

quotes.quote: In our work we examine the dwarf galaxy Segue 1, since it combines several advantages: it is the closest satellite to Earth, it is located at high Galactic latitude (and, consequently, low gamma-ray background), and among the dwarf galaxies, Segue 1 is expected to produce the strongest dark-matter signal . Due to it's proximity to the Sagittarius stream, the nature of Segue 1 has been disputed: it was argued to be a disrupted star cluster originally associated with the Sagittarius dSph . However, a kinematic study of a larger member-star sample (66 stars compared to the previous 24-star sample) has recently confirmed that Segue 1 is indeed a dwarf satellite galaxy .

We should emphasize significant uncertainties of the Segue 1 density profile: almost all the stars lie inside 10' ( 67 pc) from the center , which is significantly smaller than r_s . In order to test how sensitive the annihilation signal is to the choice of profile, we used a second ( minimal ) model.

A binned likelihood analysis is performed on a map with 0.05^ pixel size in gnomonic (TAN) projection, measuring 10^ on a side and centered on _ J2000 =151.767^ and _ J2000 =16.0819^ , the nominal position of Segue 1. We only used photons with reconstructed energy greater than 2.4 GeV , for which the 68 containment radius of the point-spread function (PSF) is narrower than 0.45^ .

gmw_00006539.name: C10
gmw_00006539.overview: C10 is a gas-rich, dusty star-forming galaxy located approximately 65 kiloparsecs from the center of the SPT2349-56 protocluster [arXiv:2301.01328]. The galaxy is characterized by bright [C II] line emission, though its velocity suggests it is unlikely to power the overlapping Lyman-alpha blob [arXiv:2301.01328].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C10 as a [C II]-bright DSFG overlapping the Lyman-alpha blob, but finds its velocity and modeling make it unlikely to power the Lyman-alpha emission.

quotes.quote: A comparison of the position of the LAB with the location of the previously-identified SMGs in this field miller18,hill20 shows that three sources overlap spatially with the western part of the blob. These sources, called C10, C14 and C17 using the nomenclature of , were identified based on their bright [ C ii ] line emission, with fluxes of 2.96, 1.70 and 0.93 Jy km s ^ 1 , respectively. These galaxies are not the most luminous in the sample of [ C ii ] emitters in the SPT2349-56 system, and are located at about 65 kpc from the center of the protocluster.

In this case, the Lyman- spectrum of the LAB is found 300 km s ^ 1 redward of the protocluster core velocity ( v=0 km/s) and 100 km s ^ 1 of the C14 and C17 SMGs. A much larger velocity difference is seen between the LAB and the systemic velocity of the C10 galaxy. The inconsistency in velocities for C10 suggest this source might be unrelated to the Lyman- emission.
This solution is less preferred, since the assumed emitting source (the C10 galaxy) is a gas-rich, dusty galaxy contrary to the result of a low dust optical depth.

gmw_00006540.name: C14
gmw_00006540.overview: C14 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located in the SPT2349-56 protocluster [arXiv:2301.01328]. It is characterized by bright [C II] line emission and is a likely ionizing source for a spatially overlapping Lyman-alpha blob [arXiv:2301.01328].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C14 as a [C II]-bright DSFG spatially overlapping the Lyman-alpha blob and spectrally coincident enough to be a likely ionizing source.

quotes.quote: A comparison of the position of the LAB with the location of the previously-identified SMGs in this field miller18,hill20 shows that three sources overlap spatially with the western part of the blob. These sources, called C10, C14 and C17 using the nomenclature of , were identified based on their bright [ C ii ] line emission, with fluxes of 2.96, 1.70 and 0.93 Jy km s ^ 1 , respectively. These galaxies are not the most luminous in the sample of [ C ii ] emitters in the SPT2349-56 system, and are located at about 65 kpc from the center of the protocluster.

In this case, the Lyman- spectrum of the LAB is found 300 km s ^ 1 redward of the protocluster core velocity ( v=0 km/s) and 100 km s ^ 1 of the C14 and C17 SMGs. A much larger velocity difference is seen between the LAB and the systemic velocity of the C10 galaxy. The inconsistency in velocities for C10 suggest this source might be unrelated to the Lyman- emission.

Two of the spatially overlapping DSFGs C14 and C17, are found to also coincide spectrally, when comparing their [ C ii ] emission lines with that of the Lyman- emission from the LAB miller18,hill20 . This observation could be explained by the high star-formation activity seen in the DSFG protocluster members. Based on their locations and redshifts, the main suspects to be producing the ionizing photons and thus the Lyman- emission are the C14 and C17 DSFGs, or the protocluster core.

gmw_00006541.name: C17
gmw_00006541.overview: C17 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located approximately 65 kiloparsecs from the center of the SPT2349-56 protocluster [arXiv:2301.01328]. Characterized by bright [C II] line emission, its spatial and spectral alignment with a nearby Lyman-alpha blob suggests it may act as an ionizing source [arXiv:2301.01328].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C17 as a [C II]-bright DSFG whose position and redshift align with the Lyman-alpha blob, making it a plausible ionizing source with C14.

quotes.quote: A comparison of the position of the LAB with the location of the previously-identified SMGs in this field miller18,hill20 shows that three sources overlap spatially with the western part of the blob. These sources, called C10, C14 and C17 using the nomenclature of , were identified based on their bright [ C ii ] line emission, with fluxes of 2.96, 1.70 and 0.93 Jy km s ^ 1 , respectively. These galaxies are not the most luminous in the sample of [ C ii ] emitters in the SPT2349-56 system, and are located at about 65 kpc from the center of the protocluster.

In this case, the Lyman- spectrum of the LAB is found 300 km s ^ 1 redward of the protocluster core velocity ( v=0 km/s) and 100 km s ^ 1 of the C14 and C17 SMGs. A much larger velocity difference is seen between the LAB and the systemic velocity of the C10 galaxy. The inconsistency in velocities for C10 suggest this source might be unrelated to the Lyman- emission.

Two of the spatially overlapping DSFGs C14 and C17, are found to also coincide spectrally, when comparing their [ C ii ] emission lines with that of the Lyman- emission from the LAB miller18,hill20 . This observation could be explained by the high star-formation activity seen in the DSFG protocluster members. Based on their locations and redshifts, the main suspects to be producing the ionizing photons and thus the Lyman- emission are the C14 and C17 DSFGs, or the protocluster core.

gmw_00006542.name: NGC 3657
gmw_00006542.overview: NGC 3657 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy characterized by a strong central concentration of light [arXiv:astro-ph/0311348, arXiv:astro-ph/0601431]. It features an extended ultraviolet disk and a significantly lower dust-to-atomic hydrogen ratio than normal systems [arXiv:1201.2762].

sources.summary: NGC3657 is highlighted as an HRS outlier with low dust-to-H I ratio; the authors reject measurement problems, note its extended ultraviolet disk, and suggest pristine gas may explain excess H I.

quotes.quote: In addition to deficient galaxies showing a higher M_ dust /M_ HI ratio than normal, it is interesting to note the presence of a few outliers having M_ dust /M_ HI significantly lower ( 10^ 3 ) than the average value for normal systems ( 10^ 2.1 ), namely: NGC3657, NGC4203, NGC4262, NGC4636 and UGC8045.

Conversely, we can exclude a measurement problem for NGC3657, NGC4203 and NGC4262 and conclude that these systems have significantly higher amounts of atomic hydrogen compared to their dust content. This is confirmed when we look in detail at the properties of these systems. NGC4203 and NGC4262 have peculiar \ morphologies krumm85,vandriel88 and several independent analyses have proposed that these galaxies might have recently accreted cold gas (see cortese09 and references therein).

Although no \ interferometric data are available for NGC3657, the huge ultraviolet extended disk revealed by GALEX images (see Fig. hrs52 ) suggests that this galaxy likely contains an extended \ reservoir. Thus, we speculate that the unusually low M_ dust /M_ HI ratio observed in these systems may be due to the presence of a considerable amount of pristine gas not yet enriched by star formation, either because it has been recently accreted or because it has too low column density to form stars at high rate.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3657 as an LSB example where central light concentration closely matches the steeply rising rotation curve and maximum-disk fit.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1 shows the luminosity profile (Swaters 1999) and the rotation curve (Zwaan, Van der Hulst and Bosma 2003) of the LSB galaxy NGC 3657 (UGC 6406). This galaxy is dominated by a very bright central concentration. The rotation curve shows a steep rise and a peak inside 50 arcsec (5 kpc) instead of the slow rise characteristic of LSB systems. This feature in the rotation curve corresponds to the strong central concentration in the luminosity profile. Maximum disk fits give M/L values (R-band) between 2.9 and 4.2 for the central concentration and between 13.8 and 14.7 for the outer disk (Zwaan et al. 2003).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3657 as a MOND example where baryonic surface-density structure closely matches the observed rotation curve, including evidence for a central cusp.

quotes.quote: Another example is the dwarf galaxy, NGC 3657. Fig. \ 4 shows the surface densities of the baryonic components, stars and gas, compared to the observed rotation curve. Again the dotted and dashed curves are the Newtonian rotation curves of the stellar and gaseous components and the solid curve is the resulting MOND rotation curve. The agreement with observations is obvious. For this galaxy, there is evidence from the rotation curve of a central cusp in the density distribution-- and, indeed, the cusp is seen in the light distribution.

gmw_00006543.name: LCG-1
gmw_00006543.overview: LCG-1 is a high-redshift Lyman-break starburst galaxy with a systemic spectroscopic redshift of 3.2890 [arXiv:2003.03905]. It is a primary candidate for a high Lyman continuum escape fraction and exhibits two small Lyman continuum clumps separated from its rest-frame ultraviolet continuum peak [arXiv:2003.03905].

sources.summary: The paper treats LCG-1 as the primary high-escape-fraction LyC candidate, with LyC clumps, MOSFIRE emission-line detections, and confirmed systemic redshift.

quotes.quote: LCG-1 is reported by Mic17MNRAS.465..316M as ``LBG03'' in their paper. In the LyC image (Subaru/ NB359 filter), two small LyC clumps are observed, separated by 0.8^ see figure 2 in Mic17MNRAS.465..316M from the peak of the rest-frame UV continuum (Subaru/ R filter). We set the MOSFIRE slitlet on the peak of the UV continuum and use the wider slit width due to the complex morphology of LyC and UV continuum. The spectroscopic redshift is z_ spec, Ly = 3.287 which is measured from the optical low-resolution spectroscopy for the Ly emission line Mic17MNRAS.465..316M . Due to the blue NB359 - R color, we consider LCG-1 to be the primary high- \ candidate in our sample.

For LCG-1, we detect the three emission lines ( [ O iii ] 4959, 5007 and H ), and then confirm its systemic spectroscopic redshift from [ O iii ] 5007 to be z_ spec, sys = 3.2890 . This value is consistent with (or slightly larger than) that from the Ly emission line inferred from optical low-resolution spectroscopy.

gmw_00006544.name: 3CR 103
gmw_00006544.overview: 3CR 103 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.33 [arXiv:2011.04668]. It exhibits nearby extended X-ray emission that may correspond to an overdensity of galaxies or a galaxy cluster [arXiv:2011.04668].

sources.summary: The paper finds no significant cross-axis emission from 3CR 103 but detects nearby extended X-ray emission possibly associated with an overdensity or cluster.

quotes.quote: 3CR 103 ( z=0.33 ). We did not detect significant emission perpendicular to the radio axis arising from this galaxy, but we detected extended emission at 93 arcsec (446 kpc at the source redshift) above 5 confidence level to the south-west of the galaxy that could indicate the presence of a galaxy cluster. However, there is a bright star close to the position of the extended emission that prevented us from obtaining precise redshift estimates of nearby optical sources, necessary to verify the presence of a galaxy cluster/group and deserving future follow up observations. The left panel of Fig. fig:cluster1 shows an optical image from Pan-STARRS of the field of 3CR 103 with radio contours (at 1.4 GHz) and X-ray contours (in the 0.5 - 3 keV band). The extended X-ray emission appears to be associated with the radio galaxy and seems to correspond to an overdensity of galaxies.

gmw_00006545.name: 3CR 403.1
gmw_00006545.overview: The radio galaxy 3CR 403.1 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class II low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1903.10768] with complex ionized gas morphology [arXiv:1903.10768]. Its radio axis orientation suggests possible radio-jet precession [arXiv:2004.13025], while perpendicular radio and X-ray structures may indicate the presence of a radio relic [arXiv:2011.04668].

sources.summary: MUSE reveals complex 3C 403.1 line morphology, including a rotating central region and elongated eastern structures with compact knots amid other field galaxies.

quotes.quote: 3C 403.1: FR II/LEG, 1 = 1.07 kpc. The line morphology in this source is particularly complex. There is a central region extending 9 kpc around the host galaxy and apparently showing well ordered rotation. However, the ionized gas also forms a series of elongated structures, mainly on the E and SE direction, with compact knots joined by more diffuse emission. There are several galaxies in the MUSE field of view, but only a few emission line knots are associated with them.

sources.summary: The paper questions the 3C 403.1 host, noting two AGN candidates, unresolved VLA ambiguity, and a radio-axis rotation suggestive of precession.

quotes.quote: Another 3CR source for which we question the existing host-galaxy identification is 3C 403.1. This appears in the G4Jy Sample as G4Jy 1582 (GLEAM J195222 011550 and GLEAM J195232 011729), with its current identification coinciding with AllWISE J195230.53 011720.7 and g1952305 011721 (in 6dFGS). There is weak (0.5--7 keV) X-ray emission at this position Massaro2012 , but the same can be said for the nearby source, AllWISE J195231.25 011716.6. Hence, there are two AGN candidates for G4Jy 1582, and the VLA image at 300 MHz does not allow this ambiguity to be resolved. However, interestingly, the orientation of the radio axis at 300 MHz (cyan contours in Figure disagreementoverlays d) appears to be at 45 \ with respect to the radio axis at 150 MHz (yellow contours in Figure disagreementoverlays d), and so is suggestive of radio-jet precession (see Section sec:XandSorZshaped ). Like the `unclassifiable' sources described in Section sec:unclassifiable , each of the above four sources are being followed up using MeerKAT (PI: White).

sources.summary: The paper reports significant emission along and across 3CR 403.1, with X-ray and radio structures suggesting a possible radio relic.

quotes.quote: 3CR 403.1 ( z=0.0554 ). We detected significant emission along and in the perpendicular direction to the radio axis. The extended X-ray emission is aligned with the GHz radio structure (see Fig. fig:sample ); however, the TGSS structure is perpendicular to the X-ray extended emission as shown in Fig. fig:3c403p1 . This could indicate the presence of a radio relic (see e.g., Rottgering1994,Rottgering1997 , Bagchi1998 , Feretti1998 , Giovannini1999 , Feretti2012 and vanWeeren2019 ).

gmw_00006546.name: 3CR 430
gmw_00006546.overview: 3CR 430 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0555 [arXiv:2011.04668]. It exhibits extended X-ray emission that may originate from the intracluster medium of two merging galaxy clusters [arXiv:2011.04668].
