The paper classifies RGB J2229 + 3057 as a quasar and reports extended A- and C-array radio emission with two lobes and a hot spot.

quotes.quote: RGB J2229 + 3057. LM98 presented an optical spectrum of this source and classified it as a quasar. The source is extended on both A and C array images (Figs. 1(f) and (g)). In particular, on the A array image two well-defined lobes are visible at each side of the nucleus, the closest lobe being fairly extended with a hot spot.

gmw_00006594.name: RGB J2318 + 3048
gmw_00006594.overview: RGB J2318 + 3048 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:0712.1689]. Its X-ray spectrum is well fit by a single power-law with Galactic absorption, though a broken power-law or a soft excess marginally improves the fit [arXiv:0712.1689].

sources.summary: The paper fits the X-ray spectrum of RGB J2318 + 3048, comparing single and broken power-law models and noting the spectral index, break, or soft-excess behavior.

quotes.quote: RGB J2318 + 3048. The ACIS data are well fit by a single power-law with Galactic absorption and a spectral index of 1.7 . There is a hint in the data of a soft excess; the fit is marginally improved ( F test 96 ) assuming an absorption lower than Galactic, but in this case the N_ H is not well constrained. The fit is also marginally improved ( F test 99 ) if we assume a broken power-law instead of a single power-law.

gmw_00006595.name: WGA J2347 + 0852
gmw_00006595.overview: WGA J2347 + 0852 is a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718] with an X-ray spectrum that is well fit by a single power-law with Galactic absorption [arXiv:0712.1689]. Radio observations reveal an X-shaped galaxy morphology with a central core and two asymmetric, hot-spotted lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718].

sources.summary: The paper fits the X-ray spectrum of WGA J2347 + 0852, comparing single and broken power-law models and noting the spectral index, break, or soft-excess behavior.

quotes.quote: WGA J2347 + 0852. The ACIS data are well fit by a single power-law with Galactic absorption and a spectral index of 1.9 . There is a hint in the data of a soft excess; the fit is marginally improved ( F test 97 ) assuming an absorption lower than Galactic. The fit is also marginally improved ( F test 99 ) if we assume a broken power-law instead of a single power-law.

sources.summary: The paper classifies WGA J2347 + 0852 as a quasar with extended A- and C-array structure, an X-shaped galaxy morphology, and asymmetric hot-spotted lobes.

quotes.quote: WGA J2347 + 0852. Per98 published an optical spectrum of this source and classified it as a quasar. This source is extended in both our A and C array images (Figs. 1(z) and (aa)). The X-shape of the galaxy is apparent only in the C array image, whereas on A array scales we observe a central core with two almost equidistant lobes. Both lobes have hot spots, however, one lobe is brighter than the other.

gmw_00006596.name: galaxy 1134
gmw_00006596.overview: Galaxy 1134 is a disk galaxy observed as part of the GLASS survey [arXiv:1910.07803]. It features a disk scale length of 0.45 arcseconds and exhibits a strong emission complex containing nitrogen and sulfur ions [arXiv:1910.07803].

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy 1134 as a slitless-spectroscopy test case, fitting emission-line kinematics and reporting rotation, redshift, or detection significance.

quotes.quote: We present the result of the fit performed on galaxy 1134 from the GLASS survey, illustrating a very significant kinematic detection. This galaxy has a strong emission complex + [ N II ] + [ S II ] and a disc scale length r_ d = ;;0.45 . The fit residuals without (resp. \ with) kinematics is shown in the bottom left (resp. \ right) panel of Fig.

gmw_00006597.name: galaxy 19843
gmw_00006597.overview: Galaxy 19843 is a disk galaxy identified in the 3D-HST survey [arXiv:1910.07803]. It features a disk scale length of approximately 0.48 arcseconds and exhibits a strong emission complex of hydrogen, nitrogen, and sulfur [arXiv:1910.07803].

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy 19843 as a slitless-spectroscopy test case, fitting emission-line kinematics and reporting rotation, redshift, or detection significance.

quotes.quote: We present the result for galaxy 19843 from the 3D-HST survey, illustrating a weaker kinematic detection than previously shown. This galaxy has a strong + [ N II ] + [ S II ] emission complex and a disc scale length r_ d = ;;0.48 . As shown in Fig.

gmw_00006598.name: SMM J123711
gmw_00006598.overview: SMM J123711 is a bright submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of z=1.9951 [arXiv:2302.10323]. It exhibits properties typical of ultraluminous infrared galaxies [arXiv:2302.10323] and belongs to an overdense, still-assembling large-scale structure [arXiv:2302.10323].

sources.summary: The paper places SMM J123711 in a known z~1.99 overdensity, describes it as a bright ULIRG-like SMG, and finds a marginal L18 overdensity association.

quotes.quote: SMM J123711, SMM J12618, and SMM J123712 were confirmed by Bothwell13 as bright submillimeter emitters, with similar properties in terms of gas masses M_ H_2 (3-5-5.5) 10^ 10 M_ and FIR luminosities, L_ FIR /L_ =12.4-12.9 , typical of ULIRGs. They also have the same redshifts z=1.995-1.996 and relatively small pairwise projected separations of 3.3 Mpc. Therefore, we believe all three SMGs in our sample belong to the same overdense large-scale structure.

The source SMM J123711 at z=1.9951 is associated with a 3 overdensity at z_ ov =2.29 0.07 ; therefore, the two redshifts are marginally consistent with each other, |z_ ov z_ spec |/(1+z_ spec ) = 0.10 . In other cases (e.g., HDF850.1, SMM J123634, SMM J123606, SMM J123618, SMM J123707, SMM J123711, and SMM J123712) our detected overdensities have evidence for large-scale filamentary or clumpy structures, as typically found in still-assembling protoclusters such as SSA22 at z=3.09 Steidel98,Umehata2019 , the Spiderweb at z=2.16 Jin21 , and Hyperion at z=2.45 Cucciati2018 .

gmw_00006599.name: SMM J123712
gmw_00006599.overview: SMM J123712 is a bright submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of z=1.996 [arXiv:2302.10323]. It belongs to a large-scale overdense structure with two other submillimeter galaxies, showing evidence of a filamentary or clumpy protocluster environment [arXiv:2302.10323].

sources.summary: The paper groups SMM J123712 with nearby z~1.99 bright SMGs in the same large-scale overdensity and reports filamentary or clumpy overdensity structure.

quotes.quote: SMM J123711, SMM J12618, and SMM J123712 were confirmed by Bothwell13 as bright submillimeter emitters, with similar properties in terms of gas masses M_ H_2 (3-5-5.5) 10^ 10 M_ and FIR luminosities, L_ FIR /L_ =12.4-12.9 , typical of ULIRGs. They also have the same redshifts z=1.995-1.996 and relatively small pairwise projected separations of 3.3 Mpc. Therefore, we believe all three SMGs in our sample belong to the same overdense large-scale structure.

These are the triplet SMM J123711, SMM J123618, and SMM J123712 as well as the GN20--GN20.2a pair. For these sources, we obtain similar results when looking for overdensities in the three photo- z catalogs used in this work. In other cases (e.g., HDF850.1, SMM J123634, SMM J123606, SMM J123618, SMM J123707, SMM J123711, and SMM J123712) our detected overdensities have evidence for large-scale filamentary or clumpy structures, as typically found in still-assembling protoclusters such as SSA22 at z=3.09 Steidel98,Umehata2019 , the Spiderweb at z=2.16 Jin21 , and Hyperion at z=2.45 Cucciati2018 .

gmw_00006600.name: SMM J123618
gmw_00006600.overview: SMM J123618 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy associated with a nearby triplet of similar sources [arXiv:2302.10323]. It resides within a detected overdensity that exhibits a filamentary or clumpy morphology, suggesting a complex dynamical state within a still-assembling protocluster [arXiv:2302.10323].

sources.summary: The paper groups SMM J123618 with nearby SMGs in a known structure, but notes its overdensity appears only in L18 and has filamentary or clumpy morphology.

quotes.quote: These are the triplet SMM J123711, SMM J123618, and SMM J123712 as well as the GN20--GN20.2a pair. For these sources, we obtain similar results when looking for overdensities in the three photo- z catalogs used in this work. The only exception is SMM J123618, for which we find an overdensity only in the L18 catalog, at variance with SMM J123711 and SMM J123712, for which overdensities are found both in B19 and L18.

In other cases (e.g., HDF850.1, SMM J123634, SMM J123606, SMM J123618, SMM J123707, SMM J123711, and SMM J123712) our detected overdensities have evidence for large-scale filamentary or clumpy structures, as typically found in still-assembling protoclusters such as SSA22 at z=3.09 Steidel98,Umehata2019 , the Spiderweb at z=2.16 Jin21 , and Hyperion at z=2.45 Cucciati2018 . These overdensities with nontrivial morphology represent a piece of indirect evidence for the complex dynamical state of the associated protoclusters, which are indeed non-relaxed structures.

gmw_00006601.name: L2
gmw_00006601.overview: L2 is a foreground early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.3877 [arXiv:1004.3546]. It exhibits stellar absorption features, a high velocity dispersion of 278 km/s, and a large inferred stellar mass [arXiv:1004.3546].

sources.summary: L2 is described as a foreground red galaxy at z=0.3877 with stellar absorption features, high velocity dispersion, large inferred mass, and early-type-galaxy-like luminosity.

quotes.quote: Galaxy L2 has an SDSS spectrum reproduced in Figure fig:SDSS_spec . We identify a number of stellar absorption features, labelled in Figure fig:SDSS_spec , which indicate that it lies at redshift z_ abs = 0.3877 . In order to obtain an estimate of the velocity dispersion (and hence the mass) of the galaxy, we used the the penalised pixel fitting method of cappellari04 , taking into account the difference in velocity resolution between the input stellar template spectra of sanchez-blazquez06 and the SDSS spectrum. The best fit gives a stellar velocity dispersion _ L2 = 278 6 km s ^ 1 , where the uncertainty reflects the dispersion in the values of obtained with different stellar templates. The corresponding mass is M() = ^2 /G (appropriate to an isothermal sphere), or M_ L2 6 10^ 11 M_ , adopting =10 kpc .

From the SDSS measured magnitude z( L2 ) = 17.67 , we deduce an absolute magnitude M_r( L2 ) = -23.17 , given the distance modulus m - M = 41.17 in our cosmology and applying an evolutionary correction of 0.85 z to M_r , as proposed by bernardi06 . There is no SDSS spectrum for galaxy L1. If it is at the same redshift as L2, z=0.3877 , its absolute magnitude is M_r( L1 ) = -22.82 and the projected separation between the two galaxies is 28 kpc. The absolute magnitude and velocity dispersion of L2 are within the range of values found by bernardi06 in their sample of massive early-type galaxies selected from the SDSS database.

gmw_00006602.name: L1
gmw_00006602.overview: L1 is a foreground red galaxy that acts as a candidate strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1004.3546]. It is located in close proximity to the galaxy L2, with which it may share a redshift of z=0.3877 [arXiv:1004.3546].

sources.summary: L1 is treated as a foreground red lensing galaxy candidate; the paper notes its blending with an image, estimates its possible luminosity, and considers its role in producing the lensed images.

quotes.quote: In particular, while image im1 may appear to be bluer than im2 from the magnitudes listed in Table tab:maggies , we suspect that this is just due to blending with the foreground red galaxy L1, because our X-shooter spectra of all three images, im1, im2, and im3, exhibit the same continuum slope.
There is no SDSS spectrum for galaxy L1. If it is at the same redshift as L2, z=0.3877 , its absolute magnitude is M_r( L1 ) = -22.82 and the projected separation between the two galaxies is 28 kpc.

If galaxy L1 is: (a) the lens, and (b) at the same redshift as L2, then applying the measured _ L2 = 278 km s ^ 1 (which would actually be an upper limit for L1, since L1 is fainter than L2---see Table tab:maggies ) to eq. ( eq:isodef ) would result in typical deflections of 1.4 arcsec, or only about half the deflections of images im1--im4. We are thus led to one of two conclusions. Either L1 is substantially more massive than L2, as would be case if it is at a higher redshift, or L2 and other galaxies in the group significantly boost the lensing by L1. Presumably the second option is the more likely, given the proximity of L1 and L2 on the sky and their broadly similar magnitudes and colours (Table tab:maggies ).

gmw_00006603.name: B2319+051
gmw_00006603.overview: B2319+051 is a gravitational lens system [arXiv:astro-ph/0603448]. It is associated with a large foreground group containing at least 10 members, as well as another group located in the background of the lens [arXiv:astro-ph/0603448].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2319+051 as a CLASS gravitational lens system with foreground and background groups whose modeled shear fails to match the required lens shear.

quotes.quote: The B1600+434 system has a time delay measured for it, and we find the system to lie in a group with a velocity dispersion of 100  km s ^ 1 and at least six members. B2319+051 has a large group in its immediate foreground with at least 10 members and a velocity dispersion of 460  km s ^ 1 and another in the background of the lens with a velocity dispersion of 190  km s ^ 1 . There are several other small groups in the fields of these lens systems, and we describe the properties of these moderate redshift groups.

Models for B2319 were found to inadequately reproduce the image configuration without applying a large shear term = 0.14, PA _ = -22^ ; rusin . The necessary additional shear is essentially perpendicular to the expected orientation of the shear caused by the lensing galaxy, and this excess shear is therefore assumed to be caused by another object. We investigate whether the foreground and background groups we have detected can adequately account for this discrepancy.

gmw_00006604.name: IC 875
gmw_00006604.overview: IC 875 is classified as a lenticular galaxy in some databases [arXiv:1406.7121]. However, spectral analysis reveals it is a slow rotator that is dynamically consistent with a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 875 rotates slowly for a lenticular candidate and is dynamically consistent with a low-luminosity elliptical, so it is excluded from the S0 analysis.

quotes.quote: IC 875 In the HyperLeda and NED databases this galaxy is classified as a lenticular, and hence we have included it into our sample. However, after the analysis of the spectral data, we have found that it proved to possess rather slow rotation. If we adopt the inclination as i=50 from the NED, then V_ rot = V_ LOS / i 65 km s ^ 1 , while the stellar velocity dispersion is measured to be 110 km s ^ 1 , that gives the ratio of the rotation velocity to the velocity dispersion V_ max / 0.6 . The ratio of the major and minor axes, taken from NED, gives the isophote ellipticity of = 0.25 . These values put the galaxy at the Binney's diagram ( V_ max / - ) onto the line of isotropic spheroids supported by rotation; this dynamical status is also common for low-luminosity elliptical galaxies davies_1983 . So we conclude that IC 875 is probably an elliptical galaxy.

gmw_00006605.name: IC 1502
gmw_00006605.overview: IC 1502 is a lenticular galaxy with a high inclination and a regular stellar rotation profile [arXiv:1406.7121]. Its stellar population is characterized by old, solar-metallicity stars and a kinematically detached central region [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1502 as an S0 galaxy with regular rotation, old solar-metallicity stars, and a kinematically detached central region with a marginal metallicity dip.

quotes.quote: IC 1502 In the NED, this galaxy is classified as S0 ^+ . It has a rather large inclination i=64 , according to the HyperLeda. The radial line-of-sight velocity profile reveals regular rotation. The main feature of this galaxy is that having the solar metallicity, the SSP-equivalent age of the stars is about 15--17 Gyr over the whole measured radial range.

The radial profiles of the stellar parameters in IC 1502 demonstrate the central kinematically detached region, which betrays itself as a steep stellar velocity gradient and a small decrease in the stellar velocity dispersion. The metallicity profile reveals a marginal dip. All the combination of the features is probably the evidence of evolutionary isolation of the nuclear star cluster, although as for the age of the nuclear stellar population it does not stand away within the measurement error.

gmw_00006606.name: NGC 3098
gmw_00006606.overview: NGC 3098 is an isolated, edge-on lenticular galaxy characterized by a small, compact bulge [arXiv:1406.7121]. It features a ring of enhanced stellar surface brightness and a dynamically cold stellar disc [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3098 as an isolated edge-on lenticular with a small compact bulge, enhanced-brightness ring, homogeneous 5-8 Gyr stellar populations, and a cold disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3098 It is a well-known isolated, edge-on lenticular galaxy. Its photometric structure was studied by seifert_scorza . It was noted that a bulge of the galaxy was small and compact, and between the bulge and the disc, approximately at the radius of 15 , there was a ring of enhanced stellar surface brightness. Our results (Fig. kin_stpop_profiles:Katkov_n ) confirm the small effect of the bulge---the rotation velocity growth in the center of the galaxy is very slow---and demonstrate a homogeneous age of 5--8 billion years for the stellar populations across the whole galaxy as well as a sharp drop of the stellar metallicity in the transition from the central part of the galaxy to the region dominated by the large-scale stellar disc. The stellar disc looks dynamically cold, <60 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006607.name: NGC 6615
gmw_00006607.overview: NGC 6615 is a barred galaxy that features a compact bar oriented almost orthogonally to the major axis of its galactic disc [arXiv:1406.7121]. Its surface brightness profile reveals an extended stellar lens, which contains a uniformly old stellar population [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6615 as barred, with a compact bar, extended stellar lens, uniformly old studied stellar populations, possible young narrow ring, low metallicity, and no emission-line gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 6615 This galaxy is classified as barred in all the extragalactic databases. Visual inspection of the SDSS images confirms that a compact bar is present and oriented almost orthogonally to the major axis of the galactic disc. The surface brightness profile reveals a noticeable extended stellar lens with a flat brightness distribution, while the exponential disc itself starts at the radii greater than 40 . The sensitivity of our spectroscopy with the high-resolution VPHG2300 holographic grating (the grism) was not enough to reach the disc, and we have identified the characteristics of the stellar populations only in the bulge and in the lens. The age of the stellar population is uniformly old throughout the entire studied part of the galaxy.

gmw_00006608.name: NGC7252W
gmw_00006608.overview: NGC7252W is a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy located in the north-western tidal tail of NGC 7252 [arXiv:1802.09493, arXiv:2309.06478]. It exhibits star formation [arXiv:1802.09493], a rotating gas disk structure [arXiv:1511.00689], and disturbed, asymmetric ionized gas likely affected by stellar feedback [arXiv:1509.05404].

sources.summary: NGC 7252NW is a tidal dwarf candidate with high metallicity, IFU H-alpha observations, disturbed ionized gas, and a disc model that closely reproduces its H I emission.

quotes.quote: We also obtained IFU observations of NGC 7252NW to investigate its H kinematics. We used GIRAFFE on the UT2 of the VLT Pasquini02 , obtaining 55 exposures of 23 minutes each. The observations are summarized in Table tab:IFUobs . The data were reduced using the ESO pipeline. Additionally, we corrected each reduced cube to heliocentric velocity, before combining all 55 exposures into a variance-weighted mean cube. This final cube was then inspected and analysed using Gipsy.

The H emission, indeed, does not coincide with the dynamical centre of NGC 7252NW but is shifted towards the Western, approaching side (see cross in Fig. fig:N7252_Ha , middle panel). In general, the ionized gas appears more asymmetric and disturbed than the gas. Most likely, we are observing regions dominated by turbulence and/or non-circular motions due to stellar feedback.
NGC 7252NW is closely reproduced by our disc model.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC7252NW as a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy with SFR=0.03 M_/yr, intense southern NUV emission, and a flux gradient likely tied to fallback tidal material.

quotes.quote: The star formation rates in the associated tidal dwarf galaxies (NGC7252E, SFR=0.02 M _ /yr and NGC7252NW, SFR=0.03 M _ /yr) are typical of dwarf galaxies in the local Universe.

(H) The feature is a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy (NGC7252NW) Lelli_2015 . The intense NUV emission is seen from the southern region of NGC7252NW. The north western tidal tail may have material falling back to the galaxy Hibbard_1995 . We note that NGC7252NW has a gradient in flux towards the main body of galaxy: this could be due to star formation in the tidal material falling back towards the main galaxy.

Significant star formation is detected in the tidal dwarf galaxy candidates (NGC7252E, SFR=0.02 M _ /yr and NGC7252NW, SFR=0.03 M _ /yr) which is comparable to the integrated star formation rates observed in dwarf galaxies in local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper reports CO detection in the tidal dwarf NGC7252W, derives its molecular mass from CO(2-1), and places it at an intermediate compactness and enhancement stage among TDGs.

quotes.quote: CO detections were obtained for the NGC 7252 West (hereafter NGC7252W), NGC 4676 North (hereafter NGC4676N), NGC 5291 North and NGC 5291 South (hereafter NGC5291N and NGC5291S), Stephan's Quintet source 'B' (hereafter NGC7319E), and very probably the NGC 4038/9 South (``The Antennae", hereafter NGC4038S) TDGs. Figures 1 -- 7 show optical images of these galaxies with contours showing the HI emission.

For NGC7252W and NGC4038S, only the CO(2--1) line was detected and so this was used to estimate the H _2 mass via Eq. (3) but with _0 = 230 GHz instead of 115 GHz and assuming an intrinsic CO( 2-1 1-0 ) line ratio of 0.75. In these two sources the CO emission may well be optically thin so the molecular gas mass should be viewed as an upper limit.
NGC7252W is clearly more enhanced and compact than either NGC4038S or NGC4676N but nothing like Arp105S. The same is true for the much more massive Arp245N.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7252NW as a tidal dwarf galaxy whose gas distribution and velocity field resemble a rotating disk, with an axisymmetric disk model matching its position-velocity diagrams.

quotes.quote: Although a rotating disc provides a good description of the kinematics, there is an important issue with this model. The orbital times at the edge of the disc are longer than the dynamical timescale for the interaction/merger event estimated from numerical simulations. Hence, these discs have undergone less than a full rotation since the epoch of the TDG formation, raising the question of whether they are in dynamical equilibrium. For collisionless stellar systems, the process of relaxation takes a few orbital times due to the energy exchange between individual particles and the total gravitational potential LyndenBell67 . For dissipative gas systems as TDGs, the energy can probably be exchanged and radiated away on a local crossing time ( 10 Myr), thus one may speculate that the process of relaxation in collisional systems is faster than in collisionless ones.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 7252NW as a bona-fide tidal dwarf in NGC 7252’s north-western tail and excludes NGC 7252SE for lacking single-dish CO.

quotes.quote: hibbard1994cold identified two TDG candidates in the North-Western and Eastern tails (NGC 7252NW and NGC 7252E). These star-forming complexes were confirmed as bona-fide TDGs based on their H kinematics Bournaud2004 , \ kinematics lelli2015gas and relatively high gas metallicities lelli2015gas . In this paper, we focus on NGC 7252NW because no single-dish CO observations are available for NGC 7252SE.

gmw_00006609.name: NGC4676N
gmw_00006609.overview: NGC4676N is a young, weakly condensed tidal dwarf galaxy that is still forming from a tidal tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513]. It is characterized as a non-compact system with only a small density enhancement relative to its parent tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO in NGC4676N, combines two observed areas for its molecular mass, and classifies it as a young, weakly condensed tidal dwarf still forming from a tail.

quotes.quote: For NGC5291N and NGC4676N CO(2-1) a first order (i.e. linear) baseline was subtracted. CO detections were obtained for the NGC 7252 West (hereafter NGC7252W), NGC 4676 North (hereafter NGC4676N), NGC 5291 North and NGC 5291 South (hereafter NGC5291N and NGC5291S), Stephan's Quintet source 'B' (hereafter NGC7319E), and very probably the NGC 4038/9 South (``The Antennae", hereafter NGC4038S) TDGs.

In NGC4676N, the two areas (see Fig. 5) were added together, so the molecular gas mass refers to the sum of the two positions. All derived molecular gas masses are given in Tab. 2; they range from 2 10^6 M _ to 4.5 10^8 M _ with an average value of 1.8 10^8 M _ .

Arp105S is clearly very compact and, at the opposite end, NGC4038S and NGC4676N are only just condensing from the tidal tail, being non-compact and with only a small density (light or HI) enhancement with respect to the tail. NGC7252W is clearly more enhanced and compact than either NGC4038S or NGC4676N but nothing like Arp105S.

gmw_00006610.name: NGC4038S
gmw_00006610.overview: NGC4038S is a young, non-compact tidal dwarf galaxy candidate that is currently condensing from a tidal tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513]. It has a probable CO detection, which indicates the presence of molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513].

sources.summary: The paper reports a probable CO detection for NGC4038S, estimates its molecular mass from CO(2-1), and describes it as a young, non-compact condensation in a tidal tail.

quotes.quote: CO detections were obtained for the NGC 7252 West (hereafter NGC7252W), NGC 4676 North (hereafter NGC4676N), NGC 5291 North and NGC 5291 South (hereafter NGC5291N and NGC5291S), Stephan's Quintet source 'B' (hereafter NGC7319E), and very probably the NGC 4038/9 South (``The Antennae", hereafter NGC4038S) TDGs. Figures 1 -- 7 show optical images of these galaxies with contours showing the HI emission.

For NGC7252W and NGC4038S, only the CO(2--1) line was detected and so this was used to estimate the H _2 mass via Eq. (3) but with _0 = 230 GHz instead of 115 GHz and assuming an intrinsic CO( 2-1 1-0 ) line ratio of 0.75. In these two sources the CO emission may well be optically thin so the molecular gas mass should be viewed as an upper limit.

Arp105S is clearly very compact and, at the opposite end, NGC4038S and NGC4676N are only just condensing from the tidal tail, being non-compact and with only a small density (light or HI) enhancement with respect to the tail. NGC7252W is clearly more enhanced and compact than either NGC4038S or NGC4676N but nothing like Arp105S.

gmw_00006611.name: Arp245N
gmw_00006611.overview: Arp 245N is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located at the tip of a stellar tidal tail [arXiv:1010.2201]. It contains an evolved stellar population originating from its parent galaxies [arXiv:1010.2201], alongside ongoing star formation [arXiv:1010.2201] and extended CO gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513].

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp245N as a detected tidal dwarf, more massive and extended than several younger TDGs, with substantial disk-star contribution and CO roughly as extended as other tracers.

quotes.quote: The compact tidal dwarf associated with the NGC 3561 (Arp 105) system, Arp105S, and the tidal dwarf of the NGC 2992/3 (Arp 245) system, Arp245N, were detected in the first run and described in Paper I. The immediate result is that almost all sources were detected in CO.

NGC7252W is clearly more enhanced and compact than either NGC4038S or NGC4676N but nothing like Arp105S. The same is true for the much more massive Arp245N. For the NGC5291 TDGs the stellar enhancement is total and the HI enhanced by a factor of a few, although it is still extended; they are clearly more evolved by these criteria than Arp245N.

The only extended TDGs, with respect to the resolution of our observations, are NGC4038S and Arp245N. Arp245N is roughly as extended in CO as at other wavelengths; NGC4038S was only observed at one position.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 245N as a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate at a tidal-tail tip, with evolved stars from parent galaxies, ongoing star formation in blue regions, and a 115 Myr fitted burst.

quotes.quote: As shown by , Arp 245N is a Tidal Dwarf Galaxy candidate located at the tip of a prominent stellar tidal tail Fig. 2 of boquien2009a . The optical images of Arp 245N show without any possible doubt the presence of an evolved population originating from the parent galaxies. The TDG exhibits several bluer regions, with H , UV and mid--IR counterparts, indicating the presence of on-going star formation. Stellar populations The best fit (shown in Figure fig:fit-Arp-245N ) corresponds to a burst strength of only =-0.75 , t=115 10^6 years, a short timescale of =10^6 years, an attenuation of 1.1 magnitude for the evolved population and 1.5 magnitudes for the population formed in the collision debris.

gmw_00006612.name: NGC 4666
gmw_00006612.overview: NGC 4666 is a nearby, highly inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1507.04257, arXiv:2401.15052] that hosts both a starburst and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1903.11042, arXiv:2403.05709]. Driven by intense star formation, the galaxy exhibits a wind-dominated radio and X-ray halo [arXiv:1903.11042, arXiv:1906.10650] and shows tidal features indicating an interaction with its neighbor, NGC 4668 [arXiv:1508.05153, arXiv:2403.05709].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4666 with Fabry-Perot observations, measuring diffuse ionized gas kinematics and modeling its halo as thick and thin ionized-gas disks with outflowing components.

quotes.quote: In this paper we studied the kinematics and morphology of the diffuse ionized gas (DIG) in the disk and in the halo of the star forming spiral galaxy NGC 4666 to derive information about its kinematical properties. Especially, we searched for infalling and outflowing ionized gas. We determined surface brightness, radial velocity, and velocity dispersion of the warm ionized gas via high spectral resolution (R 9000) Fabry-P erot interferometry. This allows the determination of the global velocity field and the detection of local deviations from this verlocity field.

We calculated models of the DIG distribution and its kinematics for comparison with the measured data. In this way we determined fundamental parameters such as the inclination and the scale height of NGC 4666, and established the need for an additional gas component to fit our observed data. We found individual areas, especially along the minor axis, with gas components reaching into the halo which we interpret as an outflowing component of the diffuse ionized gas. As the main result of our study, we were able to determine that the vertical structure of the DIG distribution in NGC 4666 is best modeled with two components of ionized gas, a thick and a thin disk with 0.8 kpc and 0.2 kpc scale height, respectively.

sources.summary: CHANG-ES describes NGC 4666 as a superwind starburst with a boxy radio/X-ray halo, AGN fingerprints, filamentary halo structures, advective CR transport, and X-shaped magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: The CHANG-ES radio continuum data of the superwind and starburst galaxy NGC 4666 (Figure  fig:n4666 and ) show a boxy radio halo in total intensity as well as in the X-ray. The wind is likely driven by supernovae across almost the entire disk. For the first time, the central point source was observed at radio wavelengths. In our X-ray data, clear AGN fingerprints were detected, which supports previous findings from X-ray observations that NGC 4666 harbors an AGN.

NGC 4666 is characterized by an extended X-shaped structure with magnetic field vectors reaching far into the halo. The mean disk magnetic field strength is 12.3 G, which is in good agreement with the field strength of other spiral galaxies bec16 . The analysis of the C-band RM-synthesis map along the major axis of NGC 4666 shows indication of one disk magnetic field reversal at a radius of 30" (4 kpc). The field orientation is likely axisymmetric and points inwards and then outwards with increasing radial distance from the centre (please see for more details on the analysis).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4666 as a starburst/AGN galaxy with a prominent wind, extended HI, tidal features toward NGC 4668, and an established radio halo best fit with two components.

quotes.quote: NGC 4666: This galaxy is known to host both a starburst and an AGN Persic2004 . Despite its low inclination, it exhibits a prominent wind, which has been studied by several authors in X-rays Ehle2004 , H _ Voigtlander2013 , and also shows HI emission more extended than the optical disk, with spurs and tidal features which suggest that it is interacting with NGC 4668, located to its southeast Zheng2022 . This galaxy was studied in radio by , who found a halo scale height of 2.16 kpc, almost twice the value found in this work. However, these two results are consistent if we take into account that they consider a distance of 27.5 Mpc, while in this work, we adopted the updated value of 12.82 Mpc.

Using our method, we initially attempted to fit the z profile with a single exponential function, but this resulted in an extremely large disk scale height. One possible explanation is that the relative intensity of the halo emission with respect to the disk emission is so high that it dominates the shape of the profile, making it impossible to separate the disk component. This effect could be amplified by the low inclination of the galaxy, which causes the disk emission to be more widespread. However, since the disk scale height resulting from a single-component fit was excessively large, and the existence of a radio halo produced by star formation activity in this galaxy has been well established Heesen2018,Heesen2019 , we accurately fitted the z profile using a two-component function with a Gaussian fit.

sources.summary: NGC 4666 is used as a contrasting CHANG-ES spiral with a bright, wind-driven radio halo and faster cosmic-ray transport than the diffusion-dominated halo in NGC 4013.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the ratios of the integrated flux densities for the thin disk and the halo of NGC 4666 are 0.35 in C-band and 0.33 in L-band, much smaller than in NGC 4013 and almost independent of frequency. The halo of NGC 4666 contributes about 74 and 75 of the total flux density in C-band and L-band, respectively, while the corresponding values for the halo of NGC 4013 are about 37 and 56 . The contribution of the halo of NGC 4013 becomes dominant at 150 MHz with 77 of the total flux density. NGC 4666 is transporting the CRs much faster into the halo by an advective wind emerging from the entire galaxy. The halo of NGC 4666 is relatively bright and the disk/halo flux density ratio does not vary with frequency.
The values of the last row ( w * z ) show different frequency dependencies for the two galaxies NGC 4013 (diffusion-dominated) and NGC 4666 (wind-dominated).

sources.summary: The paper uses ASASSN-14lp to estimate NGC 4666 distance and discusses why its high inclination complicates Cepheid work but helps TRGB halo measurements.

quotes.quote: Using ASASSN-14lp we derive a distance modulus for NGC 4666 of = 30.8 0.2 corresponding to a distance of 14.7 1.5 Mpc. However, adding ASASSN-14lp to the calibrating sample of Type Ia supernovae still requires an independent distance to the host galaxy.

While there is a Tully-Fisher estimate of the distance (e.g., sorce14, springbob07 ), a better measurement of the distance to NGC 4666 with HST is needed to add ASASSN-14lp to the surprisingly small calibrating sample (currently 9; riess11 and SN 2011fe) of . However, NGC 4666 is highly inclined, so crowding and extinction will make a Cepheid distance determination difficult. Fortunately, the inclination of NGC 4666 is actually an advantage for the tip of the red giant branch method of determining distance because it is easier to probe the halo population with reduced contamination from disk stars madore98 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4666 as a superwind comparison galaxy, noting no LOFAR data, a high-velocity advective wind from high star formation, nearly constant disk-halo flux ratio, and CRE transport dominated by galaxy-wide wind.

quotes.quote: For NGC 4666, no LOFAR data were available.
NGC 4666 is a superwind galaxy with a high-velocity advective wind originating from a high star formation rate across the entire galaxy steinetal2019a .

The slope, alpha CL, is the smallest for NGC 4666, where the absolute flux density ratio between disk and halo stays nearly the same. This is probably caused by the galaxy-wide wind, which is the dominating transport of the CREs. This affects both frequencies in the same way.

sources.summary: The survey paper describes NGC 4666 as an extended, bright, almost edge-on spiral target that dominates the XMM-08 field and causes many spurious detections nearby.

quotes.quote: XMM-08 contains an extended, bright galaxy (NGC 4666) at the center of the image, plus a companion galaxy located South-East of it. The presence of these galaxies leads to a large number of spurious object detections in their surroundings in all bands.

The target of this field was the almost edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 4666 which dominates the center of the field. Although the target was optically and X-ray extended, the target was included in the public survey since the target blocks only about 5 of the field of view. The X-ray image is a combined image of the three EPIC cameras of the single observation of the target, all taken in full frame mode.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4666 has an asymmetric C-band spectral index and matching polarization asymmetry, possibly connected to NGC 4668 or motion through intergalactic gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4666 Despite its small angular size well within the symmetric primary beam, the C-band spectral index displays an asymmetry between the two halves of the disk, such that it is flatter on the south-west side compared to the north-east. This asymmetry corresponds to an asymmetry in the polarization at the same frequency. Interaction with a nearby companion, NGC 4668 (to the south-east) could be a factor, and/or motion through an intergalactic medium. No other technical issues (antenna flexure, solar influence) are problematic.

sources.summary: The study reports that NGC 4666 has starburst UV-halo filaments, multiwavelength extraplanar halo emission, and UV/H I filaments that coincide near the disk.

quotes.quote: Filaments are most easily visible in the FUV, and they are seen in most starburst galaxies (M82, NGC 253, NGC 3079, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, and NGC 5775), as well as two normal galaxies (NGC 4522, NGC 4330). The filaments in the latter two are associated with gas stripped by ram pressure in the Virgo cluster.

The starburst galaxies all have eDIG, soft X-ray halos, extraplanar thermal dust emission (except for NGC 3628), and radio halos. H 1 emission is also seen around all galaxies in the sample with high resolution maps, although in some cases the beamsize makes it unclear how much of the emission is truly extraplanar.

The UV and H 1 filaments do coincide near the disk in the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, NGC 5775, and NGC 3079 (not all shown in Figure figure.multiwave_sb ). The UV halo is also brighter and more vertically extended on the side of the galaxy with the large H 1 filament in NGC 891, and a similar phenomenon is seen in NGC 3044.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4666 as its worked example, measuring H and 1.49 GHz cuts to compare star-forming, cosmic-ray disk, and radio-halo extents.

quotes.quote: In the following subsections we will use data of NGC 4666 as an example to demonstrate these techniques.
The placement of the slice through our H image along the disk plane of NGC 4666 is displayed in Fig. fig:n4666haz0 .

The placement of the cut through the 1.49 GHz disk emission of NGC 4666 is exactly the same as that for the H cut (Fig. fig:n4666haz0 ). The resulting surface brightness profile as a function of galactocentric distance is shown in Fig. fig:n4666z0cut \ as a solid line.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4666 as the nearby spiral host of SN 2019yvr, gives its heliocentric redshift, and adopts a distance based on its earlier Type Ia supernova.

quotes.quote: With J2000 coordinates of R.A. = 12^ h 45^ m 08^ s .135 and Decl. = -00^ 27'32 83 , SN 2019yvr is located within the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4666.

NED reports a heliocentric redshift for the host galaxy NGC 4666 of z = 0.005047 , along with a handful of Tully-Fisher based distances. Fortuitously, NGC 4666 also hosted the Type Ia SN ASASSN-14lp that suggests a distance of 14.7 1.5 Mpc, or = 30.8 0.2 mag 2016ApJ...826..144S .

gmw_00006613.name: Mrk 935
gmw_00006613.overview: Mrk 935 is a galaxy member of the HCG 100 group [arXiv:0805.1709]. It features a striking neutral hydrogen trail extending to the east, which may indicate ongoing stripping as the galaxy falls into the group core [arXiv:0805.1709].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 935 as an HCG 100 member whose eastern H i trail and possible X-ray asymmetry may indicate ongoing stripping during infall.

quotes.quote: In addition, the VLA data reveal a striking H i trail extending to the east away from the group core, protruding from one of the galaxies in the field, Mrk 935. This galaxy is not included in the original hick82 catalogue, but is a group member on the basis of its projected distance from the optical group centre (6.7 arcmin 130 kpc) and small radial velocity difference of v 250 km s ^ 1 relative to the group mean, as listed in NED. The associated H i feature may therefore indicate ongoing stripping as the galaxy falls into the group.
Mrk 935, with the remarkable H i tail extending to the east, also presents evidence of some X-ray asymmetry in this direction, but the S/N is again too low to enable firm conclusions.

gmw_00006614.name: RG
gmw_00006614.overview: RG is a tailed radio galaxy located north of the Abell 3266 cluster core [arXiv:2201.09629, arXiv:2208.00395]. The galaxy features a 630 kpc radio tail that exhibits a steepening spectrum [arXiv:2110.09961, arXiv:2201.09629] and a sharp 35-degree bend [arXiv:2110.09961].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this Abell 3266 galaxy as the optical host of a tailed radio galaxy with a resolved, bent, ribbed radio tail and steepening spectrum.

quotes.quote: The identification of \ as a tailed radio galaxy, however, is indicated by the existence of a radio core and coincident bright galaxy at one end (see right-most inset in Figure radioID ), coupled with the spectral behavior described below. The optical galaxy is WISEA J043045.39 612335.6 (6dFGS g0430454-612336), at a redshift of 0.0626, within the velocity distribution of the Abell 3266 core. The tail is resolved transversely ( 20 ^ wide in the faint regions, with a 7.2 ^ beam), but shows no signs of bifurcating into a twin-tailed structure. It is punctuated by quasi-periodic bright patches, and shows much more extended transverse structures, reaching over an arcminute in extent, which we label ``ribs''. At 2.1 ^ ( 150 kpc) from the core, the tail shows a sharp bend of 35 ^ .

Spectral index variations The overall spectral index behavior, shown in Figure huein , is similar to what is seen in other tailed radio galaxies. The index is 0.6 near the core, steepening to 1.4 at the bend in the tail. Beyond the second bend, there is a dramatic steepening with values ranging from 2 to 3.4 (except for the putative core of the triple, with =-1.4 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes RG2 as a complex Abell 3266 radio galaxy with an uncertain historical interpretation, MeerKAT-confirmed radio-galaxy morphology, and a steep integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: RG2: a complex radio galaxy Source RG2 lies to the NW of the cluster centre. This source was first identified in relatively shallow narrow-band observations 1999Murphy,1999PhDTReid,Murphy2002 . However, the nature of this source has historically remained unclear due to the poor sensitivity and resolution of previous observations. It has previously been hypothesised that this source represents a dying radio galaxy tail, possibly associated with the elliptical galaxy to the immediate SW 1999Murphy or alternatively a radio relic DehghanThesis .

Integrated spectrum and radio luminosity To measure the total integrated flux of RG2, we defined a region corresponding to the 4 contour of our ASKAP map at 15 arcsec resolution. Integrating over this area, we measure a total flux of S_ int, 943 MHz = 235 24 mJy with ASKAP and S_ int, 2.1 GHz = 55 3 mJy with the ATCA. This implies an integrated spectral index _ 943 MHz ^ 2.1 GHz = -1.81 0.14 .

sources.summary: The paper describes RG as a 630 kpc head-tail radio galaxy north of Abell 3266, detected across MWA data with a steepening tail and steep integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Radio galaxy (RG) To the north of the cluster centre, an elongated, 630 kpc radio galaxy is observed, detected completely across the MWA data as well as in SUMSS and the RACS image. This source is also detected in the ATCA data presented by murphy99 , who note a possible optical host at the south-west `head' and a spectral steepening along the `tail'. As seen in fig:a3266:mwa1 , the RG is almost completely detected in the RACS image, except for the steepest-spectrum component at the `tail' end to the NE. Observationally, the source is similar to the large head-tail radio galaxy in Abell 1132 Wilber2018 .
The integrated spectral index of = -1.26 0.22 across the MWA band highlights the contribution from the steep-spectrum components along the `tail' of the source.

gmw_00006615.name: DLA galaxy counterpart
aliases_local: emission counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.583 DLA
gmw_00006615.overview: The DLA galaxy counterpart is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of 2.5832 [arXiv:1806.01715]. It is detected at a projected distance of approximately 16 kiloparsecs from the background quasar Q0918+1636 [arXiv:1806.01715, arXiv:1704.08075] and exhibits strong optical emission lines but no Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1704.08075].

sources.summary: The paper reports no ALMA CO(3-2) detection from the high-metallicity Q0918+1636 DLA galaxy counterpart, while summarizing its impact parameter, emission lines, stellar mass, and star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: We report on the results of a search for CO(3-2) emission from the galaxy counterpart of a high-metallicity Damped Ly Absorber (DLA) at z=2.5832 towards the quasar Q0918+1636. We do not detect CO emission from the previously identified DLA galaxy counterpart. The limit we infer on M_ gas / M_ is in the low end of the range found for DLA galaxies, but is still consistent with what is found for other star-forming galaxies at similar redshifts.

The galaxy counterpart is detected at a projected distance of 1.98 arcsec from the quasar (i.e. 16.2 kpc at z=2.5832 ). The optical emission lines from [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , H and H are seen in the combined X-shooter spectrum, and using emission line diagnostics, hereafter F13 Fynbo13 found a metallicity of 12 + (O/H) = 8.8 0.2 ( [ X/H ] _ em = 0.11 0.20 ), consistent with that inferred in absorption (up to the uncertainties that remain on the absolute calibration of emission line metallicity diagnostics). The difference between the absorption and emission redshift amounts to only 36 20 km s ^ 1 (F13). From an Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) fit to the optical and near-IR photometric data, F13 derived a stellar mass of (M_ /M_ ) = 10.10^ +0.17 _ 0.11 and a star-formation rate (SFR) of 27^ +20 _ 9 M_ yr ^ 1 for the DLA emission counterpart.

sources.original_name: emission counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.583 DLA
sources.summary: The paper discusses the Q 0918+1636 z=2.583 DLA counterpart through emission lines, HST continuum detection, impact parameter, position angle, stellar mass, and extinction-corrected photometry.

quotes.quote: The detection of the emission counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.583 DLA was reported by Fynbo2011 and Fynbo2013 as part of the VLT/X-Shooter campaign. The authors detect emission lines from [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , H and H associated with the DLA at z_ abs = 2.583 and infer a star formation rate of 8  \ based on the observed H line flux. Using HST data, Fynbo2013 detect the continuum emission of the DLA at z_ abs = 2.583 at an impact parameter of 1.98 arcsec from the quasar at a position angle of 115 east of north, and obtain a stellar mass of ( M _ / M_ ) = 10.1_ 0.1 ^ +0.2 . In addition to the re-reduced HST images and their associated magnitudes, we also include the Galactic extinction corrected SDSS u , g , and K _s band observations from the Nordic Optical Telescope NOTCam Abbott2000 reported by Fynbo2013 . For the F606W, F105W and F160W magnitudes we adopt extinction corrections of 0.058, 0.018 and 0.012 mag, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats DLA 0918+1636-2 as a dusty emission counterpart whose high extinction and 16 kpc impact parameter explain why nebular lines are detected but Ly emission is not.

quotes.quote: However, for the DLA 0918+1636-2, the A_V inferred for the emission counterpart (1.54 mag) is much higher than the A_V inferred from the absorber ( 0.2 mag). The impact parameter for DLA 0918+1636-2 is also much larger ( 16 kpc) than for DLA 2222--0946 ( 6 kpc), hence the variations might depend on impact parameter.

Particularly for the DLA 0918+1636-2, Fynbo2013b report detections of [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , H , and H , but no emission from \ is observed. When including the amount of extinction reported by Fynbo2013b in our modelling for this target, the non-detection is fully consistent with our model.

gmw_00006616.name: CO-emitting galaxy
aliases_external: CO-emitting galaxy in the Q0918+1636 DLA field
gmw_00006616.overview: The CO-emitting galaxy J0918+1636 is an intensely star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1806.01715] located at a redshift of approximately 2.58 [arXiv:2009.04001]. It has a high molecular gas mass [arXiv:1806.01715, arXiv:2206.13518] and is likely a companion galaxy within a group at the damped Lyman-alpha absorber redshift [arXiv:2009.04001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a separate intensely star-forming CO(3-2) emitter near the Q0918+1636 sightline, argues it may drive absorption-traced outflows, and derives its high molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: Instead we detect CO(3-2) emission from another intensely star-forming galaxy at an impact parameter of 117 kpc from the line-of-sight to the quasar and 131 km s ^ 1 redshifted relative to the velocity centroid of the DLA in the quasar spectrum. In the velocity profile of the low- and high-ionisation absorption lines of the DLA there is an absorption component consistent with the redshift of this CO-emitting galaxy. It is plausible that this component is physically associated with a strong outflow in the plane of the sky from the CO-emitting galaxy. If true, this would be further evidence, in addition to what is already known from studies of Lyman-break galaxies, that galactic outflows can be traced beyond 100 kpc from star-forming galaxies.

The star-formation rate of this galaxy (see Sect. ssec:sed ) is similar to the galaxy discussed in Neeleman18 , where we assumed an _ CO = 4.3 M_ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 conversion factor and L'_ CO (3-2) / L'_ CO (1-0) = 0.57 . To facilitate comparison, we will use similar values here, although recent work suggest some absorption-selected galaxies might show more starburst-like interstellar medium conditions Klitsch2018 . To account for this uncertainty, the lower uncertainty on the mass includes the assumption of starburst-like conditions. Using these conversion factors then yields a molecular gas mass of M_ mol = (1.8^ +0.2 _ 1.6 ) 10^ 11 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(1-0) from DLA J0918+1636, infers a high molecular gas mass and compact NUV size, and finds thermal excitation of its J=3 and J=4 CO levels.

quotes.quote: We detect CO(1--0) emission from DLA B1228-113 at z 2.1933 and DLA J0918+1636 at z 2.5848 ; these are the first detections of CO(1--0) emission in high- z H i selected galaxies. We obtain high molecular gas masses, M_ mol 10^ 11 ( _ CO /4.36) \ M_ , for the two objects with CO(1--0) detections, which are a factor of 1.5-2 lower than earlier estimates. We determine the excitation of the mid J CO rotational levels relative to the J=1 level, _ J1 , in H i selected galaxies for the first time, obtaining _ 31 =1.00 0.20 and _ 41 =1.03 0.23 for DLA J0918+1636, and _ 31 =0.86 0.21 for DLA B1228-113.

The CO(1--0) line luminosities yield molecular gas masses of (10.0 2.4) ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ (DLA B1228-113), (11.5 2.2) ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ (DLA J0918+1636) and < 5.0 ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ ( 3 limit; DLA B0551-366), where we have assumed = 4.36 \ Bolatto13,Tacconi20 . Both DLA J0918+1636 and DLA B1228-113 are detected in HST rest-frame NUV imaging. The HST images of DLA J0918+1636 are presented and described in Fynbo18 , and yield a half-light radius of 0 30 (Fynbo, private communication).

Remarkably, we find that DLA J0918+1636 has values of _ 31 = 1.00 0.20 and _ 41 = 1.03 0.23 , consistent with thermal excitation of the J= 3, 4 levels. We note that Klitsch22 had earlier measured _ 43 = 1.03 in DLA J0918+1636, suggesting that the J=4 and J=3 levels are likely to show thermal excitation.

sources.summary: The paper says the J0918+1636 CO source has large impact parameter and velocity offset, likely making it a companion in a DLA-redshift galaxy group rather than the closer absorber galaxy.

quotes.quote: Line emission was clearly detected (at > 5 significance) from galaxies in five DLA fields, B0551-366 at z = 1.9622 , J0016-0012 at z=1.9730 , J2225+0527 at z = 2.1310 , B1228-113 at z = 2.1929 , and J0918+1636 at z = 2.5832 , at velocities in good agreement with those of the low-ionization metal absorption lines.

Finally, the CO emission in the field of J0918+1636 has a large impact parameter ( b 100 kpc), a significant velocity offset ( 130 ) between the low-ionization metal absorption lines and the CO emission line, as well as a galaxy at the DLA redshift much closer to the QSO sightline; this indicates that the CO emission here is likely to arise from a companion galaxy within the group of galaxies at the DLA redshift fynbo13,fynbo18 .

In the three cases where the associated galaxy has been confirmed by optical or near-IR spectroscopy (J2206-1958, J2222-0946, and J0918+1636), we did not obtain a detection of CO emission from the galaxy with the optical emission in the case of J0918+1636, we detected a second galaxy at the DLA redshift; fynbo18 .

gmw_00006617.name: KURVS 11
gmw_00006617.overview: KURVS 11 is a large, clumpy, edge-on spiral galaxy that exhibits regular kinematics and a centrally peaked velocity dispersion profile [arXiv:2305.04382]. Its infrared colors indicate the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.12304, arXiv:2305.04382], although its X-ray luminosity remains below standard AGN thresholds [arXiv:2202.12304, arXiv:2305.04382].

sources.summary: The paper flags KURVS 11 as a candidate AGN based on WISE color, gives its X-ray luminosity below the Luo threshold, and retains it in analysis.

quotes.quote: KURVS 11 and KURVS 12 have WISE The All WISE Source Catalog Cutri2013 colour W1 [ 3.6 ] W2 [ 4.5 ] > 0.8, whilst KURVS 12 is also highlighted in the Luo2017 catalogue as an AGN candidate with an intrinsic 0.5 - 7.0 kev luminosity of L_ X,int =5.045 10 ^ 42 ergs ^ 1 . KURVS 11 has an intrinsic 0.5 - 7.0 kev X-ray luminosity of L_ X,int =5.312 10 ^ 41 ergs ^ 1 , but this is below the AGN threshold of the Luo2017 catalogue ( L_ X,int 3 10 ^ 42 ergs ^ 1 ). In the following analysis we highlight KURVS 11 and 12 as candidate AGN in the relevant plots but do not omit them from our analysis.

sources.summary: KURVS-11's infrared colors indicate an AGN, but it lacks highly perturbed kinematics; individual notes describe it as a large clumpy edge-on spiral with regular kinematics and a central dispersion peak.

quotes.quote: Of the 22 sources in the KURVS-CDFS sample, we identify two galaxies (KURVS-11 and KURVS-12) whose infrared colours indicate the presence of an AGN. KURVS-12 also has a X-ray counterpart and an intrinsic 0.5 -- 7.0 kev luminosity of L_ X-ray, int = 5 10^ 42 \ erg \ s ^ 1 in the Luo17 catalogue, which also supports the presence of an AGN. Three other galaxies in the sample have X-ray counterparts but luminosities that lie below the AGN threshold. These objects likely hosting an AGN do not show highly perturbed velocity or velocity dispersion maps, hence we do not exclude them from our analysis.
KURVS-11 or cdfs 30557. This is a large clumpy, edge-on spiral with regular kinematics and centrally-peaked velocity dispersion profile.

gmw_00006618.name: B0008--421
gmw_00006618.overview: B0008--421 is a faint, somewhat diffuse galaxy associated with a bright radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. It is classified as an extreme steep-spectrum source [arXiv:1909.00813] with a noisy optical spectrum that suggests an uncertain redshift of approximately 1.130 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600, arXiv:1909.00813].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B0008--421 with a faint diffuse galaxy, notes its noisy spectrum, and proposes an uncertain redshift from two dubious emission lines.

quotes.quote: B0008--421 : Following unsuccessful attempts by Serego94 , Vries95 and Costa01 , we have now identified the bright radio source B0008--421 with an R =24.3, somewhat diffuse galaxy ( Figure maps ). This magnitude is consistent with previous non-detections (which had detection limits down to magnitude 23). We detect a faint object, 0.2 \ from the VLBI position. The spectrum is consequently faint and very noisy making it difficult to distinguish emission lines from noise ( Figure spec ).

Comparison with the GPS Hubble diagram see Figure Hubble O'Dea96 shows that for an apparent R magnitude 24, the expected redshift is between 1 and 2. We find two dubious emission lines consistent with this range of redshift: [ Ne v ] 3425 and [ O ii ] 3727 at 7294 \ and 7940 , which would correspond to a redshift of z = 1.130.

sources.summary: PKS 0008 42 is an extreme steep-spectrum source targeted with lower-frequency observations to search associated H I absorption despite difficult prior optical spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: This includes PKS 0008 42, an extreme steep spectrum source Callingham2015 , which had a redshift measurement just above z = 1 Labiano2007 .
This source has two observations carried out at a lower frequency to the rest of the sample (see Table tab:obslist ) which enabled a search for associated H i absorption for PKS 0008 42.
Additionally, the optical spectra observed of PKS 0008 42 in was described as noisy, and hence difficult to distinguish emission lines.

gmw_00006619.name: B0316+161
gmw_00006619.overview: B0316+161 is a compact radio galaxy host located at a redshift of z = 0.907 [arXiv:2202.13119, arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. It is classified as a compact steep-spectrum and GHz-peaked spectrum radio source [arXiv:2202.13119], displaying a weak continuum with bright emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper reports CTA 21 as a compact steep-spectrum/GHz-peaked radio source whose observed position changed dramatically, likely because a new compact component altered its brightness distribution.

quotes.quote: CTA 21 is a compact steep-spectrum and GHz-peaked spectrum source at z = 0.907 2007A&A...463...97L very rarely observed in the geodetic VLBI programs. The positional offset was detected due to the two VLBA experiments in 1996 (1996 Jan 2 and 1996 Mar 13). Occasional observations by VLBA and Asia-Oceania VLBI Group for Geodesy and Astrometry (AOV) networks in the 2010s highlighted another dramatic change in its position since the first two epochs 2021RNAAS...5...60F .

On the other hand, a new unresolved bright component appeared 35 mas south-southeast of the brightness peak, i.e. along the same position angle, by 2018. Based on its compactness and variability, it might be considered as a new core candidate. This major change in the source brightness distribution illustrated in Fig. cta21-img could be responsible for the significant offset detected in the astrometric position of CTA 21.

sources.summary: The paper confirms B0316+161/CTA21 as a compact GPS-radio-source host, flags nearby contamination, and measures a redshift of 0.907 from emission lines.

quotes.quote: B0316+161 : Our deep image ( Figure maps ) confirms the earlier identification of this well known GPS radio source (also known as CTA21), by Stanghellini93 . We find a rather compact host, and note that the object seen 8 \ NNW of the CTA21 identification in the Stanghellini et al. image is unrelated. The relative faintness of B0316+161 , and the proximity of bright objects, may be affecting our measured magnitudes. The spectrum shows a weak continuum with bright [ O ii ] and [ O iii ] lines ( Figure maps ). We measure z = 0.907 based on five lines.

gmw_00006620.name: B0407--658
gmw_00006620.overview: B0407--658 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.96 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600, arXiv:2011.12592]. The object is classified as a possible compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:2011.12592] and features extended emission-line gas that spans 25 kiloparsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper treats B0407--658 as a galaxy counterpart with extended emission-line gas, measures z = 0.962, and argues updated radio data make it likely non-GPS.

quotes.quote: B0407--658 : Stickel96 identified the optical counterpart of this radio source as a galaxy. The spectrum ( Figure spec ) shows a faint continuum spectrum where we identify nine emission lines at z = 0.962. The emission line gas is more extended (3.2 , 25 kpc) along the slit than the stellar continuum. We observe no shift in wavelength along the spatial direction in the spectra. Our resolution ( 18 ) yields a velocity resolution limit of 500 km/s for the central part of the spectrum.

Recent radio spectral data (NED) NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database. show no peak around 1 GHz for this source, suggesting a CSS or larger radio source. However, the source is unresolved in ATCA observations resolution 5x3 Morganti93 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS B 0407-658 as a quasar/CSS source at redshift 0.96 and reports a simple Faraday spectrum with one detected thin component.

quotes.quote: PKS B 0407-658 shows a simple Faraday spectrum while PKS B 1934-638 is undetected in polarization. PKS B 1934-638 is a quasar and GPS source at redshift 0.18 while PKS B 0407-658 is a quasar and possible compact steep-spectrum (CSS) source ODea1991,Labiano2006 at a redshift of 0.96.

PKS B 0407-658 : The Faraday spectrum of this source displays a single Faraday-thin component and does not show any other components above the threshold (Figure PKSB0407-rmclean . The lone component is detected at _ = 8.0 0.1 rad m ^ 2 with a peak |F| = 0.15 . More sensitive MeerKAT commissioning observations show that PKS B 0407-658 is polarized at 0.03 - 0.05 levels in the L-band Hugo2018 .

gmw_00006621.name: B0437--454
gmw_00006621.overview: B0437--454 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2111.02082] that exhibits a bright optical continuum without emission or absorption lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. Located at a redshift of 2.017 [arXiv:2111.02082], it displays pronounced radio variability and a marginally peaked radio spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper flags 4FGL J0438.9-4521 as a BL Lac with redshift 2.017 and an unusually low published black-hole mass, arguing C IV absorption may underestimate its mass.

quotes.quote: It is found that there are 2 BL Lacs in red open circles, 4FGL J0407.5+0741 and 4FGL J0438.9-4521, that are marked as `1' and `2' respectively.

4FGL J0438.9-4521 has a redshift of 2.017 and a black hole mass of log (M_ BH /M_ )=7.8 . This black hole mass is relatively small with respect to the average values of the three classes in our sample. We notice that its BH mass was obtained according to the C_ IV ( =1549 \ ) emission line profile.

We believe that the mass of this source could be underestimated due to its lower emission line luminosity of C_ IV . We re-calculate the M_ BH via the method that we have proposed in section mass_formula for 4FGL J0438.9-4521 and obtain a log (M_ BH /M_ )=9.23 . Then the plot is updated with a red dot marked as `2' with an updated BH mass.

sources.summary: The paper reports a featureless bright continuum for B0437--454, notes marginally peaked and variable radio emission, and classifies it as probably a BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: B0437--454 : The spectrum shows a bright continuum but no emission or absorption lines. Updated radio data indicate that the radio spectrum of B0437--454 is only marginally peaked. In addition, pronounced variability was reported: 0.6 Jy vs. 1.4 Jy at 5 GHz e.g., Wright94, Wright90 . Also, given the identification with an optical point source, we conclude that the object is probably a BL Lac object and should be removed from the GPS list.

gmw_00006622.name: B0554--026
gmw_00006622.overview: B0554--026 is a bright, extended galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600] with a redshift of 0.235 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501068] or 0.283 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. Its VLBI components align in an east-west direction without an obvious core, making it a candidate compact symmetric object [arXiv:astro-ph/0501068].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0554 026 as a redshift 0.235 galaxy whose VLBI components align east-west, lack an obvious core, and leave it as a CSO candidate.

quotes.quote: PKS 0554 026 ( NVSS J055652 024105 ) This is a galaxy with m_ V =18.5 and redshift 0.235 (de Vries et al. 2000). Both VLBI images (Fig. 0554S and Fig. 0554X ) presented here show a structure where a series of well resolved components are aligned in the East-West direction. There is no indication of a possible core candidate. Based on these data a proper classification of this source is not possible, although it may be still considered a CSO candidate.

sources.summary: The paper confirms B0554--026 as a galaxy at z = 0.283, describes it as bright and extended, and reports B, V, and R photometry.

quotes.quote: B0554--026 : We confirm the identification of Vries00b ( z =0.283) of this galaxy. We find a rather bright ( B =18.3, V =17.5) and extended source ( 5 ). We obtain R = 16.4 but this band may be affected by calibration problems (see Section subsec:mags ).

gmw_00006623.name: B0904+039
gmw_00006623.overview: B0904+039 is a faint gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600] identified with a slightly extended galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501068] at a redshift of z = 0.830 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. The host galaxy is located within a small group of faint galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0501068, arXiv:astro-ph/0611600] and is considered a compact symmetric object candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0501068].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0904+039 as a slightly extended galaxy in a small group, with resolved VLBI components and a CSO-candidate interpretation.

quotes.quote: PKS 0904+039 ( NVSS J090641+034242 ) The source is identified with a galaxy, m_ I =22.1, which appears slightly extended in an I band image. This object seems to be located in a small group of objects (presumably galaxies, de Vries et al. 2000). In the radio, some extended emission to the South is visible in the NVSS image.

Indeed PKS 0904+039 is slightly resolved in the FIRST survey and with a total flux density exceeding by about 10 the one measured in the NVSS. The VLBI image at 2.3 GHz (Fig. 0904S ) shows a series of resolved components roughly aligned p.a. 140^ with a possible additional weak component to the South. Our image at 8.4 GHz (Fig. 0904X ) detects three blobs in which component A in Fig. 0904S is resolved. None of them is particularly compact enough to be considered as the source core. We can consider this source a CSO candidate.

sources.summary: The paper confirms B0904+039 as a faint GPS host in a group of faint galaxies and measures z = 0.830 from oxygen emission lines.

quotes.quote: B0904+039 : The deep UT1 image ( Figure maps ) reinforces the earlier identification of this GPS radio source with a faint host ( V =24.9) in a group of faint galaxies. The spectrum shows a weak continuum ( Figure spec ). We measure z =0.830 based on the [ O ii ] and [ O iii ] lines.

gmw_00006624.name: PMN J1300--1059
gmw_00006624.overview: PMN J1300--1059 is a candidate gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. It features a relatively bright continuum and a possible broad emission line, which are consistent with a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J1300--1059 as a candidate GPS source with a possible broad emission line and a bright continuum consistent with a QSO.

quotes.quote: PMN J1300--1059 : This source is from a list of candidate GPS sources found by comparing VLA NVSS and WSRT WISH survey data. We find one emission line at 6385 . The emission may consist of a narrow ( 80 ) and a broad ( 200 ) component but the edge of the chip is too close to deblend it accurately.

This line could be Mg ii 2799 at z =1.283, and in that case the [ O ii ] doublet at 3727 is not detected. If it were C iv 1549 , we would expect C iii ] 1909 at 7826 . The relatively bright continuum suggests a QSO, which would be consistent with broad Mg ii .

gmw_00006625.name: B1433--040
gmw_00006625.overview: B1433--040 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.796 [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. Its optical spectrum exhibits a strong continuum with both broad and narrow emission lines, and the source displays variability in its radio flux densities [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper separates B1433--040 from 4C --04.51, describes broad and narrow emission lines, notes radio variability, and identifies it as a quasar at z = 0.796.

quotes.quote: B1433--040 : Vries00b already drew attention to the fact that the GPS source B1433--040 should not be identified with the considerably brighter radio source 4C --04.51 . The optical spectrum shows a very strong continuum with broad ( 200 ) and narrow ( 30 ) emission lines ( Figure spec ). The spectral shape and presence of bright broad lines is consistent with a QSO.

The radio spectrum has a relatively broad peak around 1 GHz e.g., Spoelstra85 . However, more observations, especially at frequencies 1 GHz, are needed. There seems to be some variability in the 408 MHz and 1.4 GHz Large81,Wright90, White92 observed flux densities. Given the unresolved optical host, we suggest that the optical counterpart of B1433--040 is a quasar at z =0.796.

gmw_00006626.name: B1601--222
gmw_00006626.overview: B1601--222 is a radio galaxy with a moderately bright continuum [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. Although it has been observed with a featureless, noisy spectrum, a redshift of z = 0.141 was previously measured based on absorption features [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper reports a noisy featureless spectrum for B1601--222, notes a literature redshift based on absorption features, and explains why its observations do not confirm those features.

quotes.quote: B1601--222 : Featureless, very noisy spectrum with a moderately bright continuum. Snellen02 measure z = 0.141 based on the absorption features: G-band 4300, H 4861, Mg b 5169 and Na D 5899, which correspond to 4900--6800 . We covered the range between 5700 and 9200 and observe no features. However, their spectrum has higher wavelength resolution and longer exposure time (2700 seconds).

gmw_00006627.name: B1648+015
gmw_00006627.overview: B1648+015 is an astronomical source characterized by a featureless, relatively faint continuum [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600]. It has been identified as a quasar, and its radio spectrum variability suggests it is likely either a flat-spectrum quasar or a BL Lac object [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600].

sources.summary: The paper finds B1648+015 featureless, cites its quasar identification, and uses radio variability to suggest it is a flat-spectrum quasar or BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: B1648+015 : Featureless with a not very bright continuum. Stickel96 identifies this source as a quasar. The radio spectrum shows variability so it is probably either a flat spectrum quasar or a BL Lac object.

gmw_00006628.name: Arp's Loop
gmw_00006628.overview: Arp's Loop is an outlying neutral hydrogen concentration that may be a tidal dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1507.04889]. It contains young stars with ages of 30 to 160 Myr and has a clear counterpart in red giant branch stellar maps [arXiv:1507.04889].

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp's Loop as an outlying H I concentration and possible tidal dwarf, with young stars, a possible RGB counterpart, and star formation synchronized with other tidal features.

quotes.quote: Deep photometry from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) has been used to argue that the outlying HI concentrations Arp's Loop (AL), and Holmberg IX (HoIX) may be tidal dwarf galaxies formed as a result of these interactions Makarova2002AA, deMello2008AJ, Sabbi2008ApJ . The young MSs are mainly concentrated in the spiral arms of M81, at the north-west side (hereafter NW-arm), AL, HoIX, BK3N, the Garland, and SE-stream. However, the fainter substructures -- SE-stream, M82-arc, some clumps around HoIX and AL, and BK3N -- can not be seen in this map due to the shorter lifetime and the lower number of RSGs compared to MSs.

Bright stars are mainly located in the inner disk of M81, while most of young stars in AL, NW-arm, BK3N, Garland, and other debris features are fainter than 24 and have similar luminosity distributions to the SE-stream, implying ages of 30-160 Myr old. Of these concentrations, only AL appears to have a clear counterpart in the RGB map.

gmw_00006629.name: host galaxy of iPTF 16hgs
gmw_00006629.overview: The host galaxy of iPTF 16hgs is a low-metallicity, star-forming dwarf spiral galaxy [arXiv:1806.10623]. It contains a mixture of both young and old stellar populations and exhibits multiple blobs of active star formation [arXiv:1806.10623].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the iPTF 16hgs host as a low-metallicity, star-forming dwarf spiral with young and old populations and multiple prominent star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Second, with Integral Field Unit observations of the host galaxy, we find that iPTF 16hgs occurred in the outskirts (projected offset of 6 kpc 1.9 R _ eff ) of a low metallicity ( 0.4 Z _ ), star forming, dwarf spiral galaxy.
Taken together, we conclude that the host galaxy of iPTF 16hgs is a metal-poor, star forming dwarf galaxy with a mixture of both young and old stellar populations.
As shown, the host galaxy of iPTF 16hgs exhibits multiple prominent blobs of star formation, reaching surface densities 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 .

gmw_00006630.name: Galaxy D36123
gmw_00006630.overview: Galaxy D36123 is a high-redshift quiescent galaxy with a redshift between 1 and 2 [arXiv:2403.07414]. Its spectrum shows a steep radial surface brightness profile and contains numerous near-infrared absorption features attributed to TP-AGB stars [arXiv:2403.07414].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy D36123 as the primary high-redshift quiescent target, with an exceptionally high-SNR PRISM spectrum showing NIR absorption features attributed to TP-AGB stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We retained three galaxies, IDs = 8595, 9025, and D36123 with redshifts at 1<z<2 , whose spectra clearly show the D4000 break, no obvious emission lines, and also have relatively high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). This is a small yet representative sample fulfilling these criteria. The spectrum of D36123 Galaxy D36123, the primary target in this work, exhibits an exceptionally high-quality PRISM spectrum with an average of SNR 187 per spectral pixel (Fig. fig01 ; plotted as a double histogram to show the 1 noise). Its relatively bright flux (mag _ AB =21.4 at 3.6 m), steep radial surface brightness profile (S e rsic n 4, see Table tab3 ), longer exposure time and accurate centering into the micro-shutters, which minimizes the effect of path loss and aperture correction (see Methods), are the reasons for the remarkable quality of this spectrum. A large number of features are clearly detected at rest-frame wavelengths from 0.3 to 2.0 m, for which we propose identifications (see labels in Fig. fig01 ).

Besides common absorption features at _ rest <5500 \ typical of quiescent galaxies, such as Ca H+K, H , H +G4300 and absorption from heavy elements (such as Mg, Ca, and Fe), which have been observed in quiescent galaxies' spectra at high redshift , the rest-frame NIR spectrum exhibits numerous absorption features and band edges. The relatively low spectral resolution (R 30--300) of the NIRSpec PRISM helps in the detection of these broad features. The proposed identifications are obtained by comparing our spectrum with empirical libraries of C-rich and O-rich TP-AGB stars (see Fig. fig02 ).

gmw_00006631.overview: The object 8595 is a faint quiescent galaxy with an apparent magnitude of 24 at 3.6 microns [arXiv:2403.07414]. Its spectrum reveals individual absorption features, including titanium oxide absorptions, cyanogen spectral edges, and tentatively carbon dimer absorption [arXiv:2403.07414].

sources.summary: The paper treats 8595 as a fainter quiescent galaxy whose PRISM spectrum still reveals absorption features and whose spectral fits favor TP-AGB-mild M13 models.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the other two fainter quiescent galaxies, 8595 (mag _ AB =24 at 3.6 m, observed with the PRISM) and 9025 (mag _ AB =23 at 3.6 m, observed with the medium-resolution gratings but rebinned to the PRISM resolution), have substantially lower SNR of 15 and 7. Nevertheless, the SNR is still sufficient for the direct detection of individual absorption features in object 8595 (Fig. fig04 ), some of the strongest also observed in object 9025 (Fig. fig05 ). This includes some TiO absorptions and CN spectral edges, and tentatively a C _2 absorption.
