Table tab2 presents the results of the best spectral fits of objects 8595 and 9025, based on the different models. For object 8595, we carefully account for a neighbouring bright galaxy when measuring its photometry to estimate spectral aperture corrections, following a similar approach to what is done for object D36123 (see Methods). Again, M13 yields the best-fit spectral fits for both galaxies, with ^2_R 0.8 for object 8595, and ^2_R 1.7 for object 9025. The derived stellar masses differ at most by 0.16 dex between the M13 and BC03 models, but the best-fit ages are even more strongly dependent on the specific model used (stellar age differences from 0.1 to 1 Gyr). In Table tab2 , the values ^2 denote the ^2 difference with respect to the M13 model. The small cumulative probability P( ^2) suggests the preference for the M13 model is statistically significant at the ( >99.9 confidence level) in the overall spectral fit for these two galaxies.

gmw_00006632.overview: 9025 is a faint quiescent galaxy with a magnitude of 23 at 3.6 microns [arXiv:2403.07414]. Its spectrum shows detectable absorption features, including strong titanium oxide absorptions and carbon-nitrogen spectral edges [arXiv:2403.07414].

sources.summary: The paper describes 9025 as a fainter quiescent galaxy observed with medium-resolution NIRSpec gratings, with detectable absorption features and spectral fits favoring TP-AGB-mild models.

quotes.quote: The spectra of the other two fainter quiescent galaxies, 8595 (mag _ AB =24 at 3.6 m, observed with the PRISM) and 9025 (mag _ AB =23 at 3.6 m, observed with the medium-resolution gratings but rebinned to the PRISM resolution), have substantially lower SNR of 15 and 7. Nevertheless, the SNR is still sufficient for the direct detection of individual absorption features in object 8595 (Fig. fig04 ), some of the strongest also observed in object 9025 (Fig. fig05 ). This includes some TiO absorptions and CN spectral edges, and tentatively a C _2 absorption.

Table tab2 presents the results of the best spectral fits of objects 8595 and 9025, based on the different models. For object 8595, we carefully account for a neighbouring bright galaxy when measuring its photometry to estimate spectral aperture corrections, following a similar approach to what is done for object D36123 (see Methods). Again, M13 yields the best-fit spectral fits for both galaxies, with ^2_R 0.8 for object 8595, and ^2_R 1.7 for object 9025. The derived stellar masses differ at most by 0.16 dex between the M13 and BC03 models, but the best-fit ages are even more strongly dependent on the specific model used (stellar age differences from 0.1 to 1 Gyr). In Table tab2 , the values ^2 denote the ^2 difference with respect to the M13 model. The small cumulative probability P( ^2) suggests the preference for the M13 model is statistically significant at the ( >99.9 confidence level) in the overall spectral fit for these two galaxies.

gmw_00006633.name: ESO 462-16
gmw_00006633.overview: ESO 462-16 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1603.03868] classified as SA(s)cd [arXiv:1907.06753]. It belongs to a pair of binary galaxies [arXiv:1603.03868] and hosted the supernova SN 2012dn [arXiv:1603.03868, arXiv:1907.06753].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 462-16 as SN 2012dn's spiral host, notes its binary-galaxy setting, estimates the host mass, and derives local host-environment metallicity from nearby H II regions.

quotes.quote: SN 2012dn is located 35'' west and 3'' south of the purportedly normal spiral (SAcd) galaxy, ESO 462-16 (= PGC 64605; Bock12 ), which belongs to a pair of binary galaxies Soares95 . To estimate the host galaxy mass of SN 2012dn and hosts of other SN Ia ( 6.2.2), we implement the empirical relation of Taylor11 : logM/M_ = 0.70(g-i) - 0.40M_ I, AB 0.68 and values obtained from NED and LEDA.

Following the same procedure for the host of SN 2012dn, and taking the above discrepancy for M101 as a conservative error estimate, we obtain a broad window ( 2.2) for all points in Figure Fig:logM below; i.e. approximately two orders of magnitude too broad to draw association with a particular progenitor environment or scenario for the present study. We estimate the metallicity of SN 2012dn's progenitor from optical spectroscopy of the host environment. The SN flux dominates the host galaxy emission lines at the precise location of the explosion site, so we instead extract spectra from three nearby H II regions which intersected our spectroscopic slit.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 462-016 as the spiral host of SN 2012dn and adopts a distance from Tully-Fisher and kinematic estimates for the supernova analysis.

quotes.quote: SN 2012dn was discovered on UT 2012 July 08.52 at = 20^ h 23^ m 36 26 and = -28 16 43 4 (J2000) in the SA(s)cd spiral galaxy ESO 462-016 bock2012a .

The distance of ESO 462-016 is not constrained by Cepheid measurements. A recent Tully-Fisher measurement by lagattuta2013a yielded a distance of 43.7 13.5 Mpc ( = 33.19 0.69 mag). This is in good agreement with kinematic distances mould2000a of 43.9 4.2 Mpc ( = 33.21 0.21 mag; corrected for infall onto the Virgo cluster) or 47.5 4.2 Mpc ( = 33.38 0.20 mag; corrected for infall onto the Virgo cluster, the Great Attractor and the Shapley supercluster). All these numbers have been converted assuming H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , and the uncertainties in the kinematic distances have been computed assuming a peculiar velocity up to 300 km s ^ 1 for ESO 462-016.

gmw_00006634.name: AX J0140.1+0629
gmw_00006634.overview: AX J0140.1+0629 is a type I active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.50 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029]. It is classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy or a low-luminosity type I quasar that exhibits heavy X-ray absorption despite having an unobscured optical broad-line region [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AX J0140.1+0629 as a hard, X-ray absorbed type I AGN at z=0.50, with broad optical lines and QSO-like X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: AX J0140.1+0629 (S1): Chandra reveals an object with a very hard spectrum, with 43 counts detected in the hard band (2-10 keV) but only 7 in the soft (0.5-2 keV). The equivalent spectrum is inverted, with =-1 , strongly implying heavy X-ray absorption of 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . Despite this, it has the optical spectrum of a type I AGN, a very broad H line (FWHM 9000 km s ^ 1 ). Clearly then, the optical broad line region of this object is not obscured, while the X-rays are. It is at z=0.50 and the 2-10 keV luminosity is 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , classifying this as a Seyfert 1, or low luminosity type I QSO.

gmw_00006635.name: AX J0836.2+5538
gmw_00006635.overview: AX J0836.2+5538 is an optically faint broad-line quasar located at a redshift of z=1.29 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029]. It exhibits a high X-ray luminosity of approximately 10^45 erg s^-1 and shows evidence of X-ray obscuration [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AX J0836.2+5538 as an optically faint broad-line QSO at z=1.29 with high X-ray luminosity and likely X-ray obscuration.

quotes.quote: AX J0836.2+5538 (S2): This object is quite faint optically ( R=20.2 ) but it shows clear evidence of a broad emission line (8000 km s ^ 1 FWHM) which we identify as Mg II at z=1.29. It is therefore a QSO with an X-ray luminosity of approximately 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . Given this is a type I AGN that is very bright in the X-ray band, perhaps the most interesting thing about this source is its relative optical faintness.
S2, also a broad line QSO, must also be obscured, based both on its Chandra colour and the fact it was observed but not detected by ROSAT.

gmw_00006636.name: AX J0836.6+5529
gmw_00006636.overview: AX J0836.6+5529 is a standard quasar located at a redshift of approximately z=0.98 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029]. It is associated with a faint X-ray source and a faint optical counterpart that exhibits a broad emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029].

sources.summary: The paper treats AX J0836.6+5529 as an ambiguous but likely QSO counterpart, noting its faint Chandra source, faint optical counterpart, broad line, and z=0.98 identification.

quotes.quote: For S3 one object was detected at 2-10 keV, but none was detected in the 5-10 keV image. We assign the 2-10 keV source as the counterpart but note that this object has a flux well below the original ASCA detection threshold. We discuss this further below, but note that the lack of a clear bright, hard counterpart does leave some ambiguity about the identification in this case.

AX J0836.6+5529 (S3): We have discussed above the relative faintness of the Chandra sources in the error cirlce of this SHEEP source. The optical counterpart of the most likely Chandra object is the faintest of all we attempted spectroscopically (R=20.8). The signal-to-noise ratio of the spectrum is poor, but there does seem to be a broad emission line ( 6000 km s ^ 1 ), which we again tentatively identify as Mg ii , this time at z=0.98. This object is therefore also most likely to be a standard QSO.

gmw_00006637.name: AX J1035.1+3938
gmw_00006637.overview: AX J1035.1+3938 is a resolved, hard X-ray source located at a redshift of z=0.11 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029]. Its optical emission line ratios place the object on the dividing line between active galactic nuclei and H II galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029].

sources.summary: The paper describes AX J1035.1+3938 as a resolved, hard X-ray source at z=0.11 whose optical line ratios place it between AGN and H II galaxies.

quotes.quote: Only S4 was resolved. For S4, the extension is at the 10-12'' level and we extracted the counts from a 10'' radius aperture.

AX J1035.1+3938 (S4): The hardness ratio of this object is similar to that of S1, again implying a heavy absorption. The optical spectrum shows several narrow lines including H , H and [ O iii ] 5007 at z=0.11 . It has an X-ray luminosity of L_ X = 4 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . In order to classify this object optically we have measured several line ratios, including [ O III ] 5007/H = 2, [ N II ] 6583/H = 0.5, and ( [ S II ] 6716 + 6731)/H = 0.5. According to the diagnostic diagrams of Veilleux Osterbrock (1987) this places the object roughly on the dividing line between AGN and H ii galaxies.

gmw_00006638.name: AX J1153.7+4619
gmw_00006638.overview: AX J1153.7+4619 is a faint active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.59 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029]. It is classified as a type II quasar with strong, narrow optical emission lines and unexpectedly soft X-ray colors [arXiv:astro-ph/0311029].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AX J1153.7+4619 as a faint, narrow-line type II AGN at z=0.59 with type II QSO X-ray luminosity and unexpectedly soft X-ray colors.

quotes.quote: AX J1153.7+4619 (S5): This is the only object discussed in the current paper with a ROSAT counterpart, and therefore without a Chandra observation. The optical counterpart is again quite faint, with R=20.6 , but shows strong, narrow O ii , O iii and H emission lines characteristic of a type II AGN at z=0.59. The inferred X-ray luminosty is 7 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the 2-10 keV band making it a type II QSO. Most interestingly its X-ray colors (from ASCA: paper I) show an effective 1.7 , nowhere near as hard as those of the type I AGN S1 or the composite galaxy S4.

gmw_00006639.name: Yagi 090
gmw_00006639.overview: Yagi 090 is a ultra-diffuse galaxy [arXiv:1803.06298] that exhibits an extended star-formation history [arXiv:1803.06298]. This system displays a secondary peak in its stellar mass fraction at ages of 8 to 9 Gyr [arXiv:1803.06298] and has a recovered maximum rotation velocity of 124.4 km/s [arXiv:1803.06298].

sources.summary: The paper includes Yagi 090 as a previously identified UDG with an extended star-formation history, a secondary mass-fraction peak, and a high rotation upper limit.

quotes.quote: Three of these systems (DF26, Yagi 090, and Yagi 418) were previously identified as UDGs and their structural parameters determined 2015ApJ...798L..45V, 2016ApJS..225...11Y .

For instance, while DF26, OGS1 and OGS2 best examplify the described behaviour, Yagi 418 and Yagi 090 display more extended SFHs as well as an apparent secondary peak in the mass fraction at ages 8-9 Gyr, probably a consequence of a delayed formation. In addition, the rate and time-scale at which the mass fraction decreases with time as well as the period of star forming inactivity slightly differ from galaxy to galaxy.
The values of V_ Max that we recovered for DF26, Yagi 090, Yagi 418, OGS1, and OGS2 are 66.5, 124.4, 80.5, 50.3, and 45.9 km/s, respectively.

gmw_00006640.name: OGS1
gmw_00006640.overview: OGS1 is a ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:1803.06298]. It is characterized as one of the smallest ultra-diffuse galaxies in the sample, showing almost no star formation after its first 6 Gyr [arXiv:1803.06298].

sources.summary: The paper newly characterizes OGS1 as an OSIRIS-field visual candidate and clear Coma UDG, with high metallicity for its mass and little star formation after the first 6 Gyr.

quotes.quote: The other two (OGS1 and OGS2) were visual candidates within the observed OSIRIS field not previously characterised. For the latter, we determined their morphological parameters from deep photometric follow-up observations with the Optical Ground Station (OGS) telescope in the Iza na Observatory, Tenerife, Spain.

We confirm Coma membership for all of them and find that 4 (DF26, Yagi 090, Yagi 418 and OGS1) are clear examples of UDGs (using the 2015ApJ...798L..45V size criterion), whereas OGS2 might be better considered an extended, low-surface brightness dwarf galaxy.
While a clear dependence of SFH with size (R _ eff ) does not appear, it is intriguing to note that one of the smallest UDGs (OGS1) has almost no star formation after the first 6 Gyr.

gmw_00006641.name: OGS2
gmw_00006641.overview: OGS2 is a member of the Coma galaxy cluster [arXiv:1803.06298]. It is characterized as an extended, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy with a recovered maximum circular velocity of 45.9 km/s [arXiv:1803.06298].

sources.summary: The paper newly characterizes OGS2 as an OSIRIS-field visual candidate and Coma member better classed as an extended low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: The other two (OGS1 and OGS2) were visual candidates within the observed OSIRIS field not previously characterised. For the latter, we determined their morphological parameters from deep photometric follow-up observations with the Optical Ground Station (OGS) telescope in the Iza na Observatory, Tenerife, Spain.

We confirm Coma membership for all of them and find that 4 (DF26, Yagi 090, Yagi 418 and OGS1) are clear examples of UDGs (using the 2015ApJ...798L..45V size criterion), whereas OGS2 might be better considered an extended, low-surface brightness dwarf galaxy.
The values of V_ Max that we recovered for DF26, Yagi 090, Yagi 418, OGS1, and OGS2 are 66.5, 124.4, 80.5, 50.3, and 45.9 km/s, respectively.

gmw_00006642.name: H1419+480
gmw_00006642.overview: H1419+480 is a bright Seyfert 1 active galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.072 [arXiv:astro-ph/0304210]. Its spectrum reveals a complex, variable warm absorber containing OVII and OVIII ions, which is linked to a variable ultraviolet outflow [arXiv:astro-ph/0304210].

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton observations of the Seyfert 1 galaxy H1419+480, finding variable soft X-ray flux, an OVII/OVIII warm absorber, and plausibly related variable UV CIV absorption.

quotes.quote: The bright Seyfert 1 galaxy H1419+480 ( z 0.072 ), whose X-ray colours from earlier HEAO-1 and ROSAT missions suggested a complex X-ray spectrum, has been observed with XMM-Newton . The EPIC spectrum above 2 keV is well fit by a power-law with photon index =1.84 0.01 and an Fe K line of equivalent width 250  eV. At softer energies, a decrement with respect to this model extending from 0.5 to 1 keV is clearly detected. After trying a number of models, we find that the best fit corresponds to OVII absorption at the emission redshift, plus a 2 detection of OVIII absorption.

An IUE observation of H1419+480 conducted in 1982 shows 3 detection for a CIV associated absorber, outflowing with a velocity 1800 90 km s ^ 1 . This confirms the close link between X-ray ionized absorbers and UV associated absorption reported, among others, by Crenshaw et al (1999). This absorption line was not present in an IUE observation obtained roughly 1 year later. We find that a plausible common origin for all these variable absorption phenomena can be understood in terms of the same (or similar) amount of photoionised gas, but with varying ionisation state.

gmw_00006643.name: NGC 4632
gmw_00006643.overview: NGC 4632 is a spiral galaxy situated in a group environment alongside NGC 4666, NGC 4668, and two dwarf galaxies [arXiv:2203.00694]. The galaxy features a northern CO spiral arm that may indicate a past tidal interaction [arXiv:2203.00694], as well as a major component of anomalous HI gas structured as a misaligned polar ring [arXiv:2309.05841].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4632 as a potential HI polar ring galaxy whose anomalous gas is misaligned with the stellar disk, ring-like in position-velocity space, and a major HI component.

quotes.quote: A visual inspection of PDR1 source datacubes revealed two detections with ring-like \ components that appear to have a very different geometry from that of the inner \ disk: NGC 4632 (WALLABY J124232-000459), and NGC 6156 (WALLABY J163452-603705). \ and optical imaging of these systems black are presented in Figures  Fig:NGC4632_HSCOverlay - Fig:NGC6156_DECamOverlay , and their basic properties are listed in Table  tab:GalaxyCharacteristics . The anomalous \ components in both systems are separable from that of the main disks in position-velocity space.

In both the 3D projection and the moment 0 map, the main body of NGC 4632 appears as a cigar-shaped structure in position-velocity space, while the anomalous gas appears as a tilted ring with a black significantly different orientation than that of the main body. The ring is less distinct from the main body in the moment 1 map and velocity profile given its sky projection, an issue to which we return in Section  Sec:Detectability .

We find a ratio of main body to anomalous gas mass of M_ anomalous /M_ body 0.9 , which may be overestimated due to the tightness of the mask adopted, but the anomalous gas is clearly a significant contribution to the total \ content of NGC 4632.

sources.summary: NGC 4632 is excluded from the final sample because its group environment, prominent northern CO spiral arm, and possible past interaction could affect its kinematics and dark-matter halo.

quotes.quote: Finally, we exclude NGC 4632. This galaxy is in a group with NGC 4666 (a large starburst galaxy), NGC 4668, and two other smaller dwarf galaxies garcia93,walter04 , though these ALMA observations only cover NGC 4632. The CO in NGC 4632 follows the spiral arms, with the northern spiral arm being much more prominent. A single prominent spiral arm can be a sign of a past interaction e.g., oh08_tidal , which is possible for this galaxy given its known group membership. We therefore exclude this galaxy from the sample as the presence of other close, large galaxies and the hints of tidal interaction may affect the kinematics and dark matter halo of this system.

gmw_00006644.name: NGC 6156
gmw_00006644.overview: NGC 6156 is a luminous infrared galaxy located in the Norma cluster [arXiv:2309.05841]. It is classified as an SAB(rs) pec galaxy and contains an anomalous neutral hydrogen component that is oriented roughly perpendicular to its main body [arXiv:2309.05841].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6156 as a potential HI polar ring galaxy with anomalous gas roughly perpendicular to the main body, separated in velocity space, and contributing much of its HI mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 6156 black The second potential \ PRG, NGC 6156, black has been classified as an SAB(rs) pec deVaucouleurs1991 , but it is important to note that it is located in the Norma cluster near the zone-of-avoidance. Despite the high extinction towards this galaxy, NGC 6156 has been detected in IRAS black Sanders2003 with a bright IR flux and is therefore a Luminous Infra-Red Galaxy (LIRG). Figure Fig:NGC6156_WallabyOverview shows an overview of the WALLABY observations of NGC 6156.

In contrast to NGC 4632 in Figure  Fig:NGC4632_WallabyOverview , the moment 0 map of the full \ emission for this galaxy shows little sign of anomalous gas, whereas the 3D projection and moment 1 map show an anomalous component roughly perpendicular to the main body in position-velocity space with a bipolar velocity structure. This highlights the importance of examining the full kinematics of the gas distribution to identify anomalous components, which we discuss in the context of polar ring detectability in Section  Sec:Detectability . As with NGC 4632, we use iDaVIE to separate out the main body from the anomalous gas in NGC 6156, and these components are shown in the black second and third columns of Figure  Fig:NGC6156_WallabyOverview respectively.
There is a projected spatial overlap between the outer main body and inner anomalous \ in NGC 6156, but the anomalous gas is separated from the main body in velocity space.

gmw_00006645.name: F615-1
gmw_00006645.overview: F615-1 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that lacks current star formation based on its lack of H-alpha emission [arXiv:2012.09911]. It features unusually wide red giant branch and asymptotic giant branch sequences, which suggest multiple historical star-formation episodes [arXiv:2012.09911].

sources.summary: The paper describes F615-1 as a gas-rich LSB dwarf lacking current H-alpha emission but showing redder colors, wide RGB/AGB sequences, and evidence for multiple star-formation episodes.

quotes.quote: We present new HST WFC3 near-IR observations of the CMD's in two LSB galaxies, F575-3 and F615-1, notable for having no current star formation based on a lack of H emission. F615-1 has unusually wide RGB and AGB sequences suggesting multiple episodes of star formation from metal-poor gas, possibly infalling material. The two galaxies chosen for near-IR HST imaging are F575-3 (also known as KDG215 and D575-3) and F615-1 (also known as KDG023).

F615-1, in contrast, is deficient in bHeB stars with a weak bMS population and few young AGB's. F615-1 also has unusually wide RGB and AGB branches. F615-1 is lower in mean surface brightness, but more extended in size.

F615-1 has a intermediate color RGB and a strong AGB population to match its early-type optical and near-IR colors. F615-1 also compares well with dwarfs that display older population features, but is unusual in having much wider RGB and AGB sequences and a detectable bMS population.

gmw_00006646.name: NGC 7710
gmw_00006646.overview: NGC 7710 is a galaxy featuring a thick spheroid and a thin, nearly edge-on disk with an associated dust lane [arXiv:2005.02355]. Its atomic hydrogen gas displays a double-horned profile and a mildly asymmetric distribution that rotates in the same direction as its ionized gas [arXiv:2005.02355].

sources.summary: The paper detects HI in NGC 7710, describing a double-horned profile, a mildly asymmetric disk, and likely association with the thin blue stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 7710 shows a modestly asymmetric \ disc, and the atomic gas in PGC 056772 is centrally peaked but too poorly resolved to identify the direction of rotation. NGC 7710 and PGC 056772 NGC 7710 is unusual amongst the 2 galaxies in that its optical image (Figure 7710m0 ) clearly shows multiple components: a thick spheroid and a very thin, nearly edge-on disc with an associated dust lane. \ emission in NGC 7710 shows the classic double-horned profile typical of galactic discs (Figure 7710spec ).

The gas distribution and kinematics (Figures 7710m0 and 7710vels ) show little sign of recent disturbance, though there is a mild asymmetry with approximately 30 more \ on the receding (southeast) side of the galaxy than the approaching side, as that's where the dust and the blue stellar populations are found. The \ rotates in the same direction as the ionized gas.

gmw_00006647.name: PGC 056772
gmw_00006647.overview: PGC 056772 is a galaxy with an unremarkable optical appearance and multiple stellar components that are most apparent in its stellar kinematics [arXiv:2005.02355]. It exhibits faint, centrally peaked atomic gas emission and a possible extraplanar tail extending to the southeast [arXiv:2005.02355].

sources.summary: The paper reports faint, centrally peaked HI emission in PGC 056772, noting an unremarkable optical image, unresolved kinematics, and a possible extraplanar tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 7710 shows a modestly asymmetric \ disc, and the atomic gas in PGC 056772 is centrally peaked but too poorly resolved to identify the direction of rotation. PGC 056772, like most of the 2 galaxies, has a rather unremarkable optical image (Figure pgc056m0 ) and its multiple stellar components are much more obvious in the stellar kinematics davor than in the optical surface brightness distribution. It also shows very faint \ emission (Figures pgc056m0 and pgc056spec ).

The galaxy has a 1.4 GHz continuum source with a flux density of 4.7 0.4 mJy, so the coincidence of the line source and the continuum source boosts the reliability of the line detection. The \ emission is not well resolved, so we have little kinematic information (Figure pgc056chans ), and it is not yet possible to associate the \ emission with a specific stellar component. There is a hint of an extraplanar \ tail, stretching to the southeast, but it should be verified with data of higher sensitivity and resolution.

gmw_00006648.name: IC 0718
gmw_00006648.overview: IC 0718 is a late-type companion galaxy that shows clear signs of a recent interaction [arXiv:2005.02355]. It features an asymmetric stellar distribution with a northern arm and a southern tail, as well as a strongly asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:2005.02355].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 0718 as IC 0719's interacting companion and likely gas donor, citing its asymmetric stellar and HI distributions, tidal tail, and low HI content.

quotes.quote: IC 0718 is not a member of the \ sample and is not known to be a 2 galaxy, but it is detected in \ emission in our data and we discuss it in the context of a probable interaction with IC 0719. IC 0718, the companion, is a late-type galaxy and it also shows signs of an interaction. Its stellar distribution is asymmetric, with a relatively bright arm extending to the north and a stubby, faint tail extending to the south (Figures both and ic0718 ).

The \ spectrum is also strongly asymmetric (Figure spec ), suggesting the \ distribution is far from a relaxed equilibrium state; the brighter \ emission is on the south side of the galaxy, coinciding with the low surface brightness stellar tail. These stellar and \ asymmetries demonstrate that the galaxy has been involved in a recent interaction and therefore it is most likely the source of the \ recently accreted onto IC 0719. An hypothesized gas transfer from IC 0718 to IC 0719 suggests that IC 0718 should be deficient for its stellar properties.

gmw_00006649.name: IC 4566
gmw_00006649.overview: The galaxy IC 4566 features a central spectrum that can mimic the classification of an active galactic nucleus host on BPT diagnostic diagrams [arXiv:2001.00099]. However, its central ionization is actually dominated by hot low-mass evolved stars, which provide a hard ionizing field with a low equivalent width [arXiv:2001.00099].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 4566 as an example where BPT classification could mimic an AGN host, but low equivalent width indicates central ionization dominated by HOLMES.

quotes.quote: An example of this cut effect could be seen in fig:examples where the galaxy IC 4566 (upper-right triad of panels) and NGC 1667 (lower-left triad of panels) are very similar regarding RGB images and BPT planes and both could be classified by AGN hosts if only the BPT plane of the central spectrum was considered. However, IC 4566 has the central ionization dominated by HOLMES, i.e., a hard ionizing field with low- , 1.8 . On the other hand, NGC 1667 has 11.4 , showing a signature of a strong AGN.

gmw_00006650.name: J0841
gmw_00006650.overview: J0841 is a double-cored quasar system located at a redshift of z > 2 [arXiv:2105.03298]. Spatially resolved spectroscopy reveals two cores with similar spectra, which may represent either a physical pair or a gravitationally lensed quasar [arXiv:2105.03298].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0841 as a z>2 double quasar, spatially resolved by HST and Gemini, with similar spectra and an unresolved lensing-versus-physical-pair interpretation.

quotes.quote: To further illuminate the nature of the double quasars discovered by Gaia astrometry, we acquired optical slit spectroscopy for one of the double-cored targets (J084129.77+482548.5, hereafter J0841) with Gemini under excellent seeing conditions. The system is spatially resolved in the slit spectroscopy (see Methods and Extended Data Figure 6) at the locations of the two HST cores. The two spatially-resolved spectra are presented in Figure 2, which are rather similar quasar spectra.

While there are spectral differences in the two cores of J0841 (see Methods and Extended Data Figure 7), notably in the strengths of the broad emission lines, the possibility of lensing cannot be conclusively ruled out. We thus model the system as a gravitationally lensed quasar, where the lens galaxy is undetected in HST imaging (see Methods). There are only three other gravitationally lensed double quasars with <0 8 separations known at z>2.5 [ ref. 22-24 ] , all of which were serendipitously discovered, unlike J0841 which is from a targeted search.

gmw_00006651.name: NGC 4809
gmw_00006651.overview: NGC 4809 is a dwarf galaxy currently merging with NGC 4810 [arXiv:2401.16172]. The galaxy features an ionized-gas velocity gradient [arXiv:2308.01128], older stellar ages than its companion [arXiv:2308.01128], and localized metal enrichment from the Type Ic supernova SN 2011jm [arXiv:2401.16172].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4809 has a clear ionized-gas velocity gradient, older stellar ages than NGC 4810, and likely earlier interaction-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: As seen in these maps, the ionized gas displays an obvious northeast-southwest velocity gradient in NGC 4809, while it is approximately oriented along the northwest-southeast direction in NGC 4810. We observe that the ionized gas in NGC 4809 is clearly separated into different velocity ranges, while it appears to be mixed in NGC 4810. Moreover, the southwest regions of NGC 4809 (e.g., knots ID: 48, 49, 50, 53, 56) exhibit similar velocities to those in NGC 4810 (knot 39).
The power index of age in NGC 4809 is 1.03, which is close to 1.0, indicating that the stellar mass is expected to increase with the stellar age, i.e., M_* age , at a fixed area size.

It is important to note that the stellar ages in NGC 4810 are consistently smaller than those in NGC 4809, with median log(age/yr) values of 7.3 and 7.8, respectively. We suggest a possible scenario in which the interaction first destabilized the molecular gas cloud in NGC 4809, triggering star formation and then enriching the metallicity of ISM by stellar activities (e.g., supernovae, winds) in the past 100 Myr to 30 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4809/4810 with VLT/MUSE, finding SN 2011jm in a metal-poor region of the merging dwarf system and localized oxygen and nitrogen enrichment.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the clear evidence of metal enrichment by a type Ic SN 2011jm in the galaxy NGC 4809, utilizing high spatial resolution Integral Field Units (IFU) observations obtained from the Very Large Telescope (VLT)/Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE). Despite SN 2011jm being surrounded by metal-deficient ISM ( 0.25 \ Z_ ) at a scale about 100 pc, we clearly detect enriched oxygen abundance ( 0.35 \ Z_ ) and a noteworthy nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio at the SN site. Remarkably, the metal pollution is confined to a smaller scale ( 13 pc).

SN2011jm, classified as a Type Ic supernova with an initial star mass of 20.7 howerton2011a,Kuncarayakti2018 , is located in the metal-poor regions of the merging dwarf galaxies NGC 4809/4810 gao2023 . The redshift ( z ) is 0.00326 obtained from NED, http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu , corresponding to a luminosity distance of 14.0 Mpc and a scale of 67 pc per arcsecond. We will compare the oxygen and nitrogen abundance at the SNe site with their surrounding ISM, offering a more detailed understanding of metal enrichment from massive progenitor stars and SNe.

gmw_00006652.name: GRG J0318+6829
gmw_00006652.overview: GRG J0318+6829 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:1604.05142]. Its merged radio map shows a lack of a distinct radio cocoon, with only slightly more emission visible between its bright radio lobes and outside of the southern lobe [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: For WNB 0313+683, the merged data add little diffuse emission and show no distinct cocoon, with most of the total flux already visible in NVSS.

quotes.quote: GRG J0318+6829 In the case of this source, WNB 0313+683, the addition of the single-dish data did not enhance in any significant manner the diffuse radio emission (Fig. 3). The merged map shows only slightly more emission between the bright radio lobes and outside of the southern lobe. This correspondence of interferometer and single-dish data has been noted before by Schoenmakers et al. (1998), who also presented a WSRT map at 325 MHz. Its radio extent is comparable to that of our merged map. The lack of a distinct radio cocoon around this source is reflected in the ratio of the NVSS flux to the total (merged map) flux.

gmw_00006653.name: GRG J0702+4859
gmw_00006653.overview: GRG J0702+4859 is a giant radio galaxy that lacks any signs of a radio halo [arXiv:1604.05142]. Its merged radio map is nearly identical to the NVSS map, except for a weak extension south of its western lobe [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: The source lacks a radio halo; its merged map is nearly identical to NVSS except for a weak western-lobe extension and a flux discrepancy.

quotes.quote: GRG J0702+4859 This radio source lacks any signs of a radio halo (cocoon), as the merged map looks almost exactly like the NVSS one (see both panels of Fig. 5). The only visible difference is a small extension south of the western lobe. Nevertheless, this extension is at a 3 level only. What is a bit surprising, the NVSS map includes only 77 of the total (merged map) flux.

gmw_00006654.name: GRG J1428+2918
gmw_00006654.overview: GRG J1428+2918 is a giant radio galaxy that lacks any signs of a diffuse radio halo [arXiv:1604.05142]. However, radio maps of the source reveal continuous emission linking its central source to both of its lobes [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: The source shows no diffuse radio halo, though the merged map reveals continuous emission linking the central source and both lobes.

quotes.quote: GRG J1428+2918 This source shows no signs of any diffuse radio halo (Fig. 11). However, the merged map reveals continuous emission between the central source and both lobes. The NVSS map includes 82 of the total (merged map) flux.

gmw_00006655.name: GRG J1628+5146
gmw_00006655.overview: GRG J1628+5146 is a giant radio galaxy that lacks a radio halo [arXiv:1604.05142]. Single-dish observations of the source reveal only diffuse radio emission connecting its central source to the northern lobe [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: The paper describes this radio galaxy as lacking a halo, with single-dish data adding only diffuse emission between the central source and northern lobe.

quotes.quote: GRG J1628+5146 This radio galaxy is another source showing no clues for any radio halo. The single-dish data contributed only with diffuse emission that connects the central source with the northern lobe (Fig. 13, bottom). Also the lower frequency WENSS data presented by R ottgering et al. (1996) show no signs of diffuse radio emission.

gmw_00006656.name: GRG J2145+8154
gmw_00006656.overview: GRG J2145+8154 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:1604.05142]. Its radio maps, including both the NVSS and merged maps, do not show any diffuse radio emission [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: This source has the smallest NVSS flux deficit in the sample, with merged and NVSS maps both lacking diffuse radio emission.

quotes.quote: GRG J2145+8154 This source shows the smallest deficit of the radio emission in the NVSS map (Fig. 15, top). As much as 95 of the merged map (Fig. 15, bottom) is included. Consequently, both radio maps do not show any diffuse radio emission.

gmw_00006657.name: Harry
gmw_00006657.overview: Harry is a disk galaxy that features a strong central bar and spiral arms with ordered rotation [arXiv:2108.05361]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus that, combined with central star formation, ionizes a centrally concentrated region and drives a blueshifted outflow [arXiv:2108.05361].

sources.summary: Harry has the strongest bar in the four-galaxy sample, AGN-ionized outflow signatures, and the lowest ionized gas mass, accretion rate, extent, and outflow age.

quotes.quote: Harry (J0813+5422) Harry has the strongest bar feature of each of the four galaxies targetted in this study (as seen in Figures fig:hsttargets fig:kcwitargets ). The spiral features are picked up in the emission seen in the top left panel of Figure fig:hbetafour , with the velocity map revealing the ordered rotation in this feature. The narrow emission, shown in Figure fig:oiiinfour , is centrally concentrated and shows some ordered rotation, suggesting this emission is ionised by a combination of the AGN and central star formation. The blueshifted, broadened emission shown in Figure fig:oiiiwfour however, does not show clear rotation in the velocity map. Figure fig:ratios reveals how the central region and the outflow have high / ratios, suggesting that the outflow is indeed ionised by the AGN.

gmw_00006658.name: Padma
gmw_00006658.overview: Padma is a disk galaxy that exhibits a bar-lens feature surrounded by spiral structure [arXiv:2108.05361]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus and displays ordered rotation alongside an ionized gas outflow that extends 2.4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2108.05361].

sources.summary: Padma shows a bar-lens and spiral structure, ordered rotation, offset broadened flux, and the largest gas mass, black-hole mass, accretion rate, velocity, extent, and outflow rate.

quotes.quote: Padma (J1012+1017) Figure fig:hsttargets reveals that Padma has a bar lens feature surrounded by spiral structure. This spiral structure is not detected in the emission flux shown in Figure fig:hbetafour , however the corresponding velocity map shows the most ordered rotation of the four targets studied (and similarly for the narrow velocity map in Figure fig:oiiinfour ). The brightest point in the broadened flux is offset from the brightest point in the narrow flux (shown by the blue ross in Figure fig:oiiiwfour ). Padma has the largest ionised gas mass of all four targets, at an order of magnitude larger than Harry. Padma also has the largest SMBH mass, SMBH accretion rate, outflow velocity and physical extent ( 2.4 kpc ), leading to the largest outflow rate of the four targets of 0.7 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00006659.name: Theodore
gmw_00006659.overview: Theodore is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a strong central bar and faint, loosely wound spiral arms [arXiv:2108.05361]. Observations of the bar feature reveal ongoing star formation, while the central region of the galaxy shows signs of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2108.05361].

sources.summary: Theodore has a strong bar and faint spiral arms; KCWI detects bar emission, star-formation-like narrow ratios, AGN-ionized broad outflow ratios, and the lowest outflow velocity.

quotes.quote: Theodore (J1314+4218) Theodore has a strong bar feature with faint, loosely wound spiral arms emerging from the ends (as seen in HST imaging in Figure fig:hsttargets ). Figure fig:kcwitargets reveals how only the bar feature is picked up in the KCWI observations. This is particularly apparent in the flux in the emission shown in Figure fig:hbetafour , which also reveals some rotational structure in the corresponding velocity map. This bar feature is also just noticeable in the narrow emission (Figure fig:oiiinfour ), suggesting ionisation due to ongoing star formation in the bar. This could also extend into the central regions of the galaxy as the narrow / ratio in the top right panel of Figure fig:ratios is low, suggesting ionisation dominated by star formation.

gmw_00006660.name: HG 20221219A
gmw_00006660.overview: HG 20221219A is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.554 [arXiv:2405.14182]. It is identified as a modestly star-forming galaxy with a stellar mass of 10.20 solar masses in log scale [arXiv:2405.14182].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HG 20221219A as the FRB host galaxy, assigns it high association confidence, measures its redshift, and models it as modestly star-forming.

quotes.quote: Using the 10 ^ th data release of the Legacy Survey https://www.legacysurvey.org/dr10/ DR10 R-band image; Dey2019 and the Pan-STARRS1 PS1; Chambers2016 optical survey, we identify a plausible host galaxy for FRB 20221219A (henceforth HG 20221219A) consistent with the radio localization ellipse (see R-band field in Figure  fig:niharidesifield ). We also identify two closely neighboring galaxies (henceforth IGH1 and IGH2). We use astropath Aggarwal2021 for host galaxy validation, which implements a Bayesian approach to estimate association probabilities for neighboring galaxies with tunable priors based on the sky position of the FRB, as well as their R-band magnitude, position, and R-band radius (see SED fit in Figure  fig:niharised ; Sharma2023 and Law2023 provide details regarding host association methods). We were able to successfully associate the host galaxy to FRB 20221219A at a confidence level of 99.6 using Legacy Survey DR10 R-band imaging data from the Beijing-Arizona Sky Survey (BASS).

This places the host galaxy at a redshift of z_ host = 0.554 , and find the two neighboring galaxies in the foreground at z_ igh1 = 0.492 and z_ igh2 = 0.438 (see Table  tab:hostintprops ). SED Modeling Using the spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting software Prospector Johnson2021 , we perform a non-parametric fit for the stellar properties of the HG 20221219A, IGH1 and IGH2. While we were unable to obtain star formation rates for IGH1 and IGH2 due to low S/N in the FIR region of the spectrum, we were able to identify HG 20221219A as a modestly star-forming galaxy, M _ = 1.78_ 0.23 ^ +0.24  M_ yr ^ 1 , of stellar mass log _ 10 (M_ , host ) = 10.20^ +0.03 _ 0.04  M_ (see Figure  fig:niharised ), and IGH1, IGH2 as galaxies with stellar masses straddling that of HG 20221219A, log _ 10 (M_ , igh1 ) = 10.60^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ , log _ 10 (M_ , igh2 ) = 10.01^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ (see Table  tab:hostintprops ).

gmw_00006661.name: IGH1
gmw_00006661.overview: IGH1 is an intervening foreground galaxy located at a redshift of 0.492 [arXiv:2405.14182]. It has an intermediate stellar mass of approximately 10.2 billion solar masses and lies along the sightline to the fast radio burst FRB 20221219A [arXiv:2405.14182].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGH1 as a foreground intervening galaxy near the FRB sightline, with a measured redshift, impact parameter, and stellar mass from photometry/spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We identify two intervening galaxy halos at redshifts z_ igh1 = 0.492 and z_ igh2 = 0.438 , with low impact parameters, b_ igh1 = 43.0_ 11.3 ^ +11.3 kpc and b_ igh2 = 36.1_ 11.3 ^ +11.3 kpc, and intermediate stellar masses, log _ 10 (M_ , igh1 ) = 10.01^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ and log _ 10 (M_ , igh2 ) = 10.60^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ . The presence of two such galaxies suggests that the sightline is significantly overcrowded compared to the median sightline to this redshift, as inferred from the halo mass function. We perform a detailed analysis of the sightline toward FRB 20221219A, constructing both DM and scattering budgets. Our results suggest that, unlike most well-localized sources, the host galaxy does not dominate the observed scattering. Instead, we posit that an intersection with a single partially ionized cloudlet in the circumgalactic medium of an intervening galaxy could account for the substantial scattering in FRB 20221219A and remain in agreement with typical electron densities inferred for extra-planar dense cloud-like structures in the Galactic and extragalactic halos (e.g., high-velocity clouds).
HG 20221219A, IGH1, and IGH2 are characterized following the methods outlined in Sharma2023 .
This places the host galaxy at a redshift of z_ host = 0.554 , and find the two neighboring galaxies in the foreground at z_ igh1 = 0.492 and z_ igh2 = 0.438 (see Table  tab:hostintprops ).

gmw_00006662.name: IGH2
gmw_00006662.overview: IGH2 is a foreground intervening galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.438 [arXiv:2405.14182]. It has an intermediate stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 10.60 solar masses and lies close to the sightline of the fast radio burst FRB 20221219A [arXiv:2405.14182].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGH2 as a foreground intervening galaxy close to the FRB sightline, measuring its redshift, impact parameter, and stellar mass alongside IGH1.

quotes.quote: We identify two intervening galaxy halos at redshifts z_ igh1 = 0.492 and z_ igh2 = 0.438 , with low impact parameters, b_ igh1 = 43.0_ 11.3 ^ +11.3 kpc and b_ igh2 = 36.1_ 11.3 ^ +11.3 kpc, and intermediate stellar masses, log _ 10 (M_ , igh1 ) = 10.01^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ and log _ 10 (M_ , igh2 ) = 10.60^ +0.02 _ 0.02  M_ . The presence of two such galaxies suggests that the sightline is significantly overcrowded compared to the median sightline to this redshift, as inferred from the halo mass function. We perform a detailed analysis of the sightline toward FRB 20221219A, constructing both DM and scattering budgets. Our results suggest that, unlike most well-localized sources, the host galaxy does not dominate the observed scattering. Instead, we posit that an intersection with a single partially ionized cloudlet in the circumgalactic medium of an intervening galaxy could account for the substantial scattering in FRB 20221219A and remain in agreement with typical electron densities inferred for extra-planar dense cloud-like structures in the Galactic and extragalactic halos (e.g., high-velocity clouds).
HG 20221219A, IGH1, and IGH2 are characterized following the methods outlined in Sharma2023 .
This places the host galaxy at a redshift of z_ host = 0.554 , and find the two neighboring galaxies in the foreground at z_ igh1 = 0.492 and z_ igh2 = 0.438 (see Table  tab:hostintprops ).

gmw_00006663.name: SDSS J143212.84+441620.4
gmw_00006663.overview: SDSS J143212.84+441620.4 is a bright, red elliptical galaxy that is possibly the brightest cluster galaxy of its host cluster [arXiv:1804.01099]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus with a radio counterpart, which may supply relativistic electrons to a nearby diffuse synchrotron source [arXiv:1804.01099].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J143212.84+441620.4 as a red elliptical, possible brightest cluster galaxy, and active AGN that may supply electrons for the diffuse synchrotron source.

quotes.quote: A bright, red elliptical galaxy (SDSS J143212.84+441620.4), possibly the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), has a radio counter-part and appears to be active. It has a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.180 , an -band magnitude of 16.94 and lies at coordinates 14h32m13s, +44d16.26m. The cluster redshift has been quoted as 0.170 photometric redshift only, Lopes2004 . Three SDSS cluster galaxies (based on their colors) with spectroscopic redshift are available in the vicinity that yield a redshift of z=0.178 , consistent with the photometric redshift. It is quite plausible that the BCG could be one of the suppliers of the relativistic electrons that produce the synchrotron emission in the diffuse source.

A bright, red elliptical galaxy (SDSS J143212.84+441620.4), possibly the BCG of the cluster, has a radio counterpart indicating it is currently active AGN. Hence, it is conceivable that it is one of the sources of the relativistic electrons that produce the synchrotron emission.

gmw_00006664.name: Pavo
gmw_00006664.overview: Pavo is a faint, extremely isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 2 Mpc [arXiv:2310.01478]. It hosts both an old stellar population and a young population that formed within the last 150 million years, though it currently shows no H-alpha emission [arXiv:2310.01478].

sources.summary: The paper reports Pavo as a newly discovered, isolated, star-forming irregular dwarf near 2 Mpc, with both old and young stellar populations, recent star formation, and low stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of Pavo, a faint ( M_V = -10.0 ), star-forming, irregular, and extremely isolated dwarf galaxy at D 2  Mpc. Pavo was identified in Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey imaging via a novel approach that combines low surface brightness galaxy search algorithms and machine learning candidate classifications. Follow-up imaging with the Inamori-Magellan Areal Camera Spectrograph on the 6.5 m Magellan Baade telescope revealed a color--magnitude diagram (CMD) with an old stellar population, in addition to the young population that dominates the integrated light, and a tip-of-the-red-giant-branch distance estimate of 1.99^ +0.20 _ 0.22  Mpc. The blue population of stars in the CMD is consistent with the youngest stars having formed no later than 150 Myr ago. We also detected no H emission with SOAR telescope imaging, suggesting we may be witnessing a temporary low in Pavo's star formation.

These findings indicate that Pavo hosts both an old stellar population and a recently formed one. It therefore cannot be a transient object, and instead is a long-lived, bona fide dwarf galaxy, albeit at a very low mass. It must also have recently contained enough gas to ignite a recent episode of star formation.

gmw_00006665.name: UGC 11872
gmw_00006665.overview: UGC 11872 is classified as a low-ionisation nuclear emission region (LINER) galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386]. The galaxy exhibits very strong nitrogen emission in its nucleus, which becomes much weaker in the outer regions of its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 11872 has very strong nuclear nitrogen emission, LINER classification in NED, weaker disk emission, and the largest nitrogen fraction in the five-galaxy subsample.

quotes.quote: UGC 11872 Figure fig:u8403_u11872 (right) shows very strong 6583 emission in the galaxy nucleus, with a ratio of 6583 to \ of 1.33 over the central 20 ^ in radius. UGC 11872 is listed on NED as containing a low-ionisation nuclear emission region (LINER). The 6583 emission is much weaker in the outer regions of the disk, but the total 6583/ \ ratio for the galaxy is still 0.54, easily the largest fraction in the 5 galaxies studied. The fraction of \ to the \ + total flux is 0.58 for the entire galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 7177 is described as too dusty for accurate nuclear star cluster morphology; its nucleus is obscured even in F160W and is assigned to the poor-fit quality group.

quotes.quote: In two galaxies (NGC 289 and NGC 7177) we are unable to determine accurate NSC morphologies due to dust obscuring the centers of all three bands.
The quality values are as follows: Quality 0: These galaxies are those for which we do not obtain good fits for the NSCs -- this includes the galaxies discussed above, NGC 289, NGC 1566 and NGC 7177.

In three galaxies, the presence of a nuclear cluster can't be constrained due to dust opaque enough to obscure the nucleus even in F160W (NGC 289 and NGC 7177), or the presence of a very bright AGN (NGC 1566) as discussed in Section subsec:qualitymodel .

gmw_00006666.name: UGC 2210
gmw_00006666.overview: UGC 2210 is classified as an Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386]. Photometric observations of the galaxy show virtually no nitrogen emission, yielding only an upper limit on its nitrogen-to-hydrogen ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386].

sources.summary: The paper reports multiple X-ray sources in NGC 1073, including quasars and a strong radio/X-ray compact object treated as a BL Lac-type ULX candidate.

quotes.quote: NGC 1073: In the CP02 catalog there are two X-ray sources in this galaxy which are at about the same distance from the nucleus and much brighter in X-rays than the source which they designated IXO 5 (this is associated to a HII region if our identification was correct, as said). These two were not include in the IXO CP02 catalog because it was known that they were quasars (Arp and Sulentic 1979; Burbidge et al. 1979). But clearly these fit the definition of ULX's and are listed in Table 1. Also included in Table 1 is the third known quasar in NGC 1073 since it has L_X = 38.7 erg s ^ 1 only slightly above the general criterion for ULX's. The strong X-ray quasar with z = 1.411 is also a strong radio source.

sources.summary: The paper reports virtually no nitrogen emission in UGC 2210, giving only an upper limit, and later contrasts its weak profile with UGC 2855 despite both being Sc galaxies.

quotes.quote: UGC 2210 Figure fig:u2141_u2210_u2855 (centre) shows virtually no \ emission. The \ growth curve shows no significant departures from zero and it is only possible to put an upper limit on the \ to \ ratio for the whole galaxy. The upper limits shown in this figure correspond to the highest value detected in any of the photometric apertures used. The 3 upper limit on the 6583/ \ ratio for the whole galaxy is 0.02, which is equivalent to a lower limit of 0.97 on /( \ + total).

gmw_00006667.name: UGC 8403
gmw_00006667.overview: UGC 8403 is a spiral galaxy that is relatively bright [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386]. It exhibits strong nuclear nitrogen emission that peaks in its center, though there is very little nitrogen emission detected in its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0410386].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 8403 has strong central nitrogen emission but little disk nitrogen emission, yielding a low whole-galaxy average ratio despite being relatively bright.

quotes.quote: UGC 8403 Figure fig:u8403_u11872 (left) shows strong nuclear 6583 emission, peaking in the centre with an 6583/ \ ratio of 0.59. This falls rapidly and there is very little sign of \ in the disk. The average ratio for the whole galaxy is just 0.07, which corresponds to /( \ + total) = 0.91.

gmw_00006668.name: S-galaxy
aliases_external: Arp 194 southern galaxy / UGC 06945 NED02 / VV 126a
gmw_00006668.overview: The S-galaxy is an active star-forming component of the interacting system Arp 194 [arXiv:1607.08761]. It exhibits a distinct velocity gradient caused by its rotation, and its spectrum is dominated by a young stellar population from a current intense starburst [arXiv:1607.08761].

sources.summary: The paper treats S-galaxy as the southern active star-forming component of Arp 194, measuring its rotation, stellar mass, metallicity, burst activity, and interaction with region A.

quotes.quote: From Figs. fig2 , fig3 (on the right-hand side) one can see a distinct velocity gradient through S-galaxy evidently caused by its rotation. When the slit PA=138 passes through the visible edge of S-galaxy and comes to the region A, velocity decreases abruptly by 90 km s ^ 1 within the interval of only 7 arcsec ( 5 kpc). Actually this change should be even more steep since the observed gradient is smoothed by the overlapping of S-galaxy and the region A, as it was noted earlier by .

Thus the mass enclosed within the visible radius of the galaxy is about 4.6 10^ 10 M_ which agrees with the estimate of stellar mass of this galaxy (see Table tab_phot ) and the moderate relative mass of the dark halo ( M_ halo 1.4 M_* ).

In the case of S-galaxy the spectrum is defined mostly by young stellar population with the age lower than 10^8 yr. However, it reflects the current intense burst of star-formation rather than the overall youth of the galaxy.

gmw_00006669.name: COS4 03493-M4 121150
gmw_00006669.overview: COS4 03493-M4 121150 is a highly inclined disk galaxy [arXiv:2210.03106]. It features a bright, high-Sérsic index bulge component and a large extended disk [arXiv:2210.03106].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS4 03493-M4 121150 as an inclined outlier where JAM overpredicts dynamical mass, likely from fitting high mass-to-light to a bright bulge.

quotes.quote: There is only one galaxy for which the JAM estimates and the estimates are significantly different over a large range in radius, COS4 03493-M4 121150 (top right). At R_ e, F814W , the JAM measurement is higher by M_ dyn =0.35 dex compared to the H model. For this highly inclined galaxy ( i 68-84^ ), the kinematic major axis and the F814W and F160W position angles all align within 1^ . However, the F814W structural parameters indicate a high Sérsic index ( n_ S, F814W =5.1 ) and a large disc ( R_ e, F814W =8.2 kpc).

Yet, adopting structural parameters from F814W imaging for the H model has negligible effect on the dynamical mass constraints. Instead, it is likely that JAM fits a high M/L to the bright and higher- S/N bulge component, leading to an overestimate of the mass in the extended disc (see also discussion in Section s:multiple ).

gmw_00006670.name: NGC 5291S
gmw_00006670.overview: NGC 5291S is a star-forming tidal dwarf galaxy candidate associated with a collisional HI ring [arXiv:1509.05404, arXiv:2309.06478]. Kinematic modeling of the galaxy reveals a high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1509.05404] and a young starburst that peaked several million years ago [arXiv:astro-ph/0703002].

sources.summary: NGC 5291S is modeled as a tidal dwarf candidate whose cube requires high velocity dispersion; its disc model reproduces the object while noting anomalous gas.

quotes.quote: The three largest TDG candidates (NGC 5291N, 5291S, 5291SW) are associated with velocity gradients that are kinematically decoupled from the underlying debris (B07). In Sect. sec:3Dmodels we model the kinematics of these TDGs.

For _ we find typical values between 7 and 12 apart from NGC 5291N and NGC 5291S. For these two TDGs, a velocity dispersion of 20 is required to reproduce the observed cube: this high value may be either physical (high turbulence in the ISM) or due to unresolved non-circular motions within the beam.
NGC 5291S is nicely reproduced by our disc model. At approaching velocities ( V_ l.o.s. 4645-4635 ), there is some anomalous gas on the receding side of the disc.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5291S as a focused tidal dwarf target, noting possible dual complexes, ALMA pointing effects, and MUSE observations.

quotes.quote: Three star-forming complexes (NGC 5291N, NGC 5291S and NGC 5291SW) are associated with strong \ concentrations that display a velocity gradient decoupled from the underlying collisional material, pointing to rotation within a self-gravitating potential well Bournaud2007, lelli2015gas . In this paper, we focus on NGC 5291N and NGC 5291S because no single-dish CO observations are available for NGC 5291SW.

Since the ALMA primary beam is significantly larger than the CO emitting area, the primary beam attenuation has little effect on the moment-zero maps, with the exception of NGC 5291S. For this TDG, the pointing center was chosen in-between two main SF complexes so that the CO emission in the Northern complex (the Southern is not detected) is 10 '' offset from the pointing center (see Fig. fig:overview ). It is also possible that the two SF complexes are two distinct objects, whose individual kinematics cannot be discerned with the available \ observations lelli2015gas .
NGC 5291S was observed on 22 January 2018 as part of Program ID 097.B-0152 (PI: M. Boquien) without AO for a total exposure time of 1800 sec.

sources.summary: NGC 5291S is identified with region 3, one of the brightest HI-ring knots and a possible dwarf-galaxy progenitor, with young starburst and spectral properties discussed.

quotes.quote: We observe that star-forming knots are spread over 180 kpc, all along the HI structure. They are actually located on or close to individual HI peaks. The brightest regions (in H , NUV and 8.0 m) are located near the apparent tip and points of maximum column density of the HI ring: they correspond to our regions 1, 3 and 21; the latter two, also known as NGC 5291S and NGC 5291N, have been discussed in the literature as the possible progenitors of dwarf galaxies, and are probably associated with gravitationally bound gas clouds bournaud2004a .

The IRS spectra of the two most luminous regions (regions 3 and 21 in this paper) also show variations in their PAH strengths and dust continuum shortwards of 5 m and above 9 m, which are not yet understood.

Combining the two criteria, we conclude that the regions that are currently the most active ones (i.e. region 1, 3 and 21) are all very young but should have had their SF peak already 5 10^6 years ago while the more quiescent ones may be a few 10 10^6 years older, still sustaining star formation but at a lower rate than before.

gmw_00006671.name: NGC 5291SW
gmw_00006671.overview: NGC 5291SW is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate associated with velocity gradients that are kinematically decoupled from the underlying debris [arXiv:1509.05404]. It exhibits complex kinematics with a low signal-to-noise ratio, lacking a continuous, coherent kinematical structure suitable for disc modeling [arXiv:1509.05404].

sources.summary: NGC 5291SW is identified as a tidal dwarf candidate; the paper estimates molecular mass indirectly and reports complex, low-signal kinematics unsuitable for the disc model.

quotes.quote: The three largest TDG candidates (NGC 5291N, 5291S, 5291SW) are associated with velocity gradients that are kinematically decoupled from the underlying debris (B07). In Sect. sec:3Dmodels we model the kinematics of these TDGs.

For the remaining two TDGs (NGC 5291SW and NGC 7252E), CO observations are not available, thus we estimated M_ mol assuming that TDGs and spiral galaxies have the same star-formation efficiency: SFE = SFR /M_ mol 5 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 Leroy08 . This is observationally motivated by the results of Braine01 for a sample of eight TDGs.

NGC 5291SW is a complex case due to the low signal-to-noise ratio. The emission at V_ l.o.s. 4730-4720 does not show a continuous, coherent kinematical structure, hence it has not been considered in the disc model.

gmw_00006672.name: NGC 7252E
gmw_00006672.overview: NGC 7252E is a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1509.05404, arXiv:1802.09493] that is well reproduced by a disc model [arXiv:1509.05404]. It exhibits star formation with a rate of 0.02 solar masses per year, which is typical of dwarf galaxies in the local Universe [arXiv:1802.09493].

sources.summary: NGC 7252E is one of the NGC 7252 tidal dwarf candidates; the paper obtains spectra, estimates molecular mass indirectly, and finds its disc model reproduces the data.

quotes.quote: For both NGC 7252NW and NGC 7252E, low-resolution long-slit spectra (covering the 4500-7500 \ wavelength domain) were obtained in July 1994 with the red channel of the EMMI camera installed on the New Technology Telescope (NTT) at ESO/La Silla. Details on observing conditions and data reduction are given in Duc98b . The spectrum of NGC 7252NW is shown in Fig. fig:FORS_EMMI (grey line). New observations were carried out in September 2000 with the FORS1 multi-object spectrograph installed on the Unit Telescope 1 (UT1) of the VLT. We simultaneously obtained the spectra of 19 objects, including NGC 7252E and NGC 7252NW. The position angle of the slit-lets was fixed to 72.9^ . We used grism 600B (1.2 \ per pixel) with a total exposure time of 4 1800 sec. The resulting spectrum has higher spectral resolution and sensitivity than the EMMI one, but only covers the wavelength domain 3700-5500 . Data reduction was carried out using IRAF.

For the remaining two TDGs (NGC 5291SW and NGC 7252E), CO observations are not available, thus we estimated M_ mol assuming that TDGs and spiral galaxies have the same star-formation efficiency: SFE = SFR /M_ mol 5 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 Leroy08 . This is observationally motivated by the results of Braine01 for a sample of eight TDGs.
NGC 7252E is well reproduced by our disc model despite it is a poorly resolved case with low signal-to-noise ratio.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC7252E as a candidate tidal dwarf galaxy in NGC7252 and reports significant star formation with SFR=0.02 M_/yr, typical of local dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: The star formation rates in the associated tidal dwarf galaxies (NGC7252E, SFR=0.02 M _ /yr and NGC7252NW, SFR=0.03 M _ /yr) are typical of dwarf galaxies in the local Universe.
(B) The candidate tidal dwarf galaxy (NGC7252E) Lelli_2015 .

Significant star formation is detected in the tidal dwarf galaxy candidates (NGC7252E, SFR=0.02 M _ /yr and NGC7252NW, SFR=0.03 M _ /yr) which is comparable to the integrated star formation rates observed in dwarf galaxies in local Universe.

gmw_00006673.name: Mrk 474
gmw_00006673.overview: Mrk 474 is a Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.041 [arXiv:astro-ph/0004226]. It is a very strong source of X-ray emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0004226].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes reported X-ray filament emission near Mrk 474 and finds the apparent connection to a nearby quasar is not statistically significant.

quotes.quote: Mrk 474 is a z=0.041 Seyfert galaxy adjacent to a z=1.94 quasar (Arp 1996). The Seyfert is a very strong X-ray source, while the point X-ray source identified by Arp with the quasar, located 170 arcsec to the NW, is only weakly detected in the PSPC observations. Arp (1996) analysed the 0.5-2.0 keV PSPC data, and saw a filament connecting the Seyfert and quasar. It is not clear what binning scheme and smoothing Gaussian was used to produce his figures 1 and 2, but I obtain very similar results by binning by a factor 7 (so that each pixel is 3.5 arcsec) and smoothing with a Gaussian of = 4 pixels (14 arcsec).
The detection of the filament in this source is thus certainly not statistically significant.

gmw_00006674.name: SDSS J 141519.50-003021.6
gmw_00006674.overview: SDSS J 141519.50-003021.6 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0408123] located at a redshift of z=0.135 [arXiv:astro-ph/0408123]. X-ray observations of the source reveal a degree of variability and a high-energy excess that may be explained by a partial covering model [arXiv:astro-ph/0408123].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first 0.5-10 keV XMM-Newton study of SDSS J 141519.50-003021.6, finding a steep X-ray spectrum, variability, and possible hard-excess partial covering.

quotes.quote: Two SDSS sources, SDSS J 141519.50-003021.6 ( z=0.135 ) and SDSS J 030639.57+000343.2 ( z=0.107 ), turned out to be in the XMM-Newton observed fields. The distance of the sources from the boresight was 603'' and 170'' , respectively. For the sake of simplicity, they will be thereafter called SD1 and SD2, respectively.

The optical counterparts of SD1 have been found in 2MASS (Cutri et al. 2003) and USNO B1 (Monet et al. 2003) catalogs. Only SD1 displays a certain degree variability, although the limited effective exposure ( 6 ks) prevents further studies. Both sources present an excess at high energy, that could be explained by a partial covering model for SD1 and by a gaussian line for SD2.

gmw_00006675.name: 3C 438
gmw_00006675.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 438 is a low-redshift Fanaroff-Riley class II source [arXiv:1702.01939] that serves as the brightest cluster galaxy in its host cluster [arXiv:1611.03489]. It is classified as a low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:0804.3369, arXiv:astro-ph/0603090] hosting an active nucleus [arXiv:1611.03489] and strong radio jets [arXiv:1611.03489, arXiv:0804.3369].

sources.summary: The paper places 3C 438 in the Brightest Cluster Galaxy, describes its jets, hotspots, active nucleus, and 3-9 Myr radio age, and argues the 45 kpc source is too small to drive cluster-scale discontinuities.

quotes.quote: When describing the gas density of the inner discontinuity, it is important to realize that the surface brightness profile was not centered on the peak of the X-ray surface brightness, coincident with the Brightest Cluster Galaxy, the host galaxy of 3C 438.

In spite of its inclusion in the 3C catalog of radio galaxies Bennett1962 , 3C 438 has been relatively poorly studied because of its low Galactic latitude. Low-resolution radio imaging was presented by , and high-resolution radio imaging showing the unusual strong jets and weak hotspots of the radio source was carried out by and . made maps of the central radio galaxy at 90 GHz detecting the nucleus and hot spots, and the broad band emission from the active nucleus has been well-studied as part of several radio galaxy surveys mingo14,Massaro15 . Most recently have carried out a study of the spectral and dynamical ages of the radio galaxy, finding that it began to be active between 3 and 9 Myr ago.

Thus, it seems unlikely that the current radio source has any relationship to the large-scale disturbances seen in the cluster, as argued on energetic grounds by . Any shock features driven by the currently active radio galaxy would be expected to be seen very close to the radio lobes. The linear scale of the radio galaxy 3C 438 is 45 kpc Kraft2007 , hence it is about an order of magnitude smaller than the surface brightness discontinuities presented in this work.

sources.summary: The paper detects 90 GHz jet emission from 3C 438 and treats its extended emission as possibly lobe-related while finding no resolved lobe steepening.

quotes.quote: Jet-related emission is clearly detected in three of the newly observed sources, 3C 388, 3C 401, and 3C 438. This is the first time that FRII jets have been detected at these high radio frequencies. The three detected sources are all part of the class of low-excitation radio galaxies with prominent jets and diffuse hotspots, possibly lying in rich environments, identified by Hardcastle \ (1997).

As noted above, extended emission in 3C 388 E, 3C 401 and 3C 438 cannot unambiguously be associated with either the lobes or the hotspots, but it is plausible that some or all of this emission is also related to the lobes. In 3C 401 and 3C 438 the flattest-spectrum regions are the jets (already discussed above) and there is no clear evidence for resolved spectral steepening in the lobes.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C438 as a low-redshift FR II radio source with a strongly bending spectrum whose KDA EXT fit is favored over KDA.

quotes.quote: 3C438 Similarly to 3C217, 3C438 is a typical but low-redshift FR II type source with linear size not exceeding 100 Mpc . Also, its observed radio spectrum shows considerable bending from the middle frequencies to the higher ones. The spectra predicted with the KDA and KDA EXT models are shown in Figure 15. Again, the relevant ^ 2 _ red values in these two models are similar to those for 3C184 and 3C217, and confirm the superiority of the KDA EXT age solution.

sources.summary: The paper keeps 3C 438 in the LERG class despite a conflicting NLRG classification, and finds its luminosity and photon index consistent with prior analysis.

quotes.quote: The one disagreement is over 3C 438, which is classed by LRL as absorption-line only (following Smith Spinrad 1980) but which Jackson Rawlings class as a high-excitation NLRG. The evidence for detected [ OIII ] in 3C 438 (Rawlings \ 1989) is very weak, however, and its flux is certainly low, and so for consistency we retain 3C 438 in the LERG class.
3C 438 Our luminosity and photon index for this radio galaxy are consistent with the analysis of the same data in Donato \ (2004).

gmw_00006676.name: COS-1009842
gmw_00006676.overview: COS-1009842 is a massive Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.76 [arXiv:2010.03566, arXiv:2112.14779]. It is actively forming stars, likely undergoing a starburst, and resides within a compact trio of galaxies [arXiv:2010.03566].

sources.summary: The paper places COS-1009842 in a compact z~7 trio of massive Ly-alpha emitters with high UV luminosity, active star formation, blue IRAC colors, and burst-like specific star formation rates.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopic results indicate that COS-940214 ( = 6.748), COS-1009842 ( = 6.761), and COS-995126 ( = 6.813) all lie within a spherical volume with radius R = 1.7 physical Mpc (Fig. fig:bubble ), consistent with the expected sizes of HII regions at z=7 e.g. Furlanetto2004,Mesinger2007,Lin2016 . These three sources have luminosities ranging from 0.8--2.3 L ^ _ UV and therefore are likely among the most massive galaxies present at z 7. All three are also inferred to be actively forming stars with SFRs of 7--28 yr ^ 1 using the SED fitting procedure described in sec:BEAGLE . Their very blue IRAC colours ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] < 0.6) further suggest large sSFRs ranging from approximately 10--130 Gyr ^ 1 (see Table tab:galaxy_properties ). Because the typical sSFR at z 7 is 5 Gyr ^ 1 ( Endsley2021 ), these three galaxies are likely undergoing a burst of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS-1009842 as a z~6.8 Ly-alpha emitter with an unusually symmetric line profile and photometry disfavouring low-redshift line interpretations.

quotes.quote: However, one of our galaxies (COS-1009842) appears to show a highly symmetric velocity profile with k>2 at 92 confidence (Fig. fig:LyA_Detections ). Data of COS-1009842 indicate that this symmetric emission feature is unlikely to be a strong non-resonant optical line (i.e. H , H , [ OIII ] 4959, or [ OIII ] 5007) at lower redshift. Using the SED fitting procedure described in sec:BEAGLE , we find that the photometry of COS-1009842 strongly disfavors such low-redshift solutions (reduced ^2 > 2.2 ) while the solution well matches the data (reduced ^2 = 0.6 ). Furthermore, if this feature were H , [ OIII ] 4959, or [ OIII ] 5007, at least one of the other two lines would lie in a skyline-free region of the spectrum where no emission is detected.

gmw_00006677.name: NGC5921
gmw_00006677.overview: NGC 5921 is an Sbc-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1112.4058] characterized by a stellar bar enclosed by a sharp inner ring-like structure [arXiv:0909.1816]. The galaxy has a poor molecular and neutral gas content [arXiv:0909.1816], with two grand-design spiral arms extending outward from the ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0106019].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5921 has contradictory inclination estimates; a low disk M/L remains possible, but the authors favor bar-driven noncircular motions rather than stellar-population anomalies.

quotes.quote: NGC 5921 is an Sbc-type galaxy with a noticeable bar surrounded by a ring. As for NGC 4214, the inclination estimates for this galaxy are contradictory. According to Hernandez et al. (2005), the isophotes ellipticity leads to i=36.5^o . Our photometric data give i=43^o (see Fig. 2), which agrees with the inclination determined kinematically from H_ observations (Hernandez et al. 2005). In Table table4 , the rotational velocity v_ max disk determined photometrically in the same way as for NGC 5347 (see above) is compared with the two values of rotation velocity v_ max found from H_ measurements for i=43^o and i=36.5^o for radial distance corresponding to v_ max disk .

As we see from Table table4 , for i=36.5^o v_ max disk is lower than v_ max , while the opposite conclusion is true for i=43^o . Since our inclination estimates agree with the latter value, an abnormally low mass-to-light ratio for the disk of NGC 5921 remains quite possible. However, an underestimation of the circular rotational velocity caused by the bar cannot be ruled out. This is suggested by the fact that the rotational velocity estimated from the H I line width (from the RC3 catalog) for i=43^o is v_ HI =136 km s^ 1 , which exceeds v_ max disk (see Table table4 ). Thus, the abnormally low M/L for NGC 5921 is most likely related to noncircular motions and not to stellar composition anomalies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC5921 as a real VLA HI test case for SL1M, describing the calibrated data, deconvolved grid, channel processing, and multi-scale reconstructions.

quotes.quote: NGC5921 To test with real data, we deconvolve the NGC5921 dataset that is distributed as a tutorial with the CASA radio astronomy software package http://casa.nrao.edu . This dataset consists of 63 channels of LL and RR polarisations, taken with the 27 telescopes of the VLA in a band centred on HI with a total bandwidth of 1.6MHz. In total, 11,934 visibilities for each channel were measured. The visibilities were calibrated and continuum subtracted according to the recommendations in the CASA software tutorial and exported for analysis.

The image is deconvolved to a 256 256 grid with a pixel spacing of 7 arc seconds. Each channel was processed until the relative change in the total errors was less than 10^ 9 . Generally only around 200 iterations per channel were necessary, and this took around 30 seconds per channel.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5921 as a gas-poor barred spiral with a prominent stellar bar, ring-like structure, weak bar H-alpha emission, and a spiral pattern speed matching the bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 5921 has a very poor molecular and neutral gas content Verter1985 and relatively poor ionised gas in a 8-12 kpc prominent stellar bar which is enclosed by sharp inner ring-like structure. The bar contains two straight dust lanes, and the spiral arms host many \ regions GonzalezDelgadoPerez1997, Aguerrietal1998, Rautiainenetal2005 , and form the ring at the bar ends, just before they become very open, broad, and diffuse. At the north end of the bar, the arm becomes brighter as it leaves the ring Eskridgeetal2002 . MartinFriedli1997 found that the stellar bar has an axis ratio of 0.5 and is oriented 18 degrees in the north-east direction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5921 as having a ring-like structure, a weakly visible bar with emission regions at its ends, a central velocity gradient, and no gas between ring and center.

quotes.quote: NGC 5921: This galaxy shows a ring like structure in both the and the XDSS blue images. The bar is not clearly seen in but many \ regions lie at the end of the bar. The centre of galaxy is the host of a strong velocity gradient well defined in the velocity map. Nevertheless, no gas is present between the ring like structure and the centre.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5921 as an inner-ringed barred spiral example, where Fourier contours trace grand-design arms outside the ring and support pitch-angle measurement.

quotes.quote: To illustrate what the Fourier method is extracting in a typical inner-ringed system in Table 1, consider the SB() galaxy NGC 5921 (Figs. 1 and 3). Fig. 3 shows the m =2 inverse Fourier transform contours superposed on a deprojected image.
