The contours trace out the two grand design arms extending outward from the ring (see also panel 206 in `The Carnegie Atlas of Galaxies', Sandage Bedke 1994).

gmw_00006678.name: HerBS-70
gmw_00006678.overview: HerBS-70 is a massive, Herschel-selected binary system of dusty star-forming galaxies at redshift z=2.3 [arXiv:2404.18991]. The system resides in a protocluster-scale dark matter halo, and its eastern component, HerBS-70E, hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.18991].

sources.summary: The paper studies HerBS-70 as a massive Herschel-selected z=2.3 binary dusty star-forming system in an overdense environment, with short gas depletion times and a protocluster-scale halo mass.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We report on deep SCUBA-2 observations at 850 and NOEMA spectroscopic measurements at 2 mm of the environment surrounding the luminous, massive ( M_ * 2 10^ 11 M _ ) Herschel selected source HerBS-70. This source was revealed by previous NOEMA observations to be a binary system of dusty star-forming galaxies at z= 2.3 , with the East component (HerBS-70E) hosting an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). The SCUBA-2 observations detected, in addition to the binary system, twenty-one sources at > 3.5 over an area of 25 square comoving Mpc with a sensitivity of 1 _ 850 = 0.75 mJy. The surface density of continuum sources around HerBS-70 is three times higher than for field galaxies.

The NOEMA spectroscopic measurements confirm the protocluster membership of three of the nine brightest sources through their CO(4--3) line emission, yielding a volume density 36 times higher than for field galaxies. All five confirmed sub-mm galaxies in the HerBS-70 system have relatively short gas depletion times ( 80 - 500 Myr), indicating the onset of quenching for this protocluster core due to the depletion of gas. The dark matter halo mass of the HerBS-70 system is estimated around 5 10^ 13 M _ , with a projected current-day mass of 10^ 15 M _ , similar to the local Virgo and Coma clusters.

gmw_00006679.name: HerBS-70E
gmw_00006679.overview: HerBS-70E is a high-redshift galaxy at z = 2.3077 that hosts a radio-luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.18991]. It is a dusty star-forming galaxy with an extreme star formation rate of 1500 to 3000 solar masses per year and a companion galaxy located 140 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2404.18991].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HerBS-70E as the eastern z=2.3077 component, a dusty star-forming AGN host with broad CO lines, radio excess, high star formation, and feedback-linked quenching.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies of the HerBS-70 system, HerBS-70W and HerBS-70E, are separated by a projected distance of 16.5” (140 kpc at z = 2.3 ), and the spectroscopic redshift difference between the galaxies is relatively small, corresponding to a velocity shift of 350 km/s ( z_ spec = 2.3077 and 2.3155 for HerBS-70E and -70W, respectively; neri2019 ). The source HerBS-70E displays strong double-peaked emission lines of CO (3--2) and (4--3) with widths of 770 km/s. In contrast, HerBS-70W has significantly narrower ( 140 km/s) single-peaked emission lines. Subsequent observations using the VLA measured the CO(1--0) emission line of the HerBS-70 binary system and revealed a bright radio continuum at 30 GHz in HerBS-70E indicating the presence of a radio luminous AGN in this source Stanley2023 .

HerBS-70E poses an interesting case of feedback through multiple mechanisms. Firstly, the detection of a buried AGN in HerBS-70E suggests ongoing quenching through negative feedback Stanley2023 . Secondly, star-formation feedback is likely present, given the excessive 1500-3000 M _ /yr star-formation rate of HerBS-70E RowanRobinson2016 . Finally, the depletion timescale (i.e., the star-forming gas mass divided by the star-formation rate) is on the order of 170 Myr swinbank2014,Bakx2020IRAM,Stanley2023,Berta2023 , which places a strong limit on the quenching timescale in the case of no inflowing gas.

gmw_00006680.name: HerBS-70W
gmw_00006680.overview: HerBS-70W is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.3155 [arXiv:2404.18991]. It forms the western component of a binary system, separated by a projected distance of 140 kiloparsecs from its companion galaxy, HerBS-70E [arXiv:2404.18991].

sources.summary: The paper describes HerBS-70W as the western member of the z=2.3 binary system, separated by 140 kpc, with narrow CO lines, high luminosity, star formation, and gas mass.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies of the HerBS-70 system, HerBS-70W and HerBS-70E, are separated by a projected distance of 16.5” (140 kpc at z = 2.3 ), and the spectroscopic redshift difference between the galaxies is relatively small, corresponding to a velocity shift of 350 km/s ( z_ spec = 2.3077 and 2.3155 for HerBS-70E and -70W, respectively; neri2019 ). The source HerBS-70E displays strong double-peaked emission lines of CO (3--2) and (4--3) with widths of 770 km/s. In contrast, HerBS-70W has significantly narrower ( 140 km/s) single-peaked emission lines.

Based on these results, the following properties were derived for HerBS-70E and W. The 8-1000 microns infrared luminosities are 1.8 10^ 13 and 4 10^ 12 L _ for HerBS-70 E and W, respectively, yielding star formation rates of 2000 and 470 M _ /yr, assuming a initial mass function Berta2023 , and dust masses of 4 and 1 10^9 M _ Draine2007dustmodel,neri2019 . The molecular gas masses of HerBS-70E and W are 2.7 10^ 11 and 1.5 10^ 11 M _ , with estimated depletion time scales of 170 and 660 Myr, respectively, although these values vary strongly between different gas-mass and star-formation estimates Berta2023,Stanley2023 .
HerBS-70E is forming stars at a high rate ( 1500-3800 M _ /yr), while having an additional galaxy in its vicinity that could indicate an overdensity of objects within its environment (HerBS-70W). ii.

gmw_00006681.name: CID-947
gmw_00006681.overview: CID-947 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.328 [arXiv:1507.02290]. The galaxy hosts an extremely massive, actively accreting black hole and exhibits intense star formation alongside a black hole-driven gas outflow [arXiv:1507.02290].

sources.summary: The paper describes CID-947 as a z=3.328 AGN host whose extremely massive black hole outgrew its star-forming host, with no evidence that AGN feedback quenched star formation.

quotes.quote: We present spectroscopic observations of a galaxy at redshift z = 3.328 , which hosts an actively accreting, extremely massive BH, in its final stages of growth. The SMBH mass is roughly one-tenth the mass of the entire host galaxy, suggesting that it has grown much more efficiently than the host, contrary to models of synchronized coevolution. The host galaxy is forming stars at an intense rate, despite the presence of a SMBH-driven gas outflow.

The rich collection of ancillary COSMOS multiwavelength data available for \ enables us to study the basic properties of its host galaxy (see details in section SM_sec_SED in the supplementary materials). A previously published analysis of the observed spectral energy distribution of the emission from the source reveals an appreciable stellar emission component, originating from 5.6_ 0.4 ^ +2.8 10^ 10 in stars . Our own analysis provides a yet lower stellar mass, of =4.4_ 0.5 ^ +0.4 10^ 10 . However, we focus on the previously determined, higher stellar mass, as a conservative estimate. The source is also detected at far-IR and (sub)millimeter wavelengths, which allows us to constrain the SFR in the host galaxy to about 400 year ^ 1 .

gmw_00006682.name: OIIIB-4
gmw_00006682.overview: OIIIB-4 is a giant green pea galaxy at a redshift of z=0.838 [arXiv:1904.11510]. It is a low stellar mass, starbursting system with extended emission-line regions likely powered by star-formation-driven gas outflows [arXiv:1904.11510].

sources.summary: The paper identifies OIIIB-4 as a giant green pea at z=0.838 with extended emission, high ionization, low metallicity, and likely star-formation-driven outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: One of such star-forming \ blobs, OIIIB-4, at z=0.838 has an \ equivalent width of 845 27 \ and an \ to 3726,3729 ratio of / = 6.5 2.7 that are as high as those of typical green peas (Cardamone et al. 2009). The spatially resolved spectrum of OIIIB-4 shows / =5-10 over 14 kpc in the entire large \ extended regions of OIIIB-4, unlike the known green peas whose strong \ emission region is compact. Moreover, OIIIB-4 presents no high-ionization emission lines unlike green beans that have extended \ emission with a type-2 AGN.

OIIIB-4 is thus a giant green pea, which is a low stellar mass ( 7 10^7 ) galaxy with a very high specific star-formation rate (sSFR =2 10^2 Gyr ^ 1 ), a high ionization parameter ( q_ ion 3 10^8 ), and a low metallicity similar to those of green peas. Neither an AGN-light echo nor a fast radiative shock likely takes place due to the line diagnostics for spatially-resolved components of OIIIB-4 and no detections of 4686 or 3346,3426 lines that are fast-radiative shock signatures. There is a possibility that the spatially-extended \ emission of OIIIB-4 is originated from outflowing gas produced by the intense star formation in a density-bounded ionization state.

gmw_00006683.name: PKS 2243 123
gmw_00006683.overview: PKS 2243 123 is a blue radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z=0.63 [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171]. The galaxy exhibits near-infrared micro-variability in both its J and H bands, though its optical and near-infrared spectral energy distribution shows no evidence of synchrotron emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2243 123 as a blue radio-loud quasar whose optical/NIR SED lacks synchrotron evidence and whose NIR J and H bands show significant micro-variability.

quotes.quote: PKS 2243 123: A quasar, z=0.63 , that was described as optically variable by Shen et al. \ (1998), although they did not give a reference for this classification (it possibly comes from the description as variable in Hewitt Burbidge (1993)). It has a blue optical/NIR spectral energy distribution (SED) from FWW, with B-K=2.15 . WWF modelled the SEDs of many PHFS sources by fitting a combination of a blue power law plus a synchrotron model with a turn-over. This source was best fit with a power law of the form ^ 1.92 , and showed no evidence for synchrotron emission in its optical/NIR SED.

RESULTS Near-infrared micro-variability is detected in one source, PKS 2243 123. There is significant variability in both J and H bands, while at K_n band, despite both the Q-S curves showing variability (with a greater _T than the other two bands), there is also significant noise present in the comparison light-curve, making it impossible to say at 99 confidence that the quasar itself is varying.

gmw_00006684.name: IRAS F10173+0829
gmw_00006684.overview: IRAS F10173+0829 is a galaxy classified as an hydroxyl megamaser [arXiv:1211.2023]. Although previous observations reported features consistent with Zeeman splitting in the galaxy, subsequent analysis indicates that this apparent signal is not real [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper revises the earlier Zeeman-splitting claim for IRAS F10173+0829, finding weaker Stokes V features and attributing the apparent signal to interference rather than magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10173+0829 : R08 reported features consistent with Zeeman splitting in their observations of this OHM. Unfortunately, the Stokes V spectrum composed by taking the median of 1-second and 4-minute spectra found weaker features in the Stokes V spectra than were seen by R08. Further, the Stokes V spectra of other sources at the same frequency had Stokes V features similar to those seen in IRAS F10173+0829.

Figure fig:10173 shows the Stokes V spectrum of the 1612 MHz line of IRAS F15107+0724 compared to that of IRAS F10173+0829. IRAS F15107+0724 showed no evidence of maser emission at frequencies between 1589.2--1589.4 MHz in its Stokes I spectrum, yet had a similar Stokes V signal to IRAS F10173+0829. This not only means that the apparently detected Zeeman splitting is not real, but also prevents placing meaningful limits on magnetic fields in this OHM.

gmw_00006685.name: IRAS F08071+0509
gmw_00006685.overview: IRAS F08071+0509 is a galaxy classified as a composite active galactic nucleus and starburst [arXiv:1211.2023]. It hosts a detected hydroxyl megamaser, which exhibits a 1667 MHz line flux of 35 mJy but lacks circular polarization features [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper clearly detects the maser in IRAS F08071+0509, measures its 1667 MHz flux and luminosity, and sets a magnetic-field upper limit despite no convincing circular polarization.

quotes.quote: IRAS F08071+0509 : Bottinelli1989 reported this OHM with = 2.25 , but did not publish a spectrum or the flux of the 1667 MHz line. Baan1998 classified it as a composite AGN/starburst in a survey to optically classify megamaser galaxies. We clearly detect the maser, but there are no circular polarization features, as can be seen in Figure fig:08071 . The flux of the 1667 MHz line is 35 mJy, and the isotropic luminosity is = 2.12 . For their likely choice of cosmology, this would correspond to = 2.03 , which is roughly 70 less luminous than they reported.

gmw_00006686.name: IRAS F09039+0503
gmw_00006686.overview: IRAS F09039+0503 is an OH megamaser galaxy [arXiv:1211.2023]. Its spectrum features multiple narrow peaks that have yielded some of the strongest confident magnetic field detections in its survey class [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper reports absent or marginal features compared with earlier spectra, but clear Stokes V Zeeman detections in IRAS F09039+0503 with the strongest confident fields in the survey.

quotes.quote: IRAS F09039+0503 : DG01 reported this OHM, identifying weak 1665 MHz emission as well as a broad blueshifted 1667 MHz component at 37,300 in addition to the main features between 37,500--37,900 . The 1665 MHz feature is apparently absent in this more recent spectrum, shown in Figure fig:09039 , and the blueshifted 1667 MHz component is marginally detected. The central part of the spectrum features multiple narrow peaks, ideal for Zeeman detections. The Stokes V spectrum shows two clear features, each associated with the bright, narrow peaks, and fit by Gaussians 0 and 2.

The field reported for Gaussian 0 is -16.1 2.7 mG, while Gaussian 2 has an associated field of -27.6 5.5 mG. The Stokes V feature fit by Gaussian 2 is clearly asymmetric, possibly as a result of the relatively wide splitting relative to the line width. None of the other fitted components, shown in Table tab:09039 , are considered significant. Of all sources in the survey with magnetic fields detections in which we are confident, the fields reported here for IRAS F09039+0503 are the strongest.

gmw_00006687.name: IRAS F16255+2801
gmw_00006687.overview: IRAS F16255+2801 is a galaxy that hosts a 1667 MHz hydroxyl megamaser [arXiv:1211.2023]. Its emission spectrum features multiple overlapping peaks and exhibits a clear Zeeman structure associated with strong magnetic fields [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes IRAS F16255+2801 as a 1667 MHz OHM with multiple overlapping peaks and clear Zeeman structure, though derived field strengths remain somewhat uncertain.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16255+2801 : This OHM was discovered and characterized by DG01. Like DG01, we only detect 1667 MHz emission. The Stokes I spectrum, shown in Figure fig:16255 (with fits listed in Table tab:16255 ), features four main overlapping peaks, and there is significant structure in the Stokes V spectrum at the same frequency. The Stokes I could be fit well with five Gaussian components, but the addition of one more low amplitude, narrow component on the red side of the spectrum significantly improved the fit to the Stokes V spectrum, and even then the Stokes V fit is still poor.

There is clear, strong structure, and the fields associated with Gaussian 1, 2, and 3 are all significant: 7.0 1.9 mG, 9.6 2.0 mG, and 26.8 4.0 mG. Given the large field claimed for Gaussian 3, and the relative narrowness of the lines, the narrow line assumption we use in fitting fields is perhaps pushed past its limit, as was the case for IRAS F04332+0209. We experimented with separately fitting the RCP and LCP, using only five Gaussian components, and deriving magnetic field estimates using the frequency separation. The results are actually fairly similar, though the reported fields for each component are 20--30 smaller.

gmw_00006688.name: IRAS F18588+3517
gmw_00006688.overview: IRAS F18588+3517 is a galaxy that hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:1211.2023]. The megamaser emission contains multiple narrow components in the 1667 MHz line, as well as a weaker 1665 MHz component [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper finds multiple narrow components in IRAS F18588+3517, including matched 1665/1667 MHz features with consistent magnetic fields and another significant field detection.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18588+3517 : DG01 discovered this OHM, which has multiple narrow components in the 1667 MHz line, as well as a weak, but clearly distinguished, 1665 MHz component. Figure fig:18588 shows the Stokes I and V features for this source, and Table tab:18588 lists the fits. To fit the 1667 MHz features, we used six components, including one broad component and three narrow components where the emission is strongest. We also fit three components to the 1665 MHz line, which at first glance seems a large number given the strength of the 1665 MHz features.

Gaussian 2, in particular, is fit to a feature that is of smaller amplitude and width than we would ordinarily fit, but it is motivated by the fact that the velocity of the slight peak to which Gaussian 2 is fitted matches extremely well with the velocity of Gaussian 5. The widths are moderately different, but close enough to plausibly be the same feature. The reported magnetic fields are also in good agreement, with Gaussian 2 giving 16.3 4.2 mG, and Gaussian 5 giving 12.8 2.1 mG. Combining the two gives 13.9 1.9 mG for the region that produces the matched 1665/1667 lines.

gmw_00006689.name: Z3146 BCG
gmw_00006689.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy Z3146 BCG is an active, infrared-luminous system with a massive reservoir of cold molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0610164, arXiv:2102.02300]. It exhibits strong star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0610164, arXiv:1005.1211], possible radio active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0610164], and tidal features that suggest a merging galaxy [arXiv:2102.02300].

sources.summary: The paper presents the Z3146 brightest cluster galaxy as an infrared-luminous, star-forming BCG with possible radio AGN activity and unusually strong molecular hydrogen emission and warm gas.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) is exceptionally active, showing a blue continuum and luminous nebular emission lines Allen92, Hicks05 and an infrared luminosity of 4 10^ 11 L _ Egami06 . The mid-infrared spectral energy distribution (SED) suggests that the source of the large infrared luminosity is star formation at a rate of 70 M_ yr ^ 1 , broadly consistent with the mass deposition rate mentioned above. This may indicate that the active star formation is caused by cooling cluster gas accreting onto the BCG, i.e., the cooling flow Cowie77, Fabian77 . At the same time, the BCG seems to contain a radio AGN that may contribute to its luminosity Egami06 .

The final combined IRS spectrum of the BCG in Z3146 is shown in Figure irs . The measured line fluxes and rest-frame equivalent widths are listed in Table flux . Along the spatial direction, the lines and continuum are unresolved (FWHM 2 pixels), which sets an upper limit of 3 6 in SL and 10 2 in LL, respectively, on the size of the mid-infrared emitting region. This is consistent with the fact that the Z3146 BCG is barely resolved at 8 m with a spatial resolution of 2 \ Egami06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Zw3146 as a strong-MIR BCG with relatively bright CO, marginal long-band SPIRE detection, varied radio-FIR-optical SED behavior, and a strongly star-forming FIR SED.

quotes.quote: Abell 1068 and Zw3146 both have strong MIR emission (O'Dea et al. 2008, Egami et al. 2006) with a relatively bright CO detection (Edge 2001) and a weak central radio source (McNamara et al. 2004).

A2597 and Zw3146, while clearly detected in PSW and PMW bands, have a 1--2 detection in the PLW band. Table 1 gives the photometric results for the three galaxies, with 2 upper-limit for A2597 and Zw3146 in PLW. Figure 1 presents the radio to optical spectral energy distributions (SEDs) for the three targets. These plots show the significant variation in the relative radio-FIR-optical contributions for each of our galaxies.

The FIR emission is much stronger relative to the optical in A1068 and Zw3146 as compared to A2597. The SEDs of A1068 and Zw3146 resemble those of strongly star-forming systems and, based on the total FIR luminosity derived here, we find star formation rates (SFR) of 60 and 44 M _ yr ^ 1 in these two systems using the Kennicutt (1998) conversion factor.

sources.summary: ALMA reveals the Zw 3146 BCG contains a massive central molecular reservoir, X-ray-cavity-trailing extensions, an offset continuum source, and possible merger-related tidal features.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The BCG of the ZwCl 3146 galaxy cluster (2MASX J10233960+0411116 -- R.A.: 10:23:39.6340, decl.: +4:11:10.660)

quotes.quote: Here we present a new ALMA observation targeting the CO(1-0) emission line in the BCG of Zw 3146, which is one of the most extreme cool core clusters and brightest CO emitters known. If left unimpeded, its soft ( 0.1-2.4 ) X-ray luminosity of 2 45 Ebeling98, Bohringer00 would lead to a mass deposition rate of 1250 Edge94 . Indeed, the BCG in Zw 3146 is exceptionally active, showing a blue continuum and luminous nebular emission lines Allen92, Hicks05 , an infrared luminosity of 4 11 Egami06 , an 8 10 reservoir of cold molecular gas Edge01, Edge03 , and strong molecular H _2 emission lines from 10^ 10 of warm H _2 Egami06b .

The molecular gas and continuum source are mutually offset by 2.6 kpc, with no detected line emission coincident with the continuum source. It is the molecular gas, not the continuum source, that lies at the gravitational center of the brightest cluster galaxy. As the brightest cluster galaxy contains possible tidal features, the displaced continuum source may correspond to the nucleus of a merging galaxy. We also discuss the possibility that a gravitational wave recoil following a black hole merger may account for the displacement.

sources.summary: The paper finds ZwCl 3146 favors very young stellar populations, multiple outbursts extending to 6 Gyr, and similar SFRs for 10-Myr and 2-Myr burst models.

quotes.quote: While RXC J1504 is best fitted by a model with a single burst at 10 Myr, the other three seem to prefer multiple bursts with the shortest possible spacing (10 Myr), with ZwCl 3146 and A 1795 going up to the oldest age of 6 Gyr and A 1835 going up to the oldest age of about 3.5 Gyr.

In order to investigate whether even younger starbursts might fit the SEDs better, we designed bursts 2 Myr apart for ZwCl 3146 and found that best-fit results were still favouring the youngest stellar population 2 Myr old with multiple outbursts going up to 6 Gyr.
The median star formation rates inferred from the two models for ZwCl 3146 based on multiple starbursts 10-Myr (14.4 ) and 2 Myr (16 ) apart are nearly the same.

gmw_00006690.name: UGC 10738
gmw_00006690.overview: UGC 10738 is an edge-on disk galaxy with a stellar mass and color highly comparable to the Milky Way [arXiv:2105.10649]. Located at a distance of 99 Mpc, its stellar populations show that the mean age of stars increases with height above the galactic plane [arXiv:2105.10649].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 10738 as a Milky-Way-like edge-on galaxy, mapping its stellar populations and finding vertical metallicity, alpha-abundance, age, and disk-flaring trends.

quotes.quote: UGC 10738 has a stellar mass of 8 10^ 10 M _ and a (g-) colour of 0.95, highly comparable to the Milky Way and references therein Bland-Hawthorn:2016 . We note that UGC 10738 is larger than the Milky Way by 50 per cent in effective radius, though a precise comparison is unfeasible given the very different approaches to measuring size in extragalactic objects and the Milky Way.

Observations and data reduction UGC 10738 was observed with MUSE on the VLT as part of observing program 0101.B-0706(A) (PI: van de Sande) on 16-04-2018 and 17-04-2018 in service mode. MUSE provides a 1 \ field-of-view with a spatial sampling of 0 2 and spectral coverage from 480 to 930 nm at a resolving power R 2700 . At our adopted distance, D = 99 \ Mpc Tully:2013 , the spatial sampling is 91 pc/spaxel.

In Figure fig:population_maps we show the average, mass-weighted age, [ Z/H ] and [ /Fe ] of UGC 10738 in the extracted regions. From low to high |z| , [ Z/H ] generally decreases and [ /Fe ] generally increases at all radii, with the exception of the |z| < 0.5 \ kpc region which is somewhat lower in [ Z/H ] than the adjacent height bins, which may be due to dust extinction as described above. The mean age of stars increases with height above the plane but declines with radius, such that the oldest stars typically lie in the centre of the galaxy at large heights.

gmw_00006691.name: HDF 130
gmw_00006691.overview: HDF 130 is a massive elliptical galaxy at redshift z=1.99 [arXiv:0902.3117, arXiv:1107.0824] hosting a compact active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0902.1528, arXiv:0902.3117]. It features an old stellar population [arXiv:0902.1528, arXiv:0902.3117] and double-lobed X-ray emission, which is interpreted as an inverse-Compton ghost of a former giant radio galaxy [arXiv:0902.3117, arXiv:1107.0824].

sources.summary: The paper presents HDF130 as a massive z~2 radio galaxy with compact core radio emission, two-sided low-resolution structure, old stellar population, and little evidence for dusty star formation.

quotes.quote: We present high spatial resolution MERLIN 1.4GHz radio observations of two high redshift (z 2) sources, \ (HDF147) and \ (HDF130), selected as the brightest radio sources from a sample of submillimetre-faint radio galaxies. They have starburst classifications from their rest-frame UV spectra. However, their radio morphologies are remarkably compact ( < 80mas and < 65mas respectively), demanding that the radio luminosity be dominated by Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN) rather than starbursts. Near-IR imaging ( HST NICMOS F160W) shows large scale sizes (R _ 1/2 0.75 , diameters 12kpc) and SED fitting to photometric points (optical through the mid-IR) reveals massive ( 5 10 ^ 11 M _ ), old (a few Gyr) stellar populations. Both sources have low flux densities at observed 24 and are undetected in observed 70 \ and 850 , suggesting a low mass of interstellar dust.

The radio structure of HDF130 (J123617+621540) is compact in the high resolution images presented here, however from lower resolution (0.5 ) imaging of these data Muxlow et al. (2005) showed this source to have compact core with two-sided radio emission extending over a linear extent of 1.3 . The HDF147 radio morphology of Fig. 1 exhibits a low luminosity ( 20 ) extension along the galaxy's minor axis. This is a possible candidate for radio jet outflows but provides no obvious clue to source orientation. If star formation were the source of radio emission, both sources would exceed the maximum star formation density possible for a given mass and dynamical timescale elmegreen99a . HDF147 and HDF130 have limits on their radio size smaller than most local ULIRGs.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HDF 130 as a massive z=1.99 elliptical with a compact AGN, interpreting its double-lobed X-ray emission as an inverse-Compton ghost of a former giant radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: Casey et al (2008) have recently found that object HDF 130, at the centre of one of these extended sources (Source 2, CDFN J123620.0+621554, of Bauer et al 2002), is a massive elliptical galaxy at redshift z=1.99 , and not a starburst galaxy as earlier supposed. HST/NICMOS and Spitzer/IRAC imaging have revealed that the object has a massive ( 3 10^ 11 ) old stellar population with a large diameter of about 8 kpc. It is intrinsically weak at submillimetre wavelengths and has a compact radio source, indicative of a central Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). We investigate here the possibility that the double-lobed structure of the extended X-ray source is not then due to a surrounding merging cluster, as suggested by Bauer et al (2002), but instead to inverse Compton (IC) emission from a past outburst of the galaxy. The object would in the past have appeared as a giant radio galaxy, but inverse Compton losses have downshifted the high energy electrons responsible for radio emission so what remains is X-ray IC emission produced by Lorentz factor 1000 electrons scattering on the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB).

The photon index of the emission around HDF 130 is steeper ( 2.65 ) than that associated with 6C 0905+39 ( 1.8 ) but only marginally so when uncertainties are considered. This may indicate that spectral ageing is significant for the electrons in the HDF 130 source. The highest energy electrons lose their energy at a faster rate than lower energy ones, so leading to a steepening of the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper interprets HDF 130 as a massive z=1.99 elliptical and inverse-Compton ghost of a former FR II radio galaxy, with large X-ray lobes and weak radio emission after jet shutdown.

quotes.quote: We present new GMRT observations of HDF 130, an inverse-Compton (IC) ghost of a giant radio source that is no longer being powered by jets. We compare the properties of HDF 130 with the new and important constraint of the upper limit of the radio flux density at 240 MHz to an analytic model. We learn what values of physical parameters in the model for the dynamics and evolution of the radio luminosity and X-ray luminosity (due to IC scattering of the cosmic microwave background (CMB)) of a Fanaroff-Riley II (FR II) source are able to describe a source with features (lobe length, axial ratio, X-ray luminosity, photon index and upper limit of radio luminosity) similar to the observations. HDF 130 is found to agree with the interpretation that it is an IC ghost of a powerful double-lobed radio source, and we are observing it at least a few Myr after jet activity (which lasted 5 - 100 Myr) has ceased.

Previous HDF 130 observations HDF 130 was most recently analysed in the X-ray by . The source is a massive ( 5 10^ 11 M _ stellar population 2009MNRAS.395.1249C ) elliptical galaxy at z=1.99 with a roughly double-lobed structure in the X-ray and a compact radio nucleus. Each lobe is approximately 345 kpc long with an axial ratio (single lobe length divided by width of the lobe) of 2 2009MNRAS.395L..67F . The X-ray image of HDF 130 observed in the Chandra Deep Field North 2 Ms exposure was best modelled with a photon index of =2.65 by and has a 2 - 10 keV luminosity of 5.4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006692.name: J104806+573035
gmw_00006692.overview: J104806+573035 is a restarted radio-source candidate that exhibits a structure composed of two double lobes [arXiv:2011.08239]. While it resembles a double-double radio galaxy, it possesses an uncommon Fanaroff-Riley class I morphology with ultra-steep spectrum outer lobes and no hotspots [arXiv:2011.08239].

sources.summary: The paper describes J104806+573035 as a restarted radio-source candidate with two double lobes, USS outer lobes, and an FRI-like morphology uncommon among double-double radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: J104806+573035 is located in the area covered by the old WSRT mosaic and was already noted to have extreme spectral properties by and . However, no analysis of the resolved spectral index was presented. This source has a structure made by two double lobes, with the external ones being USS. To the first order, the structure resembles that of a double-double radio source, although it has the morphology typical of an FRI, with no hot-spots at the end of the lobes. This, together with the USS outer lobes, are uncommon properties in double-double radio galaxies ( Mahatma19 ).

gmw_00006693.name: J104842+585326
gmw_00006693.overview: J104842+585326 is an uncertain radio source that exhibits a very low surface brightness in LOFAR imaging [arXiv:2011.08239]. It possibly possesses a double-lobed structure and may represent an FRII radio galaxy in the process of switching off [arXiv:2011.08239].

sources.summary: The paper treats J104842+585326 as an uncertain radio-source case with low LOFAR surface brightness, possible double-lobed structure, steep-spectrum emission, and a possible FRII switching-off interpretation.

quotes.quote: J104842+585326 is a more uncertain case. It has very low surface brightness in the LOFAR image at 6 arcsec resolution and possibly has a double-lobed structure. Nevertheless, most of the structure shows > 1 . Thus, based on the likely optical identification, the northern side (where the spectral index still has the typical value of 0.8) could actually be the location of a \ hot-spot. Therefore, it could possibly be an FRII source in the process of switching off instead of a restarted source.

gmw_00006694.name: J104913+575008
gmw_00006694.overview: J104913+575008 is an extended radio source hosted by a disk-like galaxy [arXiv:2011.08239]. The source features a central unresolved region surrounded by highly asymmetric, ultra-steep spectrum diffuse emission and a radio lobe [arXiv:2011.08239].

sources.summary: The paper describes J104913+575008 as an unusual disk-galaxy-hosted extended radio source with asymmetric USS emission, possible jet-ISM interaction, and an uncertain switched-off classical-double alternative.

quotes.quote: J104913+575008 is also located in the area covered by the old WSRT and was mentioned by and . It is an intriguing source with a central, unresolved region surrounded by highly asymmetric, USS diffuse emission and a lobe (undetected at 1400 MHz). It is hosted by a disk-like galaxy (see Fig. \ B.1 in Mahony16 ), which is quite uncommon for extended radio sources. Even rarer, it has the spectral information of the extended emission. The disk-like galaxy may suggest the presence of a gas-rich interstellar medium (ISM), where the jet (which in this case has relatively low power, 10^ 24 ) may experience strong interaction and even (temporary) disruption.

Thus, in this source, the relatively bright core may also be the result of the interaction (e.g. \ similar to the case of PKS 1814-637; Morganti11 ). Another possibility is that this source is a switched-off classical double, with buoyancy directed towards the south. The real nature of this source will require follow-up observations (including of the environment).

gmw_00006695.name: J105554+563537
gmw_00006695.overview: J105554+563537 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a double-lobed structure and a detected radio core [arXiv:2011.08239]. It features an extremely steep spectrum, which suggests it is an aged remnant source that may reside at a high redshift [arXiv:2011.08239].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J105554+563537 as an especially interesting radio galaxy with an extremely steep spectrum, double-lobed structure, detected core, candidate optical identification, and possible high redshift.

quotes.quote: One particularly interesting object is J105554+563537, which shows an extremely steep spectrum ( 1.8 ) but a double-lobed structure. This source (as well as J110255+585740) was already listed by based on the integrated spectral index. The extreme steepness of the spectrum suggests an aged source that, nevertheless, appears to have maintained the double-lobed structure. Interestingly, the J105554+563537 radio galaxy has a core detected in deep VLA observations at 6 GHz (Jurlin et al. \ in preparation) and a candidate optical identification. The photometric redshift for this object is highly uncertain, but we cannot exclude a relatively high redshift z 2 based on available, but limited, photometric data and requiring higher quality observations to be confirmed.

While an impact from inverse Compton scattering cannot be excluded, the spectral index of J105554+563537 is extreme, even for high- z radio galaxies. Therefore, we consider that the extreme spectrum of this object must be (at least partly) indicative of a remnant source.

gmw_00006696.name: J110317+573757
gmw_00006696.overview: J110317+573757 is a radio galaxy candidate for restarted activity that features a core detected in high spatial resolution LOFAR imaging [arXiv:2011.08239]. The source exhibits two asymmetric lobes, with ultra-steep spectrum emission in the western lobe and an uncertain eastern component [arXiv:2011.08239].

sources.summary: The paper describes J110317+573757 as a restarted-source candidate with a prominent core, asymmetric lobes, USS emission, and an uncertain eastern component.

quotes.quote: J110317+573757 is another case of a source with a relatively prominent core, as can be seen from the higher spatial resolution LOFAR image shown in Fig. fig:partUSS_HR . The source shows two asymmetric lobes, the western of which has USS emission and a flatter spectral index (still at least 1 ) at its edge, perhaps indicating the presence of a (remnant) hot-spot. The eastern lobe has a spectral index steeper than 1 . It is actually unclear whether the eastern-most component is part of the source.

Four of the candidates in this group (J104913+575008, J105221+585054, J110135+580653, and J110317+573757) show relatively prominent nuclear or core emission. This can be seen from their LOFAR images at full spatial resolution (6 arcsec) and presented in Fig. fig:partUSS_HR . This is interesting because used this property (i.e. \ the core prominence) as a signature of restarted activity.

gmw_00006697.name: VUDS 511227001
gmw_00006697.overview: VUDS 511227001 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.64 [arXiv:2004.00158, arXiv:2202.02859]. It exhibits Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2004.00158] and has been identified as a Lyman continuum emitting candidate [arXiv:2004.00158, arXiv:2202.02859].

sources.summary: The paper treats Candidate id 368 as a q1 z=3.64 star-forming LCG: Ly and UV/ISM features support the redshift, LyC is detected, and MOSFIRE lines rule out low-redshift contamination.

quotes.quote: Candidate id 368 has detected Ly with rest frame EW(Ly ) 25 . The Ly spectral profile, appropriately strong Ly forest break, UV continuum profile, and ISM line absorption features are consistent with a z=3.64 star forming galaxy. Further inspection of the spectra did not reveal any other lines that could be related to low redshift objects.

On the contrary, in this work using CLAUDS u band photometry we detect a LyC signal from candidate id 368, and its z=3.64 has now been additionally confirmed with MOSFIRE spectroscopy where the nebular emission lines [ OIII ] doublet and H are detected (Bassett et al., in prep). Beside nebular lines, the MOSFIRE spectrum reveals no other lines, which additionally confirms that detected LyC signal is not contaminated by low redshift interlopers. VUDS and MOSFIRE spectra are shown in the Appendix B. In Section 5 , we will refer to the candidate id 368 as a first quality candidate (q1).

sources.summary: The paper focuses its LyC escape-fraction analysis on VUDS 511227001, a z=3.64 galaxy detected in CLAUDS u-band data and modeled with SED/IGM transmission ensembles.

quotes.quote: The final galaxy in our sample is selected from the VIMOS Ultra Deep Survey VUDS ID 511227001 marchi15 , and already has a secure spectroscopic redshift measurement of z=3.64 . We carried out spectroscopic observations using MOSFIRE to confirm ZFOURGE photometric redshifts for the remaining targets (see Section section:lineflux ). Accurate redshifts are critical as our key selection criteria for LyC emitting candidates is clean u band detection see, e.g. bassett19 , probing LyC flux above z 3 (exclusively so above z=3.4 ).

Thus, the remaining ZFOURGE galaxies are removed from the sample for further analysis and only VUDS 511227001 will be considered in the context of LyC escape. The LyC emission for VUDS511227001 has already been explored by using VIMOS spectroscopy, 1.3 marchi17 and mestric20 . The latter study makes use of the same CLAUDS u band observations used here in which VUDS 511227001 is detected at 27.82 mag (3.05 ).

gmw_00006698.name: ID 24
gmw_00006698.overview: ID 24 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.9552 [arXiv:1405.6484]. It features a peculiar radio structure consisting of four components, including a core galaxy and a pair of lobes where the southern lobe undergoes a diffuse 90-degree bend [arXiv:1405.6484].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 24/S447 as a powerful high-redshift radio source with four components, a core galaxy and bent lobes, possibly one of the most distant bent-tailed sources known.

quotes.quote: ID 24 or S447 has a peculiar radio structure consisting of four radio components with an overall 1.4 GHz rest-frame power of 5.98 10 ^ 26 . As stated in mbf13 , the north-eastern component coincides with an optical ID and appears to be a single separate source in the high resolution VLA image. Although ID 24 may not be unambiguously classified as a BT, it appears to consist of a core galaxy with a pair of lobes to the north and south, of which the latter undergoes a diffuse 90 ^ bend. ID 24, which was reported the farthest such source ever detected in djm11 , has a central component identified with an optical counterpart located at z=1.9552. The discovery of ID 24 along with ID 52, at z=2.1688, extends the existence of distorted radio sources as far back as the formation of galaxy clusters, which adds a valuable clue to the mutual evolution hypothesis.

gmw_00006699.name: ID 52
gmw_00006699.overview: ID 52 is a bent-tailed radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.1688 [arXiv:1405.6484]. The galaxy features a continuous tail with three hotspot regions that extends over 870 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1405.6484].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 52/S306 as a z=2.1688 bent-tailed radio galaxy with an optical counterpart, an extended hotspot tail, and possible source blending in its northern peak.

quotes.quote: ID 52 or S306 in the ATLAS survey is a BT radio galaxy with an optical counterpart located at z=2.1688, making it the farthest BT galaxy discovered to date. The tail of ID 52 with three hotspot regions extends to 104 arcseconds, which corresponds to over 870 kpc at the host galaxy's redshift. Note that the ID 52 continuous tail is only visible in the third data release of ATLAS given the improved signal to noise ratio. Alternatively, ID 52 may consist of two unique sources, considering that one of the intensity peaks towards the north is associated with an optical identification (MRSS 418-056517). Nevertheless, whether or not the latter scenario is the case, the southern section is still classified as a BT radio galaxy.

gmw_00006700.name: PID 203
gmw_00006700.overview: PID 203 is a bright, radio-loud Type I quasar located at a redshift of 0.544 [arXiv:1412.5326, arXiv:2004.07604]. It exhibits X-ray flux variability [arXiv:1412.5326], modest X-ray absorption [arXiv:1412.5326], and broad Fe K emission that suggests its X-ray spectrum is dominated by disc emission [arXiv:1412.5326, arXiv:2004.07604].

sources.summary: The paper presents PID 203 as a bright CDFS Type I AGN/QSO with X-ray variability, modest absorption, and broad Fe K emission likely from its accretion disk.

quotes.quote: We present results from the deep XMM-Newton observations of the two brightest X-ray sources in the Chandra Deep Field South (CDFS), PID 203 ( z=0.544 ) and PID 319 ( z=0.742 ). The long exposure of 2.5 Ms over a 10 year period (net 4 yr with a 6 yr gap) makes it possible to obtain high quality X-ray spectra of these two Type I AGN with X-ray luminosity of 10^ 44 , typical luminosity for low-redshift PG quasars, track their X-ray variability both in flux and spectral shape. Both sources showed X-ray flux variability of 10-20 in rms which is similar in the soft (0.5-2 keV) and hard (2-7 keV) bands. PID 203, which has evidence for optical extinction, shows modest amount of absorption ( 1 10^ 21 ) in the X-ray spectrum. Fe K emission is strongly detected in both objects with EW 0.2 keV.

The X-ray source is classified optically as broad-line AGN ( z=0.544 ) in Szokoly et al (2004) with a detection of broad Mg ii (Fig. 2). However, the optical spectrum shows self-absorbing features in the emission-lines (Mg ii and H ) and a redder continuum than optically-selected QSOs normally exhibit, indicating the presence of moderate extinction.

sources.summary: S404 is compact in VLBI and X-ray classified as a type 1 QSO, with a flat radio spectrum, unresolved HST counterpart, and mild radio excess.

quotes.quote: S404 The VLBI images of this source display almost all the flux density found in the ATCA observations. It has a compact morphology and its X-ray properties classify it as a type 1 QSO ( Luo2008 , Szokoly2004 ). It has a spectral index of 0.0 ( Kellermann2008 ), indicating a compact, self-absorbed radio source, and its redshift is 0.54 ( Norris2006a ). The HST images show an unresolved object with diffraction spikes, indicative of a point-like optical source. Its q_ 24 , however, is 0.31 which is only a mild radio excess over the radio-infrared relation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID 203 as a radio-loud, very bright unobscured AGN whose X-ray slope resembles radio-quiet AGN and whose broad Fe K emission suggests disc-dominated X-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: These 16 radio-loud AGN include the two brightest X-ray sources in the field, PID 203 and PID 319, and the optically faint, X-ray bright obscured quasar at z=1.6 , PID 352 vignali15 . PID 319 and PID 352 are reported to have an extended radio morphology hales14,huynh15 : PID 352 is a powerful radio galaxy with log P_ 1.4 =27.1 [ W Hz ^ 1 ] with a double radio-lobe vignali15 .

The other three, PID 203, PID 319 and PID 407 have X-ray slopes = 0.8 0.9, similar to the standard radio-quiet AGN slope. The former two are the bright X-ray sources and exhibit broad Fe K emission i15brtwo , suggesting that the disc emission dominates their X-ray spectra.
The two brightest sources (PID 203 and PID 319) in the field are unobscured AGN and indeed are broad Fe K line emitters i15brtwo .

gmw_00006701.name: PID 319
gmw_00006701.overview: PID 319 is a radio-loud, unobscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.07604] and type 1 quasar [arXiv:1011.2400] located at a redshift of approximately 0.74 [arXiv:1412.5326]. It exhibits an extended radio morphology [arXiv:2004.07604], broad iron K emission [arXiv:1412.5326, arXiv:2004.07604], and X-ray and ultraviolet variability [arXiv:1412.5326].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID 319 as the brightest XMM-CDFS QSO, with no excess absorption, broad Fe K emission, continuum curvature, and notable X-ray/UV variability.

quotes.quote: PID 319 is the brightest X-ray source in the XMM-CDFS and is identified with a QSO at redshift z=0.742 . The optical (rest-frame UV) spectrum presented in Szokoly et al (2004) shows clear, broad Mg ii emission (FWHM 11,000 km s ^ 1 ) on a blue continuum, typical of broad-line QSOs. The UV emission of PID 319 observed with the OM instrument (Antonucci et al 2014) showed a notable decrease in luminoisty from 1 10^ 30 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 during the 2001-2002 period (X1-X2) to 0.6 10^ 30 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 during the 2007-2010 period (X3-X6) at 2500 .

The mean X-ray spectrum of PID 319 obtained from the full exposure (Fig. 12) shows no evidence for excess cold absorption ( N_ H <6 10^ 19 , 90 upper limit). The Fe K feature (Fig. 12) is found at the rest-frame energy of 6.48 0.07 keV with significant broadening ( = 0.29^ +0.12 _ 0.09 keV, or FWHM 21,000 km s ^ 1 ) when a Gaussian is fitted (Table 5). The line flux is estimated to be 4.2^ +1.1 _ 0.9 10^ 7 , corresponding to a rest-frame EW of 0.20^ +0.05 _ 0.05 keV.

sources.summary: S437 is an AGN and powerful X-ray type 1 QSO; VLBI finds it unresolved while HST imaging shows a point-like component.

quotes.quote: S437 This source has been classified as AGN based on q_ 24 and the literature ( Norris2006a ). Only 4 of its ATCA flux density were recovered, and the VLBI images show an unresolved source. Yet it is a powerful X-ray source exceeding a luminosity of 10^ 37 W (0.5 keV -- 8 keV) and it has a hardness ratio of -0.48, indicating a type 1 QSO ( Luo2008 , Szokoly2004 ). The redshift of this object is 0.73 ( Afonso2006 ). It is one of four objects covered by the Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey (GOODS, Giavalisco2004 ) with the Hubble Space Telescope.

sources.summary: The paper describes PID 319 as a radio-loud, extended, very bright unobscured AGN with broad Fe K emission and an X-ray slope resembling standard radio-quiet AGN.

quotes.quote: These 16 radio-loud AGN include the two brightest X-ray sources in the field, PID 203 and PID 319, and the optically faint, X-ray bright obscured quasar at z=1.6 , PID 352 vignali15 . PID 319 and PID 352 are reported to have an extended radio morphology hales14,huynh15 : PID 352 is a powerful radio galaxy with log P_ 1.4 =27.1 [ W Hz ^ 1 ] with a double radio-lobe vignali15 .

The other three, PID 203, PID 319 and PID 407 have X-ray slopes = 0.8 0.9, similar to the standard radio-quiet AGN slope. The former two are the bright X-ray sources and exhibit broad Fe K emission i15brtwo , suggesting that the disc emission dominates their X-ray spectra.
The two brightest sources (PID 203 and PID 319) in the field are unobscured AGN and indeed are broad Fe K line emitters i15brtwo .

gmw_00006702.name: SDSS J022337.76+003230.6
gmw_00006702.overview: SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 is a radio-loud weak-line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3.05 [arXiv:1010.5386]. Unlike other objects in its class, this quasar exhibits an unusually low level of optical variability in the u and g bands [arXiv:1010.5386].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 as a radio-loud weak-line quasar at z about 3.05 with unusually low optical variability for its class.

quotes.quote: The three unusual quasars with the highest radio-loudness parameters are SDSS J000051.56+001202.5 ( R_ i = 1.89 ), a high-redshift ( z = 3.859 ) BAL quasar with broad Ly , Si iv , and C iv absorption troughs, SDSS J024224.02+010452.5 ( R_ i = 1.52 ), a medium-redshift ( z = 2.431 ) quasar with apparently weak emission lines and associated absorption lines, and SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 ( R_ i = 1.77 ), a weak-line quasar at z 3.05 . The first two are strongly variable in the optical, whereas SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 exhibits a remarkably low level of variability in the u and g bands.

However, the value of V_ u for SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 is a factor of 20 smaller than the mean value at z = 3 and, surprisingly, SDSS J022337.76+003230.6 is a member of the rare class of weak emission-line quasars (WLQs; Diamond-Stanic et al. ). WLQs are defined as quasars with extremely weak or undetectable emission lines of rest-frame equivalent width EW(Ly + N v ) < 10 \ compared to 50 100 \ for typical SDSS quasars (e.g., Fan et al. ; Diamond-Stanic et al. , and references therein). For SDSS J022337.76+003230.6, Diamond-Stanic et al. measured EW(Ly + N v ) = 8.6 .
This is clearly not the case for SDSS J022337.76+003230.6.

gmw_00006703.name: NGC 4242
gmw_00006703.overview: NGC 4242 is a low-surface-brightness disk galaxy classified as SAB(s)dm [arXiv:2105.13394]. It features a U-shaped color profile indicating inside-out growth, with heightened star formation in its mid-galactic regions and mostly symmetric neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2105.13394].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4242 as a low-surface-brightness disk galaxy with mid-disk young populations, a U-shaped color profile, and mostly symmetric H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 4242 NGC 4242 is classified as an SAB(s)dm (RC3), with a low surface brightness disc eskridge2002 . The B-R color and sSFR profiles for NGC 4242, shown in Figure fig:ngc4242_fulldata , suggest a younger stellar population with heightened star formation exists at mid-galactic regions. The signature "U-shaped" B-R colour profile (i.e., with a minimum blue colour in the middle region) indicates a galaxy that may have undergone inside-out growth in addition to the substantial building up of an older stellar halo through accretion/mergers bakos2008,Dale2016 .

Most of the HI is coincident with the stars, although there are detections of low surface density HI outside of the immediate region of the stellar disc (see Figure fig:ngc4242_4panel ). The area of highest current star formation, as indicated by both the EW(H ) profile and the SED-fitted SFH, is just within D _ 25 (as shown as the white ellipse in the velocity field panel of Figure fig:ngc4242_4panel ). The HI is mostly symmetric with only a slight extension to the southwest of the galaxy where the HI, as well as the H , extends further than the stellar population traced at 3.6 m.

gmw_00006704.name: J1428 + 3938
gmw_00006704.overview: J1428 + 3938 is a giant radio galaxy with a size exceeding 1 Mpc [arXiv:1702.01939]. Its central radio core coincides with a faint optical galaxy, suggesting a high redshift that is estimated to be approximately 0.5 [arXiv:1702.01939].

sources.summary: The paper models J1428 + 3938 as a giant radio galaxy with uncertain redshift, previously modeled lobes, and spectrum fits compared across KDA variants.

quotes.quote: J1428 + 3938 J1428 + 3938 is a giant-sized (D > 1 Mpc ) radio galaxy identified as a giant radio galaxy (GRG) by Machalski et al. (2006). Its central radio core that is precisely coincident with a faint optical galaxy with R=21.11 mag implies a high redshift; unfortunately, it has not been confirmed spectroscopically. Therefore, the authors estimate its value from the Hubble m_ R z relation for giant radio sources as about 0.5. Extended radio lobes of this source have been modelled by Machalski (2011) using the DYNAGE algorithm -- contrary to a simpler approach where a dynamical model is fitted to the spectrum of entire source, i.e. to a mean of its two lobes.

It is particularly interesting to examine how the KDA EXT model fits the observed spectrum of this source. I use the 151 MHz measurements as the lowest one and include the very uncertain Miyun measurement at 232 MHz .

gmw_00006705.name: J1453 + 3308
gmw_00006705.overview: J1453 + 3308 is a well-known double-double radio galaxy [arXiv:1702.01939]. Its structure consists of an extended pair of outer lobes and a slimmer pair of inner lobes, which are believed to result from secondary nuclear activity [arXiv:1702.01939].

sources.summary: The paper discusses J1453 + 3308 as a well-studied DDRG with outer and inner lobes, comparing KDA, KDA EXT, and prior model spectra.

quotes.quote: J1453 + 3308 J1453 + 3308 is the well-known DDRG galaxy whose radio structure has already been studied by several authors (e.g. Schoenmakers et al. 2000; Kaiser et al. 2000; Konar et al. 2006). It consists of two pairs of lobes, the extended outer lobes and slimmer inner ones, considered to be the result of secondary nuclear activity occurring after a termination of the primary jets. Spectral aging analysis of these pairs of lobes was published by Konar et al. (2006), while their dynamical analysis was undertaken by Machalski et al. (2009) (in the frame of a subsample of ten GRGs), and, independently by Brocksopp et al. (2011).

The values of ^ 2 _ red indicate that the KDA EXT fit is better than MJS2009 and KDA, though the differences in the goodness of the fits are low. These two continuum injection models reproduce well the low-frequency part of the observed spectrum; starting at some point, the KDA EXT spectrum is clearly more bent towards high frequencies.

gmw_00006706.name: AGC 219533
gmw_00006706.overview: AGC 219533 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2201.12852] with a low gas velocity dispersion [arXiv:2004.14392]. Its specific angular momentum is measured to be three to four times higher than the best-fit relation [arXiv:2004.14392].

sources.summary: The paper models AGC 219533 using known rotation and surface-density data, but finds NLG unsuitable and emphasizes severe observational limitations.

quotes.quote: AGC 219533 Among UDGs studied in , AGC 219533 has sufficient observational data to be explored here. More specifically, its observed rotation curve and the total surface density are known . The right panel of the first row of Fig. AGC114905 shows the total surface density of the galaxy. The black curve is obtained using emcee code by finding the best fit values of R_1 , R_2 , R_3 and _0 in the following function (R)= 1- R R_1 + R^2 R_2^2 _0 e^ R^2 R_3^2 ; indeed, the best fit values are R_1=31 kpc, R_2=14.20 kpc, R_3=10 kpc, _0= 4.03 M_ / pc ^2 .

For AGC 219533, we find ^2/ dof =5.99 , which confirms that NLG does not give a suitable fit. However, it is worth noting that there is a serious limitation to the validity of the observational data of this galaxy. The analysis done in LHJ suffers from a lack of careful kinematic modeling for the rotation curve and the surface densities.

sources.summary: The paper models AGC 219533 with two independent H I resolution elements, finds low gas velocity dispersion, and measures specific angular momentum 3-4 times above the best-fit relation.

quotes.quote: AGC 219533: Beam size of 14.93" 13.62", with orientation at 25.5 ^ . The projected beam radius along the kinematic major axis of the galaxy is 13.6". We use two independent resolution elements with RADSEP = 14".
The mean velocity dispersion for AGC 114905, AGC 219533, AGC 248945 and AGC 749290, with a channel width of _ v 4 km s ^ 1 , is = 3 2 km s ^ 1 , which is below _ v .
AGC 219533 and AGC 334315 have a j_ about 3--4 times larger than the best-fit line, although the observational scatter is relatively large at those values of M_* .

gmw_00006707.name: host of GRB 100418A
gmw_00006707.overview: The host of GRB 100418A is a luminous star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.6239 [arXiv:1807.04281]. It has a star formation rate of 12.2 solar masses per year and features two emission components with differing velocities [arXiv:1807.04281].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 100418A host as a luminous star-forming galaxy with two emission components, absorption and emission diagnostics, SFR 12.2 solar masses per year, and near-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: GRB 100418A, at a redshift of z=0.6239 , had a bright optical and radio afterglow, and a luminous star-forming host galaxy. This allowed us to study the radiation of the explosion as well as the interstellar medium of the host both in absorption and emission.

The host galaxy shows two components in emission, with velocities differing by 130 km s ^ 1 , but otherwise having similar properties. While some absorption and emission components coincide, the absorbing gas spans much higher velocities, indicating the presence of gas beyond the star-forming regions. The host has a star formation rate of SFR =12.2 M _ yr ^ 1 , a metallicity of 12+log(O/H) =8.55 , and a mass of 1.6 10^9 M _ .

gmw_00006708.name: UDS11655
gmw_00006708.overview: UDS11655 is a high-redshift disk galaxy candidate [arXiv:1608.01676]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet morphology reveals a spiral arm pattern, and its kinematics are well fitted by a rotating disk model with a structured velocity dispersion [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: UDS11655 is a disk candidate with spiral-arm UV morphology, a well-fit OSIRIS disk model, structured velocity dispersion, substantial gas fraction, and aligned HST and emission sizes.

quotes.quote: UDS11655 This is one of four IROCKS disk candidates whose HST rest frame UV image shows disk-like morphology with a spiral arm pattern. OSIRIS kinematic map is well fitted by a disk model (P.A. = 125 ^ , V_p = 140 km s ^ 1 ), with a small residual < > = 13.4 km s ^ 1 . It has of 55 km s ^ 1 , with higher dispersion ( 80 km s ^ 1 ) along the rotation axis and lower ( 20 km s ^ 1 ) off axis. This is the typical velocity dispersion structure for a disk galaxy.

gmw_00006709.name: UDS10633
gmw_00006709.overview: UDS10633 is a high-redshift galaxy with a large estimated stellar mass [arXiv:1608.01676]. Rest-frame ultraviolet imaging reveals a complex structure consisting of a northern compact source, a southern bar-like structure, and a southwestern compact source [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: UDS10633 has the largest stellar mass estimate in the sample, multiple UV components incompletely detected in emission, high dispersion, and OSIRIS dynamical masses treated as lower limits.

quotes.quote: UDS10633 This source has the largest stellar mass estimate ( M_*/ = 11.24) among our sample. The rest frame UV image from HST shows a compact source in the north, a bar-like structure in the south, and another compact source in the south-west. The does not show all of these components.

To align the two image, we match the south tip of the bar in HST to that of the image. Due to its unresolved size, value is negligible, but is still high ( = 54.5 km s ^ 1 ). Since some components are not detected, dynamical mass estimates from OSIRIS should be considered lower limits.

gmw_00006710.name: DEEP2-42042481
gmw_00006710.overview: DEEP2-42042481 is a disk galaxy candidate with a high velocity shear [arXiv:1608.01676]. It features a low gas fraction of 22 percent and a velocity dispersion profile characteristic of disk galaxies [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-42042481 is the largest single-component IROCKS galaxy, a disk candidate with high velocity shear, a good disk-model fit, disk-like dispersion structure, and low gas fraction.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-42042481 This is the largest single component galaxy in the IROCKS sample. It is one of four IROCKS disk candidates and has one of the higher ( = 180 km s ^ 1 ) and / ( / = 2.70) values in our sample. The OSIRIS kinematic map is well fitted by a disk model (P.A. = 153 ^ , V_p = 152 km s ^ 1 ) with a low residual, < > = 23.6 km s ^ 1 . The velocity dispersion has a slope that is perpendicular to the rotation axis, which is similar to disk galaxy velocity profiles. It has the stellar mass M_ * / = 10.62 and the lowest gas fraction (22 ) among the four IROCKS disk candidates.

gmw_00006711.name: J033249.73
gmw_00006711.overview: The high-redshift galaxy J033249.73 exhibits a compact eastern component in rest-frame ultraviolet imaging [arXiv:1608.01676]. This eastern structure is connected to a western arch component that displays a higher velocity dispersion [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: J033249.73 shows a compact eastern UV component linked to a western arch, no velocity gradient, and spatially varying dispersion between its eastern and western components.

quotes.quote: J033249.73 HST imaging in rest-frame UV shows a compact component in the east connected to a stretched arch component in the west. We match the bright compact source in HST with the bright detection to the south-east. The kinematic map does not show a velocity gradient, and the velocity dispersion varies across the whole galaxy. The east component has a lower dispersion of 30 km s ^ 1 while the west arch component has higher ( 100 km s ^ 1 ) dispersion.

gmw_00006712.name: TKRS11169
gmw_00006712.overview: TKRS11169 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of approximately 1.4, identified through X-ray observations [arXiv:1608.01676]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals two distinct components in the east and west, with the western component being 1.5 times brighter [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: TKRS11169 is the only z~1.4 source, an X-ray AGN with two HST-resolved components whose velocity dispersions exceed those of the z~1 sources.

quotes.quote: TKRS11169 This is the only source at z 1.4 in our sample. It is classified as AGN by X-ray observation. HST image shows two distinct components in the east and west, and both are resolved by and aligned to the HST images. The west component is brighter than the east by a factor of 1.5. Both components have significantly higher of 90 km s ^ 1 than the rest of the z 1 sources.

gmw_00006713.name: TKRS7187
gmw_00006713.overview: The galaxy TKRS7187 features three bright ultraviolet knots and a structure resembling two spiral arms [arXiv:1608.01676]. However, it lacks a supporting velocity gradient, and its unresolved western component likely represents an interacting system [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: TKRS7187 has multiple UV knots and spiral-arm-like structure but lacks a supporting velocity gradient; its unresolved western component has the sample’s highest velocity shear and likely interaction.

quotes.quote: TKRS7187 HST image shows three brighter spots (east, center, west) and m=2 like spiral arm, but does not show velocity gradient to support the disk model. The central bright spot in HST is matched with the central nod in detection. After separating components using 1D spectrum, the west component still has more than one peak in 1D spectrum that could not be spatially separated. This component has the highest of 240 km s ^ 1 in our sample and hence is most likely an interacting system.

gmw_00006714.name: TKRS9727
gmw_00006714.overview: TKRS9727 is a massive disk galaxy candidate with a high gas fraction of 55 percent [arXiv:1608.01676]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet morphology reveals a face-on spiral structure with a high star formation rate [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: TKRS9727 is a massive, high-SFR disk candidate with face-on spiral UV morphology, the smallest disk-model residual, high gas fraction, and matched HST-emission structures.

quotes.quote: TKRS9727 Among resolved IROCKS sample, this has the largest stellar mass ( M_*/ = 11.0) in our sample. It also has the highest SFR _ SED (159 ) and HII+ISM corrected SFR (SFR _ ^ 00 = 108 ) with the highest _V (3.66). This is one of four IROCKS disk candidates whose HST image at rest frame UV shows m=2 face-on spiral galaxy morphology, and OSIRIS kinematic map is well fitted by a disk model (P.A. = 224 ^ , V_p = 110 km s ^ 1 ) with the smallest residual < > = 13.2 km s ^ 1 . The galaxy has 55 gas fraction.

gmw_00006715.name: TKRS7615
gmw_00006715.overview: TKRS7615 is a galaxy with a face-on grand-design spiral morphology in the rest-frame ultraviolet [arXiv:1608.01676]. Its velocity dispersion is roughly uniform at approximately 70 km/s, but the rotation variation is too small to fit a disk model [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: TKRS7615 has face-on grand-design spiral UV morphology and roughly uniform dispersion, but its OSIRIS rotation variation is too small for disk-model fitting.

quotes.quote: TKRS7615 HST image at rest frame UV show face-on grand design spiral galaxy morphology, and OSIRIS rotation map shows subtle variation/gradient across the galaxy. Velocity dispersion is roughly uniform across the whole galaxy at around 70 km s ^ 1 . This can be a face-on disk, but the rotation variation detected by OSIRIS is too small to fit a disk model. The bright north part is matched with the central nod in HST, and the arch like south-east extended component to the south-west nod in are matched to the spiral arm in HST.

gmw_00006716.name: DEEP2-12008898
gmw_00006716.overview: DEEP2-12008898 is a galaxy system composed of two distinct components, featuring a smaller structure in the north and a larger structure in the south [arXiv:1608.01676]. The northern component contains two nodes visible in Hubble Space Telescope imaging and exhibits a velocity gradient [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-12008898 is one of the best detected IROCKS sources, with distinct northern and southern components, nearly uniform dispersions, and a southern component likely interacting.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-12008898 This is one of the best detected sources in our observations and used for local rotation and smoothing correction analysis in sec_kine_map . The system has two distinct components, small one in the north and the large one in the south. Both components are seen both in HST and . The north component: while HST shows two nods (northeast and southwest) extended 1 arcsec, shows one nod of 0.5 arcsec in northeast. It has some velocity gradient, but it is too under-sampled to fit to a disk model.

gmw_00006717.name: DEEP2-12019627
gmw_00006717.overview: DEEP2-12019627 is a galaxy system with a patchy morphology that is likely undergoing an interaction [arXiv:1608.01676]. It consists of a northern component made of three small nods, alongside southeastern and southwestern components that exhibit undersampled velocity gradients [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-12019627 has patchy HST and emission morphology with northern and southeastern/southwestern components, undersampled velocity gradients, and a probably interacting system.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-12019627 Both HST and show patchy morphology. We separate three north nods as the north component and the south ribbon shape part into the south-east and south-west components. The north component: three 0.5'' nods are spatially separated but are individually too small to form separate peak in the 1D spectrum, and hence three are together to form a north component.

They are also individually too small to see individual rotation. The south-east and -west components: both velocity fields show gradient but are undersampled to fit with disk models. Due to their complicated morphologies, the system is probably an interacting system.

gmw_00006718.name: DEEP2-13017973
gmw_00006718.overview: DEEP2-13017973 is a galaxy that exhibits a spiral disk-like morphology with several distinct knots [arXiv:1608.01676]. It features a high star formation rate but does not show a velocity gradient in its rotation map [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-13017973 was observed with the old OSIRIS grating, has spiral-like HST knots distinct from emission morphology, no velocity gradient, and high star formation rate.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-13017973 This is the only source in our sample detected with the old OSIRIS grating, and therefore the emission is slightly noisier than the majority of the sample. HST shows a few distinct knots in a spiral disk-like morpholog, but is quite distinct from the observed morphology. We match a few western knots in the HST image with that of the extended component observed in the west in . The rotation map does not show a velocity gradient. This galaxy has the highest uncorrected SFR (42.7 yr ^ 1 ), and the second highest HII+ISM corrected SFR (65.8 yr ^ 1 ) among z 1 IROCKS sample.

gmw_00006719.name: DEEP2-13043023
gmw_00006719.overview: DEEP2-13043023 is a galaxy with an irregular morphology featuring three northwestern knots and two southeastern knots [arXiv:1608.01676]. It displays no velocity gradient, with a uniform velocity dispersion of approximately 50 km/s except for a northeastern arm that reaches 120 km/s [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-13043023 has irregular HST morphology with knots, matched emission along an arch, no velocity gradient, and mostly uniform dispersion except a high-dispersion northeast arm.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-13043023 The HST image shows an irregular morphology with three knots in the north-west region and two in the south-east region. The image was matched to the HST arch connecting the south knot to the north-east knots in HST. The rotation map does not show a velocity gradient, and the dispersion is uniform around 50 km s ^ 1 except for the north east arm at = 120 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006720.name: DEEP2-32040603
gmw_00006720.overview: DEEP2-32040603 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.0338 [arXiv:1608.01676]. Although its emission shows signs of potential rotation, its detected surface area is too small to fit a disk model, and its velocity dispersion remains constant at 55 km/s [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-32040603 is the highest-redshift z~1 IROCKS galaxy; its emission may rotate, but the detected area is too small for disk fitting and dispersion is constant.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-32040603 This is the highest redshift galaxy ( z = 1.0338) among IROCKS z 1 sample. It can be rotating (blueshift at the northwest and redshift at the southeast), but the surface area of is not sufficient to fit a disk model. The velocity dispersion is constant over the galaxy at 55 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006721.name: DEEP2-33009979
gmw_00006721.overview: The DEEP2-33009979 system consists of two well-separated northern and southern components [arXiv:1608.01676]. The northern component is not well fit by an inclined disk model, whereas the southern component is a disk candidate with a uniform velocity dispersion of 60 km/s [arXiv:1608.01676].

sources.summary: DEEP2-33009979 has separated northern and southern components; the northern component is not disk-fit, while the southern disk candidate fits a disk model with uniform dispersion.

quotes.quote: DEEP2-33009979 This system has two well separated components in the north and south. The north component shows a slight velocity gradient, but it is not well fit to an inclined disk model. The velocity dispersion in the north component is uniform at 50 km s ^ 1 . The southern component is one of the four disk candidates and is well fitted by a disk model (P.A. = 250 ^ , V_p = 81.7 km s ^ 1 ) with a velocity residual of < > = 30.8 km s ^ 1 . The velocity dispersion is uniform over the galaxy at 60 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006722.name: Q0454-220 G1
gmw_00006722.overview: Q0454-220 G1 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.4838 [arXiv:0912.2746]. It exhibits a perturbed morphology characterized by a compact bulge and a single extended spiral arm [arXiv:0912.2746].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q0454-220 G1 as a perturbed spiral with a compact bulge, extended arm, flattened rotation curve, and absorption velocities partly consistent with its blueshifted rotation side.

quotes.quote: Q0454-220 G1 A WFPC--2/ HST image of the Q 0454-220 field is shown in Figure  fig:0450_0454 . The z=0.4838 galaxy, G1, was targeted for this study. G1 was spectroscopically identified by chen98 . The absorption was first reported by begeron84 and then confirmed by tytler87 .

G1 is a spiral galaxy that has a perturbed morphology with one extended spiral arm. It has a compact bulge and several bright regions. The galaxy has an inclination of i=41^ and a position angle of PA=76^ . It has an absolute B- band magnitude of M_B=-21.0 which translates to L_B=0.90L_B^ .

gmw_00006723.name: Mrk 34
gmw_00006723.overview: Mrk 34 is a Compton-thick [arXiv:1502.07353, arXiv:2202.06788] Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2209.14326, arXiv:0905.3349] hosted by a morphologically disturbed Sa-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2202.06788]. The host galaxy features a weak bar structure [arXiv:2202.06788, arXiv:1809.09105], a luminous water megamaser [arXiv:0905.3349, arXiv:astro-ph/0503070], and energetic, large-scale gas outflows [arXiv:2202.06788, arXiv:1809.09105].

sources.summary: The paper compares torus and mytorus fits for Mrk 34, finding poor opening-angle constraints and a very high column where mytorus reaches its limit.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity. Particular attention was paid to selecting sources in the low ( 10^ 42 ) and high ( 10^ 44 ) luminosity regimes, thus enveloping the bulk of the local CT AGN population, which is at moderate luminosities, so that our sample could be as representative as possible, despite its small size.

While the fit to Mrk 34 gives a fairly large , the constraints are poor, with statistically allowed values ranging from 26--80 . The case is similar for 2MFGC 2280, albeit with slightly tighter constraints. In two sources, NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, we discover bimodality in statistic space for , with local minima at small and large opening angles. We show their \ as a function of \ in Figure fig_thetacon . For NGC 3079, the large opening angle provides a marginally better fit, whereas for NGC 4945 the small opening angle is a significantly better fit. For two sources, NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, we find that the best-fit \ reaches the lower limit on this parameter (26 ), which implies that both the sources are highly covered. We also find that the values for these fits deviate significantly from the canonical value of 1.9 (steep at 2.52 for NGC 1386 and flat for NGC 4945 at 1.58). The fact that the deviations from the canonical value occur at the edge of the \ parameter space suggests that the true \ of these sources lies outside of the range of the torus model (i.e. < 26 ). also present a model of a fully covered source (i.e. 0 \ opening angle), which includes variable iron and elemental abundances, known as sphere . We fit this model, with the iron abundance free, in place of the torus model for NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, and present the results in Table table_specpar . This is also done for NGC 3079 since the small opening angle also provides a good fit to that source. An improvement in the fit statistic is found for both NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, where the spherical model produces a significantly better ( > 90 significance) fit than the torus model for both ( ^2 =4 and 136, respectively). The iron abundance has been estimated to be slightly higher, albeit statistically consistent with solar metalicity. The inferred from the sphere model is also consistent with the canonical value for both sources. For NGC 1386, the sphere model produces extreme and iron abundances values and iherefore it is not likely to be a good description of the spectrum. Due to the range of \ allowed for the torus model, we cannot test if a very large opening angle ( 90 ) would provide as equally good a solution for NGC 3079 or NGC 4945. However for the local minimum at the largest angle, the for NGC 4945 is even flatter (1.43) than the smallest opening angle and does not suggest that a large opening angle would describe the spectrum well. For this solution, =2.64 10^ 24 \ and =2.27 10^ 42 . We conclude from this that NGC 3079 and NGC 4945 are heavily buried sources with a covering factors close to unity. For all further analysis we assign a value of =0 \ to these sources. We discuss this result later, especially for NGC 4945 where previous results have favoured a very low covering factor e.g. madejski00 . Parameters determined by the torus model are listed in the top rows. For NGC 1386, IC 3079 and NGC 4945 we also present the best-fit parameters from the sphere model as the torus model finds that these sources are highly covered (bottom rows).
