We also determined the shape of the SLED for individual ``blue'' and ``red'' components (named according to their position relative to the systemic redshifts of Table tab:prop ) in PLCK G045.1+61.1, PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G244.8+54.9, the three GEMS where two spectral components are detected over several CO lines. To obtain a robust separation, we fixed the central velocities of the individual components to those measured on the lowest- J transition with the highest S/N value, typically either CO(3--2) or CO(4--3) (see Table tab:linefit ). The two Gaussians are centered at about --420 and 30 km s ^ 1 , --120 and 170 km s ^ 1 , --210 and 150 km s ^ 1 (with respect to the spectroscopic redshifts of Table tab:prop ) for PLCK G045.1+61.1, PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G244.8+54.9, respectively.

For PLCK G045.1+61.1, the two modeled CO SLEDs peak between J_ up =4 and J_ up =5 similar to that obtained for the global spectra in Fig. fig:sled3 . The density and temperature of the single gas excitation components are therefore consistent, within the uncertainties, with log (n_ H_2 / cm ^ 3 )=3.61^ +0.70 _ 1.02 and log (T_ k / K )=2.17^ +0.25 _ 0.27 , as measured on the overall SLED. Comparing the CO(1--0) fluxes extrapolated from the best-fitting models shown in Fig. fig:sled4 with those extrapolated from the LVG models of the overall SLED (Fig. fig:sled3 ) suggests that the blue and red components each comprise about half of the total gas mass in PLCK G045.1+61.1.

sources.summary: The paper presents PLCK G045.1 + 61.1 as a lensed z=3.427 starburst with extended C II emission, redshifted absorption, intense star formation, and possible inflowing gas or merger debris.

quotes.quote: We present spatially resolved ALMA [ CII ] observations of the bright (flux density S_ 350 = 400 mJy at 350 m), gravitationally lensed, starburst galaxy PLCK G045.1 + 61.1 at z=3.427 , the ``Garnet''. This source is part of our set of ``Planck's Dusty GEMS'', discovered with the Planck's all-sky survey. Two emission-line clouds with a relative velocity offset of 600 km s ^ 1 extend towards north-east and south-west, respectively, of a small, intensely star-forming clump with a star-formation intensity of 220 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , akin to maximal starbursts.

The absorber could be part of a cosmic filament or merger debris being accreted onto the galaxy. We discuss our results also in light of the on-going debate of the origin of the [ CII ] deficit in dusty star-forming galaxies.

gmw_00006741.name: HM4-55878
gmw_00006741.overview: The galaxy HM4-55878 is characterized by very strong emission lines that affect its broadband spectral shape [arXiv:2311.16232]. It exhibits active galactic nucleus-like line ratios and a significantly elevated star formation rate [arXiv:2311.16232].

sources.summary: The paper flags HM4-55878 as an unusual primary target with strong emission lines, elevated inferred star formation, AGN-like line ratios, and photometry requiring emission-line correction.

quotes.quote: For galaxy HM4-55878 the emission lines are very strong and affecting the broadband spectral shape. Thus, for this galaxy we first correct the photometry for the emission line fluxes (see Sect. fig:halpha ). Furthermore, we mask the wavelength regions affected by emission lines while fitting.

HM4-55878 has a significantly higher SFR than expected based on its UVJ colors and the initial photometric analysis. This disparity is attributed to the influence of strong emission lines on the broadband SED.

gmw_00006742.name: HM1-213931
gmw_00006742.overview: HM1-213931 is a merging galaxy system composed of multiple blended galaxies with a velocity offset [arXiv:2311.16232]. The system exhibits a low star formation rate that may be fueled by low-metallicity infalling gas or associated with a nearby companion galaxy [arXiv:2311.16232].

sources.summary: The paper treats HM1-213931 as a blended or merging galaxy system with a poor continuum fit, discrepant star-formation indicators, low SFR, and possible low-metallicity inflow or companion-associated star formation.

quotes.quote: First, for HM1-213931 the fit is quite poor, probably because it is a blended spectrum of multiple galaxies, which have a velocity offset. Though we cannot deblend the spectra of the sources, we find a different spectrum when assuming different weighing profiles for the extraction (see A. Beverage et al. in prep for more information on the implications).

Only for HM1-213931 and HM4-56163, for which H is thought to originate from star formation, the two SFRs are inconsistent. HM1-213931 seems to be a merger between several galaxies (see Fig. fig:speclz ), and thus it may not be surprising to find a low SFR of 4 1 M_ yr ^ 1 . In particular, different physical regions for the stellar and nebular components may explain the discrepant values. The low [ N II ] /H ratio implies low metallicity, which makes it more likely that the star formation is either fueled by low-metallicity infalling gas or associated with a nearby smaller galaxy.

gmw_00006743.name: HM3-107590
gmw_00006743.overview: HM3-107590 is a young, compact galaxy classified as a post-starburst or young quiescent candidate [arXiv:2311.16232]. It is among the smallest of its population, with a stellar mass that exceeds its dynamical mass [arXiv:2311.16232].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HM3-107590 as a young, compact post-starburst or quiescent candidate whose stellar mass exceeds its dynamical mass, possibly due to orientation or underestimated velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Based on the photometric redshifts, all primary targets were initially selected to be quiescent. However, when re-deriving the rest-frame colors using the spectroscopic redshifts, two of the primary targets (HM3-107590 HM4-55878) just shift outside the quiescent box. Given their location though, we expect these galaxies to be post-starburst or young quiescent galaxies e.g., KWhitaker2012,SBelli2019,KSuess2021,MPark2023 , and thus still have quiescent populations.

For two galaxies the dynamical masses are below their stellar masses, with one galaxy (HM3-107590) being off by >3 . These galaxies are among the smallest (based on the light-weighted size) and youngest, as shown in the top-right panel of Figure fig:Mdyn .

gmw_00006744.name: Ursa Minor dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00006744.overview: The Ursa Minor dwarf spheroidal galaxy is a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:astro-ph/0503620]. It moves on a retrograde orbit that is inclined by 124 degrees to the Galactic plane [arXiv:astro-ph/0503620].

sources.summary: The paper measures Ursa Minor’s HST proper motion, derives its Galactic-rest-frame velocity and orbit, and argues it is unlikely to belong to proposed Milky Way satellite alignments or streams.

quotes.quote: This article presents a measurement of the proper motion of the Ursa Minor dwarf spheroidal galaxy determined from images taken with the Hubble Space Telescope in two distinct fields. Each field contains a quasi-stellar object which serves as the ``reference point.'' The measured proper motion for Ursa Minor, expressed in the equatorial coordinate system, is ( _ , _ )=(-50 17, 22 16) mas century ^ 1 . Removing the contributions of the solar motion and the motion of the Local Standard of Rest yields the proper motion in the Galactic rest frame: ( _ ^ Grf , _ ^ Grf ) = (-8 17 ,38 16) mas century ^ 1 . The implied space velocity with respect to the Galactic center has a radial component of V_ =-75 44 km s ^ 1 and a tangential component of V_ t =144 50 km s ^ 1 .

The orbit is retrograde and inclined by 124 (94,136) degrees to the Galactic plane. Ursa Minor is not a likely member of a proposed stream of galaxies on similar orbits around the Milky Way. Nor is the plane of its orbit coincident with a recently-proposed planar alignment of galaxies around the Milky Way.

gmw_00006745.name: host galaxy of GRB 050826
gmw_00006745.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050826 is an irregular galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.296 [arXiv:0704.3069]. Its optical afterglow was detected at an offset of 1.76 kiloparsecs from the galactic center [arXiv:0704.3069].

sources.summary: The paper identifies and spectroscopically measures the GRB 050826 host at z = 0.296, finding an irregular galaxy with the afterglow offset 1.76 kpc from its center.

quotes.quote: We present the optical identification and spectroscopy of the host galaxy of GRB 050826 at a redshift z = 0.296 0.001 . Image subtraction among observations obtained on three consecutive nights, reveals a fading object 5 hr after the burst, confirming its identification as the optical afterglow of this event. Deep imaging shows that the optical afterglow is offset by 0^ .4 (1.76 kpc) from the center of its irregular host galaxy, which is typical for long-duration gamma-ray bursts. Combining these results with X-ray measurements acquired by the Swift XRT instrument, we find that GRB 050826 falls entirely within the subluminous, subenergetic group of long gamma-ray bursts at low redshift ( z 0.3 ).

gmw_00006746.name: SRGeJ131118.5+463502
gmw_00006746.overview: SRGeJ131118.5+463502 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.271 [arXiv:2309.11308]. It is a candidate for a tidal disruption event within a double-core system containing two supermassive black holes separated by approximately 250 parsecs [arXiv:2309.11308].

sources.summary: The paper treats SRGeJ131118.5+463502 as a quasar whose Gaia-interval transient luminosity may indicate a tidal disruption event in a double-core system separated by about 250 pc.

quotes.quote: For quasar SRGeJ131118.5+463502, the lower estimation of the luminosity of the transient event within the GAIA catalog interval ( L_T ) is about 1.3 10^ 44 erg/s (in Fig. fig:mu_epsi in the panel with quasars, this source is highlighted by a blue circle). This luminosity exceeds the maximum luminosity values for supernova events and is a candidate for a tidal disruption event (TDE) in a double-core system. At the picture plane, the two SMBHs are separated by an angular distance of at least 60 mas , which at redshift z=0.271 corresponds to distances of the order of 250 parsecs. Of course, to model the TDE it is necessary to use a more sophisticated dependence than FRED, involving an exponential function at large times after the flare maximum 2021ApJ...908....4V .

gmw_00006747.name: SRGeJ143701.5+264019
gmw_00006747.overview: SRGeJ143701.5+264019 is a spectrally confirmed quasar [arXiv:2309.11308]. Its light curve shows a monotonic rise in brightness followed by an approximately twofold drop in flux [arXiv:2309.11308].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SRGeJ143701.5+264019 as a quasar matched to the GNT catalog and describes a Gaia-era light curve with a monotonic rise and later twofold flux drop.

quotes.quote: Among the spectrally confirmed sources, two matches with GNT were found: SRGeJ143701.5+264019 and SRGeJ151721.7+465812. The light curve of the source SRGeJ143701.5+264019 known as a quasar is shown in Fig. fig:QSO . The data corresponding to the GAIA eDR3 catalog period is indicated with red circles (vertical dashed line is the upper boundary of the catalog). A monotonic rise in brightness is observed which continued even after the end of the GAIA eDR3 catalog with an approximately twofold drop in flux between 1800 and 2000 days on the graph.

gmw_00006748.name: SRGeJ151721.7+465812
gmw_00006748.overview: SRGeJ151721.7+465812 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.154 [arXiv:2309.11308]. Its light curve exhibits a flare with a fast-rise exponential-decay profile, which is consistent with a supernova event occurring at a distance of 214 pc from the core [arXiv:2309.11308].

sources.summary: The paper describes SRGeJ151721.7+465812 as a GNT-matched source with a FRED-like flare whose Gaia motion and redshift imply a transient about 214 pc from the core.

quotes.quote: The SRGeJ151721.7+465812 light curve has a completely different appearance where a flare with a FRED profile definitely stands out. The light curve is shown in Fig. fig:SN and the green dashed line represents the fit of the flare with model parameters: t_0=745^d, R_0=2.6, =15^d, =111^d . The proper motion ( 17.63 2.3 mas/yr ) and astrometric noise ( 12.2 mas ) measured in GAIA, together with the flare parameters, are consistent with a transient distance from the core of X_T 80 mas . Using the source redshift z=0.154 determined from the RTT-150 spectral data, we obtain that in the object reference frame the transient event took place at a distance of 214 pc. An estimation of the luminosity of the flare at maximum corresponds to L_ max =2.2 10^ 43 erg/s, which together with the exponential decay indicates that a Supernova event has occurred.

gmw_00006749.name: NGC 3190
gmw_00006749.overview: NGC 3190 is a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:1801.04328] that contains a dust lane [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:1801.04328] and a LINER-type nucleus [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:1801.04328]. The galaxy exhibits quiescent star formation [arXiv:1302.5011] and is linked to the neighboring galaxy NGC 3187 by a weak radio bridge [arXiv:1705.06210].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3190 as HCG 44's central radio source, embedded in a radio bubble, possibly polarized at 4.85 GHz, and linked to a weak bridge toward NGC 3187.

quotes.quote: Its central galaxy (NGC 3190) is immersed in a bubble of radio emission with total flux density of 13.8 1.0 mJy. The peak flux density refers to a point that is located south from the centre of this galaxy. A bridge of radio emission that reaches 5 .m.s. contour near NGC 3190, extends northwestward pointing at another member object, i.e. the barred spiral galaxy NGC 3187; surprisingly, this bridge is visible only at 4.85 GHz.

Its central galaxy NGC 3190 dominates the group at both frequencies. Subtraction of the compact sources, including two background objects south from the galaxy disk leaves an extended structure that is still significant (nearly 70 of the original flux density), with the total flux density of 9.2 1.0 at 4.85 GHz. The strength of the magnetic field in the disk of NGC 3190 is thereby 6.7 1.3 G and its energy density is equal to 1.8 0.7 10^ 12 erg cm^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3190 as the group’s massive, nearly edge-on LINER, with a prominent dust lane, weak UV relative to visible emission, and quiescent star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3190: The most massive of the three galaxies in this group, NGC 3190 is a nearly edge on LINER and has a dust lane, that appears prominently in absorption in UV and is correspondingly bright in IR emission. Its SED shows particularly low UV relative to its visible emission, but presumably the geometry of this dust lane explains the poor fit to the UV and the disagreement in the estimates of its SFR. The DECOMPIR best-fit requires no contribution from an AGN. NGC 3190 has a negative IRAC slope, but the low SFR value derived by MAGPHYS places it in the quiescent category of Tzanavaris (2010).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3190 as SN 2002bo's host, using HST imaging and SPIRE pixels to show the supernova lies in a clumpy dusty spiral arm.

quotes.quote: At the distance of NGC 3190, a Herschel 500 m pixel corresponds to 1.45 kpc. Figure ngc3190 shows a Hubble Space Telescope ACS/WFC (PropID: 10594) composite image of NGC 3190. SN 2002bo's position and the 500 m SPIRE map pixel are marked. SN 2002bo is inside a spiral arm of NGC 3190 surrounded by clumpy dust features (ACS image); the dust is not smoothly distributed within the SPIRE pixel.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3190 as an edge-on spiral with dust lanes and a LINER-type nucleus, while its SED does not suggest an AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3190 : NGC 3190 is an edge-on spiral galaxy with prominent dust lanes. CIGALE places a 3 upper limit for the AGN contribution at 1 , although it has been shown to have a LINER-type nucleus gon15 . L13 also described a best-fit model with no AGN contribution, and nothing in the SED suggests an AGN.

gmw_00006750.name: SR6
gmw_00006750.overview: SR6 is a luminous Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.676 [arXiv:1706.06591]. Hubble Space Telescope observations show that the galaxy is marginally resolved, consisting of a single component with a dust-uncorrected star formation rate of approximately 10 solar masses per year [arXiv:1706.06591].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SR6 as a luminous z=5.676 Ly-alpha emitter, measures its Ly-alpha line properties, HST photometry, UV luminosity, stellar mass, size, and very high equivalent width.

quotes.quote: SR6 is robustly confirmed to be a luminous Ly emitter at z=5.676 0.001 (Fig. fig:spectra ). We measure a Ly line-width of v_ FWHM = 236 16 km s ^ 1 and Ly luminosity of 2.7 0.2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . We do not detect continuum in the X-SHOOTER spectrum (with a 1 depth of 3.0 10^ 19 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ^ 1 , smoothed per resolution element), such that we can only provide a lower limit on the EW, which is EW _0 250 .

HST follow-up We observed SR6 with our ongoing HST /WFC3 follow-up program (PI Sobral, program 14699), and is detected in the F098M and F140W filter, see e.g. Fig. fig:thumb_goku , with a total integration time of 4076 s and 3176 s. The source is marginally resolved, consists of a single component that is separated by 0.2'' from the peak Ly flux. We measure magnitudes of F098M= 25.68 0.13 and F140W= 25.60 0.10 in a 0.4 '' aperture. Correcting for magnification, this results in M _ 1500 = -21.1 0.1 , which corresponds to a dust-uncorrected SFR 10 M _ yr ^ 1 and is thus a M_ UV ^ source at that redshift Bouwens2015 .

gmw_00006751.name: UDO-SW
gmw_00006751.overview: UDO-SW is an ultra-diffuse object located near the main body of the galaxy NGC 1068 [arXiv:1708.06358]. It appears to be part of a loop-like stream structure, suggesting it is a tidal remnant of a merging satellite galaxy [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDO-SW as one of three ultra-diffuse objects near NGC 1068 and argues that it is part of a loop-like tidal remnant physically connected to the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Among them, three are very faint objects, being similar to ultra diffuse galaxies (UDGs) found in galaxy cluster environments (e.g., ). Since all these are close to the main body of NGC 1068 (their projected distance from NGC 1068 are less than 45 kpc; see table tbl2 ), they are considered to be associated with NGC 1068. Their central surface brightness is fainter than 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 ( band) and the effective radius of these galaxies are larger than 1.5 kpc. Assuming that g- color is not extremely red ( g-<1.5 : note that the typical g- color of galaxies is g-<0.77 ; ), we find that these three objects satisfy the criteria for the UDGs vDm15 . Taking account of this similarity, we call them UDO-SW, UDO-NE, and UDO-SE where UDO = Ultra Diffuse Object, SW = southwest, NE = northwest, and SE = southeast; note that UDO-SE was already found in the SDSS Stripe 82 data bakos12 .

It is intriguing to note that both UDO-NE and UDO-SW appear to show a loop or stream structure around the main body of NGC 1068, providing evidence for the physical connection to NGC 1068 (see section 4.1).

The detailed morphological properties of both UDO-NE and UDO-SW suggest that these two structures would make a single loop surrounding the main body of NGC 1068, being similar to those found in our Galaxy newberg02 , NGC 5907 martinez-delgado08 , and so on. If this is the case, the two UDOs (UDO-NE and UDO-SW) is a so-called stream structure that is a tidal remnant of a merging satellite galaxy.

gmw_00006752.name: UDO-NE
gmw_00006752.overview: UDO-NE is an ultra-diffuse object located near the main body of the galaxy NGC 1068 [arXiv:1708.06358]. It exhibits a loop or stream structure that suggests it is a tidal remnant of a merging satellite galaxy [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDO-NE as one of three ultra-diffuse objects near NGC 1068 and argues that it is part of a loop-like tidal remnant physically connected to the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Among them, three are very faint objects, being similar to ultra diffuse galaxies (UDGs) found in galaxy cluster environments (e.g., ). Since all these are close to the main body of NGC 1068 (their projected distance from NGC 1068 are less than 45 kpc; see table tbl2 ), they are considered to be associated with NGC 1068. Their central surface brightness is fainter than 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 ( band) and the effective radius of these galaxies are larger than 1.5 kpc. Assuming that g- color is not extremely red ( g-<1.5 : note that the typical g- color of galaxies is g-<0.77 ; ), we find that these three objects satisfy the criteria for the UDGs vDm15 . Taking account of this similarity, we call them UDO-SW, UDO-NE, and UDO-SE where UDO = Ultra Diffuse Object, SW = southwest, NE = northwest, and SE = southeast; note that UDO-SE was already found in the SDSS Stripe 82 data bakos12 .

It is intriguing to note that both UDO-NE and UDO-SW appear to show a loop or stream structure around the main body of NGC 1068, providing evidence for the physical connection to NGC 1068 (see section 4.1).

The detailed morphological properties of both UDO-NE and UDO-SW suggest that these two structures would make a single loop surrounding the main body of NGC 1068, being similar to those found in our Galaxy newberg02 , NGC 5907 martinez-delgado08 , and so on. If this is the case, the two UDOs (UDO-NE and UDO-SW) is a so-called stream structure that is a tidal remnant of a merging satellite galaxy.

gmw_00006753.name: UDO-SE
gmw_00006753.overview: UDO-SE is an ultra-diffuse object located near the main body of the galaxy NGC 1068 [arXiv:1708.06358]. Rather than being simple tidal debris, it is likely a normal dwarf galaxy with its own dark-matter halo [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDO-SE as an ultra-diffuse object near NGC 1068, likely a normal dwarf galaxy with its own dark-matter halo rather than simple tidal debris.

quotes.quote: Among them, three are very faint objects, being similar to ultra diffuse galaxies (UDGs) found in galaxy cluster environments (e.g., ). Since all these are close to the main body of NGC 1068 (their projected distance from NGC 1068 are less than 45 kpc; see table tbl2 ), they are considered to be associated with NGC 1068. Their central surface brightness is fainter than 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 ( band) and the effective radius of these galaxies are larger than 1.5 kpc. Assuming that g- color is not extremely red ( g-<1.5 : note that the typical g- color of galaxies is g-<0.77 ; ), we find that these three objects satisfy the criteria for the UDGs vDm15 . Taking account of this similarity, we call them UDO-SW, UDO-NE, and UDO-SE where UDO = Ultra Diffuse Object, SW = southwest, NE = northwest, and SE = southeast; note that UDO-SE was already found in the SDSS Stripe 82 data bakos12 .

Assuming that the total mass of NGC 1068 is 3 10^ 11 M_ within the 43.9 kpc sphere, and the self gravity of UDO-SE maintains its shape at least in <2 kpc, the mass of UDO-SE in 2 kpc radius should be larger than 8.5 10^ 7 M_ binneytremine87 . As the luminosity of UDO-SE is 1.6 10^ 7 L_ in band, the M/L of UDO-SE is 5.3 , which is similar to the nearby normal dwarf and the LSB galaxies (average 7.7 ; ). Therefore, UDO-SE is probably not a simple tidal debris without dark matter, but seems to be a normal dwarf galaxy with its own dark-matter halo.

gmw_00006754.name: LSB-FSW
gmw_00006754.overview: LSB-FSW is a very faint low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1708.06358]. It is considered a possible companion galaxy belonging to the M77 group, where it may represent a relic of a loop-forming event [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper names LSB-FSW as a very faint low-surface-brightness object, treats it as a possible M77-group companion, and suggests it may be a relic of the loop-forming event.

quotes.quote: We also find another UDO-like object that is 2 magnitudes fainter and smaller by a factor of 2 than those of the three UDOs. This object may belong to a class of low surface brightness galaxy. We call this object, LSB-FSW (F means `far'). Note that this object is out of the field of views in the Suprime-Cam R_ band data.

Although it seems uncertain if this object is physically associated with NGC 1068, we assume that this object is also a member of M 77 group and a possible companion galaxy. It is interesting to note that this object is located at the line connecting both UDO-NE and UDO-SW. Therefore, we suggest that this object may be a relic of the formation of the loop structure.

gmw_00006755.name: SDSS J024310.38-001543.2
gmw_00006755.overview: SDSS J024310.38-001543.2 is a normal dwarf companion galaxy of NGC 1068 [arXiv:1708.06358]. Its spectrum displays strong hydrogen absorption without emission, indicating that it is a post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J024310.38-001543.2 as a normal dwarf companion of NGC 1068 with a close redshift and post-starburst spectral features.

quotes.quote: The other two galaxies are identified as SDSS J024310.38-001543.2 (hereafter SDSS J0243) We note that there are three IDs on the position of the object in the NED (others are SDSS J024310.55-001546.1 and SDSS J024310.39-001543.8). According to the SDSS Sky Server, the coordinate of the referred object better refers to the position of the object. and 2MASX J02420036+0000531 (hereafter 2MASX J0242). They are apparently normal dwarf galaxies. Since their redshifts, 0.00370 and 0.00362 (SDSS DR13), respectively, are very close to that of NGC 1068 ( z=0.003793 , ), they are considered as companion galaxies of NGC 1068.

The spectroscopic information of the two dwarf galaxies are given by SDSS DR13. The spectrum of SDSS J0243 shows strong H absorption (Lick H _ A index > 5.0 ) without H emission. The spectral features suggest that the galaxy is a post-starburst galaxy (e.g., ).

gmw_00006756.name: 2MASX J02420036+0000531
gmw_00006756.overview: 2MASX J02420036+0000531 is a normal dwarf galaxy that is a companion to NGC 1068 [arXiv:1708.06358]. Its emission-line ratios indicate that it is a typical star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1708.06358].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J02420036+0000531 as a normal dwarf companion of NGC 1068, with a close redshift and emission-line ratios indicating star formation.

quotes.quote: and 2MASX J02420036+0000531 (hereafter 2MASX J0242). They are apparently normal dwarf galaxies. Since their redshifts, 0.00370 and 0.00362 (SDSS DR13), respectively, are very close to that of NGC 1068 ( z=0.003793 , ), they are considered as companion galaxies of NGC 1068.
The spectrum of 2MASX J0242 shows strong emission lines. The [ N II ] /H and [ O III ] /H ratios indicate that the galaxy is a typical star-forming galaxy Baldwin1981,Kewley2001,Kauffmann2003 .

gmw_00006757.name: DESI-253.2534+26.8843
gmw_00006757.overview: DESI-253.2534+26.8843 is a galaxy-galaxy strong gravitational lensing system consisting of a massive elliptical lens galaxy at a redshift of 0.636 [arXiv:2307.12470]. This lens galaxy is surrounded by four blue lensed images of a background starburst galaxy at a redshift of 2.597, which form an Einstein Cross pattern [arXiv:2307.12470].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms DESI-253.2534+26.8843 as a galaxy-galaxy Einstein Cross, measures the lens and source redshifts, and models its Einstein radius and velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: We present spectroscopic confirmation and lens modeling of the strong lensing system DESI-253.2534+26.8843, discovered in the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) Legacy Imaging Surveys data. This system consists of a massive elliptical galaxy surrounded by four blue images forming an Einstein Cross pattern. We obtained spectroscopic observations of this system using the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) on ESO's Very Large Telescope (VLT) and confirmed its lensing nature. The main lens, which is the elliptical galaxy, has a redshift of z_ L1 = 0.636 0.001 , while the spectra of the background source images are typical of a starburst galaxy and have a redshift of z_s = 2.597 0.001 .

We report the confirmation of a new galaxy-galaxy lensing Einstein Cross: DESI-253.2534+26.8843 ( = 16:53:00.82, = +26:53:03.48), discovered by 2021ApJ...909...27H in the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) Legacy Imaging Surveys Data Release 8 using deep residual neural networks.

We modeled the gravitational lens system using GIGA-Lens 2022ApJ...935...49G . The Einstein radius of the lens is _ E =2.520 '' _ 0.031 ^ +0.032 , which corresponds to a velocity dispersion of ^ L1 _ SIE = 379 2 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00006758.overview: Located at a redshift of z=1.555, 850.95 is a massive, rotationally supported disk galaxy [arXiv:1811.01958]. It exhibits an edge-on inclination [arXiv:1811.01958], clumpy rest-frame ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2003.00016], and a starburst-level star formation rate that sits above the main sequence [arXiv:2003.00016].

sources.summary: The paper presents DSFG850.95 as a z=1.555 massive, rotation-supported disk with a flat outer rotation curve, high outer dark-matter fraction, edge-on H-band disk, and low-redshift-like dynamics.

quotes.quote: We present an observational counterexample, a galaxy named DSFG850.95 at z=1.555 (4.1 Gyr after the big bang) that hosts a flat rotation curve between radii of 6--14 kpc (1.2--2.8 disk scale lengths) and has a dark matter fraction of 0.44 0.08 at the H-band half light radius, similar to the Milky Way. We create position-velocity and position-dispersion diagrams using Keck/MOSFIRE spectroscopic observations of \ and [ NII ] emission features, which reveal a flat rotation velocity of V_ flat =285 12 \ and an ionized gas velocity dispersion of _ 0 =48 4 . This galaxy has a rotation-dominated velocity field with V_ flat / _ 0 6 . Ground-based H-band imaging reveals a disk with S ersic index of 1.29 0.03 , an edge-on inclination angle of 87 2^ , and an H-band half light radius of 8.4 0.1 kpc. Our results point to DSFG850.95 being a massive, rotationally-supported disk galaxy with a high dark-matter-to-baryon fraction in the outer galaxy, similar to disk galaxies at low redshift.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 850.95 as a rotating disk DSFG, with exponential H-band profile, clumpy UV emission offset from ALMA dust, disk-like kinematics, and starburst-level SFR above the main sequence.

quotes.quote: Rest-frame optical imaging of galaxy 850.95, published by D18, is well fit by an exponential disk profile with S ersic index n=1.29 0.03 . The rest-frame UV emission is clumpy and is offset from dust continuum emission detected with ALMA. The position-velocity and position-dispersion diagrams show clear signatures of rotation with a velocity profile well-fit by an arctangent function and a centrally peaked, symmetric dispersion curve. The Gini coefficient and M _ 20 values, G=0.481 0.005 and M _ 20 = -1.654 , place 850.95 in the disk region of the Gini-M _ 20 diagram. Despite its disk classification, 850.95 sits at a SFR 4 above the MS, placing it in the starburst regime.

sources.summary: The paper treats 850.95 as the most extended source for imaging choices, notes an imfit measurement discrepancy, and identifies it as an outlier in size.

quotes.quote: Thus, we adopt a robust value of 1 for the full sample as it maximizes the source signal-to-noise for the most extended source in the sample (850.95) while maintaining a relative high angular resolution.
There was also a discrepancy in the measurements for source 850.95, which we attribute to the imfit model extending beyond the region of significant flux associated with the source.
Source 850.95 was an outlier at 5.9 3.3 .

gmw_00006759.name: DDO71
gmw_00006759.overview: DDO 71 is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M81 [arXiv:1002.2144] classified as a transition-type dwarf based on its HI gas and H-alpha emission [arXiv:1003.0861]. The galaxy features a centrally concentrated metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:1003.0861] and hosts a single bright, intermediate-age central globular cluster [arXiv:1002.2144].

sources.summary: DDO 71 is identified as a transition-type M81 dwarf with HI or H emission, metallicity gradients, a centrally concentrated metal-rich population, and AGB stars matching that component.

quotes.quote: The M 81 group dSphs that seem to lie in this apparent transition region are KDG 61, KDG 64, DDO 44 and DDO 71. Out of these four objects, three are classified as transition-types, namely, KDG 61, KDG 64 and DDO 71 (Karachentsev Kaisin ; Boyce et al. ) based on HI detections and H emission. Also among the dwarfs that coincide with the LG dSph locus, one dwarf is classified as transition-type, namely HS 117, with HI associated with it (Huchtmeier Skillman ; Karachentsev et al. ).

Population Gradients By examining the cumulative metallicity distributions in the middle panels in Fig. sl_figure7 and Fig. sl_figure8 , we conclude that the metallicity gradients are present in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1, while the metallicity gradients are less pronounced or not present in the remaining dSphs. We again separate the RGB stars in each dSph into two samples, where we choose to separate the distributions at the observed weighted mean metallicity. The probabilities from the two-sided Kolmogorov-Smirnov (K-S) test that the two components are drawn from the same parent distribution are listed in Table table3 , column (4). The K-S results are consistent with showing spatially separated populations in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1.

If we consider the overall distribution then we note that for most of the dSphs these stars are rather more widely distributed, following the distribution of the metal-poor stars, with the exception of KDG 64 and DDO 71 where their AGB stars' distributions coincide mostly with the metal-rich population, which in the case of DDO 71 is centrally concentrated.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO71 as an M81 dwarf spheroidal satellite with one bright central globular cluster whose age, metallicity, and red-giant-star metallicity are discussed.

quotes.quote: DDO71 = KDG63 This dwarf spheroidal satellite of M81 contains only one relatively bright ( M_V = -7.2 ) GC near its optical centre (Karachentsev et al. 2000, KDG63-3-1168 in Sharina et al. 2005). It has an intermediate age and a metallicity =-0.8 0.3 , but the S/N in the spectrum is quite low and the errors of evolutionary parameters determination are large. KDG63-3-1168 appear to be younger and richer in metals compared to the many old clusters found in M81 (Schroder et al., 2002; Ma et al. 2005, 2006, 2007; Georgiev et al. 1991b,; Chandar et al. 2004). Note that the mean metallicity of the GC is consistent with the mean metallicity of red giant stars in DDO71 ( =-1.17 ) (Sharina et al., 2008). The age of KDG63-3-1168 is similar to that of KK211-3-149, the nucleus of a dSph galaxy in the Centaurus A group (Puzia Sharina, 2008).
The central GC in DDO71, a dSph satellite of M81, appears to have an intermediate age ( 6 Gyr) and metallicity ( 0.8 ). It is different from the many old clusters found in M81.

sources.summary: The paper finds no obvious SBF-color gradient in DDO 71 and places its stellar population near low-metallicity model grids, with model-dependent old age estimates.

quotes.quote: DDO 71 -- This galaxy does not show an obvious gradient (Table tab_measures ). On average, observational data are located in between models of Z 0.004, and in the age interval 4-11 Gyr. BVA01 and MA06 models predict older ages ( 10 Gyr) respect to L02 and R05.

gmw_00006760.name: DES-691022126
gmw_00006760.overview: DES-691022126 is a four-object system that exhibits evidence of strong gravitational lensing with an Einstein radius of approximately 1.7 arcseconds [arXiv:2204.05924]. The system contains two constant-brightness galaxies alongside two redder, highly variable objects that display supernova-like lightcurves [arXiv:2204.05924].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DES-691022126 as a four-object candidate lensed-supernova system, interpreting two constant objects as galaxies and variable red objects as SN-like lensed images.

quotes.quote: DES-691022126 is a system of four objects labeled in the top panel of Figure fig:candidate_lsne as A, B, C, and D. We interpret objects C and D to be galaxies based on their constant brightness over time. Objects A and B are much redder, and display a greater degree of brightness variability when looking at the typical size of the magnitude error bars compared to the 5-year median z band magnitude for each object. Furthermore, the lightcurves for objects A and B both contain a period of linear decline in magnitude on month timescales: object A in Y5 and object B in Y3. ZipperNet detected the system in Y2 and Y3, but not in Y5.

We believe it detected the linear decline of object B in Y3 and that perhaps object C contained light from a SN between Y2 and Y3 for which ZipperNet detected the beginning of. The fact that the linear decline of object A's brightness in Y5 was not detected by ZipperNet is likely due to object A being the faintest source in the system and the selection function of ZipperNet (see the bottom right panel of Figure app:selection ). The SN-like lightcurve features that are shared between objects A and B, when combined with the evidence for lensing with an Einstein radius of approximately 1.7 , support the claim of the system as a LSN.

gmw_00006761.name: DES-701263907
gmw_00006761.overview: DES-701263907 is a complex candidate gravitational lens system dominated by the foreground galaxy LEDA 135660 [arXiv:2204.05924]. The system features a highly variable source, designated object A, and a potential primary lensing galaxy, object B, which may be a dense star-forming region [arXiv:2204.05924].

sources.summary: The paper presents DES-701263907 as a complicated candidate lens system dominated by foreground galaxy LEDA 135660, with a possible primary lens, variable source, and delayed supernova appearance.

quotes.quote: By comparison, DES-701263907 is a much more complicated system shown in the middle panel of Figure fig:candidate_lsne . A large foreground galaxy (SIMBAD source LEDA 135660) at redshift 0.03 dominates the image. Object B (SIMBAD source SDSS J024352.54-003708.4) is cataloged as a galaxy also at redshift 0.03 , but may also be a dense, star-forming region based on its blue color. This dense area, combined with the gravitational potential of LEDA 135660 itself would have a large lensing cross section, increasing the likelihood that background objects would be lensed. Because the lightcurve extraction method used in the lightcurves of Figure fig:candidate_lsne does not subtract the effect of the host galaxy, the variability of these objects cannot be assessed without difference imaging techniques outside the scope of this paper.

Nonetheless, we identify object A as the most variable source in the system given the foreground contamination. In the image cutout for DES-701263907, we also note the location of an SN detected in September of 2020 (AT2020scq). It is possible that object B acts as a primary lensing galaxy, object A is an LSN identified by ZipperNet in 2018, and AT2020scq is a second appearance of object A delayed by approximately two years. Given the large foreground galaxies at redshift 0.03 and a potential Einstein radius of 3.0 , this time delay would be consistent with an LSN.

gmw_00006762.name: DES-699919273
gmw_00006762.overview: DES-699919273 is a four-object strong gravitational lensing system with an estimated Einstein radius of 2.1 arcseconds [arXiv:2204.05924]. The system consists of a lensing galaxy, an unlensed source galaxy image, and two source galaxy images containing a lensed supernova candidate [arXiv:2204.05924].

sources.summary: The paper interprets DES-699919273 as a four-object lensed-supernova candidate with a lensing galaxy, unlensed source-galaxy image, and two source images showing similar SN-like declines.

quotes.quote: Lastly, DES-699919273 is another four-object system that we enumerate as A, B, C, and D in the bottom panel of Figure fig:candidate_lsne . We interpret object C as the lensing galaxy, object D as an image of the source galaxy without an SN, and objects A and B as images of the source galaxy, where an SN was present at some point during DES observations. The Einstein radius for this system is 2.1 . Particularly, we note a linear decline in z band magnitude for object A in Y3 and a nearly identical linear decline in z band magnitude for object B in Y5. ZipperNet detected the linear decline in Y5, but had no such detection in Y3.

We interpret this event as another manifestation of the less-than-perfect recall of the classifier. Nonetheless, the SN-like temporal signal appearing in two of the images within a lensing geometry is evidence for the presence of an LSN.

gmw_00006763.name: RG1
gmw_00006763.overview: RG1 is a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy located in the Abell 3266 cluster [arXiv:2208.00395, arXiv:astro-ph/0508682]. The galaxy features a compact radio core with complex jets and lobes that indicate dynamic interactions with the cluster environment [arXiv:2208.00395].

sources.summary: The paper describes RG1 as a spectacular Abell 3266 wide-angle-tail radio galaxy with a compact core, complex jets and lobes, and detailed spectral-index structure.

quotes.quote: RG1: the wide-angle tail radio galaxy Source RG1 is perhaps the most spectacular radio galaxy hosted by Abell 3266. It is a wide-angle tail radio galaxy, identified variously in the literature as MRC 0429 616 or PKS 0429 61, for example, hosted by the galaxy J043022.01 613200.2 at redshift z = 0.0557 Dehghan2017 . From the optical substructure analysis presented by Dehghan2017 , this galaxy is a member of the WCC and lies toward the outermost edge of this sub-cluster. We present our images of this source at 9 arcsec resolution in Figure fig:WAT . Radio Continuum Morphology RG1 has a compact, bright radio core with jets that point NW and SW with a projected opening angle of around 95 degree. The NW jet terminates some 20 arcsec (21 kpc) beyond the core, expanding into a relatively bright clump of diffuse radio emission that fades into the larger-scale western lobe. Conversely, the SW jet extends much further, with two possible hotspots around 38 arcsec (39.5 kpc) and 77 arcsec (80 kpc) from the core.

Our high-resolution radio maps presented in Figure fig:WAT clearly demonstrate that this cluster-member galaxy has undergone a dynamic history and/or experienced complex interactions with the cluster environment. The radio morphology of the lobes is complex: the southern lobe extends to the SW before bending clockwise and sweeping back upon itself, whereas the western lobe extends in a SW direction before turning to the NW.
Spectral index distribution The right panel of Figure fig:WAT presents the spectral index map of RG1 between 943 MHz and 2.1 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper uses the WAT galaxy's SW-NE radio lobes, peripheral cluster position, velocity offset, and possible proper motion to test merger scenarios for A3266.

quotes.quote: One further observational aspect is not explained by either of the two above hypotheses: the orientation of the lobes of the wide-angle-tail (WAT) galaxy located 7 \ to the SW of the X-ray peak. The radio lobes of this galaxy are oriented in the SW-NE direction of the merger. Among other interpretations, it is suggested that radio lobes are bent by bulk motions of the medium in which the radio galaxy lie in (e.g., hsw05 , and references therein), especially when these galaxies are found near the centre of clusters and are expected to have only a small relative velocity with respect to the centre of the cluster itself. This is not the case for the WAT galaxy in A3266, which is i) found in rather peripheral position and ii) associated with a host galaxy of relative velocity 800 km s ^ 1 less than the mean cluster velocity (although this is still within the large dispersion of the whole cluster).

To explain the direction of the bent radio lobes, bulk gas motion of order 2000 km s ^ 1 would be needed. The order of magnitude is the same as we find from our simple dynamic calculation in the epoch +0.2 Gyr scenario. However, in this case, the shock front has not yet had the time to reach the location of the WAT. It is even more difficult to explain the orientation of the WAT lobes in the +0.8 Gyr scenario without invoking large proper motion of the galaxy itself (which in this case should have belonged to the smaller cluster) at the time of collision of the two objects.

gmw_00006764.name: RG5
gmw_00006764.overview: RG5 is a complex radio galaxy associated with a cluster-member galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.059 [arXiv:2208.00395]. It features two bent jets extending 95 kpc from its core that exhibit a striking asymmetry in surface brightness [arXiv:2208.00395].

sources.summary: The paper presents RG5 as a complex cluster-member radio galaxy with bent jets, asymmetric surface brightness, a kinked lobe, and steepening spectral-index structure.

quotes.quote: To the north-east of Abell 3266 lies the source identified in Figure fig:A3266_RGB as RG5. We present a zoom on this object in Figure fig:RG5_Overlay , which shows our ASKAP and ATCA images at 9 arcsec resolution, along with the spectral index map. Radio continuum properties RG5 is another somewhat complex radio galaxy. The core appears to be associated with the cluster-member radio galaxy J043157.29 611825.8 at z = 0.059 , which is a member of Structure 2 Dehghan2017 . Based on the optical analysis performed by Dehghan2017 , Structure 2 is a radial filamentary structure with broad velocity dispersion, and has two concentrations of galaxies that are separated in redshift, suggesting a dynamically active environment. The radio morphology of the extended jets and tails appears to support this interpretation. As is visible in Figure fig:RG5_Overlay , we detect what appear to be two jets extending from the compact (and bright) core of RG5. Both jets extend to the south-east, suggesting that this source belongs to the general `bent-tail' class although projection effects mean we cannot determine whether this may be a head-tail, a narrow-angle tail or a wide-angle tail radio galaxy.

The jets exhibit a striking asymmetry in surface brightness: the southern jet has on average a factor 4 greater surface brightness than the northern jet. This may indicate that we are seeing the jets of RG5 in projection, with the southern jet in the foreground (i.e. inclined somewhat towards us) and the northern jet in the background (i.e. inclined somewhat away from us). Overall, each jet extends some 1.4 arcmin from the core (a projected distance of 95 kpc) before blending into the larger-scale emission of what appears to be a radio lobe.

gmw_00006765.name: CRATES J 153246+234400
gmw_00006765.overview: CRATES J 153246+234400 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2105.08240, arXiv:1603.06949] and a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2105.08240]. This point source is located approximately 0.55 degrees from the galaxy Arp 220 [arXiv:1603.06949] and exhibits gamma-ray variability [arXiv:2105.08240].

sources.summary: The paper argues CRATES J153246+234400 is the likely variable GeV counterpart northwest of Arp 220, with FSRQ-like spectra, significant light-curve variability, and positional consistency.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the center coordinates (right ascension=233 ^ .19, decl.=23 ^ .73) from SIMBAD http://simbad.u-strasbg.fr/simbad/ for CRATES J153246+234400

quotes.quote: CRATES J153246+234400, as a flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ), is also known as ICRF J153246.3+234405 with an unknown redshift. CRATES J153246+234400 is from the catalog of radio sources discovered at the frequency of 4.8 GHz; its 4.8-GHz flux density is 109 mJy with spectral index 0.213, and its morphology class is flagged as a point source Healey2007 . FSRQs, a subclass of blazar, are generally believed to have Doppler-boosted relativistic jets that point toward the line of sight from observers Blandford1978,Urry1995 . FSRQs are radio-loud AGNs, and their jets can produce high-energy emissions from radio to ray energies for the Doppler beaming effect Urry1995 .

After subtracting all sources including CRATES J153246+234400, as shown in panel ( ) of Figure TSmap , we found that there was no significant ray emission excess from the TS map, suggesting that the ray excess was likely to come from CRATES J153246+234400 in this analysis, which is consistent with the results of Tang2014 . h t 0.495 ( a ) t 0.495 ( b ) t 1 ( ) Peng2016 performed a likelihood analysis for this region in their study; their calculated best-fit position was (233 ^ .24, 23 ^ .80) 0 ^ .17, while our obtained best-fit position was (233 ^ .19, 23 ^ .72) 0 ^ .04. Peng2016 checked likely candidates from three AGN catalogs for the region in the vicinity of r_ 95 from the best-fit position from their analysis. They provided four likely candidates containing CRATES J153246+234400, and the other three objects were SDSS J15337+2358, SDSS J15323+2345, and 1WGA J1533.8+2356. We found that these last three sources were all located outside of the 95 error circle of the best-fit position of our analysis, as shown in panel (b) of Figure TSmap . Therefore, we suggest that the ray emission from this region is more likely to originate from CRATES J153246+234400. Light Curve For the result of the global fit in 0.2-100 GeV from the region of CRATES J153246+234400 from Peng2016 , the photon flux was (1.45 0.52) 10^ 9 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 with the spectral index of 2.45 0.19, and the TS value was 22. With the accumulation of photon numbers from Fermi LAT, the TS value of the region of CRATES J153246+234400 increased to 80.50, with 8.27 , from our analysis, and its photon flux was (2.68 0.60) 10^ 9 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , with the spectral index of 2.63 0.14 in the 0.2-100 GeV energy band. Compared with the results of Peng2016 , we found that our photon flux was approximately two times larger, and the TS value was approximately four times larger, implying that CRATES J153246+234400 was a likely ray variability source. To confirm the likely variability from the ray emission of CRATES J153246+234400, an LC of CRATES J153246+234400 was generated with energy above 200 MeV to avoid a large point spread function (PSF) in the lower energy band. Here, we generated the LC with 15 linearly equal time bins for the entire time. The maximum-likelihood analysis method was used separately to fit each time bin. For a photon flux of the TS value < 4, a 95 flux upper limit was calculated. The ray photon flux and index in the 0.2-500 GeV band for each time bin with a TS value > 1 were all fitted using a constant flux model e.g., Zhang2016,Peng2019 , as shown in panel ( a ) in Figure LC ; the fitting results of photon flux and the reduced ^ 2 were (3.76 0.63) 10^ 9 cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 and 1.76, respectively. Then, we found that the photon data were mainly concentrated in two active periods of P1 and P2 , represented by the red dashed lines in panel ( a ) of Figure LC . For the LC with 15 time bins, we found that the values of the photon flux and TS from the middle bins of P1 and P2 were higher than those of the other bins. Moreover, the value of the photon flux of the middle bin of P2 was 2 times higher than that of the 1 uncertainty of the constant flux model, which also implied the likely variability from the LC. Subsequently, a finer LC of 45 time bins with photon energy > 200 MeV was generated to further check whether there still existed a significant variability of the photon flux over the entire time, as shown in panel ( b ) of Figure LC . Using the fit method of a constant flux model, as was done in the LC with the 15 time bins, the best-fit results of the photon flux and the reduced ^ 2 of the LC with 45 time bins were (4.95 0.62) 10^ 9 cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 and 4.84, respectively. The related best-fit results of the constant flux model for the cases of 15 and 45 time bins are listed in Table Table1 . Meanwhile, periods of P1 and P2 were compressed into periods P3 and P4, as shown in panel ( b ) of Figure LC . We found that the TS values from the two active periods of P3 and P4 were higher than those of the other time bins. Moreover, the six values of photon flux with 1 statistical uncertainty from P3 and P4 were significantly higher than the 1 uncertainty of the constant flux model in the LC with 45-time bins, and the photon flux of the fourth bin of P4 had a higher photon flux of (1.17 0.30) 10^ 8 cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 and the TS value of 55.08 than the other time bins, which further implied that the variability from the photon flux of CRATES J153246+234400 could not be overlooked. To further evaluate the variability of the LCs with 15 and 45 time bins, the variability index ( TS_ var ) defined by Nolan2012 was calculated in our analysis. For the LC of 15 time bins, the value of TS_ var 29.14 was used to distinguish variable sources at a 99 confidence level. The value of TS_ var =60.95 with 5.37 for the LC of 15 time bins suggests the variability from the photon flux of CRATES J153246+234400. Similarly, for the LC of 45 time bins, the value of TS_ var 68.71 was used to distinguish variable sources at a 99 confidence level, and TS_ var =122.04 with 5.94 also indicates that the variability of the photon flux of CRATES J153246+234400 does exist. To further confirm whether the ray excesses of P3 and P4 originate from CRATES J153246+234400, we obtained uncertainties of the 68 and 95 of the corresponding best-fit position of CRATES J153246+234400 from P3 and P4, respectively. TS maps of 3^ 3^ from P3 and P4 centered at the best-fit position of CRATES J153246+234400 were generated. As shown in Figure Fig3 , we found that the position of CRATES J153246+234400 was always within the 68 error circles of the best-fit positions of CRATES J153246+234400 for the two different active periods of P3 and P4. The best-fit positions and 68 error circles, marked as r_ 68 , of CRATES J153246+234400 from P3 and P4 are provided in Table Table2 . These results strongly confirm that the ray excesses from P3 and P4 probably came from CRATES J153246+234400.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CRATES J 153246+234400 as a nearby flat-spectrum radio quasar contaminating the Arp 220 gamma-ray field, then models it and measures its photon flux.

quotes.quote: In their analysis of Arp 220, tang suggest CRATES J 153246+234400 , a flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ, Healey_2007 ) as the likely candidate of the contamination. The position of this source is located 0.55 \ from Arp 220 and aligns with the peak emission, as indicated in Figure fig:cmaps with the thinner black circle.

We include two additional point sources, for Arp 220 and CRATES J 153246+234400 , modelled with power-law spectrums: dN/dE = N_ (E/E_ )^ _ ph , where _ ph is the photon index. We model the Galactic and extragalactic backgrounds using the most up-to-date versions: gll psc v16.fit and iso P8R2 SOURCE V6 v06.txt. For CRATES J 153246+234400 , we measure a TS value, photon index, and photon flux of 20.3 (4.5 ), 3.472 1.23 and 2.66 0.78 10^ 10 , respectively.

gmw_00006766.name: IRASF23199+0123
gmw_00006766.overview: IRASF23199+0123 is an interacting pair of galaxies connected by a tidal tail [arXiv:1711.07522]. Its eastern member, IRASF23199E, hosts a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus, OH megamaser sources associated with AGN-driven shocks, and three extranuclear star-forming knots [arXiv:1711.07522].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRASF23199E as the eastern member hosting a Seyfert 1 nucleus, OH megamasers linked to AGN-driven shocks, elongated emission, and extranuclear star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: One of the main results of this paper is the discovery of a Seyfert 1 nucleus in IRASF23199E, via the detection of an unresolved broad (FWHM 2 200 km s ^ 1 ) double peaked component in the H emission-line from the BLR. This is important in regard to the OH maser emission, because the two masing sources are detected in this galaxy which hosts an AGN, rather than the other member of the pair. In addition, the masing sources are observed in the vicinity of enhanced velocity dispersion and higher line ratios, suggesting that they are associated with shocks driven by AGN outflows. The blue and redshifted maser sources are associated with the blue and redshifted ionized gas velocity residuals. This combination of evidence from HST images, VLA line spectroscopy and IFU spectroscopy strongly indicates that in this system, the OH megamaser sources are associated with the AGN, rather than star formation.

The comparison between the HST [ N ii ] +H image and GMOS-IFU emission-line flux distributions of IRASF23199E shows that they are similar, being more elongated in the northeast-southwest direction, following the continuum emission. In addition, the GMOS H flux map reveals the presence of three extra-nuclear knots, attributed to star forming regions. From the measurement of the H fluxes from the star forming regions, we obtain: (1) star formation rates in the range (0.05 -- 0.12) M _ yr ^ 1 ; (2) ionized gas content in the range (1.8 -- 4.1) 10 ^ 5 M _ ; and (3) ionized photons rate log Q [ H ^ + ] =51.9 to 52.2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies IRASF23199+0123 as an OH megamaser interacting galaxy pair, finding a tidal connection, extended radio emission in both nuclei, and OH masers in the eastern member.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of the OH megamaser galaxy IRASF23199+0123 using the HST, VLA and Gemini North telescope. Our HST images show that this system is an interacting pair of galaxies and used integral field spectroscopic data obtained with the Gemini GMOS-IFU to observe its eastern nucleus, which we call IRASF23199E. Our observations cover the inner 9.5 13 kpc ^2 of the galaxy at a spatial resolution of 2.3 kpc and velocity resolution of 70 .

We show that IRASF23199+0123 is an interacting pair with a tidal tail connecting the two galaxies and detect two OH maser sources associated to the eastern member. Both nuclei present extended radio emission at 3 and 20 cm, with intensity peaks at the each nucleus. The 20 cm radio emission of the eastern nucleus is elongated in the direction of the most extended emission in the HST continuum image (northeast-southwest), while in the western nucleus the 20 cm radio emission is tilted by about 45 ^ eastwards relative to the orientation of the most extended continuum emission.

gmw_00006767.name: z=0.101 galaxy toward quasar PKS 0439-433
gmw_00006767.overview: The z=0.101 galaxy toward quasar PKS 0439-433 is a high-metallicity spiral galaxy [arXiv:1604.05720]. It features a resolved rotating stellar disk with a large molecular gas mass but exhibits a low star formation rate [arXiv:1604.05720].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA CO(1-0) emission from the z=0.101 galaxy near PKS 0439-433, deriving a large molecular mass, resolved rotation, low star formation, and circumgalactic absorption geometry.

quotes.quote: The ALMA detection of CO(1 0) emission from the z=0.101 galaxy toward quasar PKS 0439-433 is coincident with its stellar disk and yields a molecular gas mass of M_ mol 4.2 10^9 M_ (for a Galactic CO-to-H _2 conversion factor), larger than the upper limit on its atomic gas mass. We resolve the CO velocity field, obtaining a rotational velocity of 134 11 km s ^ 1 , and a resultant dynamical mass of 4 10^ 10 M _ . Despite its high metallicity and large molecular mass, the z=0.101 galaxy has a low star formation rate, implying a large gas consumption timescale, larger than that typical of late-type galaxies.

As part of an Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) program to study molecular gas in high-metallicity, absorption-selected galaxies, we targeted the strong metal-line absorber at z = 0.101 toward PKS0439 433 Petitjean1996 . This super-solar metallicity absorber is associated with a spiral galaxy, at a projected distance of 7.3 kpc to the quasar Petitjean1996,Chen2005 , providing an ideal quasar galaxy pair for studying the molecular gas in the galaxy, as well as its CGM.

gmw_00006768.name: SB 111
gmw_00006768.overview: SB 111 is an isolated blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1706.00881]. This cometary galaxy is dominated by a bright region with a dimmer, diffuse component extending to the west [arXiv:1706.00881].

sources.summary: SB 111 is described as an isolated, cometary blue compact dwarf dominated by a bright aperture-centered region, assigned to Virgo and observed on roughly 100 pc scales.

quotes.quote: SB 111: (SDSS J123048.60+120242.8, LEDA 41360, Plate-MJD-Fiber 1615-53166-120) An isolated BCD. This cometary galaxy is dominated by a bright region where the SDSS spectral aperture was placed, with a much dimmer diffuse component extending to the west. It was assigned membership to the Virgo cluster by Giovanelli2007 , following the group assignment procedure described by Springob2007 and the peculiar velocity model of Tonry2000 . Thus, we assume the distance of the Virgo cluster, 16.5 Mpc. At this separation, the 1.25'' radius aperture probes a physical scale of-order 100 pc.

gmw_00006769.name: SB 179
gmw_00006769.overview: SB 179 is a blue region or cluster located at the southwest end of the disturbed disk galaxy IC 700 [arXiv:1706.00881]. Also identified as J1129, this compact star-forming source shows clear evidence for the presence of very massive stars [arXiv:2308.14489].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1129 as one of two galaxies with clear VMS evidence, and VMS models broadly reproduce its UV-optical line morphology better than BPASS models.

quotes.quote: Beyond R136, the two galaxies with clear evidence for VMS are J1129, previously studied by , who suggested the possible presence of VMS in this galaxy, and J1200, a compact star-forming galaxy from the CLASSY sample berg22 .

A direct comparison between VMS models and the spectrum of the VMS source J1129 is shown in the left panel of Fig. fig_j1129vms . The models produce emission in \ and without any obvious feature at 4640 \ or in the red bump, as observed. \ is slightly underestimated by the models, although its exact shape depends on the somewhat uncertain normalization due to the wiggles on either side of the emission line. \ is bracketed by the 10 and 20 Myr CSF models. \ is also well accounted for by these two models: this is an additional constraint compared to R136, where this feature is not covered by the observed spectrum. The right panel of Fig. fig_j1129vms shows the comparison of CSF models for the other VMS source, J1200. The conclusions are similar, and the models reproduce most features relatively well. The observed spectrum is rather noisy in the blue bump region, although broad \ emission can be excluded.
This is directly visible in Fig. fig_j1129bp , where we compare all types of models (bursts and CSF) with the VMS source J1129. None of the BPASS models produces enough \ and \ emission.

sources.summary: The paper places SB 179 at the southwest end of disturbed IC 700, compares distance estimates, and adopts 24.6 Mpc for the aperture scale.

quotes.quote: SB 179: (SDSS J112914.16+203452.0, LEDA 35380, Plate-MJD-Fiber 2500-54178-84) A blue region / cluster at the southwest end of the disturbed disk galaxy IC 700. The distance of IC 700 has been estimated from the Tully-Fisher relation (typical errors of-order 20 ) to be 21.5 Mpc Bitsakis2011 . Our local flow estimate provides a distance of 24.6 Mpc, in reasonable agreement with the Tully-Fisher result. We adopt the 24.6 Mpc measurement, at which distance the aperture radius subtends 150 pc.

gmw_00006770.name: SB 191
gmw_00006770.overview: SB 191 is a region embedded in the southeast arm of the barred spiral galaxy NGC 4204 [arXiv:1706.00881]. Although distance estimates to the source vary from 8.0 Mpc to 14.3 Mpc, a distance of 10 Mpc is adopted for the region [arXiv:1706.00881].

sources.summary: SB 191 is treated as a prominent southeast-arm region of barred spiral NGC 4204; the paper evaluates several distance estimates and adopts 10 Mpc.

quotes.quote: SB 191: (SDSS J121518.58+203826.6, Mrk 1315, LEDA 39187, KUG 1212+209B, Plate-MJD-Fiber 2610-54476-421) A prominent region embedded in the southeast arm of the barred-spiral NGC 4204. A precise distance measurement is not available to this source; the Updated Nearby Galaxy Catalog provides an estimate of 8.0 Mpc based on surface fluctuations Karachentsev2013 . The Extragalactic Distance Database Tully2009 states a preferred distance estimate of 10 Mpc based on a numerical action kinematic model.

Our local flow model suggests there is some degeneracy between this position and one consistent with the Virgo cluster --- HyperLeda Makarov2014 estimates a distance of 14.3 Mpc from the flow-corrected velocity. We adopt a distance estimate of 10 Mpc for this work. At 10 Mpc, the aperture radius corresponds to 60 pc.

gmw_00006771.name: SB 198
gmw_00006771.overview: SB 198 is a bright region embedded within the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 4301 [arXiv:1706.00881]. Based on its velocity and position, NGC 4301 is considered a member of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1706.00881].

sources.summary: The paper describes SB 198 as a bright region in face-on spiral NGC 4301, adopts Virgo-cluster membership from its velocity and position, and sets a 100 pc aperture radius.

quotes.quote: SB 198: (SDSS J122225.79+043404.7, SDSSCGB 20312.2, Plate-MJD-Fiber 2880-54509-277) A bright region embedded in the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 4301. Its velocity and position suggest that NGC 4301 is in the Virgo cluster see also GildePaz2007 . Thus, we adopt a distance of 16.5 Mpc. At this distance, the aperture radius corresponds to 100 pc.

gmw_00006772.name: HELMS RED 1
gmw_00006772.overview: HELMS RED 1 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 4.163 [arXiv:1801.07266]. Its high 500-micron flux density suggests that the galaxy is gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 1 has a photometric redshift consistent with CO spectroscopy, likely lensing from its 500 micron flux, ACT excess relative to the SED, and an offset SMA position.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 1: The photometric redshift of 4.0^ +0.55 _ 0.5 is consistent with the spectroscopic redshift of 4.163 which is obtained with CO(4-3) and CO(5-4) line detections (Riechers et al. in prep). The 500 m flux density of 192 mJy suggests that the object is lensed e.g. 2017MNRAS.465.3558N . This source was also detected with ACT with flux densities of 12.49 1.74 , 35.11 2.62 mJy and 72.32 6.26 mJy at 148 (2.0), 218 (1.4) and 278 (1.1) GHz (mm), respectively. Our best fit SED predicts flux densities of 7, 24 and 47 mJy at those frequencies, which are considerably lower.

The SMA flux density at 1.1 mm is 28.6 2.3 mJy, which is less than half that of the ACT value at 278 GHz which is observed at a similar wavelength but with a much larger beam. The predicted 1.1 mm flux density form our best fit SED is 46.6 mJy. The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS source near to the SMA position is 15.6 arcsec away. The SMA position is 3.9 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position.

gmw_00006773.name: HELMS RED 2
gmw_00006773.overview: HELMS RED 2 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 4.373 [arXiv:1801.07266]. Due to its bright 500-micron flux density, the galaxy is likely gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 2 has matching photometric and CO spectroscopic redshifts, is likely lensed from its bright 500 micron flux, and has a nearby WISE/SDSS object close to the SMA source.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 2: The photometric redshift of 4.6^ +0.65 _ 0.65 is consistent with the spectroscopic redshift of 4.373, which is obtained with CO(4-3) and CO(5-4) line detections (Riechers et al. in prep). The 500 m flux density of 132 mJy means the object is likely to be lensed. The SMA flux density is 33.9 2.25 and the predicted 1.1 mm flux density form our best fit SED is 53.9 mJy. The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS object near the SMA position is 2.0 arcsec away, the location of the source is J005258.53+061317.5 and has a WISE-1 AB magnitude of 17.5 0.2 . The SMA position is 2.0 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position.

gmw_00006774.name: HELMS RED 4
gmw_00006774.overview: HELMS RED 4 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 5.162 [arXiv:1801.07266]. Its bright 500-micron flux density of 116 mJy indicates that the galaxy is likely gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 4's photometric redshift is in tension with CO spectroscopy, but its bright 500 micron flux suggests lensing and the SMA source lies close to the SCUBA-2 position.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 4: The photometric redshift of 4.15^ +0.6 _ 0.6 is in 1.7 tension with the spectroscopic redshift of 5.162, which is obtained with CO(5-4) and CO(6-5) line detections 2016MNRAS.462.1989A . The 500 m flux density of 116 mJy makes the object likely to be lensed. The SMA flux density is 21.3 1.9 mJy and the predicted flux density at 1.1 mm from our best fit SED is 29.8 mJy. The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS object near the SMA position is 1.0 arcsec away, the location of the source is J002220.73-015520.2 and has a WISE-1 AB magnitude of 17.4 0.2 . The SMA position is 1.5 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position.

gmw_00006775.name: HELMS RED 10
gmw_00006775.overview: HELMS RED 10 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1801.07266]. It has an estimated photometric redshift of 4.6 [arXiv:1801.07266], though it remains unclear if its Submillimeter Array detection represents the same source as its SPIRE and SCUBA-2 detections [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 10 has a high photometric redshift and SMA detection offset from SCUBA-2, leaving uncertain whether the SMA and SPIRE/SCUBA-2 detections are the same source.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 10: The photometric redshift is 4.6^ +0.75 _ 0.6 . The SMA flux density of 13.3 2.8 and the predicted 1.1 mm flux density form our best fit SED is 24.5 mJy. The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS object near the SMA position is 8.7 arcsec away.

The SMA observations are not centred on the SCUBA-2 position and the brightest peak is 4.7 . The SMA position is 13.4 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position. It is unclear if the SMA sources is the same source as our SPIRE/SCUBA-2 detection more detail of this sources will be provided in Greenslade et al., in prep.

gmw_00006776.name: HELMS RED 13
gmw_00006776.overview: HELMS RED 13 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 3.3 [arXiv:1801.07266]. Its submillimeter array position is located 2.9 arcseconds away from its SCUBA-2 position and 3.6 arcseconds from the nearest SDSS or WISE-1 source [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 13 is assigned a photometric redshift near 3.3, has SMA flux below the best-fit SED prediction, and lies a few arcseconds from WISE/SDSS and SCUBA-2 positions.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 13: Our photometric redshift of 3.3^ +0.6 _ 0.65 . The SMA flux density is 11.5 1.8 mJy and the predicted flux density at 1.1 mm from our best fit SED is 19 mJy. The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS object near the SMA position is 3.6 arcsec away. The SMA position is 2.9 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position.

gmw_00006777.name: HELMS RED 31
gmw_00006777.overview: HELMS RED 31 is a high-redshift infrared starburst galaxy [arXiv:1801.07266]. It has a single detected CO line suggesting a spectroscopic redshift of either 3.798 or 4.997, which is consistent with its photometric redshift of 4.15 [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 31 has one possible CO line identification, a photometric redshift consistent with the lower spectroscopic option, and a nearby WISE/SDSS source close to SCUBA-2.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 31: This object has a single line detection which might be either the CO(5-4) or the CO(4-3) transition 2016MNRAS.462.1989A suggesting a redshift of 3.798 or 4.997. The photometric redshift of 4.15^ +0.75 _ 0.75 is consistent with the lower redshift from and in a small ( 1.1 ) tension with z=4.997 . The nearest WISE-1 or SDSS source is 4 arcsec away from the SCUBA-2 position.

gmw_00006778.name: HELMS RED 69
gmw_00006778.overview: HELMS RED 69 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy with an estimated redshift of 5.19 [arXiv:1801.07266]. It is a bright infrared source that may be gravitationally lensed by a nearby foreground galaxy [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: The paper highlights HELMS RED 69 as its strongest z about 6 candidate, brighter at 500 microns than HFLS3 and possibly lensed by a nearby SDSS counterpart.

quotes.quote: Our candidate with the highest chance of being a z 6 galaxy is HELMS RED 69. Its redshift is estimated to be 5.19 ^ +0.89 _ 0.92 and 19 per cent of its redshift PDF lies above a redshift of 6. Another remarkable feature of HELMS RED 69 is that its 500 m flux density is 1.5 times higher than that of HFLS3. There is a possibility that this source has been lensed by a foreground galaxy as we find an SDSS counterpart at a distance of 3.0 arcsec.

gmw_00006779.name: HELMS RED 80
gmw_00006779.overview: HELMS RED 80 is a high-redshift infrared galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 3.65 [arXiv:1801.07266]. It is likely associated with the nearby quasar SDSS J005036.93+014449.1, which contaminates the galaxy's star formation rate estimate [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 80 lies close to a redshift 3.4351 SDSS quasar; its photometric redshift agrees, WISE detects the quasar, and AGN contamination likely affects the SFR estimate.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 80 is located 3 arcsec from SDSS J005036.93+014449.1 which has a redshift of 3.4351 0.0003 . Our estimated photometric redshift is 3.65 ^ +0.65 _ 0.7 , which is within 1 agreement with the quasars spectroscopic redshift. The quasar is furthermore detected in WISE-1, WISE-2 and WISE-3.

We use the intrinsic quasar SED derived in in combination with the WISE magnitudes to calculate the AGN contribution to the FIR luminosity. This contribution is estimated at (L_ FIR ) = 12.97^ +0.11 _ 0.12 and is a factor of 3 lower that our measured Luminosity. We thus conclude that it is likely that HELMS RED 80 is associated with SDSS J005036.93+014449.1 and that the quasar contaminates our SFR estimate.

gmw_00006780.name: HELMS RED 421
gmw_00006780.overview: HELMS RED 421 is a high-redshift infrared galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 2.95 [arXiv:1801.07266]. It is located near several WISE and SDSS sources, including a quasar, which suggests its detection may be contaminated by these nearby galaxies [arXiv:1801.07266].

sources.summary: HELMS RED 421 is offset from a quasar and several WISE/SDSS galaxies; the paper finds its redshift, position, and blending evidence make the association uncertain and possibly contaminated.

quotes.quote: HELMS RED 421 is located 12 arcsec away from SDSS J000127.11-010603.1 which has a redshift of 1.934 0.001 . Our estimated photometric redshift is 2.95 ^ +0.7 _ 0.8 , which is in 1.3 tension with the quasars spectroscopic redshift. The separation of 12 arcsec is furthermore in 2 tension with our positions. The quasar is not detected in any WISE bands, but there is a nearby (z = 0.163) SDSS galaxy 9.0 arcsec away from our SPIRE detection which is detected in all 4 WISE bands > 5 (WISE J000127.76-010607.5).

Furthermore, WISE J000126.74-010612.2 is located 9.6 arcsec away and is detected in WISE-1 and WISE-2 and WISE J000127.44-010626.6 is located 12.4 arcsec away and has besides a WISE-1 and WISE-2 detection a 3.3 detection in WISE-3. The location of our SPIRE source lies in the middles between those 4 WISE/SDSS sources, indicating that this source is likely contaminated by several of those galaxies.
HELMS RED 421 may be associated with SDSS J000127.11-010603.1 but would be consistent with a spurious coincidence.

gmw_00006781.name: 2MASX J08535955+7700543
gmw_00006781.overview: 2MASX J08535955+7700543 is a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1911.04073, arXiv:2207.07986] located at a redshift of z=0.1 [arXiv:1911.04073]. The galaxy exhibits short-term continuum and line variability [arXiv:1911.04073], with source-related polarization likely generated in its accretion disk [arXiv:2207.07986].

sources.summary: The paper presents preliminary photometric reverberation mapping of 2MASX J08535955+7700543, detecting continuum and line variability and deriving consistent ICCF and JAVELIN time-delay estimates.

quotes.quote: As a preliminary result for 2MASX J08535955+7700543 at z=0.1 we have obtained time delay estimates of (ICCF)=32.2^ 10.6 days and ( JAVELIN )=39.5_ 15.8 ^ +23.0 days that are consistent with each other and also within the accuracy of the existing calibration relations.

The light curves in the continuum and the line of one of the most frequently observed AGN 1 (2MASX J08535955+7700543) are shown in Fig.  F:LC . After subtracting the continuum flux from the total flux in the line, there is a short-term variability at the level of 0.2 AB-magnitudes of the both fluxes, and the character of the variability is repeated. The observed amplitude exceeds the average error of AGN radiation measurement: the differential photometry method provides an accuracy of 0.03 mag. To estimate the time delay between the two light curves of the object 1 of the sample, the classical cross-correlation method ICCF was used as well as the method using the code JAVELIN .

Preliminary results of the object 2MASX J08535955+7700543 ( 1) reverberation mapping are shown in Fig.  F:LC . It is seen that the observed object is variable, and the used method is stable. Applying the classical cross-correlation function and JAVELIN gave estimates of the time delay (ICCF)=32.2^ 10.6 days and ( JAVELIN )=39.5_ 15.8 ^ +23.0 days that are consistent with each other and within the accuracy of the existing calibration relations.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes spectropolarimetry of LEDA 3095839, finding source-related polarization likely generated in the accretion disk and no equatorial-scattering signatures.

quotes.quote: Within this work we provide and analyze the spectropolarimetric data obtained for two type 1 AGN, LEDA 3095839 and VII Zw 244. These two galaxies were examined with the photometric reverberation mapping provided earlier by our group in SAO Uklein19 . Currently, for the given objects we have estimated the BLR size R_ BLR and, consequently, the SMBH masses (or so-called virial products, see Section inclinZw ) malygin20 . The polarimetric observations revealed the non-zero level of the polarization in spectra of both galaxies, yet a further detailed examination showed the diverse origin of the observed effects.

Since we have not found any signs of equatorial scattering in LEDA 3095839 spectra, we further assume that the observed polarization related to the source is generated in the accretion disk. Due to the large error in determining the correction for ISM polarization, we believe that the continuum polarization level in the object is between 0.5 and 1.3 . Thus, further, to determine the physical parameters of AGN, we assume for LEDA 3095839 P = (0.9 0.4) .
