sources.summary: The paper monitors 2MASX J08535955+7700543 as an active galaxy, derives a reverberation lag and gas velocity, and estimates its central SMBH mass from new observations.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the results of observations for the two brightest galaxies with broad lines from the sample under study RM_1 , 2MASX J08535955+7700543 (hereafter J0853+77) and VII Zw 244: their light curves and detected variability time lags. Spectroscopic data were obtained at BTA to estimate the gas velocities. The result of this work is the measurement of the central SMBH masses in the nuclei of the two studied galaxies, with M_ SMBH having been estimated previously for these objects based only on indirect methods.

gmw_00006782.name: AGES J224005+244154
gmw_00006782.overview: AGES J224005+244154 is a massive neutral hydrogen background galaxy undergoing a starburst phase [arXiv:1009.4438]. Its disturbed optical and neutral hydrogen morphologies, including faint plumes, suggest it recently merged with a smaller galaxy [arXiv:1009.4438].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGES J224005+244154 as a massive H i background galaxy whose FIR/radio emission implies a starburst, with disturbed optical and H i morphology suggesting a recent merger.

quotes.quote: AGES J224005+244154 is a galaxy in the background of the NGC 7332 field with a very large H i mass of 3.1 10^ 10 M_ . Its has a 1.4 GHz luminosity of 6.9 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 (fron the NVSS; Condon et al. 1998) and a far IR (FIR) luminosity of 8.1 2.2 10^ 10 L_ (from IRAS; Moshir et al. 1990). The q value for AGES J224005+244154 thus implies that the source of the 1.4 GHz continuum is star formation rather than an AGN. Using the equations of Yun, Reddy Condon (2001), the FIR flux indicates a current SFR of 21 9 M_ yr ^ 1 while the 1.4 GHz flux gives a current SFR of 41 12 M_ yr ^ 1 .

As can be seen from Figure j2240moms , the galaxy is disturbed in both the H i and the optical. The optical disturbance can be seen as a faint plume at the eastern end of the galaxy; the H i disturbance is much more visible and takes the form of a large plume at the western end of the galaxy. AGES J224005+244154 is clearly not evolving in isolation. It seems most likely that it has recently undergone a merger with a smaller galaxy that has triggered the current starburst phase and has resulted in the disturbances seen in the optical and H i images.

gmw_00006783.name: AGES J030039+254656
gmw_00006783.overview: AGES J030039+254656 is a small dwarf galaxy located approximately 35 arcminutes north-north-east of NGC 1156 [arXiv:1009.4438]. It has a neutral hydrogen mass of 1.63 × 10^6 solar masses and a star-formation rate of 8.7 × 10^-4 solar masses per year [arXiv:1009.4438].

sources.summary: The paper presents AGES J030039+254656 as a newly discovered dwarf near NGC 1156, measuring its H i mass, star-formation rate, blue color, and weak current tidal influence.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: An optical counterpart is clearly visible on the digitized sky survey (see Figure j0300+2456 ) at 03:00:38.6, 25:47:02 (J2000.

quotes.quote: AGES J030039+254656 is a new dwarf galaxy discovered by AGES in the neighborhood of NGC 1156. CCD images in the H line and continuum were obtained during an observing run in November 2007 under seeing of 1.8 . The observations were performed by Serafim Kaisin with the BTA 6-m telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory equipped with the SCORPIO focal reducer.

AGES J030039+254656 is a small dwarf galaxy found 35 arc minutes north-north-east of NGC 1156 (80 kpc in projection), as can be seen in Figure n1156region showing the integrated H i (moment 0) map. An optical counterpart is clearly visible on the digitized sky survey (see Figure j0300+2456 ) at 03:00:38.6, 25:47:02 (J2000. It has an H i flux of 0.114 0.032 Jy km s ^ 1 , giving it a neutral hydrogen mass of 1.63 0.46 10^6 M_ . As stated in newdwarfobs , we measure a SFR of 8.7 0.9 10^ 4 M_ yr ^ 1 for AGES J030039+254656, compared to 0.71 0.07 M_ yr ^ 1 for NGC 1156. The star-formation rate and H i mass are thus both around three order of magnitudes lower than NGC 1156.

gmw_00006784.name: J0304 + 0046
gmw_00006784.overview: J0304 + 0046 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus tentatively classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations at 1.7 GHz reveal a single compact radio component without extended emission, and the source has been detected in X-rays by Chandra [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0304 + 0046 as a tentative FSRQ, noting compact 1.7 GHz EVN emission, flat total spectrum, uncertain classification, and Chandra X-ray information.

quotes.quote: J0304 + 0046: This source has reliable EVN data only at 1.7 GHz. A single compact component without any extended emission is present which could indicate a blazar core, and the flat total flux density spectrum suggests an FSRQ. However, in the absence of a Gaia optical position, a VLBI spectral index, and a clear evidence for Doppler-boosted emission, we cannot classify this object with complete certainty. Chandra X-ray measurement is presented in 2019MNRAS.482.2016Z with a 1.8 lower limit to the photon index in the interval of 0.5 8 keV.

gmw_00006785.name: J0851 + 1423
gmw_00006785.overview: J0851 + 1423 is a radio-loud quasar classified as a steep-spectrum source based on its total flux density spectrum [arXiv:2204.02114]. However, the source exhibits variability, a flat VLBI spectrum, and indications of an extended structure in the northwest-southeast direction at 5 GHz [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0851 + 1423 as steep-spectrum, describing a possible extended 5 GHz structure, a small radio-position offset, variability, and a flat VLBI spectrum.

quotes.quote: J0851 + 1423: At 5 GHz, we see an indication of an extended structure in the northwest--southeast (NW--SE) direction. The faint NE feature might as well be a noise peak at 5 . There is a 5 mas offset (within 3 ) between the two radio positions. Based on the total flux density spectrum, we classify this as a steep-spectrum source. However, as it seems to be variable and its VLBI spectrum is flat, simultaneous sensitive multi-frequency follow-up VLBI observations would be ideal to securely confirm the nature of this source.

gmw_00006786.name: J0918 + 0636
gmw_00006786.overview: J0918 + 0636 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar that has been detected in X-rays [arXiv:2204.02114]. The source exhibits a flat radio spectrum, and its radio and optical coordinates have a 5 milliarcsecond offset in declination [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0918 + 0636 as an FSRQ, citing multi-wavelength evidence, variability-affected spectra, a radio-optical offset, compact calibrator behavior, and X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: J0918 + 0636: This source, together with a few others from our sample, can be found in a multi-wavelength study of 2019MNRAS.489.2732I . They gave an X-ray photon index of 1.3 0.4 (between 0.5 10 keV) and derived a flat radio spectrum. Their classification is uncertain. Note that both the total flux density spectrum and the two-point VLBI spectrum (which is based on measurements about two decades apart) are likely affected by variability. The radio and optical coordinates have a significant offset of 5 mas in declination.

gmw_00006787.name: J1006 + 4627
gmw_00006787.overview: J1006 + 4627 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. The object exhibits a slight radio-optical offset of about 3 milliarcseconds in the direction of its jet, and its continuum spectrum is flat or inverted [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1006 + 4627 as an FSRQ, noting a slight radio-optical offset, an inverted or flat continuum spectrum, and only an upper ROSAT X-ray limit.

quotes.quote: J1006 + 4627: There is a slight radio--optical offset, about 3 mas in the jet direction. The flux density upper limit at 150 MHz is consistent with the inverted/flat continuum spectrum. Only an upper limit to the X-ray flux from ROSAT data is available 2005AJ....129.2519V .

gmw_00006788.name: J1037 + 1823
gmw_00006788.overview: J1037 + 1823 is an active galactic nucleus hosting a supermassive black hole with a mass of one billion solar masses [arXiv:2203.09527]. The source exhibits a steep radio spectrum that likely peaks [arXiv:2204.02114], and it is classified as either a blazar or a border-definition blazar [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1037 + 1823 as steep-spectrum, discussing its likely peaked total spectrum, frequency-dependent VLBI position, calibrator effects, and probable AGN core.

quotes.quote: J1037 + 1823: The total flux density spectum is steep based on the data points in the GHz range, but considering the upper limit at 150 MHz, it seems that the spectrum is in fact peaked. The VLBI position is different at the two observed frequencies, with an offset of 6 mas in the NW direction. However, this value is still within 3 . A likely explanation is that the phase-reference calibrator source (J1045 + 1735) has an extended jet structure roughly in this direction, which may not be fully accounted for. The agreement between the 5-GHz VLBI position and the Gaia position suggests that the 5-GHz component coincides with the AGN core.

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J103717.72+182303.0 has a billion-solar-mass black hole and ambiguous SED fits, making it either a blazar or a border-definition source.

quotes.quote: SDSS J103717.72+182303.0 --- The BH mass is 10^9 M_ with L_ d =20 of the Eddington luminosity. The data quality does not allow to definitely classify this source as a blazar, because a range of _ v can fit the SED equally well, as long as _ <8^ . Therefore it is either a blazar or a source viewed at the border of our definition of this class.

gmw_00006789.name: J1309 + 5733
gmw_00006789.overview: J1309 + 5733 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. Its total flux density spectrum is characterized by a log-parabolic function peaking at approximately 600 MHz, and the source exhibits high-amplitude variability [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1309 + 5733 as an FSRQ, describing EVN detections, a log-parabolic spectrum with a MHz peak, high-amplitude variability, and X-ray support.

quotes.quote: J1309 + 5733: The source is detected with the EVN at both frequencies but only short baselines between five European antennas were available at 1.7 GHz. Therefore, the angular resolution achieved is poorer than for other sources in the sample (Figure evnimages ). To characterize the total flux density spectrum, we applied a log-parabolic function as, with a 150-MHz Low-Frequency Array (LOFAR) measurement 2017AA...598A.104S included, it gave a better fit than a simple power-law. The spectral peak is (13 1) mJy at (600 100) MHz. However, the VLBI flux densities are well above the total flux density spectrum (Figure spectra ), suggesting high-amplitude variability.

gmw_00006790.name: J1325 + 1123
gmw_00006790.overview: J1325 + 1123 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. Its radio spectrum peaks at 1.5 GHz, and the source exhibits possible flux density variability alongside an extended low-frequency structure [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1325 + 1123 as an FSRQ, reporting a peaked log-parabolic spectrum, possible variability, radio-position offset, extended low-frequency structure, and X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: J1325 + 1123: This is the second source where we fitted log-parabolic function to the total flux density data. A spectrum has a peak of (72 17) mJy at (1.5 0.2) GHz. The scatter of the flux density values suggests possible variability. The two radio positions have 4 mas offset ( < 3 ), similarly to J1037 + 1823 and J1307 + 1507. The location of the 5-GHz peak falls in an extended part of the 1.7-GHz structure (Figure evnimages ).

gmw_00006791.name: J1412 + 0624
gmw_00006791.overview: J1412 + 0624 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus tentatively classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. Spectral energy distribution modeling indicates the source is a blazar hosting a supermassive black hole with a mass below one billion solar masses [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper concludes SDSS J141209.96+062406.8 is a blazar below 1e9 solar masses, based on SED models with small viewing angles near the limiting beaming angle.

quotes.quote: SDSS J141209.96+062406.8 --- The BH mass is M=6 10^8 M_ with L_ d =63 Eddington. We show two different models, one with =11 and _ v =3^ and the other with =10 and _ v =5^ . The limiting _ v for =10 is 5.7^ . We conclude that the source is indeed a blazar, but with a mass smaller than 10^9 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper tentatively classifies J1412 + 0624 as an FSRQ, while noting no clear Doppler boosting, a slight radio-optical offset, X-ray detection, and uncertain blazar status.

quotes.quote: J1412 + 0624: This source is tentatively classified as FSRQ. However, there is no clear evidence for Doppler-boosted emission. There is a slight radio--optical positional offset, still within < 3 _ pos , almost perpendicular to the structure seen in the 5-GHz image (Figure evnimages ) which shows somewhat spurious extended features around the central core. It has Chandra X-ray detection 2013ApJ...763..109W .

gmw_00006792.name: J1434 + 1628
gmw_00006792.overview: J1434 + 1628 is a faint active galactic nucleus with low radio power [arXiv:2204.02114]. Although it exhibits a steep spectrum at gigahertz frequencies, low-frequency data suggests a peaked spectrum, and the object is classified as a misaligned radio source rather than a blazar [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1434 + 1628 as a misaligned steep-spectrum radio source, emphasizing its faintness, low radio power, and low-frequency evidence for a peaked spectrum.

quotes.quote: J1434 + 1628: It is the faintest source with the lowest radio powers in our sample (Table phyparams ). Similarly to J1037 + 1823, the 150-MHz flux density upper limit suggests a peaked spectrum instead of the steep spectrum fitted to the GHz data points. We found no strong evidence for the blazar nature so we classify it as a misaligned radio source.

gmw_00006793.name: J1520 + 1835
gmw_00006793.overview: J1520 + 1835 is a faint active galactic nucleus classified as a candidate compact symmetric object [arXiv:2204.02114]. It exhibits an ultra-steep radio spectrum and displays a large offset between its Gaia optical position and its VLBI position [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1520 + 1835 as a puzzling steep-spectrum source and CSO candidate with a large optical-radio offset and possible unresolved opposite lobe.

quotes.quote: J1520 + 1835: Arguably, it is the most puzzling source in our sample, and one of the faintest. Both the total flux density and VLBI spectra are ultra-steep ( < -1 ). As in most other cases, however, low-frequency total flux density measurements would better constrain the spectral fit. The TGSS upper limit indeed suggests a peaked overall spectrum, in contrast to the steep spectrum fitted to the high-frequency data points in the GHz range. The most remarkable feature is that the Gaia optical position has a highly significant offset with respect to the VLBI position which is coincident at both frequencies.

gmw_00006794.name: J1720 + 6028
gmw_00006794.overview: J1720 + 6028 is classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2204.02114]. The source exhibits a flat total flux density spectrum, and its optical position may be associated with a faint jet component [arXiv:2204.02114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1720 + 6028 as an FSRQ, noting a flat total spectrum, a small radio-optical offset, positional uncertainty, and a possible jet-component association.

quotes.quote: J1720 + 6028: The flux density upper limit at 150 MHz does not contradict with the flat total flux density spectrum. The radio--optical positional offset is 5 mas in right ascension. However, the Gaia value has the highest uncertainty among our targets, 2 mas in both coordinates. The radio--optical offset, taking the positional uncertainties into account, is close to the jet direction.
The optical position can be associated with the fainter jet component seen in the 1.7-GHz image (Figure evnimages ). All other parameters of this source suggest that it is an FSRQ.

gmw_00006795.name: 3C 223.1
gmw_00006795.overview: The active galaxy 3C 223.1 is a classical X-shaped radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0409675] hosted by a relatively undisturbed elliptical galaxy with a central dusty disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0409675]. Its radio morphology features low surface brightness wings that exhibit flatter radio spectra than its active lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0409675, arXiv:2401.05595].

sources.summary: The paper presents low-frequency GMRT imaging of 3C 223.1, describing its X-shaped radio morphology, undetected low-frequency core, spectral-index structure, and unusually flatter wings.

quotes.quote: Source 3C 223.1 ( z = 0.1075) is a classical X shaped source, which does not reside in a rich cluster; instead it probably resides in an environment similar to `classical' FR IIs of similar radio power. Although, the host galaxy seems to be a relatively undisturbed elliptical, its HST image shows a beautiful central bulge with a dusty disk deKoff .

The first high angular resolution, high sensitivity images of 3C 223.1 at the lowest frequencies of 240 MHz (upper right panel) and 610 MHz (upper left panel) are shown in Fig. full_syn . This complex radio source shows an X shaped morphology at both 240 and 610 MHz. The angular extent is 105 arcsec along the apparently active lobes (those with hot-spots) and 150 arcsec along the wings. The nuclear source of 3C 223.1 is invisible at both these frequencies and also in the radio maps of Dennett , but is detected and is unresolved at 8.4 GHz Blacketal .

We have presented the lowest frequency image of 3C 223.1 at 240 MHz. The important consequences of these observations combined with our 610 MHz observations are as follows: 1. The low surface brightness lobes (or the `wings') have flatter spectra as compared to the high surface brightness active radio lobes.

sources.summary: The review treats 3C 223.1 as an anomalous XRG whose wings can have comparable or flatter spectra than active lobes, motivating multi-frequency mapping and interpretations involving microphysics or binary-AGN scenarios.

quotes.quote: For example, Rottmann2001 delved into this issue by examining nine prominent XRGs, further identifying 3C 223.1 and 3C 403 as candidates where the wings exhibit flatter spectral indices compared to the active lobes and the jet hotspots. Subsequent work by Dennett-Thorpe2002 on these two sources also provided evidence of comparable or flatter spectral values in the wings than in the active lobes, with the spectral values in wing-lobe regions varying between 0.8 to 0.6 . This discovery has spurred multiple groups to conduct detailed mappings of XRGs, including the aforementioned sources, using multi-frequency observations and spectral mapping. The goal is to gain a deeper understanding of such anomalous spectral behavior, whether it is associated with particle re-acceleration mechanisms, enhanced radiation losses in the hotspots, or binary AGN scenarios capable of ejecting jets. The investigation into the origin of the anomalous spectral behavior of 3C 223.1 itself, involving different observing bands and authors, has revealed potential connections to internal micro-physical processes Gopal-Krishna2022 or the possibility of binary AGN scenarios Lal2005,Lal2007,Lal2019 .

In numerous XRGs, the smooth transition of magnetic field lines into wing-lobe structures suggests that the two pairs of lobes are likely interconnected entities, implying a shared evolutionary process rather than distinct and independent developments e.g., 3C 315, 3C 223.1, 3C 34, 3C 136.1, 3C 403, PKS 2014-55 Hogbom1974,Black1992,Johnson1995,Rottmann2001,Dennett-Thorpe2002,Cotton2020 .

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 223.1 as an X-shaped source whose aged lobes, flatter wings, parsec-scale jet orientation, and possible activity gaps match a precessing-jet picture.

quotes.quote: The X-shaped source 3C 223.1 shows two pairs of structures at kiloparsec-scales: lower brightness surface lobes (labeled as East and West wings in Lal2005 ) with flatter spectral indices in comparison with the higher brightness surface lobes (labeled as North and South lobes in Lal2005 ). It implies that North and South lobes are more aged structures than East and West wings Dennett2002, Lal2005, Lal2007 , imposing an important constraint for the jet precession modeling. Indeed, the precession model for 3C 223.1 shown in fig:precmodbest respects these findings since the jet (counter-jet) helix crosses the North (South) lobe firstly, reaching the West (East) wing after 3 Myr for this particular set of parameters. This precession model also predicts the correct orientation of the parsec-scale jet of 3C 223.1 at 5 GHz (P.A. 25 ; see vlba ). Doppler boosting factor for the jet (counter-jet) ranges from 1.075 to 1.094 (from 0.914 to 0.928).

sources.summary: 3C 223.1 has a bright absorbed AGN core and detected counts, but extremely faint diffuse emission prevents its inclusion in the XRG sample.

quotes.quote: 3C 223.1 ( z=0.107 ) : 3C 223.1 actually has a bright AGN core where spectroscopy is possible, revealing a highly absorbed power law. The detection significance is 34.1 and the number of counts detected S = 206^ +25 _ 22 . However, any diffuse emission is extremely faint, with an upper limit to unabsorbed thermal luminosity of L = 8 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 in a region with = 30^ . We are therefore unable to use this galaxy in our XRG sample.

sources.summary: The paper cites 3C 223.1 as an XRG whose radio spectra, wing sizes, and lack of merger signatures strain backflow models and motivate rapid jet-axis changes.

quotes.quote: A modified version of this scheme was proposed by Dennett-Thorpe et al. \ (2002), based on their analysis of radio spectral variation across two XRGs, 3C 223.1 and 3C 403. Although a careful analysis of photometric and spectroscopic evidence led Dennett-Thorpe et al. \ (2002) to conclude there is no evidence in the galaxy or its environs for a recent merger activity in either 3C 223.1 or 3C 403 (see, also, Landt et al. \ 2010, for a similar assertion about a larger sample of XRGs), they point out that the backflow models are not consistent with the relative sizes and spectra of the wings in these sources. Hence Dennett-Thorpe et al. suggest that the required rapid change in the jet axis within several Myr is either caused by a delayed SMBH merger following ingestion of a small galaxy by the radio galaxy, which left no obvious signature in the latter's stellar population, or, alternatively, the axis flip may have occurred due to accretion disk instabilities (e.g., Natarajan Pringle 1998).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 223.1 as an X-shaped radio galaxy whose flatter-spectrum wings and double-boomerang morphology point to particle reacceleration during rebounding synchrotron backflow.

quotes.quote: These empirical facts notwithstanding, there does exist one robust example, namely the XRG 3C 223.1, where the wings do exhibit a distinctly flatter radio spectrum compared to the primary lobes, even compared to their hot spots.

Recently, Gopal-Krishna Dabhade (2023) have underscored the ``double boomerang'' type radio morphology of this XRG 3C 223.1, similar to that found by Cotton et al. \ (2020) in their spectacular MeerKAT maps of the giant XRG PKS 2014-55 .

Thus, Gopal-Krishna Dabhade propose that the spectral flattening so distinctly observed towards the wings in 3C 223.1 is probably caused due to particle acceleration (or re-acceleration) triggered during the sharp rebound of the backflowing synchrotron plasma stream.

gmw_00006796.name: PKS 2052 474
gmw_00006796.overview: PKS 2052 474 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of 1.489 [arXiv:1001.1563, arXiv:1201.5159]. It is a variable gamma-ray source [arXiv:1201.5159] that exhibits a compact parsec-scale core [arXiv:1001.1563, arXiv:1201.5159] and two-sided radio jets extending over kiloparsec scales [arXiv:1001.1563].

sources.summary: The paper presents a multiwavelength campaign on PKS 2052 47 after 2009 optical and Fermi flares, describing it as a redshift-1.489 flat-spectrum radio quasar with compact parsec-scale structure and kiloparsec radio jets.

quotes.quote: The flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 2052 47 experienced an optical flare in July August, 2009. On August 9, the LAT detector onboard Fermi observed a flare from the source, which reached its active phase in the following two months. To further investigate the physics and emission mechanisms during the flaring state of this source, we arranged a multiwavelength campaign from radio to gamma-ray to study its spectral energy distribution (SED) and its brightness variations across the spectrum.

PKS 2052 47 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar with a redshift of 1.489 . Earlier Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) and Chandra X-ray observations showed that the source has a two-sided jet at kiloparsec scales in the radio band, with no extended emission in X-rays . Very Large Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations by TANAMI http://pulsar.sternwarte.uni-erlangen.de/tanami/ (Tracking Active Galactic Nuclei with Austral Milliarcsecond Interferometry, see ) show that the parsec-scale structure is very compact, with a high brightness-temperature core of 2 10^ 12 K and a size of (0.3 0.1) mas at 8.4 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 2052 474 as a redshift 1.489 blazar and variable gamma-ray source observed by CHI during a Fermi/LAT campaign after flaring activity.

quotes.quote: PKS 2052 474 is a blazar at a redshift of 1.489. It is established to be a variable gamma-ray source. show a light curve with two flaring events, occurring around February and June 2009 respectively. Previous VLBI observations have found the source to be strongly core dominated on the milli-arcsecond scale Ojha2010,Ohja2004,Piner2007 . On the kilo-parsec scale, ATCA imaging shows some extended structure Marshall2005,Burgess2006 extending over  4 arc-seconds.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 2052-47 as a high-redshift quasi-periodic blazar candidate, measuring a 642-day GLS peak and low power-law-noise fluctuation probability.

quotes.quote: The four other blazars, 4C +01.28, S5 0716+71, PKS 0805-07, and PKS 2052-47, are new candidates to quasi-periodic blazars. Three of the four candidates are located at high redshifts of z=0.89 (4C +01.28), 1.84 (PKS 0805-07), and 1.49 (PKS 2052-47), and are much more distant than the previously reported quasi-periodic blazars. Taking that binary SMBH systems are expected to be more frequent at high redshifts into account, these distant blazars are potential candidates to binary systems of SMBHs.

We computed the GLS periodograms of these sources and found that their power peaks are at 442 days for 4C +01.28, at 340 days for S5 0716+71, at 676 days for PKS 0805-07, and at 642 days for PKS 2052-47.

Finally, we computed the chance probability to observe the power peaks in the GLS periodograms under a condition of power-law noise and found that 4C +01.28 has 0.9 chance of being a statistical fluctuation and PKS 2052-47 has 0.4 chance of being a statistical fluctuation.

gmw_00006797.name: UDF12-3895-7114
gmw_00006797.overview: UDF12-3895-7114 is a galaxy with colors that closely resemble those of a high-redshift candidate [arXiv:1301.6162]. However, its broad photometric-redshift distribution and a low-redshift best fit of approximately 0.5 cast doubt on this high-redshift nature [arXiv:1301.6162].

sources.summary: The paper says UDF12-3895-7114 has colors resembling a z>8 candidate, but its bluer color, broad photometric-redshift distribution, and low-redshift best fit cast doubt on that nature.

quotes.quote: UDF12-3895-7114: This source certainly show colors very similar to a z>8 candidate. However, we measure (YJ) - JH_ 140 = 0.5 0.5 , which is bluer than our selection color for the z 9 sample. Hence it is not included in our z 9 sample. While Ellis13 find a best-fit photometric redshift of 8.6 0.7 , the source is not present in the `robust' sample of McLure12 , and we find a photometric redshift distribution function which is very broad, with a best-fit at z 0.5 (using both ZEBRA or EAZY).

gmw_00006798.name: UDF12-4106-7304
gmw_00006798.overview: UDF12-4106-7304 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate that is significantly detected only in the H160 and JH140 filters [arXiv:1301.6162]. However, its photometry is enhanced because the source is located on the edge of a diffraction spike emanating from a compact foreground galaxy [arXiv:1301.6162].

sources.summary: The paper argues UDF12-4106-7304 is compromised by diffraction spikes from a compact foreground galaxy, reducing its detection significance and excluding it from the robust high-redshift sample.

quotes.quote: UDF12-4106-7304: The WFC3/IR PSF shows significant diffraction spikes, which are caused by the mount of the secondary mirror. While typically only seen around bright stars, these diffraction spikes are so strong that they can also emanate from compact foreground galaxies, particularly in the redder WFC3/IR filters. The source UDF12-4106-7304 of Ellis13 is located at the edge of such a diffraction spike for both the H_ 160 and the JH_ 140 filters (the only filters wherein UDF12-4106-7304 is significantly detected). This is shown in Figure fig:spike . The photometry of this source is clearly significantly enhanced by the diffraction spike.

gmw_00006799.name: WISE J101326.25+611220.1
gmw_00006799.overview: WISE J101326.25+611220.1 is an extremely luminous, dust-obscured galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.703 [arXiv:1803.00177]. Its infrared spectral energy distribution indicates that an active galactic nucleus dominates the galaxy's emission, contributing approximately 94 percent of its infrared luminosity [arXiv:1803.00177].

sources.summary: The paper reports WISE J101326.25+611220.1 as a z=3.703 extremely luminous dust-obscured galaxy whose multiwavelength SED implies enormous IR luminosity and AGN-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of an extremely-luminous dust-obscured galaxy (DOG) at z_ spec = 3.703, WISE J101326.25+611220.1. This DOG is selected as a candidate of extremely-luminous infrared (IR) galaxies based on the photometry from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey and Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer . In order to derive its accurate IR luminosity, we perform follow-up observations at 450 and 850 using the Submillimetre Common User Bolometer Array 2 on the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope, and at 870 and 1300 using the Submillimeter Array, which enable us to pin down its IR Spectral Energy Distribution (SED). We perform SED fitting using 14 photometric data (0.4 - 1300 ) and estimate its IR luminosity, L_ IR (8-1000 ), to be 2.2^ +1.5 _ 1.0 10^ 14 L_ , making it one of the most luminous IR galaxies in the Universe. The energy contribution from an active galactic nucleus (AGN) to the IR luminosity is 94^ +6 _ 20 , which indicates it is an AGN-dominated DOG.

gmw_00006800.name: UGC 00477
gmw_00006800.overview: UGC 00477 is classified as a normal-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:0712.0515] located at a high Galactic latitude [arXiv:0712.0515]. It exhibits very high emission, with detection in almost every fiber during spatial observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0604576].

sources.summary: UGC 477 has three interstitial pointings with optimal spatial coverage, very high emission detected in almost every fiber, a data-constrained position angle, and comparable isothermal/NFW fits.

quotes.quote: UGC 477: There were three interstitial pointings along the length of this galaxy and spatial coverage of the galaxy was optimal. The \ fibers are shown on the R band image obtained at the KPNO 2m telescope. Fiber velocities were the average of the H , , \ and \ lines. The amount of emission was very high in this galaxy and almost every fiber had a detection. The position angle was well-constrained by the data.

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 00477 as a normal-surface-brightness target, uses high-latitude control fields for contamination estimates, and reports its globular-cluster luminosity function reaches the turnover.

quotes.quote: The values we inferred for the B-band value of _ 0, disk are then 21.86 ( UGC 00477 ugc00477 ), 21.43 ( UGC 03459 ugc03459 ), 22.63 ( UGC 03587 ugc03587 ), 23.74 ( UGC 06138 ugc06138 ), 21.62 ( UGC 11131 ugc11131 ) and 21.75 ( UGC 11651 ugc11651 ). We used these values to re-classify our target galaxies as described below.

The samples of UGC 00477 ugc00477 , UGC 06138 ugc06138 and UGC 11651 ugc11651 reach at least the turnover, and in the cases of UGC 03459 ugc03459 and UGC 11131 ugc11131 we are sampling just the most luminous objects.

UGC 00477 ugc00477 and UGC 06138 ugc06138 . These are the two galaxies for which we used a wide selection of control fields to estimate the contamination by background galaxies, due to their location at high Galactic latitude, which also contributes to minimize the uncertainty due to reddening conditions.

gmw_00006801.name: UGC 03459
gmw_00006801.overview: UGC 03459 is a normal-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:0712.0515]. Because its field of view is located directly through the Galactic plane, strong extinction and reddening leave almost no detected globular cluster candidates [arXiv:0712.0515].

sources.summary: The paper reports UGC 03459 as a normal-surface-brightness target whose globular-cluster results are highly uncertain because strong Galactic-plane extinction leaves nearly no detected candidates.

quotes.quote: The values we inferred for the B-band value of _ 0, disk are then 21.86 ( UGC 00477 ugc00477 ), 21.43 ( UGC 03459 ugc03459 ), 22.63 ( UGC 03587 ugc03587 ), 23.74 ( UGC 06138 ugc06138 ), 21.62 ( UGC 11131 ugc11131 ) and 21.75 ( UGC 11651 ugc11651 ). We used these values to re-classify our target galaxies as described below.

UGC 03459 ugc03459 . This is the galaxy with the most uncertain results. Its field of view is located directly through the galactic plane, at a latitude of b=-3.92 , where any local reddening feature will produce dramatic changes in the results. The almost null detection of candidates in this galaxy tells us that the objects are too absorbed to be detected.

gmw_00006802.name: UGC 03587
gmw_00006802.overview: UGC 03587 is classified as an intermediate surface brightness galaxy with a peculiar morphology [arXiv:0712.0515]. It contains a high number of detected globular cluster candidates, though some may be young star clusters projected along its spiral arms [arXiv:0712.0515].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 03587 as intermediate surface brightness, finds many globular-cluster candidates, and notes possible spiral-arm contamination and imperfect galaxy-light subtraction from its peculiar morphology.

quotes.quote: Using these definitions we classify UGC 06138 ugc06138 as an LSB and UGC 03587 ugc03587 as an intermediate surface brightness galaxy.

UGC 03587 ugc03587 and UGC 11651 ugc11651 . These galaxies have a high number of GC candidates detected. Nevertheless, some of the candidates are projected along the spiral arms of the galaxies, so some contamination due to young star clusters is expected. We also noticed that due to the very peculiar morphology of UGC 03587 ugc03587 , SExtractor's model used to subtract the light of this galaxy when detecting point sources is not as good as in the other galaxies.

gmw_00006803.name: UGC 06138
gmw_00006803.overview: UGC 06138 is classified as a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:0712.0515]. It is located at a high Galactic latitude, which helps minimize uncertainties caused by reddening conditions [arXiv:0712.0515].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 06138 as the lone LSB galaxy, uses high-latitude control fields for it, and finds its cluster colors and luminosities resemble the higher-surface-brightness sample.

quotes.quote: Using these definitions we classify UGC 06138 ugc06138 as an LSB and UGC 03587 ugc03587 as an intermediate surface brightness galaxy.

UGC 00477 ugc00477 and UGC 06138 ugc06138 . These are the two galaxies for which we used a wide selection of control fields to estimate the contamination by background galaxies, due to their location at high Galactic latitude, which also contributes to minimize the uncertainty due to reddening conditions.

It is also worth noticing that the luminosity distributions of clusters hosted by UGC 03587 ugc03587 and UGC 06138 ugc06138 are consistent with those of the GCs hosted by high surface brightness galaxies in our sample.

gmw_00006804.name: UGC 11131
gmw_00006804.overview: UGC 11131 is a normal-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:0712.0515]. Because of its location close to the direction of the Galactic center, the galaxy is affected by strong extinction [arXiv:0712.0515].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 11131 as a normal-surface-brightness sample galaxy whose globular-cluster analysis is limited by strong extinction, luminous-object sampling, and only one usable control field.

quotes.quote: The values we inferred for the B-band value of _ 0, disk are then 21.86 ( UGC 00477 ugc00477 ), 21.43 ( UGC 03459 ugc03459 ), 22.63 ( UGC 03587 ugc03587 ), 23.74 ( UGC 06138 ugc06138 ), 21.62 ( UGC 11131 ugc11131 ) and 21.75 ( UGC 11651 ugc11651 ). We used these values to re-classify our target galaxies as described below.

The samples of UGC 00477 ugc00477 , UGC 06138 ugc06138 and UGC 11651 ugc11651 reach at least the turnover, and in the cases of UGC 03459 ugc03459 and UGC 11131 ugc11131 we are sampling just the most luminous objects.

UGC 11131 UGC 11131 ugc11131 . Due to its location close to the direction of the Galactic center this galaxy is affected by strong extinction. It is also very difficult to obtain good control fields in its case as in this region the stellar density has a strong dependence on the galactic longitud, thus this is the only galaxy with just one control field.

gmw_00006805.name: UGC 11651
gmw_00006805.overview: UGC 11651 is classified as a normal-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:0712.0515]. It contains a high number of detected globular cluster candidates, though some of these candidates are projected along its spiral arms [arXiv:0712.0515].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 11651 among the normal-surface-brightness targets, finds many globular-cluster candidates, but flags possible spiral-arm contamination and distance-related systematic uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The values we inferred for the B-band value of _ 0, disk are then 21.86 ( UGC 00477 ugc00477 ), 21.43 ( UGC 03459 ugc03459 ), 22.63 ( UGC 03587 ugc03587 ), 23.74 ( UGC 06138 ugc06138 ), 21.62 ( UGC 11131 ugc11131 ) and 21.75 ( UGC 11651 ugc11651 ). We used these values to re-classify our target galaxies as described below.

To the uncertainty in the distances (which introduces significant differences in the final number of clusters), we need to add the 14 and 5 Galactic GCs lacking information in the case of UGC 03587 ugc03587 and UGC 11651 ugc11651 , respectively. The difference in the final GC numbers due to these missing objects should not exceed -22/+32 and -6/+6 objects for each of these galaxies, respectively.

UGC 03587 ugc03587 and UGC 11651 ugc11651 . These galaxies have a high number of GC candidates detected. Nevertheless, some of the candidates are projected along the spiral arms of the galaxies, so some contamination due to young star clusters is expected.

gmw_00006806.name: SDSS J090524.08+575915.9
gmw_00006806.overview: SDSS J090524.08+575915.9 is a foreground disk galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z=0.4134 [arXiv:1507.01945]. It exhibits spatially extended, rotating H-alpha emission and has an estimated star formation rate of 2.4 solar masses per year [arXiv:1507.01945].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J090524.08+575915.9 as a z=0.4134 foreground disk galaxy with measured color, luminosity, stellar mass, star-formation rate, and extended rotating H emission.

quotes.quote: The slit position angle was set to -41.9 deg to include both the background (SDSS J090523.59+575912.4) and foreground (SDSS J090524.08+575915.9) galaxies (see the solid red lines in Figure fig:hst_slits ).

We measure a systemic redshift of z=0.4134 0.0001 for the foreground disk galaxy based on 3727 , 5007 , and H 6563 emission lines in the Keck/LRIS spectrum. We then use K-correct bla07 to estimate rest-frame color ( U-B=1.01 ) and absolute magnitude ( M_B=-20.91 ) based on photometry from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS aba09 and from Spitzer/IRAC. We estimate the stellar mass ( 10^ 10.46 ) and star formation rate ( SFR 2.4 yr ^ 1 ) from the same photometry using iSEDfit, the spectral energy distribution modeling code described by mou13 .

The stellar mass and luminosity of the foreground galaxy place it near M^* for the stellar mass function mou13 and L^* for the luminosity function at z 0.4 wilm06 . In particular, the foreground galaxy would be (1.2, 1.1, 0.8) L^* as defined by the B band luminosity function for (blue, red, all) galaxies at z=0.5 from wilm06 . Rotation curve and dynamical mass We show the two-dimensional Keck/LRIS spectrum in the region surrounding the H emission line for the foreground galaxy in Figure fig:ha_lris . The H emission is spatially extended and exhibits the signature of rotation with a line-of-sight amplitude v_ los =178 10 .

gmw_00006807.name: UVIT J202258.73-441623.8
gmw_00006807.overview: UVIT J202258.73-441623.8 is a foreground, star-forming diffuse galaxy with a low-surface-brightness outer disk [arXiv:2112.12720]. Its inner disk exhibits ongoing massive star formation and patchy spiral arms in the far-ultraviolet, while a bar-like feature shows enhanced metallicity [arXiv:2112.12720].

sources.summary: The paper presents UVIT J202258.73-441623.8 as a newly discovered foreground, star-forming diffuse galaxy with a low-surface-brightness outer disk, ongoing inner-disk star formation, and recent starburst episodes.

quotes.quote: NGC 6902A is at a redshift of 0.05554, but MUSE observations indicate that the interacting tail is a separate star-forming, foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.00980. We refer to the new galaxy as UVIT J202258.73-441623.8 ( ). The near-infrared observations show that \ has a stellar mass of 8.7 10 ^ 8 M . Its inner disk (R < 4 kpc) shows UV and H emission from ongoing massive star formation. The rest of the disk is extremely low luminosity, has a low stellar surface density, and extends out to a radius of R 9 kpc.

The velocity and metallicity distribution maps and the star formation history indicate that \ has undergone three bursts of star formation. The latest episode is ongoing, which is supported by the presence of widespread H and UV emission in its inner disk. The galaxy also shows patchy spiral arms in far-UV, and there is a metallicity enhancement along a bar-like feature.

gmw_00006808.name: NGC 6902A
gmw_00006808.overview: NGC 6902A is a barred spiral galaxy with a redshift of 0.05554 [arXiv:2112.12720]. It exhibits a small perturbation of old stars in its south-west region, which may have been caused by a past dry merger [arXiv:2112.12720].

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets NGC 6902A as a background galaxy rather than an interacting system with the new foreground object, while noting its redshift, perturbation, and barred morphology.

quotes.quote: The galaxy was not detected earlier due to its superposition with the background galaxy, NGC 6902A. They were together mistakenly classified as an interacting system. NGC 6902A is at a redshift of 0.05554, but MUSE observations indicate that the interacting tail is a separate star-forming, foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.00980.

NGC 6902A shows signatures of a small perturbation of old stars towards the south-west region in the K band. A few galaxies in the field have similar photometric redshifts, so that the perturbed region could be due to a past dry merger. NGC 6902A is morphologically classified as SB(s)m pec in RC3 catalogue deVaucouleurs1991S&T....82Q.621D and one of the Sersic functions used in our galfit decomposition represents a bar, thus confirming the RC3 morphology.

gmw_00006809.name: ESO 381-47
gmw_00006809.overview: ESO 381-47 is an early-type galaxy featuring an extended disk and a 30 kpc stellar ring that has undergone recent star formation [arXiv:0903.5331]. This stellar structure is surrounded by a warped, 90 kpc cold-gas ring [arXiv:0903.5331].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 381-47 as an early-type XUV-disk galaxy with a 30 kpc stellar ring, 90 kpc cold-gas ring, warped gas kinematics, and possible density-wave triggering.

quotes.quote: ESO 381-47 is an early type galaxy with an extended disk. GALEX and very deep optical images reveal a distinct stellar ring far outside the optical body with a diameter of 30 kpc, which has undergone recent star formation at 1.8 10 ^ 4 yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , consistent with other new results which detect low level star formation below the traditional Kennicutt relation in the outer parts of spiral galaxies. The morphology of this galaxy resembles the recently identified class of ultraviolet objects called extended ultraviolet disks, or XUV-disks.

New observations of this galaxy taken at the ATCA and in the CnB array at the VLA show that the cold gas lies in an extended (diameter 90 kpc) ring around the central S0 galaxy. The data cube can be well modeled by a warped ring. The faint ionized gas in the inner parts of the galaxy is kinematically decoupled from the stars and instead appears to exhibit velocities consistent with the rotation of the ring at larger radius.

The peak of the stellar ring, as seen in the optical and UV, is slightly displaced to the inside relative to the peak of the ring. We discuss the manner in which this offset could be caused by the propagation of a radial density wave through an existing stellar disk, perhaps triggered by a galaxy collision at the center of the disk, or possibly due to a spiral density wave set up at early times in a disk too hot to form a stellar bar.

gmw_00006810.name: z=5.186 quasar
gmw_00006810.overview: The z=5.186 quasar, also known as GDN3333, is an optically faint active galactic nucleus located in the CANDELS/GOODS-North field [arXiv:astro-ph/0210552, arXiv:2006.02451]. It features an unobscured X-ray power-law spectrum powered by an accreting supermassive black hole, with no evidence of intrinsic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0210552, arXiv:2006.02451].

sources.summary: The paper describes the z=5.186 quasar as an optically faint high-redshift AGN with a normal unobscured X-ray power-law spectrum and no evidence for intrinsic absorption or Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: We present X-ray spectral analyses of the three z>4 Active Galactic Nuclei (AGNs) thus far spectroscopically identified in the \ Deep Field-North Survey, at redshifts of 5.186, 4.424, and 4.137. These analyses are made possible by the extremely deep exposure ( 2 Ms) and the low \ background. The rest-frame 2.5--40 keV spectra are the first for optically faint (two of the three sources have I>24 ) z>4 AGNs. The z=5.186 quasar is well fitted by a power-law model with photon index =1.8 0.3 , consistent with those of lower-redshift, unobscured AGNs.

Spectral analysis results \ is the only z>5 quasar having an X-ray spectrum with more than 100 counts to date. Spectral fitting in the observed-frame 0.5--8 keV band (Fig. 1) shows that this source is well parameterized by a power-law continuum with photon index =1.81^ +0.31 _ 0.29 (see Table 2). This photon index is similar to those of z 0--2 AGNs, which have typical photon indices of 1.7--2.3 in the rest-frame 2--10 keV band (e.g., George et al. 2000; Reeves Turner 2000).
There is no evidence for either intrinsic absorption or Fe K emission lines (see Table 2).

sources.summary: The paper identifies GDN3333 as a z=5.186 CANDELS/GOODS-North AGN, clearly detected in Chandra X-rays and interpreted as powered by an accreting supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: GDN3333 The AGN GDN3333 lies in the CANDELS/GOODS-North field, at coordinates RA=12:36:47.96 DEC=+62:09:41.3 (J2000) and with an observed magnitude of 23.91 in the F850LP filter of HST. It has been detected in X-ray (0.5-8.0 keV) by the ultradeep 2 Msec Chandra X-ray image of the Chandra Deep Field North (CDFN), as discussed in alexander03 . It corresponds to the source with ID=247 in the catalog by alexander03 , and it has 138 counts in the 0.5-8.0 keV band.

Summarizing, given its clear X-ray detection by Chandra, as shown in alexander03 and its secure spectroscopic redshift at z=5.186 provided by barger02 , there is no doubt that GDN3333 is an AGN, as concluded also by giallongo19 . Moreover, its X-ray over optical flux ratio is 0.1 (see Fig. 2 of Giallongo et al. 2019), which is one of the highest X/optical flux ratio among the AGN candidates at magnitudes H_ 160 24 provided by giallongo19 . Based on these considerations, we conclude that its X-ray flux cannot come from star formation activity, but it is powered by an accreting SMBH. GDN3333 is the only known AGN brighter than H=24 at z>5 in the CANDELS fields and it has already been included in the AGN luminosity function at z 5.5 by giallongo19 .

gmw_00006811.name: 3C 256
gmw_00006811.overview: 3C 256 is an alignment-effect radio galaxy that lacks a massive old stellar population [arXiv:2308.04621]. Its ultraviolet and optical spectral energy distribution is dominated by a non-stellar origin, which heavily influences estimates of its host galaxy's stellar mass and age [arXiv:1608.00805].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 256 as a problematic radio-galaxy host whose UV/optical SED likely has non-stellar contamination, strongly affecting inferred stellar mass and age.

quotes.quote: The most striking example in which the spectral shape of the UV/optical SED is clearly of non-stellar origin is 3C 256 Simpson99 . A simple way to examine how the resulting physical properties are influenced by the presence of non-stellar contribution in 3C 256's host is to repeat the fit excluding the UV/optical photometric data (see Fig. fig:256 ). Comparing the two fits, the clear difference is in the region covered by the IRAC bands, and without the requirement to fit the UV/optical points, the evolved stellar component contributes with equal weight compared to the young component in this domain. Furthermore, the extreme UV emission in the revised fit is dominated by the young stellar component. Not surprisingly, major differences are found in the estimated properties of the evolved stellar populations in the two scenarios: the stellar mass goes up by a factor of ten, and the age increases from 140 to 3000 Myr (see Table tab:results ).
Given that Fig. fig:256 likely traces the extreme cases of old and young stellar component dominance in the UV domain, the results obtained for the old stellar component should be treated as limits.

sources.summary: The essay treats 3C256 as an alignment-effect radio galaxy whose large baryonic mirror is too cool for hydrostatic support, lacks rotation, and lacks an old stellar population.

quotes.quote: It's especially interesting because we argue for 3C256 (Dey \ 1996 ) that that enormous mass of baryons is too cool for hydrostatic support. There is no rotational support either, so one wonders whether all that mass is undergoing some kind of monolithic collapse This galaxy differs from the other ``alignment-effect" radio galaxies in lacking a massive old stellar population. If I'd been more sophisticated, I'd have discounted electron scattering in both NGC1068 and 3C256.

gmw_00006812.name: 3C 266
gmw_00006812.overview: The galaxy 3C 266 is a radio galaxy and an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1608.00805]. It is one of a small number of objects in the redshift range 1 < z < 1.4 for which Infrared Spectrograph spectra are available [arXiv:1608.00805].

sources.summary: The paper notes that alternate photometric apertures for 3C 266 leave its fitted physical parameters nearly unchanged and that IRS spectra are available.

quotes.quote: 3C 266 -- For this object, Inskip06 computed the UV/optical and near-infrared photometry using apertures of 4 in diameter as opposed to that of 9 used by Best97 . We applied our fitting approach using the Inskip et al. data as input, and found that the estimated physical parameters remain practically the same (within 10 ). This is one of the four objects in the redshift range 1 < z < 1.4 for which IRS spectra are available.

gmw_00006813.name: 3C 368
gmw_00006813.overview: 3C 368 is a radio-loud galaxy at redshift z = 1.131 that hosts an active galactic nucleus and exhibits vigorous, extended star formation [arXiv:1701.03736]. The galaxy shows a strong alignment effect, with likely non-stellar contributions to its optical photometry [arXiv:1608.00805].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 368 as a z=1.131 radio-loud galaxy with powerful AGN activity, vigorous star formation, no detected CO(2--1), and a likely fractionated or chemically young ISM.

quotes.quote: We present the detection of four far-infrared fine-structure oxygen lines, as well as strong upper limits for the CO(2--1) and [ N ii ] 205 lines, in 3C 368, a well-studied radio-loud galaxy at z = 1.131 . These new oxygen lines, taken in conjunction with previously observed neon and carbon fine-structure lines, suggest a powerful active galactic nucleus (AGN), accompanied by vigorous and extended star formation. A starburst dominated by O8 stars, with an age of 6.5 Myr, provides a good fit to the fine-structure line data. This estimated age of the starburst makes it nearly concurrent with the latest episode of AGN activity, suggesting a link between the growth of the supermassive black hole and stellar population in this source. We do not detect the CO(2--1) line, down to a level twelve times lower than the expected value for star forming galaxies.

A different scenario which could be used to explain the lack of CO is that the ISM in 3C 368 may be highly fractionated into small cloudlets. Such a fractioned ISM would have modest extinction to the core of individual cloudlets, allowing for considerable C ^+ emission with relatively little CO emission. Since the upper limit from our CO observations show that the line is suppressed from the expected value by more than 12 , the CO emitting cores of the molecular clouds in 3C 368 must correspondingly be more than 12 smaller in radius than the entire clouds.

sources.summary: The paper says 3C 368 likely has AGN contamination in its optical photometry, consistent with a strong alignment effect and non-stellar K-band contribution.

quotes.quote: 3C 368 -- The optical/UV spectral shape requiring a relatively low age of the evolved stellar population, together with the shape of the NIR SED traced by the shorter bands suggest that some AGN contribution is likely present in the optical photometry of this object. This might be consistent with Best98 , who argued that 3C 368 has the strongest alignment effect among the ones studied in their sample, so that the non-stellar contribution to the K-band photometry is about 30 . This is one of the four objects in the redshift range 1 < z < 1.4 for which IRS spectra are available.

gmw_00006814.name: 3C 470
gmw_00006814.overview: The high-redshift radio galaxy 3C 470 is hosted by a system that may still be in the process of formation [arXiv:1608.00805]. Its host galaxy is dominated by a young stellar component that accounts for approximately 65 percent of its total stellar mass [arXiv:1608.00805].

sources.summary: The paper highlights 3C 470 as unusually young-star dominated among the sample, and compares its mid-infrared SED decomposition with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: An exception to this trend is the host of 3C 470, wherein the young stellar component contains about 65 of the total stellar mass. Given that 3C 470 is one of the objects with the highest redshifts, and the fact that the young stellar component amounts to about 50 of the total stellar mass of the host of 3C 257 (redshift record-holder in the 3CR sample), these objects could also be supporting the view that their hosts are still going through the process of formation, with a significant amount of stellar mass yet to form.

We briefly compare the results obtained for two objects, 3C 368 and 3C 470, for which much of the same The input flux densities are marginally different, whereby we use the photometry by Podigachoski15a and Drouart16 used that by Drouart14 . input data is used in both studies. Considering the best-fit SEDs of these two objects, Drouart16 found that the rest-frame wavelength domain between 10 and 50 m is dominated by the starburst emission, whereas we find that the emission in this domain is largely dominated by the torus emission. This most striking difference is a direct consequence of the adoption of the Siebenmorgen15 models in the current work, which results in overall somewhat better fits to the photometry.

gmw_00006815.name: Mrk 202
gmw_00006815.overview: Mrk 202 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.021 [arXiv:1905.01503]. Monitored in the optical V-band and near-infrared J-band, the system features a host galaxy that is visible in the observed V-band images [arXiv:1905.01503].

sources.summary: The REMAP paper describes Mrk 202 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy monitored in optical and near-infrared bands, but with insufficient data to measure a dust lag.

quotes.quote: Mrk 202 Mrk 202 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.021. Monitoring of this object started in the year 2016, however, as the observing run was affected by bad weather, we could accumulate 14 data points for Mrk 202 in optical V-band and NIR J-band. This sources has a prominent host galaxy in the observed V-band images (Figure 1).

To get the flux of the AGN, GALFIT (Peng et al. 2002) was used to remove the contribution of the host galaxy for each epoch so that changes in seeing will not affect in the final flux-measurement. Aperture photometry was done on the images after removal of the underlying host galaxy component (Figure 2). The generated V and J-band light curves are shown in Figure 3.

gmw_00006816.name: AGC 226067
gmw_00006816.overview: AGC 226067 is a low-mass dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1507.01855] and star-forming stellar system located within the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2206.13971]. It is an extended, interacting system with multiple optical and gas components [arXiv:1507.01855], yet it remains extremely isolated from any candidate parent galaxy [arXiv:2206.13971].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 226067 as a Virgo-distance low-mass dwarf irregular with an optical counterpart, reduced H I mass, extended gas disk, companions, and evidence for interaction.

quotes.quote: Originally identified as an ultra-compact high velocity cloud and candidate Local Group galaxy, AGC 226067 is spatially and kinematically coincident with the Virgo cluster, and the identification by multiple groups of an optical counterpart with no resolved stars supports the interpretation that this systems lies at the Virgo distance ( D=17 Mpc). The combined observations reveal that the system consists of multiple components: a central \ source associated with the optical counterpart (AGC 226067), a smaller only component (AGC 229490), a second optical component (AGC 229491), and extended low surface brightness . Only 1/4 of the single-dish \ emission is associated with AGC 226067; as a result, we find / L_ g 6 / which is lower than previous work.

Our observations definitively associate the \ with the optical counterpart as a low mass dwarf irregular, analogous to the SHIELD galaxies. Importantly, these data reveal that AGC 226067 is an extended interacting system with multiple components, both \ and optical.
The central source, AGC 226067, is a low mass dwarf irregular with extremely low surface brightness, an extended \ disk, \ = 1.5 10^7 , / L_ g 6, and / \ =0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SECCO 1 as the prototype isolated low-mass star-forming stellar system, with metal-rich gas likely stripped from a larger galaxy by tidal or ram-pressure processes.

quotes.quote: One of the most curious cases is SECCO 1 also known as AGC 226067; secco_surv,secco1_15,sand15,Adams2015 , a low-mass ( M_ 10^5 M_ , M_ HI 2 10^7 M_ ) star-forming stellar system lying within the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Given the very low stellar mass, the high mean metallicity ( 12+ log (O/H) = 8.38 0.11 ) implies that the gas fuelling star formation in SECCO 1 was stripped from a relatively massive gas-rich galaxy secco1_17,sand17,secco1_18 , either by a tidal interaction or by ram pressure exerted by the hot intra cluster medium (ICM). Indeed, star formation is known to occur in gas clouds stripped from galaxies via both channels see, e.g., and references therein pasha2021,poggianti2019 . However, in both cases, the star-forming stripped knots are always seen in proximity to the parent galaxy and/or connected to the parent galaxy either by tidal tails or by the jellyfish structures that are the classical fingerprint of ram-pressure stripping see, e.g., Gerhard2002,yoshida2012,fuma2011,fuma2014,kenney2014,fossati2016, mb_pw1,nide_PW1,corb2021a,corb2021b . In contrast, SECCO 1 is extremely isolated, lying more than 200 kpc, in projection, from the nearest candidate parent galaxy sand17,secco1_18,jones_ALLBC . Simple theoretical arguments burk2016 as well as dedicated hydrodynamical simulations secco1_18,Calura20 , suggest that gas clouds similar to SECCO 1 may survive as long as 1 Gyr within clusters of galaxies, kept together by the pressure confinement of the ICM, thus leaving room for very long voyages from the site of origin.

gmw_00006817.name: AGC 229490
gmw_00006817.overview: AGC 229490 is an extragalactic system located at an estimated distance of 17 Mpc [arXiv:1507.01855]. It is an H I-only component that may be connected to the nearby galaxy AGC 226067 by an H I bridge [arXiv:1507.01855].

sources.summary: The paper presents AGC 229490 as an H I-only component near AGC 226067, with narrow velocity dispersion, estimated H I and dynamical masses, and possible bridge/tail interaction evidence.

quotes.quote: At D=17 Mpc, AGC 226067 has an \ mass of 1.5 10^7 \ and L_ g = 2.4 10^6 , AGC 229490 (the only component) has M_ HI = 3.6 10^6 , and AGC 229491 (the second optical component) has L_ g = 3.6 10^5 L_ . The nature of this system of three sources is uncertain: AGC 226067 and AGC 229490 may be connected by an \ bridge, and AGC 229490 and AGC 229491 are separated by only 0.5 . The current data do not resolve the \ in AGC 229490 and its origin is unclear.

AGC 229490 is offset by 19 \ in central velocity and has narrow fitted velocity dispersions, consistent with the velocity width found in the extracted spectrum but unusual for extragalactic systems. A position-velocity slice bisecting AGC 226067, AGC 229490, and part of the potential northern extension shows that the \ components may be continuous in position-velocity space.

For AGC 229490, we assume no rotation and adopt a velocity dispersion of 4 \ and a radius of 1.6 kpc (20 \ at 17 Mpc, slightly smaller than the beam size), giving a dynamical mass estimate of 1.8 10^7 .

gmw_00006818.name: AGC 229491
gmw_00006818.overview: AGC 229491 is a very blue optical-only source [arXiv:1507.01855]. It is located near AGC 229490 and is likely physically associated with the interacting AGC 226067 system [arXiv:1507.01855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 229491 as a very blue optical source near AGC 229490, likely physically associated with the AGC 226067 system and possibly involved in an interaction.

quotes.quote: We independently identify the second optical counterpart noted by 2015ApJ...806...95S . This source is located near the second \ component (AGC 229490) but offset to the East by 30 , so we assign it a unique identification number: AGC 229491. For an aperture of radius 3 \ encompassing the blue knot of emission that is AGC 229491, we find g_0= 22.38 0.03 and (g-i)_0 = -0.29 0.08 .

The VLA and deep optical data reveal two additional sources: an only source (AGC 229490) and an optical-only source (AGC 229491). Both are separated from AGC 226067 by 1.6 \ (8 kpc). AGC 229491 was noted by 2015ApJ...806...95S , but they were not able to definitively associate it with the AGC 226067 system.
The extended \ nature of this system and the close proximity to AGC 229490 (0.5 , or 2.5 kpc), strongly argue for its physical association with the AGC 226067 system.

gmw_00006819.name: IRAS 23226-3843
gmw_00006819.overview: IRAS 23226-3843 is an S0-type galaxy [arXiv:2004.01711] that was originally classified as a Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:2004.01711]. It is a changing-look active galactic nucleus that has exhibited a strong X-ray decline, optical continuum fading, and exceptionally broad, double-peaked Balmer profiles [arXiv:2004.01711].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 23226-3843 as a changing-look Seyfert galaxy with a strong X-ray decline, optical continuum and Balmer-line fading, and unusually broad double-peaked Balmer profiles.

quotes.quote: Other designations for are CTS B11.01, 2MASX J23252420-3826492, 6dF J2325242-382649, and HE 2322-3843. It was included in the NRAO VLA Sky Survey (NVSS) as a radio source with a flux density level of 4.3 mJy (Condon et al. condon98 ). was classified as Seyfert type 1 in 1991 (Allen et al. allen91 ) based on its optical spectrum. The galaxy is of morphology type SO (Loveday loveday96 ).

The case of IRAS 23226-3843 is special in comparison to HE 1136-2304 and other changing-look AGN because its X-ray drop is extremely high and its Balmer-line profile is exceptionally broad and double-peaked. A change of the luminosity is always connected with a change of the Edddington ratio L/L _ edd in AGN because the central black hole mass does not change on short timescales. showed a low Eddington ratio of L/L _ edd =0.01 in its high state in 1999.

gmw_00006820.name: AGC 215414 OC
gmw_00006820.overview: AGC 215414 OC is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located at the tip of the plume extending eastward from NGC 3628 [arXiv:2403.12130]. Optical and radio observations suggest it may be a self-gravitating system containing numerous star-forming regions [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 215414 OC as a tidal dwarf candidate at the NGC 3628 plume tip, with VLA and DECaLS evidence for a self-gravitating, star-forming system.

quotes.quote: AGC 215414 OC is located at the tip of the plume extending eastward from NGC 3628. Observations and simulations indicated that NGC 3628 and NGC 3627 (M66), members of the Leo Triplet galaxy system, experienced a tidal encounter 800 Myr ago, resulting in the formation of the plume 1978AJ.....83..219R, 1979ApJ...229...83H . AGC 215414 OC has been identified as a tidal dwarf galaxy in later studies and observations 2014ApJ...786..144N . A H i velocity distribution map of the tidal tail from the VLA observations (Project code: AB1074, PI: A. Bolatto) has also been presented in Fig. 3 of 2014ApJ...786..144N , which revealed a distinct difference in velocities between the plume and AGC 215414, accompanied by a noticeable bending of the plume towards AGC 215414 at its far end. This suggests that AGC 215414 may not be gravitationally bound to the plume and could potentially evolve into a self-gravitating system, indicating that AGC 215414 OC is a tidal-origin galaxy.

Optical images from DECaLS could further support the evidence. AGC 215414 OC exhibits the inward-bending characteristic in both the g and bands, as demonstrated in the upper panel of Fig. fig:07 , which shows the g band image overlaid by H i distribution contours from VLA. These images also reveal numerous star-forming regions within AGC 215414 OC, suggesting that a considerable number of stars have already started to form inside it.

gmw_00006821.name: AGC 229197 OC
gmw_00006821.overview: AGC 229197 OC is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located precisely at the center of the H I 1225+01 intermediate gas bridge [arXiv:2403.12130]. It is likely in an early stage of star formation triggered by a tidal interaction between the system's northeast and southwest clumps [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper places AGC 229197 OC in the H i 1225+01 bridge, arguing its size, H i association, and location favor an early star-forming tidal galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: AGC 229197 OC is precisely situated at the center of the intermediate H i bridge. Whether the optical emission we found corresponds to H i gas is up for discussion. We estimated the physical size of AGC 229197 OC based on the effective radius given in Table tab:02 . Assuming the optical counterpart is at the same distance of the H i component, we calculated a physical scale of 234 pc for AGC 229197 OC.

This suggests that AGC 229197 OC might have been a galaxy in the early stage of star formation, and it is likely to originate from the tidal process between the encounter of the NE and SW clumps. The specific location and star formation may also hold significant implications for understanding the evolutionary stage of the entire H i 1225+01 system.

Furthermore, the system possesses limited stellar components that correspond to the optical characteristics of galaxies. Therefore, it is more plausible to consider AGC 229197 OC as part of the system rather than a background galaxy.

gmw_00006822.name: AGC 123216 OC
gmw_00006822.overview: AGC 123216 OC is a faint, peanut-shaped system surrounded by several galaxies, including NGC 807 and KUG 0202+283 [arXiv:2403.12130]. It may have formed in situ or as tidal debris, and it shows signs of recent star formation [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 123216 OC as a faint peanut-shaped system with ambiguous origin, possibly tidal debris or in-situ formation, showing H i relaxation and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: AGC 123216 OC is a faint, peanut-shaped object surrounded by NGC 807, NGC 805, MRK 365, and KUG 0202+283 (numbers 3, 4, 5, and 2, respectively). Their relative positions, coordinates, and redshifts are shown in Fig. fig:08 and Table tab:05 .

The spatial attributes of AGC 123216 OC suggest that it could be tidal debris from the interaction of member galaxies in the compact group, where a recent collapse of molecular gas has triggered the star formation. However, the concentration and morphology of the three galaxies do not indicate signs of interaction, and their colors are also different from that of AGC 123216 OC. Therefore, it is also possible that AGC 123216 OC was formed in situ, isolated from other galaxies. Additionally, there is no evidence to definitively rule out the possibility that it originated from another galaxy, KUG 0202+283. Nevertheless, the distinct peanut-shaped morphological profile of AGC 123216 OC suggests that it could likely have already evolved into a self-gravitational system over time under gravitational force.

gmw_00006823.name: AGC 215415 OC
gmw_00006823.overview: AGC 215415 OC is a faint, diffuse galaxy located southeast of the Leo Triplet [arXiv:2403.12130]. It is identified as a member of the Leo Triplet Group, and its formation may be attributed to interaction processes within this gas-rich environment [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 215415 OC as a faint diffuse Leo Triplet member candidate whose LSB-rich environment suggests interaction-driven formation, possibly linked to the AGC 215414 event.

quotes.quote: AGC 215415 OC is a faint, diffuse galaxy located southeast of Leo Triplet, with consistent redshift. It has been identified as an H i only (without optical emissions) member of the Leo Triplet Group (M 66 Group) by velocity dispersions and spatial density distributions. With a Hubble flow distance of AGC 215415 at 10 Mpc, a FOV of 3.4^ corresponds to the physical scale of 0.6 Mpc, as illustrated in the lower left panel of Fig. fig:08 .

The surroundings of AGC 215415 OC are characterized by a large number of LSB objects, which could be a result of interactions in the region. Given the abundant gas composition of the environment, it is possible that these interactions are dominated by the Leo Triplet. Such interactions between galaxies will lead to the diffusion and mixing of gas and stellar components, which gradually relax into new systems under the influence of gravity and viscosity. These interactions may also trigger and promote the star formation process, finally resulting in more galaxies within the environment with bluer colors, diffuse morphologies, and LSB features. In the surrounding image of AGC 215415 depicted in Fig. fig:08 , the positions of the sources lacking optical counterparts (marked by blue circles) indicate that they are likely to share a common origin, NGC 3628 (num.2). However, it is challenging to make reliable deductions about the origin of AGC 215415 OC, as it is situated at the center of the FOV, at a distance of 1.39 from NGC 3628. Nevertheless, it is reasonable to consider the important role of the cluster environment, and the formation of AGC 215415 OC may be attributed to possible interaction processes.

gmw_00006824.name: AGC 219841 OC
gmw_00006824.overview: AGC 219841 OC is a diffuse and irregular dwarf galaxy located in a complex group environment [arXiv:2403.12130]. It likely formed through an intricate interaction process within the same neutral hydrogen cloud associated with the Arp 313 triplet [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper presents AGC 219841 OC as a diffuse irregular system in a complex group environment, likely formed through interaction within the Arp 313 H i cloud.

quotes.quote: The morphology of AGC 219841 OC appears diffuse and irregular, with additional brightness contamination from the nearby star and galaxy further complicating the discernibility. AGC 219841 is located in a complex environment surrounded by galaxies with the same redshift, as listed in Table tab:07 . The galaxies are arranged in a northeast-southwest direction, as shown in the upper right panel of Fig. fig:08 .

Particularly, the optical counterpart of H i emission is entirely absent in the western direction, where AGC 219841 OC is located. Therefore, we believe that AGC 219841 OC could have formed as a result of an intricate interaction process occurring within the same H i cloud associated with the Arp 313 Triplet. In addition, the high-sensitivity H i observations from the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical Telescope (FAST) revealed a large-scale H i gas structure of more than 30^ in the northeast-southwest direction of AGC 219841, with the H i column density contours overlaid on the surrounding image of AGC 219841 OC 2024SCPMA..6719511Z . This provides strong observational evidence for the potential co-evolution of these galaxies.

gmw_00006825.name: AGC 258609 OC
gmw_00006825.overview: AGC 258609 OC is a low-surface-brightness galaxy with a morphology that is difficult to discern by eye [arXiv:2403.12130]. It is located in a tidally disturbed, pre-merger environment around NGC 5990, suggesting the galaxy may have originated from ejected gas [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 258609 OC as the lowest peak-surface-brightness source in the sample, likely tied to a pre-merger, tidally disturbed environment around NGC 5990.

quotes.quote: AGC 258609 OC has the lowest peak-surface-brightness among the eight sources, and the morphology is difficult to discern by eye. Table tab:08 lists the galaxies with similar redshifts within 0.6 Mpc, and the lower right panel of Fig. fig:08 illustrates their positions in the environment. Among them, NGC 5990 (number 2) is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) in the Great Observatories All-sky LIRG Survey GOALS, 2009PASP..121..559A , classified as a Seyfert II galaxy. The optical Integral Field Unit (IFU) spectrum from the Wide Field Spectrograph (WiFeS) showed some post-starburst galaxy features and a lack of correlation between gas density and SFR, which suggests the possibility of significant dust shielding or gas outflows 2017ApJS..232...11T, 2018A&A...618A...6K . Visual inspection of the Spitzer Space Telescope Infrared Array Camera (IRAC) 3.6 m image revealed that it is a pre-merger 2013ApJS..206....1S . Among the five galaxies listed in Table tab:08 , CGCG 050-098 (number 3) is acknowledged as the companion galaxy of NGC 5990 2006AJ....132..197W . Additionally, CGCG 050-091 (number 6) and CGCG 050-095 (number 4) are classified as satellite galaxies of NGC 5990 with retrograde orbits, while CGCG 050-093 (number 5) is another satellite galaxy with a prograde orbit 2006ApJ...645..228A . These observations indicate that the region around AGC 258609 OC has experienced or is currently undergoing a significant star formation process and is in a pre-merger stage.

The environment consists of a primary galaxy and several companion galaxies with different orbits, which is likely to result in asymmetric tidal torques, triggering gas inflows and outflows 1996ApJ...464..641M . These findings strongly imply that tidal interactions may be responsible for the origin of AGC 258609 OC, whose progenitor is likely to be the gas ejected through these processes.

gmw_00006826.name: AGC 225880 OC
gmw_00006826.overview: AGC 225880 OC is a galaxy located in the outskirts of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2403.12130]. It is closely associated with the isolated neutral hydrogen structure Cloud 1, specifically positioned near its southern component, Cloud 1 South [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper argues AGC 225880 OC is associated with Cloud 1 South in Virgo outskirts, probably linked to isolated H i clouds and possibly shaping nearby gas structure through star formation.

quotes.quote: AGC 225880 OC is found in the outskirt of the Virgo cluster and is only 12.5^ away from the H i emission centroid of Cloud 1 South (C1S) in 2010ApJ...725.2333K , which strongly suggests that they are related. H i observations from VLA revealed that Cloud 1 (C1) is an isolated cloud, with no optical counterpart identified in previous studies. According to the research conducted by 2010ApJ...725.2333K , the nearest optical structure of C1 is positioned 3.8^ towards the northeast direction, and the closest late-type galaxy with a matching redshift of the Virgo cluster is situated at a distance of 1 towards the northeast. Moreover, the study claimed that C1 is the only gas structure that is definitively extragalactic and unambiguous, and is not associated with another galaxy outside the Local Group. Although there are strong indications that AGC 225880 OC might correspond to the H i component of C1S, we also examined other galaxies within 0.6 Mpc that share the same redshift.

Therefore, we tend to infer that AGC 225880 OC is associated with C1. More details are revealed in the zoom-in area of the left panel Fig. fig:09 , which corresponds to the beam area of ALFALFA, covered C1N, C1S, and AGC 225880 OC.
