The center of AGC 225880 OC, coincides with a depression in the gas contour of C1S, which is marked as a light green cross. AGC 225880 OC is located north of C1S, between C1N and C1S, and closer to C1S.

gmw_00006827.name: AGC 229361 OC
gmw_00006827.overview: AGC 229361 OC is a galaxy candidate located on the periphery of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2403.12130]. It exhibits an exceptionally high ratio of neutral hydrogen to stellar mass, and it appears to belong to an isolated system where star formation was suppressed [arXiv:2403.12130].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 229361 OC as the sample’s highest H i-to-stellar-mass galaxy candidate, apparently isolated and possibly formed under unusual early-universe conditions that suppressed star formation.

quotes.quote: AGC 229361 OC stands out as the galaxy candidate with the highest M_ H i /M_ * among the eight sources in this paper. It is located on the periphery of the Virgo Cluster. Two galaxies share the same redshift with it within a 0.6 Mpc scale: VirgoH i 27 and AGC 229360. Table tab:10 and the right panel of Fig. fig:09 provide detailed information about their positions, distances, and separations.

However, given the scale at which interactions may occur, a more plausible assumption based on available data is that the system constituted by AGC 229361, AGC 229360, and VirgoH i 27 appears to be isolated. Besides, although the beam size of ALFALFA may cause some overestimation of M_ H i , it is insufficient to account for the abnormally high M_ H i /M_ * displayed by AGC 229361. These findings provide valuable insights into the formation scenarios of AGC 229361 OC, which may have emerged under unique conditions in the early universe that hindered star formation for unclear reasons.

gmw_00006828.name: HE0045-2145
gmw_00006828.overview: HE0045-2145 is a nearly face-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.0272] located at a redshift of z=0.0214 [arXiv:1511.00904]. Although previously classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy, its emission is dominated by ongoing star formation rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: HE0045--2145 is described as a barred spiral with possible tidal disturbance, low AGN contribution, strong stellar features, and evidence favoring starburst dominance over Seyfert-1 classification.

quotes.quote: HE0045--2145 (also known as ESO 540-27) is a barred spiral galaxy at redshift z=0.0214 , corresponding to D_L=93.2 , i.e. one of the closest objects in our sample. An elongated, patchy structure in the direct neighbourhood indicates tidal interaction with a possibly disrupted companion. However, more detailed information about this object is missing and the galaxy itself does not show further indications for interaction. The NIR colours suggest a low AGN contribution. Prominent stellar absorption features in the analysed nuclear spectrum support the importance of the stellar contribution also in the central region.
All these arguments indicate that HE0045--2145 is rather dominated by its stellar components and ongoing star formation and that the classification as Seyfert 1 is invalid.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE0045-2145 as a nearly face-on barred spiral with a possible inner ring, blue nuclear colors, very low fitted AGN fraction, and questionable Seyfert 1 classification.

quotes.quote: 08 HE0045-2145 Almost face-on spiral galaxy with a bar and a disk scale-length of 3.3 kpc. There is possibly an inner ring that can only be seen in the residuum. The nuclear colors are bluer than the colors of the complete galaxy. Additionally, the fitted AGN fraction is very low. Optical spectra of this source (in preparation) suggest that the classification as Seyfert 1 is incorrect.

gmw_00006829.name: HE0119-0118
gmw_00006829.overview: HE0119-0118 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1511.00904, arXiv:1310.0272] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1511.00904]. The galaxy hosts a Seyfert 1.5 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.00904] and features a bar, spiral arms, and indications of an inner ring [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: HE0119--0118 is identified as a barred spiral LIRG with starburst-like IR colors, high molecular gas content, broad recombination lines, and both black-hole accretion and star formation.

quotes.quote: HE0119--0118 (also known as Mrk1503 or II Zw 1) is a barred spiral galaxy at z=0.0547 , corresponding to D_L=244 and can be classified as luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) based on the IRAS fluxes. The IRAS colours of (f_ (12 )/f_ (25 )) 0.3 and (f_ (60 )/f_ (100 )) = -0.1 lie in the region of starburst dominated galaxies. In the FIR colour-colour diagrams of (Fig. fig:firdiag ), the galaxy consistently shows a starburst contribution of at least 50 . The H _2 mass is M_ H _2 =5.4 10^9 M_ 2007A&A...470..571B .

The broad hydrogen recombination lines ( FWHM _ BLR 3000 ) and the position in the diagnostic diagram (Fig. fig:diagndiagram ) indicate black hole accretion and that the classification as Seyfert galaxy Sy1.5, 2006A&A...455..773V is correct. However, the mentioned arguments show that star formation is a significant factor. In the imaging study 2014A&A...561A.140B , we found the central stellar component (bulge) to be very compact and we were not able to disentangle it from the nuclear non-stellar component.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE0119-0118 as a small-angular-diameter galaxy with a prominent bar and spiral arms that prevent a good disk fit and show hints of an inner ring.

quotes.quote: 16 HE0119-0118 The galaxy has a redshift of almost 0.06 and thus a small angular diameter. A very prominent bar and spiral arms make a good disk-fit impossible. In the residuum, indications for an inner ring can be seen. A bulge component could not be fitted. Therefore, we excluded this galaxy when estimating BH masses from the bulge luminosity.

gmw_00006830.name: HE1248-1356
gmw_00006830.overview: HE1248-1356 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy located at a low redshift of z=0.0145 [arXiv:1310.0272]. The galaxy has a disk scale length of 2.5 kpc, spiral arms, no detected substructure, and a very low active galactic nucleus fraction [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1248-1356 as a highly inclined low-redshift spiral with a 2.5 kpc disk scale length, prominent spiral arms, no detected substructure, and low AGN fraction.

quotes.quote: 69 HE1248-1356 A highly inclined ( i 69^ ) spiral galaxy at low redshift of z=0.0145 . The disk has a scale length of h_r = 2.5 kpc and very prominent spiral arms. We see no indication for a substructure. However, because of the high inclination, the detection of a bar would be difficult. The AGN fraction is very low.

gmw_00006831.name: HE1256-1805
gmw_00006831.overview: HE1256-1805 is a dim, circular elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.0272]. Its nuclear colors indicate a low active galactic nucleus fraction of below 20 percent [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1256-1805 as a dim circular elliptical affected by a foreground star, with low AGN fraction, unreliable large-aperture colors, and a fitted Sersic profile.

quotes.quote: 70 HE1256-1805 A circular elliptical. The galaxy is quite dim and a bright foreground star was in the field of view, resulting in a poor S/N. The nuclear colors indicate a low AGN fraction. This is consistent with the decomposition result (AGN fraction below 20 ). The colors in larger apertures have large errors and thus are less reliable.

gmw_00006832.name: HE1310-1051
gmw_00006832.overview: HE1310-1051 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that appears nearly spherical in near-infrared images [arXiv:1310.0272, arXiv:1909.01395]. Modeling of the galaxy reveals a bulge component and a residual arm-like structure that may represent spiral arms or tidal tails [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper finds a constrained BLR minimum radius and marginal opening angle for PG 1310-108, while most parameters remain broad even with monitoring data.

quotes.quote: PG 1310-108 The parameter = pg1310_rmin light-days is constrained and agrees with the full light-curve result within the uncertainties. Additionally there is one parameter that can be marginally constrained for PG 1310-108, the opening angle: = pg1310_opening degrees. Looking at the posterior probability distributions for \ and \ in Fig posterior_iisz10 one can see that the probability distribution appears to have two peaks, which translates to broad 68 confidence ranges and poorly constrained angles. These two peaks are also present in the full light-curve results, indicating that it is difficult to constrain the angles for PG 1310-108, even when using monitoring data. All the remaining parameters have broad posterior probability distributions and therefore cannot be significantly constrained from the single-epoch spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1310-1051 as nearly spherical in NIR images, modeled with AGN and bulge components, with residual arm-like structure possibly tracing spiral arms or tidal tails.

quotes.quote: 71 HE1310-1051 The galaxy has no prominent features that deviate from the spherical symmetry in our NIR images. We fit an AGN component and a bulge component with effective radius r_e=2.6 kpc and Sersic index varying between n=2.5 in the J band and n=4.3 in the K band. The subtraction of the model reveals an arm-like structure that could be identified as spiral arms or tidal tails. In HST images as PG 1310-108 1998ApJS..117...25M , the arm is more prominent.

gmw_00006833.name: HE1330-1013
gmw_00006833.overview: HE1330-1013 is a disk galaxy characterized by an elongated bar-like structure surrounded by a ring [arXiv:1310.0272]. The galaxy also features a low-Sersic index bulge and a one-sided tail of unexplained origin [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1330-1013 as a disk galaxy with an elongated bar-like structure, surrounding ring, one-sided tail, and a low-Sersic bulge suggestive of a pseudo-bulge.

quotes.quote: 74 HE1330-1013 The galaxy has a disk scale length of h_r=8 kpc and shows a prominent elongated bar-like structure surrounded by a ring. On one side, a tail can be seen. Its origin cannot be explained with imaging data. A more detailed study to reveal the dynamical properties would be interesting. The bulge component has a low S ersic-index value and lies slightly offset from the Kormendy-relation (Fig. fig:fp ).

gmw_00006834.name: HE1338-1423
gmw_00006834.overview: HE1338-1423 is a disk galaxy that hosts a quasar [arXiv:1310.0272]. The galaxy features an inclined disk, a spheroidal component, and structural deviations that may indicate a warped or more complex disk structure [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1338-1423 as an inclined disk host with a spheroidal component and deviations from a purely elliptical shape, possibly indicating a warped or more complex disk.

quotes.quote: 75 HE1338-1423 The galaxy consists of a disk with an inclination i 54^ and a scale length of h_r=9.6 kpc. Additionally, we found a spheroidal component with ellipticity 0.2 and effective radius 3.1 kpc, that is, we found a slightly smaller bulge and disk than in the fit of . This could be an effect of including a bar in our fit. However, we found deviations from a purely elliptical shape, which might be indicative of a more complex structure, for instance, a warped disk.

gmw_00006835.name: HE1348-1758
gmw_00006835.overview: HE1348-1758 is an elliptical galaxy that hosts a quasar [arXiv:1310.0272]. The host galaxy is very circular, showing no signs of irregularities, perturbations, or companion galaxies in its field of view [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1348-1758 as a very circular elliptical QSO host without irregularities, perturbations, or companion-galaxy candidates in the observed field.

quotes.quote: 77 HE1348-1758 A very circular elliptical. There are no signs for irregularities or perturbations. No candidates for companion galaxies are found in the field of view.

gmw_00006836.name: HE1417-0909
gmw_00006836.overview: HE1417-0909 is an elliptical galaxy with an ellipticity of 0.4 and no other morphological irregularities [arXiv:1310.0272]. There are several neighboring galaxies at the same redshift in its region, which could be interacting with it [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper says HE1417-0909 has unreliable K-band colors and fits because of reduction residuals, appears elliptical with no other irregularities, and lies near same-redshift neighboring galaxies.

quotes.quote: 79 HE1417-0909 In the K band image, we were unable to avoid residua originating from the data reduction. Therefore, the colors and fits are not reliable. We found an elliptical with =0.4 and no other irregularities. There are several neighboring galaxies at the same redshift in the region (distance 0.5-1 , redshift from NED), which could be interacting with HE1417--0909.

gmw_00006837.name: HE2112-5926
gmw_00006837.overview: HE2112-5926 is a very circular elliptical galaxy with a low active galactic nucleus fraction [arXiv:1310.0272]. It forms the interacting galaxy pair ESO 144-IG 021 with a nearby spiral companion [arXiv:1310.0272, arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE2112-5926 as a very circular elliptical with red central and host colors, low decomposed AGN fraction, likely dust reddening, and a nearby interacting spiral companion.

quotes.quote: 80 HE2112-5926 A very circular elliptical with no sign for perturbations. The center as well as the AGN-subtracted host galaxy are very red. However, the decomposition reveals a low AGN fraction. The reddening is more likely to come from dust extinction. There is a nearby companion (spiral galaxy), which forms the interacting galaxy pair ESO 144-IG 021 with HE2112--5926.

sources.summary: HE2112--5926 is described as the elliptical member of the interacting ESO144-21 pair, with high stellar continuum contribution and a weak non-stellar point-source component.

quotes.quote: HE2112--5926 is part of the interacting galaxy pair ESO144-21. It has an elliptical shape, the interacting partner is a spiral galaxy. The source has redshift z=0.0317 ( D_L=139.1 ). The stellar contribution to the continuum emission is 80 . This is in agreement with the low luminosity fraction of the non-stellar point source found in the imaging study 2014A&A...561A.140B .

gmw_00006838.name: HE2128-0221
gmw_00006838.overview: HE2128-0221 is an elongated elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0528 [arXiv:1511.00904, arXiv:1310.0272]. Its blue continuum displays stellar absorption lines, a weak hydrogen recombination line Pa, and a broad component [arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: HE2128--0221 is presented as an elongated elliptical, the least luminous object in the study, with low signal-to-noise, stellar absorption, weak Pa, and a broad component.

quotes.quote: HE2128--0221 appears as an elongated elliptical at redshift z=0.0528 ( D_L=235.2 ) and is the least luminous object observed in this study. This explains the low signal-to-noise ratio . The continuum is blue and shows clear stellar absorption lines. The hydrogen recombination line Pa is weak compared to the continuum emission. However, a broad component is clearly seen.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE2128-0221 as a very elongated elliptical at redshift 0.0528, fitted with a Sersic profile, with residual hints of substructure but no corroborating optical or SDSS features.

quotes.quote: 81 HE2128-0221 A very elongated elliptical with =0.5 at a redshift of z=0.0528 . We fit a Sersic profile with r_e=2 kpc and n 3 . The residuum implies a substructure. However, we were unable to find indications of other structures, neither in optical, nor in SDSS images.

gmw_00006839.name: HE2129-3356
gmw_00006839.overview: HE2129-3356 is an elongated elliptical Seyfert 1.2 galaxy [arXiv:1511.00904, arXiv:1310.0272] with a low active galactic nucleus fraction [arXiv:1310.0272]. The galaxy is surrounded by several extended objects with which it is likely interacting [arXiv:1511.00904, arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: HE2129--3356 is described as an interacting elliptical Seyfert 1.2 galaxy with substantial stellar continuum, AGN-dominated diagnostics, coronal emission, and very broad hydrogen lines.

quotes.quote: HE2129--3356 is an elliptically shaped galaxy at redshift z=0.0293 ( D_L=128.3 ), classified as Seyfert 1.2 by . The galaxy is surrounded by several objects it is most probably interacting with. Stellar absorption features are present and the stellar component contributes 75 to the continuum emission. Nevertheless, the position in the diagnostic diagram (Fig. fig:diagndiagram ) is that of an AGN-dominated galaxy and the presence of the coronal line [ Si vi ] emission indicates a type-1 AGN. Also the hydrogen recombination lines are very strong and show the broadest line widths ( 5000 ) in this study.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE2129-3356 as an elongated elliptical whose host is redder than its nucleus, with low AGN fraction and surrounding extended objects of unconfirmed interaction status.

quotes.quote: 82 HE2129-3356 We found an elongated elliptical. The color analysis shows that the host is redder than the nucleus. Decomposition indicates that the AGN fraction is very low and even drops in the K band. We found the galaxy to be surrounded by several objects, some of them extended.

gmw_00006840.name: HE2204-3249
gmw_00006840.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy HE2204-3249 has an unexpectedly low H-band active galactic nucleus fraction [arXiv:1310.0272]. Its host galaxy is redder than the nucleus, and residual structure suggests a dust lane follows the galaxy's symmetry axis [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper says HE2204-3249 has an unexpectedly low H-band AGN fraction, a host redder than the nucleus, and residual structure consistent with a dust lane along the galaxy symmetry axis.

quotes.quote: 83 HE2204-3249 The AGN fraction in the H band is lower than expected compared with that of the J and K band. Additionally, the host galaxy is redder than the nucleus. We suspect that a dust lane follows the semi-major axis of the galaxy. This is supported by the residual image of our decomposition analysis, which clearly indicates a residual structure that follows the symmetry axis of the galaxy.

gmw_00006841.name: HE2236-3621
gmw_00006841.overview: HE2236-3621 is a dim elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.0272]. Structural decomposition of the galaxy reveals a tail-like irregularity as well as an unexplained central residual irregularity [arXiv:1310.0272].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE2236-3621 as a dim elliptical whose decomposition reveals a tail-like irregularity plus an unexplained central residual irregularity.

quotes.quote: 89 HE2236-3621 The decomposition of this quite dim elliptical galaxy reveals a tail-like irregularity. The residuum also shows an irregularity in the center (which cannot be explained by a PSF-mismatch etc.). The origin of the perturbation is unclear.

gmw_00006842.name: FCSS J033710.0-354727
gmw_00006842.overview: FCSS J033710.0-354727 is a forming polar ring or disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.051 [arXiv:1411.6007]. It features a central disk surrounded by a warped, ring-like structure with bright knots and northwestern debris, which likely originate from a disrupting companion galaxy [arXiv:1411.6007].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FCSS J033710.0-354727 as a z=0.051 forming polar ring/disk galaxy with a central disk, warped ring-like structure, bright knots, and debris from companion disruption.

quotes.quote: This object, named FCSS J033710.0-354727, is a background galaxy in the VST deep field of the Fornax cluster. The deep exposures combined with the high angular resolution of the OmegaCAM at VST allow us to carry out the first detailed photometric analysis for this system in the g and i bands to derive the galaxy structure and colors. Results show that the central object resembles a disk galaxy, surrounded by a ring-like structure 2 times more extended than the central disk. The warped geometry and the presence of bright knots observed along the polar direction, as well as the several debris detected on the NW side with colors comparable to those of the galaxy, suggest that the polar structure is still forming.

In FCSS J033710.0-354727, the warped geometry of the polar structure and the presence of bright knots along its light distribution, as well as the several debris observed on the NW side with similar colors, suggest that the polar structure is still forming. Given the high luminosity, comparable with that of the galaxy center, the two knots Y and Z (see Fig.  PRG ) could be the remnant of a companion galaxy that is disrupting in the potential of the central disk, which is 2-3 times more massive than the accreting object.

gmw_00006843.name: SPT0615-JD
gmw_00006843.overview: SPT0615-JD is a gravitationally magnified galaxy at a redshift of approximately 10 [arXiv:2404.10770]. It is less luminous than a typical galaxy at this redshift, with its light dominated by five young massive star clusters [arXiv:2404.10770].

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST imaging of SPT0615-JD as an exceptionally magnified z~10 galaxy, resolving mirror images, star clusters, a strong Lyman break, blue continuum, and candidate counterimage.

quotes.quote: Magnified to 24.5 AB mag, SPT0615-JD is several magnitudes brighter than most new z 10 candidates being discovered by JWST. Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging at 1.6  (rest-frame UV at 1500  ) identified \ as a 2 5-long arc Salmon2018 and revealed small structures with radii 25 - 70  pc Welch2023 . The superb resolution offered by NIRCam has enabled to resolve the \ into 5 young massive star clusters (YSCs) which dominate the light of the galaxy Adamo2024 . The combination of magnified brightness and resolution make the \ a unique laboratory to conduct spatially-resolved studies with JWST not possible in any other galaxy at this distance.

The \ has an intrinsic F200W apparent magnitude of = 29.7 . This corresponds to an absolute magnitude of = -17.8 in the rest-frame UV. This makes the \ less luminous than the typical M^ * galaxy at z 10 e.g. Finkelstein2023b, Adams2023b , placing it in the category of galaxies likely to have driven cosmic reionization.

gmw_00006844.name: LAE J1044 0123
gmw_00006844.overview: LAE J1044 0123 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy candidate located at a redshift of z=5.655 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301635, arXiv:astro-ph/0306409]. It exhibits a spatially extended Lyman-alpha emission structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0301635] and an unusually narrow intrinsic emission-line width [arXiv:astro-ph/0306409].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAE J1044 0123 as a z=5.655 star-forming dwarf-galaxy candidate with extended Ly emission, narrow line width, and a dynamical mass near 2e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Our follow-up optical spectroscopy of these sources reveals that one source found at (J2000)=10 ^ h 44 ^ m 27 ^ s and (J2000)= 01^ 23 ^ 45 ^ (hereafter LAE J1044 0123) is a good candidate to be a subgalactic object at high redshift Another source has been identified as a Ly emitter at z=5.687 (Ajiki et al. 2002) .

The NB816 image reveals that LAE J1044 0123 is spatially extended; its angular diameter is 1.6 arcsec (above the 2 noise level). The size of the point spread function in the NB816 image is 0.90 arcsec. Correcting for this spread, we obtain an angular diameter of 1.3 arcsec.

Together with a linear size of 7.7 h_ 0.7 ^ 1 kpc, we estimate a lower limit of the dynamical mass of this object to be 2 10^9 M_ . It is thus suggested that LAE J1044 0123 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy (i.e., a subgalactic object or a building block) beyond redshift 5 although we cannot exclude a possibility that most Ly emission is absorbed by the red damping wing of neutral intergalactic matter.

sources.summary: The review describes LAE J1044-0123 as a z=5.655 LAE with an unusually narrow Ly-alpha line, a modest inferred velocity dispersion, and a dark halo likely more massive than its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The most intriguing property of LAE J1044 0123 is that the observed emission-line width (full width at half maximum; FWHM) of redshifted Ly is only 2.2 0.3 . Since the instrumental spectral resolution is 1.7 0.1 , the intrinsic width is only 1.4 0.5 , corresponding to FWHM_ obs 52 19 km s ^ 1 or a velocity dispersion _ obs = FWHM_ obs /(2 2 ln 2 ) 22 km s ^ 1 . This value is comparable to those of luminous globular clusters (Djorgovski 1995).

If the star formation timescale is as long as the dynamical one, the stellar mass assembled in LAE J1044 0123 at z=5.655 exceeds 10^9 M_ , being comparable to the nominal dynamical mass, M_ dyn 2 10^9 M_ . Since it is quite unlikely that most mass is assembled to form stars in the system, the dark matter halo around LAE J1044 0123 would be more massive by one order of magnitude at least than the above stellar mass.

gmw_00006845.name: NGC 3626
gmw_00006845.overview: NGC 3626 is an early-type [arXiv:2107.13630] lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1306.0777] that features a companion [arXiv:2107.13630] and a broad bar structure [arXiv:1108.3662]. The galaxy also hosts a secondary bar, a clear dust ring [arXiv:1108.3662], and exhibits counterrotation between its gas and stars [arXiv:1306.0777].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3626 as an early-type galaxy with a companion excluded from measurements and an angular-momentum profile that fails the convergence criterion.

quotes.quote: BAROLO was used to derive the rotation curve for only one of the galaxies (NGC 3941), since the FAT derived rotation curve was unreliable.Two of the early--type galaxies (NGC3626 and NGC3941) have companions, which were excluded for the measurements of mass and specific angular momentum.

We find that all the galaxies except two early-type galaxies (NGC3626 and NGC6798) follow the convergence criterion. We also fit a function P = k. exp(-a/x), (which is zero at x 0 ^ + becomes constant as x ) to the last three points of the cumulative j profile. We then, take the ratio r_ k (k being baryons/gas) of j_ k at last measured point to the maximum value that P would have if extrapolated to infinity. This ratio, (i.e. the convergence factor), is calculated for all the j _ bar and j _ gas profiles and listed for all the galaxies in Tables Table4 , Table5 and Table6 . Only 2 of the early-type galaxies (NGC 3626 and NGC6798) and 1 of the ultra-diffuse galaxies have a convergence factor < 0.7.

Two of these early-type galaxies (NGC3626 and NGC6798, marked with a circle around the blue open triangles in Fig fig:S0 ) do not satisfy the convergence criterion. We note that they do not appear to have discrepant \ as that of the early-type galaxies with converged specific angular momentum curves. If the lack of convergence means that these two galaxies have a higher specific angular momentum than measured, it does not affect the result that the early–type galaxies in our sample have significantly higher \ than expected from the \ power law.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3626 as a lenticular galaxy with an outer ring and gas-star counterrotation, explaining its SED, kinematics, and morphology with a 2:1 merger.

quotes.quote: As test cases, we simulate the evolution of two such S0s: NGC 1533 and NGC 3626. The merging of two halos with mass ratio 2:1, initially just composed of DM and gas, well match their observed SEDs, their surface brightness profiles and their overall kinematics.

The peculiar kinematical features, e.g. gas-stars counter rotation in NGC 3626, depends on the halos initial impact parameters. Furthermore, our simulations allow to follow, in a fully consistent way, the transition of these S0s through the green valley in the NUV vs. M _r colour magnitude diagram, which they cross in about 3-5 Gyrs, before reaching their current position in the red sequence. We conclude that a viable mechanism driving the evolution of S0s in groups is of gravitational origin. Galaxies: evolution Galaxies: interactions Galaxies: individual: NGC 3626, NGC 1533 Method: numerical =0.5 cm The investigation of the co-evolution of galaxies and groups in the nearby Universe is of great cosmological interest since more than half of galaxies reside in groups. Since the velocity dispersion of galaxies in groups is low, i.e. comparable to the stellar velocity dispersion, mergers of galaxies are highly favoured with respect to clusters. Nearby groups provide a close-up view of phenomena driving the morphological and star formation (SF) evolution of galaxy members before they fall into clusters e.g Boselli06,Wilman09,Just10 . Among open questions, the old controversy about the nature vs. transformation origin of S0 galaxies rises above the others. Since Es and S0s are the typical inhabitants of nearby clusters, at expennse of Spirals (Sps), a debate about the transformation of Sps into S0s in such environments arose. The processes possibly driving the Sp S0 transformation are, indeed, still uncertain and debated e.g. Bekki09 . They include feedback from AGN Schawinski09 which may shut down SF, environmental effects which reduce the HI reservoir of Sps, Boselli06, Hughes09,Cortese09 and the interplay between cold gas flows and shock heating Dekel06 . Wetzeletal12 find that galaxies in groups and clusters experience no significant environmental effects until they cross within the virial radius of a more massive host halo. Boselli06 claim that the interaction with the intergalactic medium is not the origin of the cluster S0s, which likely form by Sps through gravitational interaction. Studying 116 X-ray selected galaxy groups at redshift 0.2-1, Getal13 conclude that strangulation and disk fading are insufficient to explain the observed morphological dependence on environment, and that galaxy mergers or close tidal encounters must play a role in building up the population of quenched galaxies with bulges seen in dense environments at low redshift. The comparison of clusters at z 0.1 - 0.2 with some at intermediate distance, z 0.4 - 0.5, shows that a sort of morphological conversion in the galaxy population from Sps S0s took place about 1 - 4 Gyr ago Fasano2000 . This transformation anti-correlates with the local density, i.e., in low concentration clusters the transformation happened only about 1-2 Gyr ago. The effects of the possible galaxy morphological transformation manifest themselves also in the nearly ubiquitous bimodal distribution of galaxies in the colour-magnitude diagram (CMD) e.g. Strateva01, Lewis02, Baldry04 . The red disk galaxy population should be the result of transformations of the blue, late-type galaxies via mechanisms of gas depletion and consequently of SF quenching. The galaxy transition from star forming to quiescent, is highlighted by the presence of an intermediate zone, the so--called green valley (GV), on CMDs Martin07, Wyderetal07, Fang12 . Investigating galaxies in the GV should shed light on the mechanisms governing the on-off state of the SF. S0s in groups, in particular, are key elements in identifying both the mechanisms and the environmental influence on their possible transformation. Although S0s are early-type galaxies (ETGs), so they are widely considered evolved ``red dead'' galaxies, GALEX (Galaxy Evolution Explorer) far-UV (FUV) and/or H images revealed signatures of on-going star formation in their disks in the form of outer blue ring/arm-like structures in some of them. and references therein Salim12 show a wide collection of these kind of galaxies. Signatures of ongoing ( 9 ^ +4 _ 3 ) or recent ( 47 ^ +8 _ 7 ) SF are also found in the nuclear regions of nearby ETGs e.g. Rampazzo13 . The presence of a disk and the occurrence of SF in ETGs imply that cold gas has played an important role in their evolution. In Figure HI4 we show the optical (left panels) and UV colour composite images (middle panel) of examples of nearby S0s with outer ring/arm-like structures detected in the FUV band: NGC 404 Thilker10 , NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974 Ant11_II , and NGC 1317 Gil07 . All the S0s in Figure HI4 are located in low density environments. These galaxies also have cold gas, distributed either over the main body of the galaxy or forming large-scale structures around the galaxy, as shown by HI contours over plotted in the right panels of Figure HI4 . The paper explores the evolution of these S0s, using SPH simulations accounting for chemo-photometric evolution, i.e providing the SED at each evolutionary time (snapshot). The SED takes into account chemical evolution, stellar emission, internal extinction and re-emission by dust in a self-consistent way, as already described in MC03, and references therein marilena , and marilena2 . The SED extends over four orders of magnitude in wavelength, i.e., from 0.1 to 1000 m. Each simulation self-consistently provides morphological, dynamic and chemo-photometric evolution. Simulations of groups, focusing on galaxy morphological transformation have been approached with different techniques (e.g. Kawata08, Bekki11,Villalobos12 ). Kawata08 used a cosmological chemo-dynamical simulation to study how the group environment affects the SF properties of a disk galaxy. Bekki11 , starting from already formed galaxies, show that Sps in group environments can be strongly influenced by repetitive, slow encounters so that galaxies with thin discs can be transformed into thick disks, and gas poor S0s. Villalobos12 perform 17 experiments in order to study the general evolution of disc galaxies, composed of a stellar disc embedded in a DM halo, within a group environment. This is modelled as an N-body DM halo following a NFW97 density profile including a spherically symmetric stellar component at its centre. None of the quoted simulations explore the merger scenario combining the chemo-dynamical code with evolutionary population synthesis (EPS) models providing the galaxy SED at each evolutionary time (snapshot hereafter). This upgrade in galaxy simulations is the novelty of this paper. As a case study we focus on simulations of two S0s with ring/arm like structures, detected in the far UV and/or narrow band optical images, immersed in extended HI emission, namely NGC 1533 (Fig. HI4 ) and NGC 3626 (Fig. HI4_II ). Both galaxies are members of groups Ant12,Ant11 . NGC 3626, is located within the virial radius of USGC U376 ( Rametal02 , Mazzei et al. 2013, in prep.) and NGC 1533 is a member of the Dorado group. They have extended SEDs, from FUV to far-IR (FIR), that we will use, together with their B-band absolute magnitude, to constrain our simulations. The detailed surface photometry and the 2D kinematical properties of the gas and stars of NGC 3626, also available from the literature, will be a further constraint. The SPH simulations best-fitting their global properties are selected over a large grid of encounters from halos initially composed of DM and gas. In addition, our simulations highlight the evolutionary path through the GV in the CMD, of such galaxies. The structure of the paper is as follows. Section 2 describes the observed properties of NGC 3626 and NGC 1533. Section 3 provides the details of our grid of SPH simulations. In Section 4 we compare the global measured galaxy properties with the results of our simulations. Section 5 discusses the galaxy evolution provided by the simulation focusing on the path of the near-UV(NUV)/optical colours in the ( NUV- ) vs. M _r CMD. In Section 6 we draw our conclusions. The relevant properties of NGC 1533 and NGC 3626 are summarized in Table tab:obs_e : columns 2 and 3 give the morphological type and the cosmological distance from NED NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu ; column 4 the apparent diameter corrected for galactic extinction and inclination, D_ 25 , from HYPERLEDA http://leda.univ-lyon.fr1 , Patetal03 ; column 5 the effective radius, _e from the RC3 catalogue DV91 , column 6 the absolute B-band magnitude derived from the mean distance modulus computed from redshift-independent distance measurements ( NED ), and column 7 the maximum rotation velocity corrected for inclination from HYPERLEDA . D_ 25 and r_e are reported in kpc using cosmology corrected scales from NED with cosmological parameters H _0 =70 km/s/Mpc, _ matter = 0.27, _ vacuum =0.73. NGC 1533 is a SB 0- in the RC3 catalogue DV91 , a SB 02(2)/ SBa in the RSA catalogue Sandage87 , and a (L)SB(rs)0^0 in the NED database. This latter classification implies a large lens outside the bar and an inner ring Laurietal06 . Ant11_II found a prominent outer ring in the UV images extending from 39 ^" to 80 " , and consisting of young ( 200 Myr old) stellar populations. More details on GALEX observations are given in Ant11 . Werketal10 found a large-scale distribution of HI around the galaxy, as shown in Fig. HI4 . RW03 reported HI arcs around the galaxy, spanning from 2 ' to 11 ' .7 outside the FUV ring, with a mass of 9.3 10 ^8 M _ (their Table A1). They concluded that the HI is most likely the remnant of a tidally disrupted galaxy. This HI mass estimate increases by at least 6 times adopting the distance in dG07 . From optical spectroscopy in the central region of this galaxy, Ann07 derived an age of 11.9 6.9 Gyr. dG07 describes NGC 1533 as a galaxy which is completing a transition from late to early type: it is red, but not completely dead. NGC 3626 is a lenticular galaxy with an outer (quite faint) ring (RLAT+..) in the RC3 catalogue DV91 , a normal Sa in the RSA catalogue Sandage87 and a normal S0 galaxy in the NED classification (Table tab:obs_e ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3626's outer profile as I and describes bar evidence, dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3626 (I; (R)SA(rs)0 ^+ ) : Although classified as unbarred, this galaxy has a broad bar structure of radial length between 21 (2 kpc) and 47 (4.4 kpc) (Figure fig:n3626 ), surrounded by a pseudo-ring at 71 (6.7 kpc) radius. It also has a secondary bar of radius 4 (400 pc), along with a ring at 5.2 (490 pc) and a clear dust ring at 15 (1.4 kpc). No break in the profile is seen out to at least 6 scalelengths.

gmw_00006846.name: J111100
gmw_00006846.overview: J111100 is a morphologically disturbed galaxy with tidal features that is a possible merger remnant [arXiv:1410.6552]. While its stars rotate regularly, the gas exhibits irregular kinematics and an offset ionized cloud likely powered by the central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1410.6552].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J111100 as a disturbed possible merger remnant with double-peaked emission, irregular gas kinematics, and an offset ionized cloud likely powered by the central AGN.

quotes.quote: J111100 The galaxy J111100 shows a prominent double peak in its emission line flux. The galaxy appears morphologically disturbed with tidal features and is a possible merger remnant. The distribution of the \ flux compared to the continuum light from the blue data is shown in Figure J111100_extra (top right).

When we examine the galaxy's kinematics we find that the stars are rotating regularly, but that the gas shows no large scale rotation and has several discontinuities (see Figure J111100 ). An average spectrum from a 3 3 spaxel bin is plotted in Figure J111100_extra centred on the two \ flux peaks. The central spectrum is very broad with blended emission lines, this is indicative of an outflow driven by the central AGN or potentially a contribution from the BLR.

We are thus left with the hypothesis that the offset emission line flux is due to gas ionized by the central AGN. The surprising feature here is the high luminosity of the offset emission-line peak at a distance of at least 6 kpc (projected) from the nucleus.

gmw_00006847.name: ID: 10100707
gmw_00006847.overview: ID: 10100707 is a massive, dust-rich galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.38 [arXiv:2305.09709]. It exhibits an unusually low gas-to-dust ratio for its metallicity, leading to its characterization as a main-sequence dust-giant [arXiv:2305.09709].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes ID: 10100707 as a z=1.38 dust-rich massive galaxy whose gas-to-dust ratio is unusually low, making it a main-sequence dust-giant rather than a gas giant.

quotes.quote: NOEMA Observations To confirm or rule out the high M_ gas , we have used IRAM NOEMA to conduct observations of the CO (2-1) line transition a COSMOS galaxy ID: 10100707 sdc2 , with a photometric redshift of z_ phot =1.35 from the COSMOS2015 catalog laigle2016 .

Our findings indicate that ID: 10100707 displays a _ GDR that is 2.5 times lower than what is expected from the scaling relations for star-forming galaxies, given the metallicity we have estimated ( Z_ ). For our galaxy to follow the scaling relations with its _ GDR , it would require a super-solar metallicity of >3 Z_ .

We find that the M_ gas /M_* ratio is marginally consistent with expected values, while the dust reservoir and _ GDR are abnormal compared to galaxies of similar mass and redshift. Therefore, it would be more accurate to refer to ID: 10100707 as a "dust-giant". Finally, in the right panel of fig:gdr we examine the position of our galaxy on the /M_ dust and _ GDR vs M_ dust diagram.

gmw_00006848.name: UGC 6016
gmw_00006848.overview: UGC 6016 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a large but diffuse ultraviolet envelope [arXiv:1302.5011]. The galaxy is very faint in the infrared, and its distance remains highly uncertain [arXiv:1302.5011].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 6016 as a faint infrared dwarf with a diffuse UV envelope, weak filter detections, poorly constrained parameters, and uncertain distance.

quotes.quote: UGC 6016: This dwarf galaxy is very faint in the IR, but has a large but diffuse UV envelope. Due to its lack of significant detections in several of our filters and in ancillary IRAS , we cannot constrain the galaxy parameters very tightly. Its distance is also the most uncertain of the sample as it did not have a distance modulus or a recessional velocity in the PSCz catalog.

gmw_00006849.name: NGC 3786/88
gmw_00006849.overview: NGC 3786/88 is a galaxy pair with very similar ultraviolet and near-infrared fluxes [arXiv:1302.5011]. Within this pair, NGC 3786 is a Seyfert galaxy that hosts a bright nuclear region and a partial ring of star-forming regions [arXiv:1302.5011].

sources.summary: The paper compares the pair’s UV/NIR and Herschel fluxes, similar infrared luminosities, missing ancillary fluxes, and NGC 3786’s Seyfert nucleus and partial star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 3786/88: The galaxies in this pair have very similar UV NIR fluxes, but NGC 3788, which is more edge-on, has higher fluxes in the Herschel bands. However, their best-fit SEDs have similar infrared luminosities, likely due to the relatively high contribution of the warm dust in the model for NGC 3786 compared to NGC 3788. Neither galaxy has ancillary IRAS or UBV fluxes. NGC 3786 is a Seyfert galaxy, with a bright nuclear region showing [ Ne v ] emission in its IRS spectrum and a partial ring of star-forming regions. Its decomposition requires an AGN contribution of 7 and 26 to the total IR luminosity and MIR luminosity respectively.

gmw_00006850.name: M51A
gmw_00006850.overview: M51A, the larger galaxy in the Whirlpool system, is a spiral galaxy with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1302.5011]. It exhibits a consistent multiwavelength morphology, featuring more inter-arm filamentary emission and a greater spatial extent in the ultraviolet than in the infrared [arXiv:1302.5011].

sources.summary: The paper describes M51A as the larger Whirlpool component, with consistent multiwavelength morphology, stronger UV extent, no fitted AGN infrared contribution, and companion-flux subtraction.

quotes.quote: M51A: The Whirlpool Galaxy is the larger galaxy in this well-studied system. It has quite consistent morphology across the wavelengths, but with more inter-arm filamentary emission and greater extent in the UV than in the IR. The decomposition of its IR spectrum is best fit without an AGN contribution, despite its LINER nucleus. This is one of the systems where the flux from the smaller galaxy was subtracted from the aperture of the large galaxy.

gmw_00006851.name: M51B
gmw_00006851.overview: M51B is a companion galaxy to the Whirlpool Galaxy [arXiv:1302.5011]. It is dominated by early-type stars, exhibits very little ultraviolet emission, and has far-infrared emission that is confined to its nucleus [arXiv:1302.5011].

sources.summary: The paper describes M51B as the smaller Whirlpool companion, dominated by early-type stars, with little UV emission, nuclear far-infrared emission, and an omitted discrepant MIPS measurement.

quotes.quote: M51B: The smaller companion to the Whirlpool Galaxy, M51B is dominated by early-type stars and has very little UV emission. Its FIR emission is confined to its nucleus. Its MIPS 160 measurement was a factor of three lower than the PACS measurement, and we opted to omit it from the fits.

gmw_00006852.name: NGC 5394/95
gmw_00006852.overview: NGC 5394/95, also known as Arp 84, is an interacting galaxy pair in an early stage of interaction [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:2201.02270]. Classified as a minor merger, the system features a LINER nucleus in NGC 5395 and a pair of tidal tails extending from NGC 5394 [arXiv:1302.5011, arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5394/95 as the most distant pair, with NGC 5394 tidal tails, an NGC 5395 LINER nucleus, small AGN infrared contributions, and aperture subtraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 5394/95: The smaller galaxy of our most distant pair, NGC 5394 shows a beautiful pair of tidal tails, especially in the UV. NGC 5395 has a LINER nucleus, which requires only a small AGN contributions of 3 and 12 to the galaxy's total IR and MIR luminosities respectively in the DECOMPIR fits. This is the other system where the small galaxy flux needed to be subtracted from the large galaxy aperture.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 84 as one of four interacting systems, classifies it as early-stage, and interprets it as a minor merger using the adopted mass ratio.

quotes.quote: An interacting galaxy sample consists of four systems: Arp 84, VV 219, VV 254, and the Antennae Galaxies.
Based on our definition, Arp 84, VV 219, and VV 254 are the early stage of interaction, while the Antennae Galaxies are in the mid stage.
If we use this mass ratio of 4, Arp 84 is a minor merger (a mass ratio > 3).
